It proposes an efficient and lightweight intelligent solution based on the You only look Once Version8 (YOLOv8) seg model to realize automated detection and precise segmentation of cracks, while integrating insights from advanced structural health monitoring research such as model ensembling to enhance recall and integration with Digital Twins for predictive maintenance. The research process includes dataset integration and standardization by combining public datasets like Crack500 [4] and DeepCrack [5] to cover diverse crack scenarios, baseline model training with YOLOv8n seg (train13), iterative optimization through targeted data augmentation including 90 degree random rotation and vertical horizontal flipping to mitigate orientation bias, and retraining of the final model (train142).Comprehensive performance evaluations are conducted, including comparisons between the final model and the baseline model, comparisons with two core models namely CrackDenseLinkNet and Pytorch UNet, and an ablation study on single stage versus two stage segmentation strategies. Experimental results show that the final model achieves a 191 percent increase in recall from 0.184 to 0.536 and a 48 percent increase in mAP at IoU 0.5 to 0.95 compared to the baseline, effectively solving the problem of missed detections. Additionally, the YOLOv8 seg model maintains a lightweight architecture with about 3.2 million parameters, high inference speed exceeding 30 frames per second on ordinary GPUs and low memory usage, balancing accuracy and efficiency. This solution provides a practical tool for automated inspection of bridges, dams and high rise buildings, and lays a foundation for subsequent integration with 3D structural analysis such as Finite Element Analysis using Pynite to assess building safety.

title:
8.2 A 32Gb/s 12.35Tb/s/mm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    0.36pJ/b UCIe-Like Die-to-Die Interface Featuring Edge-Triggered Transceivers in 3nm with Active LSI Packaging
authors: Wei-Chih Chen, Mu-Shan Lin, Chien-Chun Tsai, Shenggao Li, Wei-Shuo Lin, Yu-Jie Huang, Nai-Chen Cheng, Yu-Chi Chen, Wen-Hung Huang, Chin-Hua Wen, Hsin-Hung Kuo, Han-Tzung Ke, Jie-Ren Huang, Chang-Yi Li, Sheng-Tsung Lai, Shu-Chun Yang, Kuan-Ting Chou, Pei-Chen Chiou, Tsung-Hsien Tsai, Yi-Ting Chen, Yen-Ming Chen, Kenny Cheng-Hsiang Hsieh
abstract: This paper presents edge-triggered transceivers (ETT), enabling a 32Gb UCle-like chiplet interconnect when integrated within an active local silicon interconnect (aLSI). The ETT achieves power consumption of 0.07pJ/b and supports compact top-die TX/RX designs while optimizing 0.36pJ/b energy efficiency and 12.35Tb/s/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area bandwidth density. It demonstrates a 32Gb/s die-to-die link at 0.75V with 20.46ps eye width (65% UI) and 530mV eye height across 64 lanes in a 3nm CMOS process.

title: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
authors: N/A

title: 20.5 A 24-to-27.5GHz Self-Adaptive Load-Modulated Balanced Amplifier for Integrated Communication, Sensing, and Power Transfer Scenarios
authors: Luqi Yu, Peng Chen, Yucheng Yu, Gaojing Zhang, Xiaoyu Lu, Xiang Zhang, Jixin Chen, Xiaowei Zhu, Chao Yu, Wei Hong
abstract: A 24-to-27.5GHz self-adaptive load-modulated balanced amplifier (SALMBA) is proposed for 6G integrated communication, sensing, and power-transfer scenarios. Based on the proposed self-adaptive power divider, the power divide ratio can dynamically change according to the input power levels, and the back-off PAE and saturated performance of the SALMBA can be significantly improved. The SALMBA achieves the saturated PAE of 28.5 to 33%, and 10 dB OPBO PAE of 15.3 to 22.1% over 24 to 27.5 GHz.

title: Research on Online Detection Method for Aging Status of Optocouplers on Circuit Boards
authors: Zhang Tao, Zhang Yunwei, Xiao Minghui, Ma Shu, Yu Haiyang, Wang Qiang, Xia Ming, Tang Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: Optocouplers are key isolation components in power system automation equipment. The susceptibility to aging and performance degradation directly affects the reliability of functions such as relay protection, fault diagnosis, and system selfhealing, necessitating online detection of their aging status. Traditional Current Transfer Ratio (CTR) detection methods require shutdown and disassembly of components, making online detection impossible. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an online detection method for the aging status of optocouplers suitable for the circuit board environment, based on onboard electrical parameter measurements. Through circuit analysis, this method transforms the CTR parameter into a relationship between voltage and resistance that is easy to measure online, and defines a key coefficient K characterizing the degree of aging. Specific detection models are established for the different output characteristics of high-speed and ordinary optocouplers. Experimental results show that by monitoring the change in the K value, the performance degradation trend of optocouplers on circuit boards can be effectively identified, and there is a clear corresponding relationship between the K value and the aging state. This method achieves non-destructive state assessment on power system boards, providing a new technical approach for predictive maintenance and life-cycle management of power grid automation equipment, possessing significant engineering application value.

title: MoTeNet: Motion-Temporal Network for Dynamic Hand Gesture Recognition on Point Clouds
authors: Qiuxia Wu, Xinran Xie, Sangni Xu, Wenxiong Kang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: As deep learning techniques are increasingly applied to gesture recognition, point cloud-based dynamic gesture recognition methods have attracted significant attention. However, many existing approaches overlook per-point motion features and the overall temporal dynamics of gestures, thereby failing to fully exploit motion and temporal cues embedded in point cloud sequences. To address this issue, we propose Motion-Temporal Network (MoTeNet), a novel framework for dynamic gesture recognition on point clouds, which preserves spatial structural information while modeling both global frame-level temporal changes and per-point motion. MoTeNet extracts spatial geometric features through a Hierarchical Graph Convolution (HGC) module, and incorporates a Motion Feature Encoding Module (MFEM) to encode point-level motion features across adjacent frames. This approach provides fine-grained dynamic information for subsequent temporal modeling. Furthermore, an Adaptive Temporal Feature Fusion (ATFF) module integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformers to adaptively fuse short-term and long-term temporal dependencies, enabling comprehensive modeling of the dynamic evolution of gestures. Experimental results demonstrate that MoTeNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on point cloud gesture recognition benchmarks, including SHREC’17, DHG, and NVGesture, with ablation studies further validating the effectiveness of the proposed framework.

title: Analyzing Capacitive Swipe Gesture towards User Identification
authors: Kiran KC, Md Shafaeat Hossain, Abu Saleh Md Tayeen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Traditional physical biometrics—such as fingerprints, facial recognition, and iris scans—have long been utilized for user identification in areas like border control, military operations, law enforcement, and public safety. However, the rise of smartphone technology has introduced new avenues for security research. One emerging area is behavioral biometrics, particularly the use of touchscreen interaction data as a more accessible and user-friendly identification method. In this context, our study focuses on a novel form of touchscreen input: capacitive swipe gestures for user identification. We compiled a comprehensive dataset of capacitive swipe gestures collected over multiple sessions from 30 participants. To evaluate this modality, we conducted thorough experiments using established machine learning algorithms, including Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, and XGBoost. Additionally, we developed a new preprocessing algorithm tailored for capacitive swipe data. Our findings reveal that this algorithm significantly enhances identification performance compared to existing methods. Overall, our results highlight the strong potential of capacitive swipe gestures as a viable biometric modality for user identification.

title: Optimized Handwritten Digit Recognition Using Transfer Learning for Smart License Plate and Text Detection
authors: M Kathiravan, Terrance Frederick Fernandez, M Manikandan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Handwritten digit recognition has become more important in many applications. Handwritten digit categorization and identification are essential to mobile number verification, Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR), Optical Character Recognition (OCR), and PIN code verification. Current systems manage handwritten digit size and style variations. Traditional handwritten digit recognition approaches rely on CNNs, which have 95.67 percent accuracy. Numerous frameworks have been proposed to enhance the effectiveness and accuracy of the models. The proposed approach tries to enhance the model through the application of transfer learning and highly intricate pre-trained CNN architectures like MobileNetV2 and ImageNet. The model demonstrates the flexibility of the system with the implementation of tests on real-world tasks such as OCR and ANPR. Our system could recognize both printed text and handwritten digits on license plates, demonstrating its flexibility. Our research not only improves digit recognition but also computer vision, deep learning, and transfer learning for specialized recognition tasks. At 99%, the model outperforms the baseline model by 4.5 percentage points.

title: Fully Integrated Mm-Scale 5G RF MIMO Harvester with −40dBm Sensitivity and Spatial MPPT via Hybrid Transformer-Based Combining/Shifting
authors: Andrea Ballo, Ruiyuan Yang, Karim Ali, Massimo Bruno Alioto
abstract: A MIMO RF harvester in the 5G 28-GHz band with spatial MPPT is introduced to self-align high-gain harvesting direction with maximum power availability. Spatial scanning is enabled by a hybrid 2-level RF/DC antenna power combining and phase shifting based on transformers, while eliminating conventional off-chip capacitor(s). A 22nm test-chip shows −40dBm harvesting sensitivity, and power conversion efficiency of 56.7% at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0 \text{dBm}(1.3 \times$</tex> better than prior art at 28GHz).

title: A 16.4nJ/Class Patient-Independent Prototype-Based Spatio-Temporal CNN Processor with Forward-Inference-Based Adaptation for Robust and Low-Latency Seizure Detection
authors: Yang Wang, Longyang Lin, Jerald Yoo, Jiamin Li
abstract: We present a patient-independent, prototype-based spatio-temporal CNN processor for seizure detection, achieving high accuracy without patient-specific data, at an energy of 16.4nJ/class and latency of <120 ms. An optional forward-inference-based zero-shot adaptation improves robustness to variability and low-quality inputs. The 40nm IC achieves 94.3%/94.9% sensitivity/specificity without patient data, improving to 95.4%/97.9% with adaptation.

title: A Multi-Objective Optimization-Based Reinforcement Learning Framework for Intelligent Energy Storage System Scheduling
authors: Ziyan Li, Zheng Zhou, Fuliang Ma, Xiaohong Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy in modern power systems, energy storage systems (ESS) play a critical role in balancing supply and demand, enhancing renewable energy utilization, and ensuring economic operation. This paper proposes a multi-objective reinforcement learning (MORL) framework for intelligent scheduling of ESS, integrating operating cost minimization, renewable energy curtailment reduction, and battery degradation mitigation into a unified learning objective. The ESS scheduling process is modeled as a constrained Markov decision process, considering electricity prices, load demand, renewable generation, and state-of-charge dynamics. A multi-objective reward function with normalized weight coefficients is designed using weighted-sum scalarization to guide the learning agent in obtaining balanced and sustainable control policies. Simulation results under realistic load and renewable scenarios demonstrate that the proposed MORL approach outperforms rule-based (RB) and single-objective reinforcement learning (SORL) strategies. Specifically, the proposed method reduces operating costs by 17.7 % compared with RB and 6.5 % compared with SORL, decreases renewable energy curtailment from 12.8 % (RB) and 9.6 % (SORL) to 5.1 %, and lowers the battery degradation index by 19.6 % relative to SORL, thereby extending expected battery lifespan. These results demonstrate that the MORL framework provides an effective solution for coordinated ESS scheduling in renewable-dominated power systems, offering a promising pathway toward sustainable and economically viable grid operation.

title: Low-Frequency Electric Field Communication System Based on Optomechanical Levitation
authors: Yifan Wang, Dun Qian, Mingquan Zeng, Wenbo Li, Peng Chen, Huizhu Hu, Jinsheng Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: We proposes a novel low-frequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<300 \text{kHz})$</tex> wireless communication scheme that utilizes optically captured charged nanoparticles to replace traditional antennas for electric field signal reception. By establishing a particle displacement-electric field transfer model and optimizing the demodulation algorithm, a sensitivity of 0.36 and zero-bit error rate transmission are achieved in vacuum environment of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 10^{-7} \text{mbar}$</tex>. The system supports error-free transmission of 3.75 kbps 2 FSK signals, with a 98.7% match between the theoretical and measured bit error rate, representing a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6}$</tex>-fold improvement compared to traditional capacitively coupled sensors. It overcomes the receiver size bottleneck caused by kilometer-level wavelengths, reducing the size by six orders of magnitude compared to traditional loop antennas. This provides a revolutionary miniaturized receiver solution for underwater communication and deep space exploration.

title: Process Development for Acoustic MEMS Resonator for RF Filter Applications
authors: Sujoy Paul, Vinit Kumar Yadav, Dhiman Mallick
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Surface and bulk acoustic wave (SAW and BAW) resonators are necessary elements in modern wireless communication, enabling precise frequency band selection with minimal signal distortion. This study presents a scalable fabrication method which solves significant challenges related to piezoelectric material integration, sub-micron lithography, and metal lift-off. A low-cost thermocompression substrate bonding technique utilizing 50nm ultrathin gold (Au) layer is implemented, enabling effective integration of bulk 128° Y-cut lithium niobate (LiNbO₃) onto silicon substrate. The bonded interface concurrently functions as the bottom electrode. To achieve high-resolution patterning of the acoustic MEMS resonators on LiNbO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> substrates, we further compare the performances of S1813 and AZ5214E photoresists in defining interdigitated transducers (IDTs) with a pitch of 10 μm, finger width of 5 μm, finger gap of 5 μm, and a metallization ratio of 50%. S1813 produces tapered resist profile, whereas AZ5214E results in well-defined features with an undercut angle of 70° and a 0.25 μm lateral offset. This profile allows the precise lift-off of titanium (Ti)/Au (40 nm/40 nm) film, ensuring accurate IDT formation.

title:
12.9 A 10.2-to-16.2GHz Dual-Mode-Transformer-Based Wideband Series-Resonance VCO Achieving >201.1dBc/Hz FoM
                    <sub>T</sub>
                    at a 10MHz Offset
authors: Yang Li, Yanchao Liu, Kaihang Wang, Dong Pu, Xiaohua Yu, Ronghua Ni
abstract: This work presents a 28nm CMOS SR-VCO enabled by a dual-mode transformer with magnetic control, achieving a wide tuning range of 10.2 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.2 \text{GHz}(45.2 \%)$</tex> and a high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FoM}_{\mathrm{T}} / \text{FoM}_{\text{AT}} {@} 10 \text{MHz}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$201.1 / 207.7 \text{dBc} / \text{Hz}$</tex>. A transformer optimization method is introduced to ensure consistent performance across modes, achieving less than 2 dB variation in measured PN and FoM at 10 MHz. A sampling PLL incorporating the presented SR-VCO demonstrates rms jitter <18.1fs and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FoM}_{\text{JT}}$</tex> of −265.8 dB.

title: Passivation Layer Thickness-Dependent Thermal Dissipation Properties of Source-Connected Field-Plated AlGaN/GaN HEMT
authors: Vaidehi Vijay Painter, Raphael Sommet, Jean-Christophe Nallatamby, P. Vigneshwara Raja
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This report utilizes validated TCAD simulations to explore the thermal impact of implementing source-connected field-plate (FP) architecture to 150 nm AlGaN/GaN HEMT. A significant finding is the identification of a dynamic heating mechanism unique to the FP-equipped HEMT. The main self-heating zone is shown to physically migrate across the structure as drain voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf>) is increased. This translocation results in a complex, multi-stage thermal resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) profile. A special emphasis on passivation layer thickness (t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SiN</inf>) effect on R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>, reveals a fundamental engineering trade-off. A thicker layer (> 150 nm) offers superior electrical insulation but simultaneously creates a thermal barrier, thus thermally isolating the FP and rising channel temperature. Conversely, a thinner layer improves heat dissipation but elevates the electric field stress at the FP edge and augments parasitic capacitance. A deeply integrated co-design strategy is therefore an essential requirement for developing robust RF GaN HEMT devices.

title: Detection of Blacktopped Counterfeit ICs Using Surface Texture Analysis
authors: John M. Klamut, Mai Abdelhakim, Samuel J. Dickerson, Ashish Avachat, Heng Ban, Philip Santillo
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Modern technology depends on a complex and globally distributed electronic supply chain, which is increasingly threatened by the infiltration of counterfeit integrated circuits (ICs). These unauthorized components, when introduced into critical systems and infrastructure, can create severe vulnerabilities that compromise system integrity and pose significant risks to critical operations. A common form of counterfeit is the “blacktopped” IC or resurfaced IC, where the original surface markings are removed and replaced to conceal the component's true identity, origin, or functional specifications. This paper focuses on detecting blacktopped ICs. Some counterfeit detection approaches rely on manual inspection, which is an expensive and time-consuming process. Other methods utilize tools of machine learning, deep learning, and image processing. A key challenge with machine learning-based approaches is that they require a lot of data and often struggle with limited training samples. Moreover, existing automated techniques require a known “golden” IC for counterfeit detection. As an effort to address this issue, we propose a method for detecting blacktopped ICs using texture analysis of indents. Our approach eliminates the dependency on golden references, offering a simple, efficient, and scalable solution for counterfeit detection. More specifically, the proposed method relies on the fact that the indent on the IC should have a different texture than the rest of the surface. We show that, by incorporating this prior knowledge and by utilizing texture analysis, blacktopped ICs can be detected effectively at 95.2 % accuracy. We examined the approach on actual IC samples, some of which were counterfeit.

title: Smart Pillow for Sleep Management
authors: Abinayaa S S, Logitha B, Manikandan S, L Dhiviyalakshmi, N. Sathish Kumar
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: World- wide around 10-15% of the adults are becoming insomniac or suffer chronic sleep disturbances/ disorders which significantly affect their health and social wellbeing [1]. Most of the time in chronic diseases conditions the most under rated cause is sleep but in contrast it has a greater impact and if not addressed at the initial stage it leads to adverse effect on the health [2]. Continuous monitoring or tracking of sleep habits is challenging with the existing smart pillows as they capture only limited sleep information like duration and movement. The soundness of sleep is not available to appreciate the quality of sleep. The Smart Pillow for Sleep monitoring and control is an interactive and innovative solution by integrating high-order sensors -piezoelectric sensors to detect change in pressure and an accelerometer to detect movement, with a speaker for binaural beats are designed to initiate and retain sleep This enables real-time intervention and person-specific feedback, thereby more proactive and effective sleep control. By treating sleeping habits in an integrated way, this pillow has the potential to greatly enhance the quality of sleep as well as add long- term health advantages.

title: Towards Robust and Efficient Continuous Face Authentication via Disentangled Representation Learning and Adaptive Identity Completion
authors: Xiang Li, Chi Xu, Yasushi Yagi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: In this paper, we focus on the problem of continuous face authentication, which aims to verify a user’s identity persistently over time from streaming or sequential facial inputs. Unlike conventional face authentication methods that operate in a one-time or set-to-set manner, our approach produces segment-wise identity decisions, enabling real-time identity monitoring in dynamic scenarios. To tackle challenges such as temporal variation and identity-irrelevant noise (e.g., pose, blur, occlusion), we propose a novel three-stage framework comprising: (1) disentangled representation learning to suppress identity-irrelevant components in pre-trained embeddings, (2) attention-based intra-segment aggregation to extract robust identity cues within each segment, and (3) inter-segment adaptive identity feature completion to incrementally integrate new segments with prior identity representations for stable and efficient prediction. Extensive experiments on IJB-B, IJB-C benchmark datasets, and our own collected dataset under real-world video conferencing conditions demonstrate that our method achieves competitive accuracy with notable improvements in inference efficiency (up to 2× speed-up) over state-of-the-art baselines. These results validate the effectiveness and practicality of our framework for real-world continuous authentication systems.

title: Robust IoT Botnet Detection via Residual Graph Attention Networks and Gradient Boosting: A Topology-Aware Hybrid Framework
authors: G. Priyanka, K. Vanitha
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The growing number of devices in IoT has led to an expanding "attack surface" where botnet and DDoS attacks can be launched from multiple areas. Traditional security systems based on centralized architecture do not support the high dimensional data and limited resources associated with edge computing in modern IoT networks. While several recent studies have reported nearly perfect detection rates they also report significant amounts of data leakage and model overfitting related to using features such as identifiers instead of identifying behavioral patterns of the nodes within the network. This study presents ResGAT-XGB – a hybrid system combining the structural learning ability of Residual Graph Attention Networks (ResGAT) with the classification efficiency of Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) for intrusion detection in IoT networks. The experimental work presented in this study employed a rigorous preprocessing step where correlated identifiers were removed, and random jitter was added to the network to simulate the behavior of a real world IoT network. Once a robust representation of the topology of the network was generated at each node, the nodes were classified by XGBoost. The experimental results obtained using the BoT-IoT dataset demonstrated that ResGAT-XGB achieved high accuracy when faced with adversarial noise and differentiated between morphologically similar attacks while achieving 100% detection of normal traffic. As a result of this study there is now a bridge formed between deep representation learning and realistic intrusion detection.

title: Demonstration of Reliable Operation of GaN-Based UV-A MSM Photodetector at Elevated Temperature
authors: Balkrishna Choubey, Kankat Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: In this article, we report the fabrication and characterization of a gallium nitride-based metal– semiconductor-metal ultraviolet (UV) photodetector (PD). The device exhibits an exceptionally low dark current of 1.7 pA at 5 V bias at room temperature (RT) and remains below 5 pA at 150°C, demonstrating suitability for high-temperature operation. The PD shows a sharp responsivity cutoff at ~365 nm, with a peak responsivity of 0.2 A/W at RT and 0.198 A/W at 150°C. The UV-to-visible rejection ratio reaches 2×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at RT and 2.16×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at 150°C, while the external quantum efficiency remains constant at both RT (68%) and 150°C (67.6%). The photocurrent-to-dark current ratio is 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> at RT and maintains 1×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at elevated temperature. The detectivity remains on the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sup> Jones under both RT and high- temperature (HT) conditions. These results confirm the excellent thermal stability, high sensitivity, and robustness of the PD in HT environments.

title: Images in Motion?: A First Look Into Video Leakage in Collaborative Deep Learning
authors: Md Fazle Rasul, Alanood Alqobaisi, Bruhadeshwar Bezawada, Indrakshi Ray
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) allows multiple entities to train a shared model collaboratively. Its core, privacy-preserving principle is that participants only exchange model updates, such as gradients, and never their raw, sensitive data. This approach is fundamental for applications in domains where privacy and confidentiality are important. However, the security of this very mechanism is threatened by gradient inversion attacks, which can reverse-engineer private training data directly from the shared gradients, defeating the purpose of FL. While the impact of these attacks is known for image, text, and tabular data, their effect on video data remains an unexamined area of research. This paper presents the first analysis of video data leakage in FL using gradient inversion attacks. We evaluate two common video classification approaches: one employing pretrained feature extractors and another that processes raw video frames with simple transformations. Our initial results indicate that the use of feature extractors offers greater resilience against gradient inversion attacks. We also demonstrate that image super-resolution techniques can enhance the frames extracted through gradient inversion attacks, enabling attackers to reconstruct higher-quality videos. Our experiments validate this across scenarios where the attacker has access to zero, one, or more reference frames from the target environment. We find that although feature extractors make attacks more challenging, leakage is still possible if the classifier lacks sufficient complexity. We, therefore, conclude that video data leakage in FL is a viable threat, and the conditions under which it occurs warrant further investigation.

title: Fault Type Inference for Transmission Lines via Cross-Attention Enhanced Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: Qi Xu, Haichao Peng, Guangxin Zhang, Yuhang Liu, Changlu Yu, Minzhen Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Accurate fault type inference for transmission lines is critical for maintaining the safety and reliability of modern power grids. With the rapid deployment of sensing technologies, massive heterogeneous data such as infrared images, visible-light images, acoustic signals, and electrical measurements are being collected from transmission systems. Traditional unimodal approaches are often limited in performance due to information incompleteness and noise. In this paper, we propose a novel fault type inference framework based on cross-attention enhanced multimodal data fusion. The framework employs a Transformer-based architecture to extract and align deep features from multiple modalities. A cross-attention mechanism is introduced to capture inter-modal correlations and enhance semantic consistency across the data sources. Experimental results on a constructed multimodal fault dataset demonstrate that our method outperforms several state-of-the-art baselines in terms of accuracy and robustness, especially under noisy or missing modality conditions. The proposed method provides a promising direction for intelligent fault diagnosis in complex transmission environments and lays the groundwork for future development of reliable, multimodal-aware power system monitoring solutions.

title: Forecasting in Complex Systems Using Causality-Integrated DMD Techniques
authors: Yu Wu, Shuwei Wan, Xiaowei Yang, Jian Zhang
abstract: Dynamic Mode Decomposition (DMD) has become a widely adopted technique for identifying dominant patterns that encapsulate the fundamental behavior of complex systems using observed data. Despite its popularity, DMD’s effectiveness is highly contingent upon the structure and fidelity of the input time series. To enhance the stability and generalization of the DMD framework, we incorporate two foundational conditions: Persistence of Excitation (PE) and the Granger Causality Test (GCT). By leveraging a Hankel matrix-based embedding, we extend the dynamic system’s representation by integrating additional latent variables, thereby enriching the modal subspace. The PE condition offers guidance on the minimum trajectory length necessary for accurate estimation of system dynamics, while GCT serves as a tool for determining the suitable model complexity by uncovering directional dependencies among variables. Although PE provides a basis for constructing approximate linear dynamics, it is the GCT-filtered causal structures that support trustworthy long-term forecasting. We apply this improved method to the task of Coherency Identification (CI) within multi-machine power systems (MMPS), which is vital for analyzing transient stability. Empirical results based on a 22-bus, six-generator test network illustrate how PE effectively captures key dynamic modes and confirm the importance of GCT in achieving model reliability and predictive performance.

title: Comprehensive Characterization of Inkjet Printer Ag Film on Paper Substrate for Sensor Electrodes
authors: Akanksha Arya, Sanjeev Kumar Manhas
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: Silver is a widely utilized metal in printed electronics for heaters and electrodes due to its excellent electrical conductivity and chemical stability. However, most studies are dependent on commercially available nano-silver inks, which are expensive and hence limit the large-scale experimentation and production of devices. In this work, we have developed a low-cost method for nanoparticle synthesis and electrode fabrication for sensing applications. We optimized the synthesis to develop Ag nanoink which exhibits a viscosity of 0.005 Pa.s, making it compatible for inkjet printing using a standard desktop printer. A prototype of interdigitated electrodes (IDE) is fabricated by depositing ten successive layers of the silver ink on photo paper substrate. We demonstrate a low-cost electrode fabrication using inkjet printing that has higher conductivity and lower sheet resistance compared to earlier studies. These printed electrodes have great potential for application in flexible electronic devices, including low-cost gas sensors.

title: Mixed phase lead-free hybrid halide perovskite-based optoelectronic devices
authors: Kumudini Ganesh, Tanushree H. Choudhury, Revathy Padmanabhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Tin-based perovskites have been considered as a potential replacement for toxic lead-based perovskites. In recent years, various approaches have been explored to obtain efficient and stable tin-based perovskite devices. In this work, we have proposed an optimized synthesis approach to fabricate lead-free hybrid halide perovskite-based devices using appropriate solvent, antisolvent, and additive engineering techniques, and the synthesized films were characterized structurally and electrically, and analyzed.

title: Session 14 Overview: Unusual Interconnects and Other Uses for Light
authors: N/A

title: CKADisor: Central Kernel-Aligned Distillation for Efficient Event-Driven Object Recognition
authors: Ruiqi Luo, Ran Jin, Kaixi Hu, Chenmiao Gao, Xinrong Hu
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) are one of the best practices for efficient event-driven object recognition. To achieve high recognition accuracy, existing methods generally accumulate sufficient binary spike signals over long time steps. As a result, the required power consumption can be comparable even to the traditional artificial neural networks (ANNs). This paper introduces CKADisor, leveraging an ANN teacher to guide the direct training of an SNN student under a shorter time-step setting. To mitigate the representation structure mismatch between ANNs and SNNs, we develop a central-kernel aligned distillation strategy that measures layer-wise feature discrepancies and integrates them into the corresponding optimization objectives. Extensive experiments on three popular event datasets demonstrate that our CKADisor achieves higher accuracy than several state-of-the-art methods under a short time-step setting (T=5) using the same architecture.

title: Lossless Dynamic Point Cloud Geometry Compression via Rate-Distortion Optimized Motion Estimation
authors: Qi Zhang, Yiting Shao, Lixuan Meng, Shan Liu, Ge Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Dynamic point clouds are valid representations of three-dimensional moving entities in diverse application scenarios. The comprehensive redundancy in uncompressed point clouds necessitates efficient compression methods. Motion estimation (ME) plays a crucial role in eliminating the temporal redundancy of point cloud sequences. However, existing ME methods suffer from inaccurate geometry compensation distortion measures and imbalanced rate-distortion modeling, significantly impacting the coding performance. To address these challenges, we propose a rate-distortion (R-D) optimized ME scheme for dynamic point cloud geometry compression. First, a point cloud is hierarchically decomposed into a set of macroblocks and blocks via the octree, and variable block-size ME is introduced to capture complex local motions in point clouds. Second, we propose a block-matching motion search method that integrates a new geometry compensation distortion measure, combining voxel-wise Hamming distance and point-wise Euclidean distance as a joint criterion to improve the accuracy of the ME. To mitigate complexity, local motion features are extracted to efficiently accelerate the geometry distortion measure. Third, a rate-distortion model with an adaptive Lagrange multiplier is designed to facilitate better selection of inter predictors in the motion decision stage, thereby achieving efficient coding performance. Experimental results demonstrate the improvements of our proposed scheme over rival platforms in coding performance and computational efficiency. Additional results also validate the effectiveness of key modules within the proposed R-D optimized ME framework.

title: Ambiguity Function Shaping for Weak Target Detection: A Joint Design Approach for Unimodular Waveform and Mismatched Filter
authors: Xiangqing Xiao, Jinfeng Hu, Xin Tai, Yiran Zhang, Yuankai Wang, Jun Liu, Kai Zhong, Huiyong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Unimodular waveform design for ambiguity function (AF) shaping is a key technology in cognitive radar system. Existing methods are limited either by low degrees of freedom (DOF) under matched-filter schemes or by relaxation errors in joint waveform–filter design. To address these issues, we propose a relaxation-free optimization framework with high DOF for unimodular waveform and mismatched filter design, formulated as a nonconvex minimization under constant modulus (CMC) and complex sphere (CSC) constraints. Recognizing that CMC and CSC are naturally satisfied in corresponding Riemannian manifolds, a product manifold for ambiguity function shaping (PMAFS) method is proposed. The proposed approach transforms the problem into an unconstrained optimization over a product manifold and solves it via a parallel conjugate gradient algorithm. An exact-penalty extension, EP-PMAFS, is also developed for precise energy spectral density (ESD) control. Simulation results confirm that the proposed PM optimization framework achieves stronger detection performance for weak target and precise control of the waveform ESD.

title: Coded Computing Meets Differential Privacy: Privacy-Preserving and Straggler-Resilient Distributed Machine Learning
authors: Yilei Xue, Jun Wu, Xi Lin, Heyi Zhang, Wei Zhang, Xinping Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Coded distributed machine learning mitigates straggler effects and provides privacy protection by introducing redundancy through coded computing. However, the system remains vulnerable to privacy breaches when the number of honest-but-curious nodes surpasses the designed threshold, or when outsider adversaries eavesdrop on sensitive data. To address these limitations, we propose a privacy-preserving and straggler-resilient distributed learning framework, namely, differential privacy-based Lagrange coded computing (DP-LCC). First, we design a three-layer protection strategy against privacy threats and stragglers by retaining labels at the master, obfuscating features via Lagrange interpolation, and injecting calibrated noise into local computations. Second, we theoretically prove that the aggregated gradient is an unbiased estimator with bounded variance, and derive convergence bounds under both Gaussian and Laplace mechanisms, revealing the trade-off between privacy budgets and model utility. Third, we provide a comprehensive analysis of the system's computational complexity, privacy composition, and heterogeneity to verify the framework's efficiency and adaptability in realistic distributed environments. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets validate the theoretical results, demonstrating the robustness of DP-LCC against varying system parameters and heterogeneous environments.

title: BEST: Blockchain-Enabled Sustainable Task Scheduling for Optimizing Large AI Model Workloads at the Edge
authors: Haoxiang Luo, Runhua Chen, Gang Sun, Hongfang Yu, Schahram Dustdar
abstract: The proliferation of Large AI Models (LAMs) to the network edge promises unprecedented intelligence in real-time applications. It also introduces a critical sustainability challenge due to the immense energy footprint of model inference on resource-constrained devices. While model-level optimizations have made progress, they fail to address the systemic issue of aggregate energy consumption across a network. This paper introduces BEST (Blockchain-Enabled Sustainable Task Scheduling), a novel framework that reimagines edge resource management to explicitly tackle this sustainability crisis. BEST establishes a decentralized marketplace for underutilized edge resources by introducing a novel application of Real-World Asset (RWA) tokenization to abstract and commoditize computational capacity and battery power. These tokenized resources can be securely and transparently traded among peer devices via a blockchain-based infrastructure. To navigate this dynamic marketplace, we develop a cooperative multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) algorithm based on Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). The agents learn a sophisticated scheduling policy that dynamically balances task latency, system-wide energy consumption, and task success rate. Extensive simulations against compared baselines demonstrate BEST’s unique strengths. It reduces total energy consumption by up to 26% — 46% against other schemes, while maintaining a high task success rate and latency competitive with the state-of-the-art scheme. By creating a market-driven, collaborative ecosystem, BEST provides a scalable and effective pathway toward achieving truly sustainable LAM at the edge.

title: fPIM : A Holistic Design to Optimize PIM Data Flow for High Execution Efficiency
authors: Nan Wang, Wenjie Liu, Qing Liu, Xubin He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: As applications demand more bandwidth, the the “memory wall” problem becomes increasingly severe. Therefore, the processing-in-memory (PIM) architecture has attracted significant research interest due to its ability to execute instructions offloaded by the processor. Existing works on PIM architectures are classified into two categories: regional offloading, where all instructions within a programmer-specified code region are offloaded, and selective offloading, where only instructions of interest are offloaded via hardware support. However, PIM architectures pose the amplified in-PIM traffic overhead challenge that endangers the performance of PIM and degrades the performance of the entire system. To address the challenge, we propose a PIM architecture, called fast PIM (fPIM), which integrates the PIM cache within each Channel Controller to optimize the data flow within the PIM. This design cooperates with the Processing Unit Load-balancer and Behavior-based Offloader to achieve high execution efficiency. To evaluate fPIM, we perform extensive experiments, and the results show that fPIM reduces the workload finish time by up to $88.6\%$, $87.5\%$, and $79.6\%$ (with an average of $68.7\%$, $66.2\%$, and $59.8\%$), compared to three state-of-the-art PIM designs, PEI, Fafnir, and SpaceA, respectively.

title: A Valve-Less Electro-Hydrostatic Powered Prosthetic Foot to Improve the Power Efficiency During Walking_supp1-3668985.mp4
authors: Qitao Huang
abstract: Hydraulic systems have been widely applied in lower-limb prostheses, primarily for their compact actuation and inherent damping capability. However, when applied to powered prosthetic feet, valves and other damping elements cause unavoidable energy dissipation, thereby constraining their power density. To address this limitation, we propose a valve-less electro-hydrostatic powered prosthetic foot aimed at enhancing power efficiency. In addition, a gas accumulator's nonlinear elasticity is considered, forming a hydraulic series elastic actuator with passive stiffness comparable to the human ankle during walking. This configuration lowers the motor's required speed, consequently reducing its actual electrical power. The proposed design was evaluated through a treadmill walking experiment with a non-impaired subject walking at 1.1 m/s. The results showed that the prosthetic foot provided sufficient positive power relative to human reference values. Moreover, the prosthesis achieved a peak output power of 206.3 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 15.0 W, while the corresponding motor electrical power was only 147.5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 29.5 W. Our study demonstrates that the electro-hydrostatic system holds significant potential for enhancing the power density of powered prosthetic feet.

title: Hi-LSplat: Hierarchical 3D Language Gaussian Splatting
authors: Chenlu Zhan, Yufei Zhang, Gaoang Wang, Hongwei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Modeling 3D language fields with Gaussian Splatting for open-ended language queries has recently garnered increasing attention. However, recent 3DGS-based models leverage view-dependent 2D foundation models to refine 3D semantics but lack a unified 3D representation, leading to view inconsistencies. Additionally, inherent open-vocabulary challenges cause inconsistencies in object and relational descriptions, impeding hierarchical semantic understanding. In this paper, we propose Hi-LSplat, a view-consistent Hierarchical Language Gaussian Splatting work for 3D open-vocabulary querying. To achieve view-consistent 3D hierarchical semantics, we first lift 2D features to 3D features by constructing a 3D hierarchical semantic tree with layered instance clustering, which addresses the view inconsistency issue caused by 2D semantic features. Besides, we introduce instance- wise and part- wise contrastive losses to capture all-sided hierarchical semantic representations. Notably, we construct two hierarchical semantic datasets to better assess the model's ability to distinguish different semantic levels. Extensive experiments highlight our method's superiority in 3D open-vocabulary segmentation and localization. Its strong performance on hierarchical semantic datasets underscores its ability to capture complex hierarchical semantics within 3D scenes.

title: A Robustness Indicator-Based Dual-Population Evolutionary Algorithm for Multimodal Multiobjective Optimization
authors: Caitong Yue, Wenhao Ye, Jing Liang, Mengmeng Li, Kunjie Yu, Ying Bi, Boyang Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: In practical scenarios, there may be solutions in the decision space with close objective values but located far apart, a characteristic known as multimodal multiobjective problems (MMOPs). While most multimodal multiobjective evolutionary algorithms (MMEAs) focus on finding global Pareto optimal solution sets (PSs) and local PSs demonstrating satisfactory convergence performance, decision-makers in real-world scenarios are often also interested in local PSs that exhibit strong robustness. In this study, we propose several benchmark functions in which the global and local PSs have varying levels of robustness. Then, we introduce an innovative dual-population evolutionary algorithm, termed GLR-MMEA, designed to simultaneously find both global PSs and local PSs with strong robustness. In GLR-MMEA, the convergence population focuses on identifying global PSs, providing convergence information to the diversity population. Meanwhile, the diversity population manages the detection of both global PSs and local PSs with strong robustness. In the process of updating the diversity population, a robustness indicator is proposed to access the robustness of solutions. Furthermore, a selection mechanism founded on this robustness indicator is applied to identify local PSs with high robustness. The experimental results show that GLR-MMEA performs competitively against other leading MMEAs in working on the selected benchmark functions.

title: AIRPNet: Adaptive Image Restoration With Privacy Protection in Steganographic Domain
authors: Fangyuan Gao, Chao Gao, Xin Deng, Chenxiao Zhang, Junjie Huang, Mai Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Cloud-based third-party multimedia services have become increasingly popular in last decade, however, they pose serious threats to users' privacy. To address this issue, in this paper, we propose a novel Adaptive Image Restoration network with Privacy protection, namely AIRPNet, which first attempts to perform image restoration in steganographic domain. Compared with existing methods, our method has significant advantages in invisibility, security and flexibility. Specifically, we first propose a wavelet lifting-based Adaptive Invertible Hiding (AIH) module to conceal the low-quality (LQ) secret image into a stego image. Then, instead of performing single type of restoration on the secret image, an adaptive secure restoration (ASR) module is developed to deal with multiple image degradations on the stego image. Finally, a high-quality (HQ) secret image can be extracted from the restored stego image. Here, since the secret image remains hidden throughout the whole image restoration process, the privacy of users can be greatly protected. The framework can be flexibly extended to multiple image restoration, which can restore multiple secret images from the same stego image. Experimental results on various datasets demonstrate that our AIRPNet outperforms existing methods in terms of restoration accuracy, invisibility and security on different image restoration tasks.

title: Double-clad Anti-resonant Hollow-core Fiber with Inner Cladding Tube Offset for Mode Tailoring
authors: Yang Wang, Wei Chen, Xiaobei Zhang, Qi Zhang, Tingyun Wang
abstract: We demonstrate a double-clad anti-resonant hollow-core fiber (DC-ARF) with an offset inner cladding structure for effective mode tailoring. The DC-ARF consists of six thin-walled, azimuthally offset inner tubes enclosed within a thicker outer cladding tube, which is further connected to silica jacket through a single node. Asymmetric offset arrangement of inner cladding tubes generates two enlarged gaps based on mode field distribution of LP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub>-like mode, ensuring high-order mode (HOM) suppression. Numerical simulations show that light guidance is achieved through leakage from the larger gaps followed by anti-resonant reflection back into the core. And wavelength disparity between fundamental mode and HOMs provides inherent mechanism for preferential HOM suppression. Through structural optimization, significantly improved mode purity is achieved. The fabricated DC-ARF features two large azimuthal gaps of 70° and operates at a wavelength of 1.06 μm. Experimental results confirm that only the fundamental mode remains when the fiber length exceeds 0.5 m. Furthermore, a 1-m-long DC-ARF improves the input beam quality by approximately 40%, demonstrating its mode tailoring capability. Our work offers valuable guidance for mode tailoring, and the proposed DC-ARF has great potential in single-mode laser transmission, biomedical systems, and optical communications.

title: Design of a 2/6-Pole Combined Pitch Wound Rotor Brushless Doubly-Fed Generator and its Comparative Study with an 8-Pole Doubly-Fed Induction Generator
authors: Lei Jia, Jun Xu, Jinfeng Xiao
abstract: The brushless doubly-fed generator (BDFG) is especially well-suited for constant-frequency, constant-voltage power generation in small hydropower plants utilizing variable speed hydraulic turbines. This paper presents a novel wound rotor design that combines long and short pitch coils to consider both the power winding (PW) and control winding (CW) pole pair pitches selection, thereby improving the chording factors and enhancing the overall winding factors through harmonic analysis. First, this study investigates the rotor speed regulation characteristics, analyzes the composition and magnitude of the radial electromagnetic forces density under harmonic-free condition, presents the stator PW and CW connection methods. Subsequently, the principle of employing combined pitch coils is elaborated to further increase conductor utilization and mitigate harmonic contents. A practical design example for 1/3 pole-pair is provided, including detailed implementation steps, along with a comparative analysis of coupling coefficients across different rotor configurations. Furthermore, finite element (FE) analysis of the 2/6-pole BDFG and 8-pole doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) with identical frame sizes are conducted to evaluate the difference in operational characteristics of the generators under the same load and rotor speed range. Finally, the experimental results validate the superiority of the proposed 2/6-pole BDFG rotor design across diverse loads and speeds.

title: MVPC-CLIP: Multi-Granularity Visual Prompt Co-Operative for Aerial Video Recognition
authors: Yang Zhan, Yuan Yuan
abstract: Current aerial video recognition only uses vision modality to predict fixed class probabilities and does not have open-set or zero-shot recognition capabilities. We strengthen aerial video representation with linguistic semantic supervision for the first time, modeling this task as a novel vision-language multi-modality aerial video recognition paradigm. Recent studies have effectively adapted the CLIP model to natural videos. However, the diverse viewpoints, posture changes, and fast motion of UAVs introduce challenges of complex spatio-temporal contextual understanding and dynamic spatial variation capture for the aerial video-text joint representation. In this paper, we propose a contrastive learning framework with Multi-granularity Visual Prompt Co-operative information flow (MVPC-CLIP), adapting the CLIP to zero-shot, few-shot, and fully-supervised settings. 1) To absorb effective spatiotemporal clues from complex contexts, we develop frame prompts and video prompts to enhance inter-frame interaction and temporal aggregation in the visual branch. Frame prompts provide local spatiotemporal clues to enhance frame embedding and patch embedding. Video prompts with global spatiotemporal clues help frame embedding collect frame-level temporal features. 2) To capture dynamic spatial variations, we design global spatial prompts to be equipped in the adaptive spatial text mixer. The mixer can adapt to different aerial videos according to the global video spatial information, and generate more robust and discriminative textual embedding. 3) We construct novel zero-shot and few-shot settings and extensively investigate existing benchmarks. Comprehensive evaluations reveal that MVPC-CLIP achieves state-of-the-art performance across all settings. The complete codebase and few-shot dataset splits will be released.

title: Experimental Characterization of SupraHarmonic Emissions in E14 and E27 LED Lamps
authors: Andrea Fioravanti, Giulio Antonini, Haseeb Shams, Daniele Romano, Alberto Prudenzi, Simone Mari
abstract: This paper presents an in-depth experimental study on the supraharmonic emission behavior of various real-world indoor lighting technologies, particularly Light-Emitting Diodes, within the frequency range of 9 kHz to 150 kHz. Using an AFJ LS16C/10 Line Impedance Stabilization Network in conjunction with a Rohde & Schwarz Spectrum Analyzer, measurements were conducted in both Max Hold and Average modes to analyze the peak spectral emissions of 18 lamp models from different manufacturers. Each sample’s spectral profile was studied across ten defined frequency bands to assess the harmonic variability, switching frequency signatures, and implications of electromagnetic compatibility. The results reveal significant differences in emission characteristics based on lamp design, particularly in the 51.3–65.4 kHz and 74–82 kHz bands, where many LED drivers exhibited pronounced spectral peaks. Some low-cost LED lamps, even from well-established brands, exhibited high peak and average emission levels, indicating limited effectiveness in electromagnetic interference suppression. In contrast, devices from manufacturers known for high-quality design showed well-controlled emissions and better EMC performance. These findings provide valuable insight into driver design quality, potential EMC risks, and power quality impacts, beneficial for lighting manufacturers, energy regulators, and consumers aiming to reduce harmonic pollution in modern residential power systems.

title: An Online Convolutional Neural Network Based Method for Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis in Three-Phase Inverters Under Extremely Unbalanced Loading Condition
authors: Xiaoyi Lei, Fanfu Wu, Yunting Liu
abstract: Diagnosing open-circuit (OC) faults in three-phase inverters becomes particularly challenging under extremely unbalanced loading conditions. In such cases, the phase current with a greatly reduced amplitude renders the predefined thresholds in rule-based methods unreliable and is tend to be neglected in feature extraction in data-driven methods, thereby reducing overall diagnosis accuracy. In addition, since existing data-driven methods require at least one complete fundamental cycle of three-phase current for analysis, fault detection is inherently delayed by one fundamental cycle. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a convolutional neural network (CNN)–based OC fault diagnosis method with accuracy-driven feature extraction and classification. The method encodes the three-phase currents as a current- vector trajectory in the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha$</tex-math></inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\beta$</tex-math></inline-formula> domain and converts each trajectory into a binary matrix representation. By leveraging the intrinsic feature extraction capability of CNNs and backpropagation-based training, feature extraction and classification are integrated within one network and are directly guided by diagnosis accuracy, preventing accuracy degradation under extremely unbalanced loading condition. Two CNNs are employed: the first CNN achieves sub-cycle fault detection by identifying trajectory distortions, while the second CNN recognizes the trajectory pattern under faulty conditions and pinpoints the specific faulty switches one fundamental period later. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method accurately classifies all 21 OC fault cases and the healthy condition, achieving 100% accuracy under both balanced operation and extremely unbalanced loading conditions with a phase-current unbalance rate (PCUR) of up to 88%.

title: Evolving Intelligent Network Attack Classifier Under Label Distribution Shift
authors: Miru Kim, Mugon Joe, Minhae Kwon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The next-generation internet is being reshaped by the growing intelligence and connectivity of artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) devices. This evolution emphasizes the importance of AI-enabled network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) in intelligent network environments. Two major technical challenges in developing such systems are addressing class imbalance and adapting to label distribution shifts after deployment. Class imbalance, caused by the dominance of normal traffic and the scarcity and uneven distribution of attack types, often results in low performance for minority attack type classes. In addition, evolving attack patterns necessitate efficient and continuous model post-training, despite the limited and unlabeled data available in the post-deployment phase. This study proposes a unified training framework consisting of a two-step pre-training scheme and an adaptive post-training scheme. In Step I of pre-training, balanced contrastive pair selection is performed to account for class imbalance, while Step II refines the representations of hard-to-distinguish samples located near decision boundaries using latent features. In the post-training phase, the framework leverages both model outputs and latent representations to generate reliable pseudo-labels for learning. A key insight of this work is that a well-structured latent representation space enables reliable pseudo-labeling during post-training. Extensive simulations across eight online label shift scenarios with three datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves up to an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\bf {8.6}\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> improvement in accuracy and F1-score over eight state-of-the-art approaches, along with a significant reduction in post-training complexity.

title: RFID-Powered Smart Cart: Hassle-Free Shopping & Instant Billing
authors: Allen Matthew A, Anbarasu P, Harshini T, Kirubhakaran V, Sineka R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: In modern day trade, which is tireless in nature, the need to have effective and friendly purchase transaction processes has brought about an avalanche of novel retail technologies. I propose a system RFID(radio-frequency identification)-Powered Smart Cart: Hassle-Free Shopping and Instant Billing, aimed at redesigning traditional shopping by combining the idea of RFID, Internet of Things (IoT), and embedded systems. This building design simplifies automatic recognition of items, billing in real-time, and checkout without human involvement. All the products within the store are tagged with RFID and as the customer adds-on or takes-off the products the smart cart is interrogating them. An LCD(Liquid Crystal Display) and a buzzer are used to inform a user about the status of the cart and the current amount of a bill. By the time a shopping expedition is over one can pay through an online wallet or using a card and the customer gets a digital bill. The system reduces manpower used in traditional check out mode and prevents the congestion of the lines and reduces the possibility of manual errors and thus makes the shopping process more humane and self-sufficient. It was experimentally proved that the system can update bills within 5 seconds, has high accuracy tag detection of 95 % and allows one-click online payment leading to faster checkouts, fewer errors as well as seamless shopping experience.

title: Enhancing Resume Screening with Multimodal Analysis using NLP, BERT and Social Media Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sri Devi. T, Malathy E.M, Praveen Joe IR
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Transformer model enabled AI Tools have emerged as a trend in Resume screening process. Traditionally, only text-based resumes were available for resume screening but availability of video resumes on social media platforms presents new opportunity as well. Video resumes offer concise and structured insights into candidate's skills, education, and experience and helps the candidate to create an impact with the employer and stand out from the crowd. But processing the video resumes along with text resumes presents a complex data integration challenge. This research work includes 300 video resumes into multimodal resume screening system along with text resumes. Research work leverages advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) for feature extraction and consolidate key information from resumes and job descriptions, and transformer-based models for embedding both modalities. By ranking candidates based on the similarity between their embedded resume data and job descriptions, transformer model approach achieves an 85% reduction in screening time and effort compared to traditional manual screening methods, while significantly enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of the hiring process. Sentiment analysis score for fresher video resumes are generated based on tone and polarity using the bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment model, helping recruiters identify promising fresher candidates. Bert base-multilingualuncased model performed better with Accuracy of 82 % when compared to other BERT based models twitter-roberta-basesentiment, Bertweet-base, Twitter-XLM-r, LaBSE, robertuito-sentiment-analysis, rubert-base-cased model.

title: LSTM-Potential: An Efficient Reinforcement Learning PTA Step Size Policy for Circuit Simulation
authors: Zhuoyang Xia, Tongxi Wang, Dan Niu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)
abstract: In circuit simulation, the DC operating point obtained from DC analysis is a necessary prerequisite for transient analysis and AC small-signal analysis. The essence of DC analysis is the solution of a set of nonlinear algebraic equations derived by an improved nodal method. Pseudo-transient analysis (PTA), because of its efficiency and ease of implementation, is widely used in commercial SPICE simulators for DC simulation of large-scale circuits. The step size control of PTA is crucial to its convergence and efficiency. Since the relationship between the circuit environment and the step size can be approximated as a Markov decision process (MDP), an adaptive PTA step size control algorithm based on reinforcement learning (RLS) has been proposed. However, its use of a fully connected neural network in the policy network and its reliance on expert-based rewards negatively impact the accuracy of the step size policy. This paper first applies a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to the policy network, giving it memory and enabling better handling of time-series data. Then, a reward function incorporating potential shaping is proposed, adding a compensating reward term to mitigate the sparsity of the original reward function to some extent. The fused algorithm is on average (fastest) 1.503 times faster (3.796 times) than the RL-S algorithm.

title: Robots for Older Adults: A Scoping Review

title: AI-Driven Knowledge Graph Denoising and Accuracy Optimization for Regional Sustainable Development Policies
authors: Yunqiao Cai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)
abstract: The formulation and optimization of regional sustainable development policies depend on multi-dimensional, large-scale cross-domain knowledge. However, structural redundancy, data inconsistency, and noise in policy-related knowledge graphs severely limit AI’s effectiveness in policy reasoning and decision support, becoming a key bottleneck for policy accuracy and implementation. To address this, this study proposes an AI-driven knowledge graph accuracy optimization and denoising algorithm integrating graph embedding and a confidence-weighted mechanism for regional sustainable development policy scenarios. Firstly, the TransE model is used for low-dimensional semantic representation learning of policy-related entities (e.g., resource elements, policy objectives, regional subjects) and relationships (e.g., constraint, association, influence paths). Secondly, entity similarity clustering and a relationship confidence quantification model enable accurate identification and elimination of noisy triples. Finally, a weighted embedding optimization function is constructed to enhance embedding semantic expression and policy correlation characterization accuracy. Comparative experiments on FB15K-237, WN18RR, and a regional sustainable development policy dataset show the algorithm outperforms mainstream methods in embedding semantic consistency, noise identification accuracy, and computational efficiency. It provides reliable technical support for high-quality construction and dynamic update of policy knowledge graphs, laying a foundation for AI-enabled precise formulation, evaluation, and dynamic optimization of regional sustainable development policies.

title: FTCS 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A

title: Explainable Boosting Regression-Based Approach for Demand Forecasting
authors: Murugappan Venkatachalam, Thanush A A, Chitra P, Neelima Bayyapu
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Demand forecasting is crucial to efficient supply chain management because it guarantees optimal sales, improved capacity management, and prompt stock replenishment. Most organizations depend on sales trends and demand prediction for effective planning and management of business goals. Recent works related to demand forecasting focus on developing deep learning architectures with higher accuracies. However, deep learning models are less intelligible and provide less accuracy when trained using smaller datasets. This study proposes a highly intelligible Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) model to perform the time-series forecasting of a dataset provided by an IT consultancy company based out of Japan. Six different Machine Learning models have been trained apart from the EBM regression model. Mean absolute error (MAE) and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) were used as metrics for the evaluation of the performance of the models. The proposed model achieved a 15% reduction in RMSE compared to baseline methods, enabling improved demand prediction and inventory planning. Data preprocessing procedures including outlier interpolation, dataset grouping, and feature reduction were carried out to decrease the volatility of the dataset. Our results show that the performance of the EBM was superior in comparison to other models along with an increased explainability, which is a subjective measure of the extent to which the decision-making process of a model could be explained.

title: Large-scale sparse signal recovery by modified HS conjugate gradient method
authors: Shuang’An Li, Fenghua Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The L1 regularization based methods for sparse signal reconstruction is a topic of considerable interest recently,which is widely employed in basis pursuit denoising, compressed sensing and other related fields. These problems can be cast as L1-regularized least-squares programs (LSPs).But it is challenging due to the non-smoothness of the regularization. Inspired by Nesterov’s smoothing technique, we smoothed the regularization term.Hence this paper proposed a new modified HS conjugate gradient algorithm for solving common recovery problems in signal processing. Numerical experiment shows that our algorithm is effective and suitable for solving large-scale sparse signal recovery problems.

title: Edge-Assisted Topology-Aware TDMA Scheduling for Shipborne Wireless Networks Using Array-Based Cooperative Localization*
authors: Erzhao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: In dynamic shipborne or industrial environments, mobile wireless nodes often operate in confined metallic spaces where multipath interference and topology variation lead to collisions and throughput loss. This paper proposes a topologyaware TDMA optimization framework based on cooperative localization using edge nodes with antenna arrays. Fixed edge nodes estimate the angle and time difference of arrival (AoA, TDOA) of beacon signals from mobile nodes and jointly process these parameters via a wired backbone to reconstruct a global, time-varying topology graph. The topology information guides adaptive TDMA slot allocation through a greedy graphbased scheduling algorithm that minimizes transmission conflicts. Simulations show that, compared with ALOHA and DRAND, the proposed scheme achieves higher throughput, lower collision rate, and stable latency under varying network densities. The approach offers a practical, scalable solution for intelligent wireless communication in constrained shipborne and industrial IoT environments.

title: WF-PVL Net: A Multi-Scale Recognition Algorithm for Full-Scene Defect Detection in Wind Farms
authors: Xinyu Liu, Yuhang Dai, Bo Zhao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: The operating environment of large wind turbines is complex and harsh. Consequently, the development of a real-time, accurate fault detection method has become an urgent priority. In this study, full-scene defect images from wind farm stations were collected to create a dataset, and damage mechanisms were analyzed. Various existing target detection models were evaluated, with YOLOv8n demonstrating significant potential for improvement in both speed and detection accuracy. The improved model was validated on an open-source small target dataset, achieving a 3.5% increase in accuracy, a 1% increase in recall, and a 3.0% improvement in mAP50 compared to the baseline model. As a result, the model is capable of rapidly converting Unmanned Aerial Vehicle inspection images into wind turbine defect detection results, enhancing detection efficiency, safety, and economic benefits.

title: Design and Evaluation of Novel Flexible Photovoltaic Power Plants in Medium-to-Low Solar Resource Areas
authors: Miaojun Chen, Bin Zhao, Huiming Zhou, Shenfeng Ke, Yuchun Cui, Yuzeng Zhao, Honghua Ge
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: In recent years, novel flexible solar cells have increased the adoption of flexible photovoltaic (PV) modules. However, research on designing and assessing PV power plants using these modules is limited, especially for outdoor applications in regions with medium to low solar resources where systematic testing is scarce. This study uses Shanghai as a case to explore the feasibility of deploying flexible modules in such areas. Utilizing PVsyst software, a tailored PV system was designed and simulated to estimate electricity generation for the first year and over a 25-year period. Actual electricity generation during the trial operation was also analyzed to determine if the PV power plant met expectations. The performance of flexible modules was evaluated under both sunny and overcast conditions. Simulation results indicated that the grid-connected flexible PV plant generated 856.2 MWh in the first year with an average system efficiency of 81.9%. Over 25 years, total electricity generation is projected to reach 19,959.7 MWh. During trials,Power generation in August 2024 was 27.4% higher than simulated values. On a sunny day(September 9, 2024), actual output deviated from theoretical by an average of 5.8%, and by 6.04% on a rainy day(September 10, 2024). These findings provide valuable insights for promoting new flexible PV power plants.

title: A Developed Multilevel Power Converter with Reduced Switch Count
authors: A V Suganya, T Anuradha
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: A novel multilevel inverter topology that is asymmetrical with reduced switch components has been proposed in this paper. The proposed 15 level asymmetrical inverter configuration renders a high-quality output waveform which is one of the inherent properties of the inverter. The viability of the multilevel inverter increases with reduced harmonic distortion. The feasibility of the configuration further increases with the reduction of electromagnetic interference. Lesser number of the on-state switches in the presented multilevel inverter configuration offering not only reduced voltage drop across the switches but also higher number of levels is an approach to ensure reliable operation with this topology. The effectiveness and credibility of this inverter configuration is validated by simulation results using MATLAB and with a laboratory setup it is finally verified experimentally.

title: ISNEET 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)

title: Design of a Microcontroller-Based Intelligent Detection and Cleaning System
authors: Yingying Li, Aojie Sun, Lei Chen, Jiaying Bai
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: To address the low efficiency and high risk of manual inspection and cleaning in high-risk industrial environments, an intelligent inspection and cleaning system based on the STC89C52RC microcontroller was designed and implemented. This system integrates multiple sensors, including those for temperature and humidity, hazardous gases, and infrared obstacle avoidance, constructing a closed-loop control architecture of "perception-decision-execution-interaction." It features real-time environmental parameter monitoring, over-limit audible and visual alarms, infrared and Bluetooth remote control, autonomous obstacle avoidance, and localized cleaning.
