Test results show that the system is accurate in measurement, reliable in response, and achieves intelligent linkage between inspection and cleaning, effectively improving the efficiency of industrial environmental safety management and demonstrating strong practicality and promotional value.

title: Black Start and Parameter Design for Offshore Wind Power Series Hybrid Transmission Systems
authors: Shuxin Luo, Feng Li, Yanfeng Wang, Xinghua Wang, Xingjian Tian, Renxin Yang
abstract: The integration of large-scale offshore wind power requires reliable and efficient transmission solutions. Hybrid HVDC systems, combining modular multilevel converters (MMC) with diode rectifier units (DRU), have attracted increasing attention because they enable stable AC voltage construction at offshore converter stations while allowing grid-following wind turbines to operate without extensive modifications. In this work, the operating principles of a hybrid converter-based HVDC topology are analyzed, with emphasis on the coordinated power sharing between the MMC and DRU. A step-by-step black start sequence is designed to overcome the inherent unidirectional conduction limitation of diode rectifiers, ensuring stable charging and synchronization of offshore MMCs and wind farms. Furthermore, a systematic parameter design methodology is presented for a 2 GW offshore wind scenario, covering voltage division, transformer ratios, filtering, and submodule capacitor sizing. Time-domain simulations are conducted to validate the proposed design, demonstrating smooth DC voltage buildup, stable AC voltage formation, and secure transition into steady- state operation. The results highlight the feasibility and effectiveness of hybrid HVDC transmission for large-scale offshore wind integration.

title: IoT Intelligent Sensor Based Underwater Inspection Robot for Real Time Object Detection and Tracking
authors: Sujilatha Tada, V. Jeevanantham
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Robotic inspection systems face specific challenges when operating in underwater environments, such as limited visibility and signal attenuations, and real-time decision-making while operating under severe energy and communication band-width limitations. This paper presents an intelligent underwater inspection robot with advanced IoT based intelligent sensors that are capable of real-time object detection and tracking. The intelligent standalone system deploys advanced sonar and vision sensors with built-in edge AI for autonomous robotic operation in dynamic aquatic environments. The proposed approach is a multi-stage processing pipeline. The underwater captured footage is improved via a CycleGAN-based image restoration module to correct defects like low contrast, color distortions, and blurring. The restored frames from the CycleGAN image restoration are fed into a lightweight multi-output hybrid deep learning model for inference based on fused neural network architecture of ResNeXt and DenseNet for enhanced accuracy and reduced latency of object detection. For tracking objects, the Deep SORT algorithm was selected because it utilizes appearance descriptors and Kalman throttle enabled object identity tracking between video frames. The robot is equipped with a Jetson Nano for on-board computability and communication via either acoustic modem or LoRa-based transmission for pixel metadata and alert log saving data with a surface IoT gateway. The gateway interacts with a cloud platform where a dashboard provides a real time visualization of tracked objects along with mission telemetry. Experimental validation is performed using standardized underwater datasets (EUVP, RUIE) and sequences of custom collected datasets. The experimental results demonstrate strong detection performance (mean average precision ¿ 85%) and efficient tracking performance (MOTA <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=80 \%$</tex>), with minimal communication overhead.

title: Research on Power Grid Technology for Oilfield Electrical Systems
authors: Qixian Guo, Chaoxiang Hu, Xiufan Ma
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: With the integration of high-proportion photovoltaic renewable energy into oilfield power grids, the fluctuating, random, and intermittent nature of its power generation significantly impacts grid safety and stable operation. To achieve local consumption and secure management of distributed energy, this paper investigates the optimization dispatch of generation, grid, load, and storage in oilfield power grids.First, the fundamental principles of source-grid-load-storage optimization dispatch are outlined, analyzing operational reserve calculation methods and constraint conditions from four dimensions: power generation, grid side, demand side, and energy storage. Subsequently, a multi-source coordinated operational reserve capacity allocation strategy is proposed, constructing an optimization model aimed at minimizing total operational costs.Finally, using the Tarim Oilfield grid as a case study, it analyzes dispatch schemes under two scenarios: 100% green power self-sufficiency and non-100% green power self-sufficiency. This provides technical support for efficiently integrating renewable energy and reducing energy costs in oilfield grids.

title: Unified RF Rectifier-Demodulator for Energy Harvesting in Sub-GHz Wireless Implants
authors: Taoyue Zhang, John S. Peterson, Saeed Mohammadi
abstract: A unified RF rectifier-demodulator front end has been developed for wireless neural implants and other low-power wireless sensors operating in sub-GHz bands, including the 902-928MHz ISM band. The architecture integrates both blocks to enable efficient power harvesting and data demodulation, essential for neural stimulators, channel-selective neural recorders, and compact IoT or RFID nodes. Implemented in 180nm CMOS, the design employs a 4-stage fully differential cross-coupled differential-drive (CCDD) rectifier with a low-power DC demodulator supporting 1 MHz data rate. Including matching and packaging losses, a maximum power harvesting efficiency (PHE) of 32.2% is measured at 4-6dBm input, with efficiency above 20% for inputs over 0dBm. Although parasitic effects shifted the optimum frequency to 750MHz, functionality is preserved across 750-915MHz by tuning the off-chip matching network. These results demonstrate a compact, reconfigurable rectifier-demodulator front end suitable for energy-constrained neural interfaces and ISM-band IoT and RFID applications.

title: Design and Application of Solar-Powered Electric Buses
authors: Xinghu Dong, Quanhong Yuan
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Installing solar panels on electric buses enables on-board power generation, making them more economical and environmentally friendly, while also reducing dependence on charging stations. Taking a 12-meter-long solar-powered electric bus as an example, first the engine matching calculations and selection were performed, and then it can be equipped with 4895 W of flexible photovoltaic (PV) modules, generating an average of 3313.8 kWh per year. This results in annual savings of 4970 yuan on charging costs. Over a 25-year lifespan, the cost-benefit ratio reaches 13:1, which is significantly higher than the typical 4:1–9:1 range for ground-based PV power stations and the 2.5:1–6:1 range for PV energy storage projects. A single solar-powered bus can save 30.9 tonnes of coal equivalent (tce), 101.9 tonnes of water, reduce CO₂ emissions by 69.7 tonnes, SO₂ and NOₓ emissions by 0.02 tonnes, and particulate emissions by 3.6 kg over 25 years, demonstrating significant energy-saving and emission-reduction benefits.

title: Low-Light Image Enhancement Based on CrossAttention Mechanism
authors: Weicui Sun, Zhongxu Han, Fei He, Shaoqing Xu, Shan Xiao, Guanyuan Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Low-light Image Enhancement (LLIE) aims to restore images captured under insufficient illumination to a normally lit appearance with adequate brightness, clear structures, and natural colors. However, existing methods still perform poorly in extremely dark conditions, where structural distortion, loss of details, amplified noise, and color imbalance frequently occur, making it difficult to maintain both structural fidelity and perceptual quality. To address these challenges, this paper proposes CA-YUVNet, a cross-attention-based low-light enhancement model. The model converts input images from RGB to YUV space to decouple luminance and chrominance, thereby suppressing noise propagation and color distortion commonly observed in low-light scenes. Furthermore, a cross-branch crossattention mechanism is introduced in the U and V branches to mitigate color shifts and chromatic artifacts. To further improve detail restoration and color consistency, a Bipolar Channel Attention Fusion (BCAF) module is designed, which integrates multi-scale convolution and deep feature fusion to enhance noise suppression and structural refinement. Experimental results on the LOL-v1, LOL-v2-real, and LOL-v2-synthetic datasets demonstrate that CA-YUVNet outperforms existing methods, validating its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Feature Extraction of Ship Shaft-Rate Magnetic Field Signals Based on Adaptive Fourier Decomposition
authors: Bo Wang, Yucheng Jiao, Kai Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The ship’s shaft-rate electromagnetic field signal serves as a crucial non-acoustic detection signature for targets. Addressing the challenge of extracting its features under low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) conditions, this paper proposes a novel feature extraction method based on Adaptive Fourier Decomposition (AFD). Leveraging the adaptive capability and rapid energy convergence characteristic of AFD, the proposed method decomposes the weak shaft-rate signal into a series of single components arranged in descending order of energy. This process facilitates the high-precision extraction of the fundamental shaft frequency and its harmonics. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to the traditional Fourier Transform, the proposed method achieves higher frequency resolution and superior noise suppression capability in low-SNR scenarios. It can effectively extract the line spectrum characteristics of the ship’s shaft-rate magnetic field, thereby providing a reliable technical approach for ship target detection and identification.

title: Heterogeneous Graph Modeling Method for APT Attack Detection Based on Multi-Head Attention Mechanism
authors: Mingxuan Xiao, Qinghang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: This paper proposes Multi-Head GAT (MH-GAT) to address heterogeneous graph modeling challenges in APT attack detection. While GCNs use fixed aggregation weights and standard GATs lack sensitivity to edge semantics, MHGAT employs multi-head attention to implicitly capture edge type features through parallel subspaces and integrates dynamic edge type processing. Experiments on real APT datasets confirm its superiority over baselines, particularly for file and process node detection, offering a more expressive modeling approach for heterogeneous security environments.

title: Human-Aware RT-DETR: Large-Kernel Attention and Gating for Real-Time Human Detection
authors: Xinpeng Lin, Changfeng Li, Dongjie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Human detection in the wild is challenging due to occlusion, crowding, and large scale variation, especially under real-time constraints. Existing real-time detectors such as You Only Look Once (YOLO) and Real-Time Detection Transformer (RT-DETR) are largely category-agnostic and underuse the structural regularities of the human body. We propose Human-Aware Real-Time Detection Transformer (HA-RTDETR), a humanaware variant of DETR-style detectors that augments the backbone with Human-Aware Kernel Attention (HKA), a lightweight multi-branch large-kernel block for capturing elongated limbs and body contours, and inserts a Human-Aware Gating Block (HAGate) after multi-scale fusion to emphasize human-related responses while suppressing background clutter. On a singleclass benchmark built from COCO-WholeBody, HA-RTDETR consistently improves AP over YOLOv5, YOLOv8, YOLOv11, and an RT-DETR baseline, and ablation results show that HKA and HAGate provide complementary gains and lead to more human-focused representations.

title: A Knowledge Distillation Framework for Efficient Models
authors: Zhengyang Yi, Ziqiang Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Recent advances in computing hardware have enabled the training of increasingly deep neural networks. However, the high computational and memory costs associated with such models pose substantial challenges for efficient deployment in practical applications. To address this issue, this paper proposes SoftDistill, a three-stage knowledge distillation framework designed for effective model compression and knowledge transfer. Unlike conventional distillation approaches that rely heavily on hard labels or manually selected intermediate features, SoftDistill leverages soft-label supervision across training stages and preserves semantic consistency under vocabulary compression through a lateral truncation strategy. In addition, a cyclic iterative teaching assistant distillation mechanism is introduced to progressively bridge the capacity gap between teacher and student models. Extensive experiments conducted on the Super-GLUE benchmark demonstrate that SoftDistill consistently out-performs ten representative state-of-the-art knowledge distillation methods.

title: JEE 360 - Empowering JEE Aspirants Through AI-Driven Personalized Learning and Instant Doubt Resolution
authors: Rathesha S M, Nithya Shree R, Iswarya K, Jeyaseelan R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: JEE360 is one of the most advanced AI virtual assistants designed for students preparing for the JEE and other competitive engineering examinations in India. The system is trained on advanced Large Language Model (LLM for short) and subsequently customized using key academic materials like NCERT textbooks, JEE guidebooks, and previous examination papers. JEE360 instantly resolves and summarizes complex concepts, and other topic specific questions and provides topicwise personalized context-specific quizzes. The system synthesizes RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) and CAG (cacheaugmented generation) methods to dynamically obtain pertinent information and to efficiently recycle “high-confidence” answers. The JEE360 also uses the LangChain architecture to manage the orchestrated reasoning of document loaders, embeddings, and pipelines, so that the processes of data retrieval and answer generation run in a coordinated and seamless manner. More than all of these more complex features, JEE360 is capable of offering streamlined support to students of all levels and backgrounds in order to provide quality JEE preparation and adaptability to a variety of learners.

title: Recognition of Spread Spectrum Signal of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Based on Adaptive Algorithm
authors: Yanli Shi, Haixia Yu, Lin Ren, Shunan Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Spread spectrum signal have some characteristics, for example anti multipath fading, anti-interception and so on, it had been widely used in the field of unmanned aerial vehicle communication. So studying the identification of spread spectrum signal in UAV communication link has important meaning. Combining practical application, this paper advance an improved method for the identification of spread spectrum signal in UAV communication link. This method is based on the adaptive filter, combining with multiple correlation spectrum calculation to enhance the recognition effect of spread spectrum signal, and optimizing process, thus can suppress the interference and enhance the recognition effect of spread spectrum signal in complex background. At the same time, using the MATLAB simulation experiment test the recognition function of the improved method, this can establish the theory basis for practical application.

title: Advanced Vehicular Compliance and Emergency Alert Framework for Regulated Traffic Environments
authors: R R Rubia Gandhi, Murugalakshmi S, Shaswat A, Shyam A, Pavithra C
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This paper introduces an advanced automatic vehicle speed control system that combines intelligent zone detection, real-time geo location, and Internet of Things-based vehicle management to enforce dynamic speed limits. The system's major goal is to increase road safety by reducing traffic infractions brought on by excessive speeding, which continues to be one of the main causes of traffic accidents globally. The suggested solution precisely identifies restricted areas and the associated speed limitations for certain road segments by combining GPS-based data with the Google Maps Roads API. The system ensures safer driving behaviour by automatically adjusting the vehicle's speed to meet the specified threshold when it detects that the vehicle is beyond the allowed speed. The manual override capability, which enables drivers to momentarily disable automatic speed control in the event of an emergency such as a road hazard or a medical situation is a crucial component of the system. During such incidents, local authorities receive real-time alerts via GSM connection that include vital information such as the driver's ID, car number, position coordinates, and timestamp. A cloud-based Firebase database continuously logs all override actions, speed data, and compliance events, facilitating post-event analysis, accountability, and data-driven traffic management enhancements. In comparison to traditional RF-based systems, the system has shown notable gains in speed detection accuracy, lower alert latency, and higher compliance rates through simulation-based testing and real-time scenarios. The findings show that combining autonomous vehicle control, cloud connectivity, and geo location information can greatly improve traffic safety and compliance with regulations. The suggested solution's modular design also offers scalability for future integration with intelligent transportation systems, such as predictive traffic modelling, smart city traffic management programs, and vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) and vehicle-toinfrastructure (V2I) communication. A thorough framework for proactive, data-driven, and effective vehicle speed management in both urban and highway settings is established by this study.

title: Efficient Implementation of the Sigmoid Function on FPGA Using Cordic
authors: Yangfan Sun, U-Fat Chio, Yu Pang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: Currently, deep learning algorithms are widely used in automatic classification and detection problems in the biomedical field. Implementing these algorithms on digital platforms presents many challenges. The main issue is that it requires specialized hardware to meet real-time and low-power requirements. Hence, this thesis proposes a novel hardware implementation scheme for the Sigmoid activation function. Based on a pipeline architecture and combined with the Cordic algorithm, it realizes a Cordic-based Sigmoid function hardware architecture. The design adopts a two-stage pipeline structure, reducing the critical path delay by 25%.

title: Supercapacitor and Battery Based Energy Storage for Electric Vehicle
authors: Kuldeep Singh, Mukhtiar Singh, Sudarshan K Valluru
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: A supercapacitor (SC) and battery based hybrid energy storage system (HESS) for an electric vehicle (EV) is presented in this paper. These two energy sources complement each other to improve the performance and increase the life of an EV. SC and battery are connected together at the DC bus through bi-directional DC-DC (BDDC) converter enabling separate control over each of the source. Vehicle dynamics are considered and the required load torque is calculated. To power the EV motor, a filter based energy management strategy is used which divides the power between battery and supercapacitor. Here the aim is to maximize the use of supercapacitor during various situations demanding rapid acceleration or climbing of hills. To verify the control design and assess the effectiveness of system, a typical city drive cycle for passenger cars is considered and results are obtained using Matlab/Simulink.

title: Design and Implementation of a Fully Digital Loitering Munition Simulation Platform Based on Unreal Engine and SiamRPN
authors: Miao Li, Song Wang, Xuyang Sun, Rong Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: To address the high costs of live-fire tests and site restrictions in the verification of loitering munition guidance algorithms and system performance, a fully digital simulation platform is designed and implemented based on Unreal Engine. The platform integrates a six-degree-of-freedom flight dynamics model, digital seeker system, SiamRPN target tracking algorithm, and proportional navigation guidance law, achieving closed-loop simulation covering the complete process from target search, recognition, tracking to terminal guidance. Experimental results demonstrate that the platform maintains a stable visual simulation frame rate of 60 fps, achieves a target tracking success rate of 92.3%, with an average tracking error less than 5 pixels and terminal guidance accuracy better than 2 meters. This platform provides an efficient and cost-effective digital solution for guidance algorithm verification and system performance evaluation of loitering munitions, effectively shortening the development cycle.

title: A Multi-Timescale Source-Grid-Load-Storage Cooperative Dispatch Strategy Considering Economy and Flexibility
authors: Wengang Cao, Qi Wang, Cunbang Jia, Yulin Geng
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy into power systems creates significant challenges in maintaining both economic efficiency and operational flexibility. This study presents a multi-level dispatch strategy for coordinating source-grid-load-storage (SGLS) resources, including thermal power units, renewable generation, energy storage systems, and demand response mechanisms. The approach employs a day-ahead, intraday, and real-time scheduling structure that continuously improves decision-making through updated forecasting data. Energy storage systems are utilized to compensate for renewable prediction inaccuracies and stabilize net load variations, while a flexibility ramping product (FRP) manages short-term power imbalances. Simulation results based on an enhanced IEEE 30-bus test system demonstrate that although the combined renewable and storage approach increases operational costs, it substantially improves system flexibility and reliability. The FRP mechanism proves particularly effective in maintaining grid stability in systems with high renewable energy penetration.

title: A Study on Magnetic Interference of TEC Cooling System on Diamond Quantum Current Sensor
authors: Xiaotong Li, Lei Wang, Feihu Tao, Ximing Gao, Jiguang Zhao
abstract: This paper investigates the thermal management and magnetic interference issues associated with the integration of thermoelectric cooling (TEC) systems into diamond quantum current sensors for power grid applications. With the increasing demand for advanced monitoring and control systems in modern power grids, quantum current sensors based on nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamonds offer significant potential due to their wide measurement range, high sensitivity, and excellent environmental adaptability. However, integrating these sensors into a compact System in Package (SiP) design introduces thermal challenges, particularly in managing the heat generated by the laser diode (LD). The TEC-based active cooling system, although compact and precise, generates magnetic interference that may impact sensor performance. Through finite element analysis (FEA) and experimental validation, the study reveals that the TEC causes significant magnetic interference, especially within 5 mm of its surface. A solution is proposed, positioning the TEC at a sufficient distance from the sensor to reduce magnetic interference to below 1 μT. Further analysis suggests that while reducing interference to sub-nanotesla levels requires a larger distance, this may reduce cooling efficiency and device integration. The findings provide valuable insights into the design of quantum current sensors with TECs, offering a balance between minimizing magnetic interference and maintaining system efficiency.

title: Identification of Lumbar Muscle Fatigue Status Using a Parallel Fusion Network Based on Time and Frequency Domain Features
authors: Shuhong Cheng, Jian Li, Yameng Dong, Qiang Luo, Nan Xu, Chao Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Low back pain is commonly caused by lumbar muscle fatigue. Prolonged overloading or poor posture readily leads to muscle strain, thereby triggering or exacerbating pain. Consequently, accurately assessing the fatigue state of lumbar muscles is crucial for injury prevention and pain relief. Surface electromyography (sEMG) analysis provides an objective means of identifying muscle fatigue levels, supporting early prediction and prevention. Traditional approaches predominantly rely on sEMG for post-hoc state determination, lacking prospective predictive capability. Moreover, most studies are confined to a binary classification framework of fatigue versus non-fatigue, making it difficult to capture the dynamic progression of fatigue. To address this, this paper proposes a Time and Frequency Domain Parallel Fusion Network (TFDPFNet). This model employs a Patch-LSTM Fusion Network (PLF-Net) to predict time-domain features and a TCN-Attention network to predict frequency-domain features, effectively mitigating cross-domain interference. Subsequently, four-channel dual-domain features are fused into a comprehensive vector and input into an improved Pied Kingfisher Optimizer-optimised LightGBM (IPKO-LightGBM) classifier, enabling a three-class fatigue assessment. To validate the model's efficacy, four-channel sEMG data were collected from twelve healthy subjects during isometric contraction on a Roman chair, with fatigue levels quantified using the RPE scale. Comparative experiments demonstrated superior performance in both feature prediction and classification accuracy compared to alternative methods, exhibiting robust predictive capability and adaptability. This provides reliable technical support for the prevention and rehabilitation training of lumbar muscle fatigue.

title: Analysis of Optical Parameter Effects on Image Quality in a CMOS-Based Lensless Imaging System for Transformer Oil Inspection
authors: Daoyuan Liu
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: The performance of transformer oil is directly related to the safe and reliable operation of power systems. During long-term service, various particulate contaminants are generated in the oil due to thermal aging, partial discharge, and metal corrosion, which degrade the insulation properties and may lead to breakdown failures. Traditional detection methods, such as partial discharge measurement, optical particle counting, and microscopic observation, suffer from low efficiency, complex operation, and high subjectivity in practical applications. Lensless imaging technology eliminates conventional optical lenses and records sample information through light diffraction and interference, offering advantages of structural simplicity, low cost, and a wide field of view. This study investigates the influence of illumination intensity and imaging distance on the image quality of a lensless imaging system. An experimental platform with adjustable illumination and source-to-sample distance was developed to analyze the variations of particle diffraction patterns and reconstructed images under different conditions. The results demonstrate that both illumination wavelength and distance have significant effects on image sharpness, contrast, and signal-to-noise ratio, providing theoretical and experimental guidance for high-precision detection of particles in transformer oil.

title: Feature Extraction and Online Identification of Driving Styles During Lane-Changing Maneuversuse
authors: Pengshuai Wu, Lin Zou, Yiying Wei, Li Hang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)

title: Icing Load Separation and Equivalent Ice-Thickness Estimation for Transmission Lines via MIC-Based Feature Selection and Random Forest Regression
authors: Xuguang Hu, Dejun Jin, Kai Zhu, Feng Lin, Tingrui Zhang, YiJia Jin
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Overhead transmission lines are prone to icing under low-temperature, high-humidity conditions, posing serious risks to power grid safety. Traditional tension-based monitoring approaches rely on fixed reference baselines and are easily perturbed by non-icing factors, leading to biased ice-thickness estimates. This paper proposes an ice-thickness inversion method that integrates Maximal Information Coefficient (MIC)–based feature selection with Random Forest regression. MIC is first used to identify meteorological variables strongly associated with vertical load; a Random Forest model trained on ice-free periods then establishes a dynamic baseline, enabling separation of icing-induced additional loads. Finally, conductor parameters and an equivalent-length model are combined to estimate the equivalent ice thickness.Validation with field data from Zhejiang Province demonstrates high prediction accuracy for vertical load under non-icing conditions, with the coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> reaching 0.959. The proposed method effectively suppresses interference from non-icing factors and shows strong potential for engineering application.

title: Active Thermal Control of Power Devices through Coordinated Loss Control
authors: Jun Zhang, Haiyan Sun, Jiaxin Wu, Pengju Sun
abstract: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBT) are widely used in electric vehicles, rail transportation, and energy systems, yet their high failure rate remains a critical reliability challenge. Most junction temperature control methods simplify power devices as single-input single-output (SISO) systems, assuming each chip's temperature is only affected by the power loss of the chip itself. However, these power devices comprise multiple chips and behave as multiple-input single-output (MISO) systems. This paper proposes an MPC-based method that treats power devices as MISO systems for junction temperature regulation. By designing a predictive model and an optimized cost function, this approach enables coordinated control of both IGBT and Free-Wheeling Diode (FWD) power losses, reducing stress on individual devices. Finite element simulations confirm the method's effectiveness in suppressing junction temperature fluctuations.

title: Unikernels vs. Containers: A Runtime-Level Performance Comparison for Resource-Constrained Edge Workloads
authors: Hai Dinh-Tuan
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: The choice between containers and unikernels is a critical trade-off for edge applications, balancing the container's ecosystem maturity against unikernel's specialized efficiency. However, until now, how this trade-off behaves under the severe memory constraints of industrial edge environments remains insufficiently investigated, especially across different execution models. This work presents an empirical comparison using Go and Node.js applications, representing ahead-of-time (AOT) and just-in-time (JIT) compilation, respectively. While unikernels consistently deliver faster startup times and outperform containers for Go-based workloads in resource-constrained environments, the evaluation results identify a critical performance crossover for Node.js. Below a certain memory threshold, Docker containers maintain stable performance for both I/O-bound and CPUbound applications, while the Nanos unikernel's performance degrades sharply. This reveals that Linux's memory management capabilities can outweigh the minimalist efficiency of unikernels under resource scarcity, a critical trade-off that, until now, has not been adequately quantified for JIT runtimes in this context. These findings demonstrate that the optimal deployment paradigm depends on both runtime behavior and available system resources, emphasizing the need for workload-aware deployment strategies in edge computing.

title: Enhanced Magneto-Optic Ultraviolet Response in Puckered Graphene Sheets
authors: Rajib Mahato, Maruthi Mala, Anagh Bhaumik
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: We investigate the magneto-optic behavior of puckered graphene quantum sheets (GQSs) functionalized with ~5 nm graphene quantum dots (GQDs). The introduction of magneto-optic coupling significantly enhances the performance of the fabricated photodetectors. The enhancement factor (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> /R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>) of both uncoated and coated commercial light-dependent resistors (LDRs) is systematically examined under ultraviolet illumination, with and without the application of an external magnetic field. The observed magnetic interaction originates from the presence of unpaired electrons within the GQS structure. Raman spectroscopic analysis confirms the existence of defect-induced spin polarization of delocalized charge carriers when subjected to a perpendicular magnetic field. The applied magnetic field promotes charge transport by forcing carriers to follow curved trajectories under the influence of the Lorentz force, resulting in increased electrical conductivity. The device exhibits response and recovery times of 5.56 ms and 93.28 ms, respectively, which are similar to those of commercial LDRs. A synergistic effect arising from the high sp<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> carbon content (87%), an elevated I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf> ratio (~25), and a low I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ratio (0.02) leads to an elevated magneto-optic change in response of approximately 15% at 300 K when the magnetic field is varied from 0 to 0.7 T. These uniquely engineered puckered graphene quantum materials demonstrate promising magneto-optic properties and hold potential for applications in emerging quantum-based technologies.

title:
A 5.88-to-8.69 GHz 252fs
                    <sub>rms</sub>
                    -integrated-jitter Integer-N Phase-Locked Loop for 32Gb/s SerDes Transceiver Clocking in 40-nm CMOS
authors: Jiangtao Li, Meishan Wang, Xinsheng Wang, Congyi Zhang, Xiyue Wang, Chengxu Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper presents an integer-N phase-locked loop (PLL) suitable for a 32Gb/s Serializer-Deserializer (SerDes) application. In this design, a charge pump (CP) based on multicurrent output and dual operational amplifier clamping technology, as well as a voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) based on optimized inductors and a digitally controlled tail current array, are employed to reduce the phase noise and jitter of the overall PLL system. A frequency divider chain composed of an injection-locked frequency divider (ILFD) and programmable dividers is adopted in the feedback path, providing a wide frequency locking range. Additionally, the PLL features an automatic frequency control (AFC) function to accelerate locking. The PLL is fabricated in a 40 nm CMOS process. At a reference frequency of $\mathbf{1 0 0 M H z}$, the proposed PLL can achieve fast lock within $2.3 \mu$ s and exhibits a phase noise of 108.37dBc/Hz at 1 MHz offset from an 8 GHz output frequency. The random jitter within the 1 kHz to 100 MHz integration interval is 0.252 ps. Under a bit error rate (BER) of $10^{-12}$, the maximum total jitter is less than 6.4 ps, and the total power consumption is 19.6 mW.

title: EEG Emotion Recognition in Brain Computer Interface Through Neural Networks and Adaptive Machine Learning
authors: Yuvaraj D, Jagathpranesh R, T. Velmurugan, Gokul Anand S, Meenalochini M, Mithrun R S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Human Computer Interaction (HCI), affective computing, and mental health have experienced a sharp rise in use in EEG signal-based emotion classfication. Accurate and generalized emotion finding from Electroencephalography signal is a critical step for realizing the practical usage of functional Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI). This work tracks a neat flowchart designed for dimensional emotion classification (Valence and Arousal) using public DEAP dataset. The primary innovation here is the use of a Multi-Task Time-Series Transformer (TST) model, which uses a Self-Attention method to dynamically filter subject specific noise and extract general neural patterns. The developed Time-Series Transformer framework predicts both Valence and Arousal simultaneously, by leveraging the intrinsic relation between two emotion factor. We used the Subject-Independent (LOSOCV) protocol combined with a non-leaking feature scaling strategy to ensure test data integrity. Experimental results confirm that the TST model’s effectiveness about 78.05% mean accuracy which was achieved for Valence classification, demonstrating state-of-the-art generalization capability across subjects. And the Arousal dimension came to 50.94% in the generalized test, experimentally confirming that Arousal-related features are highly subject-specific and thus demand individu-alized calibration. The proposed methodology establishes a new, verifiable benchmark for generalized EEG emotion recognition and automatically highlighting the potential and current limitations of BCI-based affective computing.

title: Research on the Performance Enhancement of the Beam Splitter in Photonics Communication Chips
authors: Hao Sun, Xingyu Liu, Jiaxi Tang, Yue Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: In the era of vigorous development of optoelectronic technology, the high-performance lithium niobate optical beam splitter, as a core component for optical signal processing and transmission, provides an excellent material basis for optical signal modulation with its outstanding electro-optical properties. At present, it has been found in research and application that three-dimensional ridge-shaped strip waveguides can enhance the interaction between light and electric fields. Multimode interferometric couplers (MMI) have the advantages of compact structure, simple fabrication and stable mode conversion. The combination of the two can achieve efficient optical beam splitting and precise modulation, effectively improving the modulation efficiency. Through theoretical analysis and numerical simulation, the light field distribution, mode characteristics and modulation performance parameters of this structure were deeply studied. The results show that compared with the traditional structure, this design significantly improves the modulation efficiency, and can effectively reduce mode crosstalk at the same time, achieving stable optical signal beam splitting and modulation. This research provides a new approach for the application of lithium niobate beam splitters in high-speed optical communication, optical signal processing and other fields, which is conducive to promoting the development of optoelectronic integrated devices.

title: 2025 6th International Conference on Power Engineering (ICPE)
authors: N/A

title: 3D Dynamic Terrain Rendering Optimization based on C # and DirectX Fusion
authors: Jijun Geng, Congcong Wu, Yang Xu, Yuxiang Wu, Jinchi Chang, Yuxiang Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: To tackle the limitations of traditional 2D topographic maps—namely, their large data volumes, low display efficiency, and poor spatial expression—this study introduces a novel 3D dynamic terrain visualization method using C# and DirectX. This approach capitalizes on DirectX's hardware acceleration and C#'s efficient development features. It also addresses the geometric distortion and material compatibility issues of DirectX's native X file format when importing 3DMax models by devising an innovative L file format and a dedicated export plugin. Through modular encapsulation of grid topology, material properties, lighting parameters, and custom node data, the method achieves high-fidelity 3D model migration. The study further establishes a dynamic terrain rendering system using world-view-projection matrix transformations for 3D space mapping and enabling real-time dynamic rotation via time-driven rotation matrices. Integrating vertex atomization models enhances depth of field, while linear texture filtering and a multi-light source material system boost visual realism. Experimental results confirm that this method delivers smooth local terrain rendering, outperforming traditional 2D displays in efficiency and effectively supporting applications like geological disaster simulation and virtual city reconstruction.

title: Comparison of Consequent-Pole and Single-Stator Single-Rotor Motors for All-Electric Aircraft
authors: Yuxuan Chen, Yan Zhu, Congli Mei, Chuanhui Zhu, Mengting Wei, Yingkang Xu, Xiaohui Yang, Jingkai Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: This paper conducts research on the performance differences between the single-stator single-rotor motor and the consequent-pole motor for All-Electric Aircraft applications. This study focuses on investigating their output performance at a constant speed. To support this investigation, finite element simulation is adopted to conduct research on their structural design, magnetic circuit distribution characteristics, and energy loss mechanisms, and building on these findings, it further evaluates their application potential and optimization directions in aircraft power systems. Quantitative results show comparable performance between the two motors, with the consequent-pole motor using only 50% of the permanent magnet material of the single-stator single-rotor motor while maintaining a stable torque output ranging from 13.5 Nm to 15.0 Nm and similar energy loss levels. The research results provide theoretical support and practical reference for the structural selection and performance improvement of motors for aircraft. They also prove that the Comparison of Consequent-Pole motor can save more permanent magnets. And gives an outlook on important future research topics.

title: "Seeing is not always believing": Exploring Bystanders’ Privacy Concerns on Multifunction IoT Devices in China
authors: Yijing Liu, Ling Liu, Yan Jia, Qingyin Tan, Zheli Liu, Tong Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet of Things (IoT), multifunction devices are becoming increasingly prevalent. These devices closely resemble their single-function counterparts in appearance but incorporate additional, often concealed, functionalities. Such hidden features can make it difficult for bystanders (e.g., guests or visitors) to fully understand device capabilities through normal passive interaction, hindering their ability to recognize associated privacy risks. To investigate this issue, we conducted an online survey with 169 Chinese participants, using videos to present a smart home scenario with seven multifunction and two single-function IoT devices. Participants, acting as bystanders, first identified possible data collection and privacy concerns based on simulated limited interactions, then repeated the task after learning the devices’ full functionalities. Results show that while participants readily recognized privacy risks in single-function devices, they frequently overlooked those in multifunction devices, particularly when additional sensing capabilities were not apparent. Privacy concern scores increased significantly once hidden functionalities were revealed, indicating that limited awareness prevents bystanders from accurately assessing risks. These findings highlight the importance of improving transparency in multifunction IoT devices. We finally propose practical recommendations for manufacturers, policymakers, and end users to enhance protection for bystanders.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach to Battery Optimal Management for Urban EV Charging and Swapping Network with Uncertain Demand
authors: Zixiang Zhu, Zhihua Chen, Jing Zhang, Yiwei Su
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: The remarkable increasing production and use of electric vehicles (EVs) has caused operation management issue for urban EV charging and swapping network. The current battery swapping stations are facing daily uncertain and high-load refueling demands with uneven spatiotemporal distribution. Therefore, this paper constructs a dynamic decision-making model based on Markov Decision Process (MDP) for the battery swapping mode of centralized charging. The Double Deep Q-learning (DDQN) algorithm is exploited to learning the optimal charging schemes with considering fluctuations of electricity bill, battery quantity and random swapping demand. The decision-making process is simplified through a greedy algorithm to reduce complexity and improve efficiency. The effectiveness of the model and algorithm are verified through simulation. The proposed approach outperforms the traditional method by about 11% in terms of total attenuation revenue.

title: A Physics-Based Simulation Framework for Tower Crane Path Planning
authors: Zilu Zhou, Yifan Lu, Weili Fang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Path planning is a critical challenge for tower crane automation, its validation in real construction environments remains costly, risky, and difficult to reproduce. To overcome these challenges, this study proposes a physics-based simulation framework for quantitative evaluation of tower crane path planning algorithms under realistic physical constraints. The framework is developed on the PyBullet engine and adopts a modular, plug and play architecture comprising four components: (1) scene construction; (2) path planning; (3) action control; and (4) performance evaluation. The integrated design enabling consistent testing of different planning strategies within a unified physics environment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework can stably execute and evaluate multiple algorithms under identical conditions. This framework provides a flexible and extensible foundation for simulation-based research and development in intelligent tower crane operation.

title: M-GGF: A Multidimensional Graph with Adaptive Graph Gated Fusion for Multimodal Emotion Recognition
authors: Mengping Zhong, Jiongxun Lin, Junkun Huang, Bao Peng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Emotion recognition, a central task in affective computing, is pursued with the objective of deriving a deeper and more robust interpretation of human emotional states through the integration of multimodal data. Although existing multimodal emotion recognition approaches have made notable progress, they still face substantial challenges in modeling complex inter-modal interactions, long-range semantic dependencies, and effective information fusion. To address these issues, this study proposes a Multimodal Emotion Recognition Model based on Multidimensional Graphs and Adaptive Graph-Gated Fusion (M-GGF). Within M-GGF, a Multidimensional Graph Construction (MGC) module is developed to build a unified heterogeneous graph that jointly models temporal dependencies, cross-modal interactions, and long-range semantic relations. In addition, an Adaptive Graph-Gated Residual Fusion (Adaptive GGRF) mechanism is introduced to achieve fine-grained integration of local and global semantics. Experimental results on the IEMOCAP benchmark confirm the superior performance of the proposed M-GGF model. Specifically, it achieves accuracies of 68.53% and 85.28% alongside weighted F1 scores of 68.76% and 84.98% on the six-class and four-class tasks, respectively. These findings validate the effectiveness and superiority of M-GGF in modeling complex emotional dynamics, providing a promising direction for next-generation affective computing systems.

title: ReNN-RV: Run-time PE Re configuration for D NN Inference Acceleration with Custom R ISC- V ISA
authors: Yueting Li, Terry Tao Ye, Ngai Wong, Zhenhua Zhu, Yongfu Li, Weisheng Zhao
abstract: Deep neural network (DNN) accelerators integrated with RISC-V Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) extensions have enabled efficient computing on resource-constrained platforms. However, their specialization in regular compute patterns limits effectiveness on irregular workloads, making it challenging to achieve high throughput and energy efficiency. To tackle these challenges, we present ReNN-RV, which integrates a computation-aware RISC-V ISA extension with an instructiondriven processing pipeline to efficiently accelerate run-time reconfigurable processing elements (RePEs). The computation-aware ISA employs configurable opcodes and custom encodings to support fine-grained task scheduling, while an instructiondriven pipeline implements it with minimal control complexity. Moreover, the RePE accelerator provides seamless switching between multiply-accumulate (MAC) and non-MAC operations by configuring a path multiplexer to realize multiple operators at run time. Experimental results demonstrate that ReNN-RV achieves average reductions of 14.6× in cycle count and 15.3× in execution time across representative DNN workloads compared with the baseline RISC-V design. On average, ReNN-RV outperforms state-of-the-art designs by 10.1× for energy efficiency and 10.3× for computational throughput.

title: Orientation Effect and Enhancement Mechanism of Magnetoelectric Conversion Performance in Multilayer Nanocomposite for Magnetic Field Perception
authors: Zecheng Qin, Jia-Wei Zhang, Xuan Meng, Wenjia Zheng, Abderrahim Aboulouafa, Bin Zhang, Fouad Belhora, Chatchai Putson
abstract: With the rapid development of the Internet of Everything (IoE) and intelligent sensing technologies, magnetoelectric (ME) materials have attracted increasing in-terest for applications in magnetic sensing, energy harvesting, and self-powered electronic systems. Enhancing the ME response through material design and cou-pling mechanism optimization remains a key challenge in advancing device per-formance. In this work, a laminated magnetoelectric composite with a P(VDF-TrFE)/Fe₃O₄–PZT architecture is proposed, in which a flexible polymer–particle composite layer is integrated with a piezoelectric ceramic to achieve enhanced magnetoelectric conversion through a synergistic multi-physics mechanism. In this structure, Fe₃O₄ nanoparticles dispersed in the P(VDF-TrFE) matrix provide magnetic-field-responsive mechanical interactions and simultaneously couple with Lorentz-force-induced strain generated in the metallic electrode layers under com-bined AC and DC magnetic fields. Owing to the cooperative contribution of mag-netic-response-induced deformation and Lorentz force effects, the proposed com-posite exhibits an average 15% enhancement in magnetoelectric voltage output compared with the P(VDF-TrFE)-PZT bilayer structure. A pronounced orienta-tion-dependent magnetoelectric response is also observed, with the output voltage following a cosine-like dependence on the magnetic field direction. Furthermore, the feasibility of magnetic energy harvesting under low-frequency excitation is ex-perimentally demonstrated, highlighting the potential of the proposed structure for flexible magnetic sensing and self-powered electronic applications.

title: Tunable Magnetically Actuated Retraction Device for Improved Control During Endoscopic Tissue Manipulation_supp1-3668134.pdf
authors: Eric Diller
abstract: Objective: Endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) is a minimally-invasive method for removing gastrointestinal lesions. Adoption has been slow due to the procedure's technical difficulty, length of surgery, and lack of appropriate tools. Tissue traction is required to reduce the technical difficulty of ESD and ensure safe lesion removal. This paper presents the first ESD retraction device that is wireless, endoscope-independent, and provides adjustable traction force control. Methods: The magnetic retraction device is 3.25 mm wide and 45 mm long. It is actuated wirelessly by a rotating permanent magnet held outside the abdomen. The device design is informed by a presented model of actuation, and we show characterization of the device actuation, validation of mechanical retraction, and clinical feasibility through an ex-vivo experiment. Results: Experiments show that the device retracts 20 mm at 0.2 millimeters per second and generates a peak retraction force of 7.98 N and a four-peak average of 5.62 N at an external magnet-to-device distance of 55 mm. Ex-vivo testing with a gastric porcine model was performed with a dissection speed of 11.3 square centimeters per hour, 1.25 times faster than the reported international benchmark. Conclusion: This enhanced tissue control would greatly improve patient clinical outcomes by expanding the use of ESD, shortening procedure times, minimizing complications, and potentially further expand its future medical applications. Significance: This work grants surgeons improved tissue control throughout an ESD procedure, providing adequate visualization of the dissection plane, control independent of the flexible endoscope, and deployment anywhere along the gastrointestinal tract.

title: Optimizing Scalable Storage for Blockchain-Based IoT Cross-Domain Authentication
authors: Deyu Luo, Gang Sun, Hongfang Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Traditional centralized authentication systems, relying on Public Key Infrastructure (PKI), face significant challenges such as single points of failure, privacy concerns, and scalability issues. Blockchain technology, with its decentralized, tamper-resistant, and transparent nature, offers a promising solution to these challenges. However, as the number of IoT devices and the volume of authentication data increase, blockchain-based systems face a storage bottleneck, limiting their scalability and performance. This paper proposes a scalable storage optimization method to address these issues. We introduce the concept of blockchain data value and develop a storage strategy that prioritizes the retention of critical authentication data while minimizing storage overhead. We formalize the problem as an optimization problem, and two heuristic algorithms are proposed to allocate storage resources efficiently. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy in alleviating storage pressure and improving the scalability of blockchain-based cross-domain authentication systems in IoT environments.

title: SEAL: Semantic-Aware Contrastive Learning for scRNA-seq clustering
authors: Yixuan Ye, Jiawen Sun, Liang Peng, Dan Yang, Jiajun Xian, Wenjun Shen, Cheng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: The development of single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) technology has enabled the exploration of biological processes at the cellular level. A critical task in scRNA-seq data analysis is the unsupervised clustering of cells to distinguish different cell types. While various clustering methods have been successfully developed for scRNA-seq data, they still face limitations, particularly in terms of unstable clustering performance. This is often due to their inability to fully capture the intrinsic properties of cells, especially in the presence of high dropout rates and noise in the data. In this work, we propose a SEmantic-Aware contrastive Learning (SEAL) approach for scRNA-seq clustering. Specifically, we randomly mask the gene expression of each cell to generate two different augmentations of the cell data, and then apply semantic-aware contrastive learning to capture semantically invariant representations across these augmentations by leveraging semantic information from generated pseudo-labels. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively learns biologically meaningful representations and accurately identifies cell types.

title: Evaluating Reinforcement Learning-Based Xapps for User-Centric Resource Allocation in Open RAN
authors: Boikobo Nokane, Bassey Isong, Moshe. T. Masonta
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Dynamic radio resource management is essential for optimising performance in an open radio access network (RAN). However, traditional methods often struggle with realtime decision-making and resource allocation in dynamic environments. Intelligent solutions, such as xApps running on near-real-time (near-RT) RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs), offer promising advancements in adaptability and efficiency. This paper evaluates the performance of 5 reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms: Advantage Actor-Critic (A2C), Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO), Deep Q-Network (DQN) and Soft Actor Critic (SAC), which were implemented as xApps for dynamic resource allocation in an open RAN setting. We analyse how effectively each algorithm learns to allocate resources by associating user equipment (UE), connected to different service classes: video, voice and data, to base stations as they move through a simulated network environment. Additionally, we evaluate the agents by comparing their throughput, latency and reward performance, and validate the results by examining them over 5 seeds run under the same network configurations. Our findings reveal that DQN achieves the highest rewards and fastest convergence, followed by SAC, PPO offers stable learning at moderate reward levels, while A2C and A3C show slower convergence and lower rewards. These results demonstrate both the advantages and limitations of using RL for dynamic resource management in open RAN systems. Finally, we discuss future directions, such as exploring multiagent systems and deploying xApps in real-world open RAN testbeds, to advance practical applications.

title: Probed Measurement Techniques for 100 Gbit/s D-Band Wireless Link Experiments
authors: Reinier Broucke, Samuel Rimbaut, Kamil Yavuz Kapusuz, Nishant Singh, Hendrik Rogier, Sam Lemey, Guy Torfs
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: We present a specialized measurement setup for active topside probing of D-band antenna arrays, designed to eliminate probe interference by placing the probe landing pad on the backside of the carrier, opposite the radiating aperture. This setup enables wireless data transmission measurements with an integrated active D-band antenna array comprising a SiGe power amplifier (PA) wirebonded to a PCB-integrated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 1$</tex> subarray. Using this setup, we demonstrate a maximum symbol rate of 22.5 Gbaud and achieve a peak data rate of 100 Gbit/s with a 20 Gbaud 32-QAM signal while maintaining an error vector magnitude (EVM) of 19% at a measurement distance of 25 cm.

title: Modernizing Distributed Data Center Architectures with AI-Orchestrated Resource Scheduling and Accelerated Computing using Blackwell GPUs and Grace CPUs
authors: Pavan Reddy Nookala, Sharath Reddy Gade, Narendra Balaji Medikonda, Sharat Chandra Kothakapu, Suresh Reddy Addula, Avinash Reddy Betheli
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) is gaining popularity in various fields, such as computer vision, speech identification, and self-driving vehicles. Blackwell graphical processing units (GPUs) and Grace central processing units (CPUs) are widely utilized in computing platforms, including portable gadgets and supercomputers. Optimizing performance for multiple-programming workloads on such devices is crucial, as a single application cannot typically utilize all available resources. Developing effective GPU resource administration and scheduling systems is crucial for effective distribution among several clients. Nevertheless, current versions have several flaws. Clients often provide wrong task resource requirements, resulting in cluster resource low utilization. DL-based resource scheduling without considering cluster network features leads to poor operational efficiency. To address these difficulties, this study develops a distributed data center (DDC) framework using Blackwell GPUs and Grace CPUs for resource scheduling and accelerated computing with DL. This study offers a regression model-based technique for estimating task resource requirements, preventing clients from overallocating computer resources. This paper demonstrates smart cluster network-effective scheduling approaches for both prompt and batch forms based on the resource need assessment methodologies described. Study findings demonstrate that the suggested system can improve task computation by 19% overall and reduce mean job completion duration (JCD) by 22% when evaluated against modern approaches. Furthermore, the presented optimization approaches are useful in a variety of contexts.

title: 36.1 ReFIND: A Resolution-Reconfigurable Bio-Signal Classification SoC Enabling $>10\times$ Savings in AFE Power Per Channel
authors: Aviral Pandey, I-Ting Lin, Dhruv Vaish, Ashwin Rammohan, Savit Bhat, Jade Pinkenburg, Rikky Muller
abstract: This work presents ReFIND, an SoC integrating together 16 resolution-reconfigurable AFEs, a feature extractor, and a classifier. The VCO ADC architecture trades power for resolution by scaling the integration time, from 13 to 10 b at 1.77 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.12 \mu \mathrm{W} /$</tex> channel. End-to-end validation shows up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$14 \times$</tex> power saving over a fixed array, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> more than channel selection. ReFIND enables runtime resolution scaling, opening up power-performance optimization in bio-signal classifiers.

title: A Novel Federated Learning Architecture for Robust Multi-Modal Mobility and Crowd-Traffic Co-Management in Smart Cities
authors: Nalini Singh Rajput, Ashutosh Mishra, Navin Singh Rajput
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Rapid urbanization, coupled with the proliferation of multi-modal transportation and high-density public gatherings, has created unprecedented challenges for mobility and crowd management in modern smart cities. Centralized artificial intelligence (AI) approaches, though widely deployed in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), suffer from inherent limitations such as communication bottlenecks, privacy risks, and poor adaptability under heterogeneous conditions. This paper proposes a novel federated learning (FL) architecture that integrates vehicular, pedestrian, and IoT-based mobility data with hierarchical aggregation across district-level Integrated Command and Control Centres (ICCCs) and a state-level high-performance computing (HPC) hub. The proposed framework enables distributed intelligence without sharing raw data, ensuring both scalability and privacy preservation. To evaluate its effectiveness, we analyze documented failures of centralized AI—including stampedes during large congregations and traffic spillovers at stadium exits and conduct extensive simulation studies using the NGSIM trajectory dataset, SUMO mobility models, and a digital twin city environment. Empirical results demonstrate that our federated approach improves congestion prediction accuracy by 9.8%, enhances crowd-density estimation by 12.7%, reduces decision latency by 34%, and decreases bandwidth consumption by 62% compared to centralized models. Furthermore, resilience under partial system failure is significantly improved, with only a 3% degradation when 20% of ICCCs are offline, as opposed to a 17% drop for centralized baselines. By unifying heterogeneous data streams under a federated paradigm and leveraging HPC infrastructure, this study advances a scalable, privacy-preserving, and resilient architecture for smart city mobility management. Future extensions include cross-city federation, adaptive model compression, and integration with emerging 6G vehicular communication networks.

title: AI for Sustainable Energy and Climate Change Mitigation
authors: Shripad Joshi, Nagalakshmi Yarlagadda, Smita Jadhav, Deepali V Patil, Bura Vijay Kumar, Jayapal Lande
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: The greater need for clean energy and the urgent need to check global warming necessitate intelligent, data-driven systems to optimize energy efficiency and reduce emissions. Traditional energy control and forecasting methods are not typically dynamic, scalable, or precise enough to meet changing environmental conditions and operating demands. This study evaluates the use of sophisticated Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques to optimize sustainable energy systems and climate change mitigation support. These models, i.e., Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Random Forest, Reinforcement Learning (RL), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and Genetic Algorithms (GA), were applied in renewable energy forecasting, carbon emissions estimation, and optimization of smart grids. Experimental results indicated remarkable accuracy increases, error reduction, and stability compared to conventional techniques. LSTM models achieved up to 24.6% prediction accuracy improvement, and RL demonstrated more adaptability under varying grid scenarios. The overall conclusion charts the revolutionary potential of AI in driving environmental sustainability and conjures the image of an adaptive, resistant model for the future of energy infrastructure and climate policy planning.

title: AI-Powered Mobile Interfaces for Dynamic Field Data Collection in Smart Agriculture Systems
authors: Vibhor Goyal, Jayanth Veeramachaneni
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The farming industry is experiencing a major transformation as modern innovations, especially artificial intelligence (AI), are integrated to improve data assessment methods and speed decision-making procedures. This study examines the incorporation of novel innovations in agriculture, with a particular emphasis on maximizing data assessment utilizing AI to improve agricultural decision-making procedures. As a result, this paper emphasizes the integration of a mobile interface with an AI-based smart agriculture system, also known as AI-SAS, and an Internet of Things (IoT) setting for data collection and classification. The suggested AI-SAS with IoT comprises 3 steps. The initial stage provides a hardware setting containing a real-time sensing device in a mobile interface, all of which are managed by a Raspberry Pi microcontroller. The 2nd stage employs a deep learning-based convolutional neural network (DLCNN) system to optimize nutrient status forecasting and crop disease identification, and categorization. In the 3rd stage, farmers can use an Android-driven mobile app linked to an IoT system to track sensor data and crop leaf disease conditions. Utilizing the smartphone app, the cultivator can constantly monitor the condition of their farm. Furthermore, the suggested AI-SAS creates an autonomous setting, which puts the entire setting under autonomous management and performs the steps required in the cultivation farm to boost output. The study outcomes on disease diagnosis and classification utilizing the suggested AI-SAS with IoT reveal outstanding outcomes in terms of accuracy, F-value of 99.2%, respectively.

title: ISSCC 2026 International Technical Program Committee
authors: N/A

title: 17.4 Maia: A Reticle-Scale AI Accelerator
authors: S. Xu, G. Mandal, S. Jahagirdar, S. Tripathy, P. Parthasarathy, S. Srinivasan, A. Levin
abstract: In Paper 17.4, the architecture and implementation of Microsoft's MAIA AI silicon, a reticle-scale 750W AI SoC, is presented. Innovation across power delivery, thermal management, physical design, validation, and packaging is detailed, resulting in a scalable approach to high-performance AI acceleration.

title: Leveraging Machine Learning and Big Data Analytics to Transform Service Delivery in Public Healthcare
authors: Mfanafuthi. A Makome, Olalekan. S Ogunleye
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: This study targets service-delivery improvements in South African public hospitals by pairing Big Data analytics with machine learning, implemented under a quantitative Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining (CRISP-DM) workflow. We (i) identified determinants of effective Big Data analytics in public healthcare, (ii) compared analytical methods, (iii) built a predictive model of service outcomes, and (iv) assessed its performance and interpretability. Using a synthetic hospital dataset, we engineered features and trained a stacked ensemble (XGBoost, Random Forest, CatBoost, LightGBM) to classify outcomes as Improved, Unchanged, or Worsened. Baseline models (e.g., Support Vector Machine (SVM), logistic regression) were explored but not retained due to scalability and multiclass performance constraints. The final ensemble achieved 84% accuracy with class-wise Area Under the Curves (AUCs) of 0.93-0.95. Post-hoc SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) analysis highlighted Satisfaction Rating, Age, and log (Wait Time) as the most influential features, and revealed interaction effects such as Wait-length interactions that align with operational intuition. To support practical use, we prototyped a Streamlit application that delivers near-real-time, explainable predictions for front-line decision support. The findings indicate that Machine Learning (ML)-driven Big Data analytics can provide scalable, interpretable insights to inform predictive decision-making, resource allocation, and patient-flow management in the public sector. While results are demonstrated on synthetic data, the approach establishes a deployable pathway for hospital analytics in South Africa and a foundation for validation on real-world datasets.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for the Study of the Effects of Facial Filters on Face Recognition Accuracy
authors: Kagan Ozturk, Louisa Conwill, Jacob Gutierrez, Kevin Bowyer, Walter J. Scheirer
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Facial filters are now commonplace for social media users around the world. Previous work has demonstrated that facial filters can negatively impact automated face recognition performance. However, these studies focus on small numbers of hand-picked filters in particular styles. In order to more effectively incorporate the wide ranges of filters present on various social media applications, we introduce a framework that allows for larger-scale study of the impact of facial filters on automated recognition. This framework includes a controlled dataset of face images, a principled filter selection process that selects a representative range of filters for experimentation, and a set of experiments to evaluate the filters’ impact on recognition. We demonstrate our framework with a case study of filters from the American applications Instagram and Snapchat and the Chinese applications Meitu and Pitu to uncover cross-cultural differences. Finally, we show how the filtering effect in a face embedding space can easily be detected and restored to improve face recognition performance.

title: High Frequency Signal Integrity Analysis of Through-Glass Vias for Heterogenous Integration
authors: Mugdha Sharma, Sumit Gupta, Pradeep Dixit, Sanjeev Manhas
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Through glass vias (TGVs) are gaining importance in RF and high-speed electronic systems due to their excellent electrical isolation, mechanical strength, and low dielectric loss. This paper investigates TGV performance for high-frequency applications up to 100 GHz. Three via geometries (cylindrical, tapered, and double-conical) were analyzed using HFSS simulation with their copper thickness being varied from 2 μm to 20 μm. Results show that cylindrical vias achieve superior performance with -0.17 dB insertion loss, while tapered vias exhibit the highest loss at -0.26 dB at 100 GHz. Copper thickness has a strong dependence on via geometry: all the three vias considered in this study are observed to benefit from minimum thickness at high frequencies. Tapered via bottom diameter emerges as the most critical design parameter, with 18 dB return loss improvement achieved by increasing diameter from 5 μm to 20 μm. This study also establishes design guidelines for geometry specific TGV optimization in glass interposer technologies for heterogenous integration.

title: Cost-Efficient and Secure SAP Workload Migration Strategies Leveraging AWS Cloud-Native Architectures
authors: Narendra Balaji Medikonda, Pell Reddy Rajender Reddy, Manish Kumar, Pavan Reddy Nookala, Sai Tharun Reddy Balpunuri, Ram Reddy Jonnalagadda
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The shift of SAP to cloud computing has become one of the strategic priorities of companies who want to achieve scalability, cost-effectiveness, and security. Nevertheless, it is difficult to maintain an ideal performance-security ratio. This is an empirical study that proposes a combined deep learning and machine learning framework to achieve cost-effective and safe SAP workload migration to AWS cloud-native systems. The paper combines predictive cost modeling based on ensemble machine learning with deep neural network-based anomaly detection as a security enhancement tool. SAP workload datasets, AWS migration logs and synthetic benchmarks were experimentally proven. The findings show a 35.6% decrease in the cost of migration and a 35.6% better threat detection rate than with the traditional rule-based strategies of migration. The results highlight the opportunity of hybrid intelligence-based models in influencing the future SAP migration pathways towards enterprise resilience and compliance.

title: Investigating the Factors That Impact the Adoption of Blockchain in Tertiary Institutions
authors: Katlego Kelvin Melamu, Karikoga Norman Gorejena, Moleboge Emma Nhlapo
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Universities should be concerned about the emergence and development of blockchain technology, which has the potential to become a most powerful disruptive innovation in the modern world. blockchain technology is able to address the challenges that both the higher education institutions and society at large are currently facing. Due to their unique qualities, such as data integrity, security, decentralization, and dependability, blockchain technologies have drawn significant interest from academia and industry. This study explores the adoption of blockchain technology in higher education institutions (HEIs), focusing on the perceptions of administrative and IT staff. Using an interpretive qualitative approach, six key themes were identified: technical integration, data security, financial constraints, regulatory challenges, skills and training needs, and organizational culture. While participants expressed cautious optimism about blockchain's potential to improve transparency, record management, and efficiency, they also highlighted significant barriers such as outdated infrastructure, lack of funding, unclear policies, and limited internal expertise. The study proposes a comprehensive framework to guide HEIs in adopting blockchain in a way that considers both technical and human factors. It concludes that successful adoption requires strategic planning, leadership support, and capacity building, offering practical insights for policymakers, institutional leaders, and researchers aiming to advance digital innovation in education.

title:
34.5 A 0.0523mm
                    <sup>2</sup>
