                    11.4mW IEEE 802.15.4a/z/ab Compatible Aliasing-Suppressing All-Digital IR-UWB Transmitter Featuring Comb-Notched Maximally Flat Amplitude Spectral Shaping
authors: Jihun Son, Minsu Park, Chansoo Park, Kyoungtae Lee, Jong-Hyeok Yoon, Junghyup Lee, Minyoung Song
abstract: This work presents an IEEE 802.15.4a/z/ab compatible IR-UWB TX using an anti-phase delayed dual-Gaussian pulse with self-delay pulse comb-notch to maximize the spectrum utilization while meeting global masks. A slope-aware non-uniform interpolation suppresses sampling aliases by 11dB. A dual-level switched-capacitor PA cuts sidelobe emission up to 12dB. The TX draws 11.4mW, reaches 83.4% spectrum utilization and near-in sidelobe (≤35dBr at 489MHz) with ≥9× smaller area than prior arts.

title: Parasitic Capacitance Analysis in Tapered CFET Devices for Advanced Technology Nodes
authors: Jaisingh Pal, Nihar R. Mohapatra
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Process variations in Complementary Field-Effect Transistors (CFETs) introduce tapered channel geometries that significantly impact parasitic capacitance. TCAD simulations reveal taper-induced increases of 10.63%, 16.94%, and 23.16% in total parasitic capacitance for 1-, 2-, and 3-sheet devices. Junction capacitance emerges as the dominant contributor (~20.34%) with strong taper angle dependence. Bottom device effective width increases by ~1.45× at 4° taper. This work establishes taper-aware design guidelines for CFET optimization at advanced technology nodes.

title: 6G Integrated Communication-Sensing-Computing Resource Coordination Mechanism for High-Dynamic Vehicular Network
authors: Zhenning Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: As vehicular networks evolve towards highly dynamic and high-reliability scenarios, the traditional separated management mode of communication, sensing, and computing resources fails to meet the low-latency and high-precision collaborative requirements. This paper proposes a dynamic integrated communication-sensing-computing resource coordination mechanism for 6G vehicular networks, achieving efficient resource utilization in highly mobile environments through joint optimization of resource slicing and task offloading strategies. Firstly, to address the rapidly changing topology caused by high-speed vehicle movement, a spatiotemporal joint dynamic resource slicing method is designed to support the collaborative allocation and rapid reconfiguration of communication, sensing, and computing resources. Secondly, a federated learning-based predictive task offloading framework is proposed, utilizing distributed vehicular sensing data to train deep learning models, dynamically predict network states, and optimize computational task scheduling. From a technical implementation perspective, a hierarchical resource coordination architecture is constructed: the communication layer employs Doppler-resilient waveforms to enhance dynamic channel stability; the sensing layer improves environmental perception capabilities through multi-vehicle radar data fusion; and the computing layer integrates reinforcement learning (Q-learning) to optimize task offloading decisions. Simulation experiments using hardware prototypes demonstrate this mechanism's effective support for low-latency communications, high-precision sensing, and efficient computational offloading in highly dynamic vehicular networks.

title: Pre-Synchronization Control of Virtual Synchronous Generator Based on Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control
authors: Qiang Li, Long Luo, Shangang Ma, Caixiong Fan, Fubao Jin, Shuang Li, Yinhang Hu, Runtian Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: In the process of inverter grid connection, it is typically necessary to accelerate the pre-synchronization speed while ensuring power quality. However, achieving both objectives simultaneously is often challenging. This paper proposes an improved pre-synchronization control strategy for Virtual Synchronous Generators (VSG) based on Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control (LADRC), aimed at balancing both the active-frequency and voltage-reactive power loops of the VSG. By introducing the LADRC controller to replace the traditional Proportional-Integral (PI) controllers in pre-synchronization control, it gradually achieves precise tracking and adjustment of grid voltage parameters. Compared to traditional PI control, the LADRC strategy shows significant advantages in pre-synchronization speed and control accuracy. Simulation results indicate that traditional PI control completes pre-synchronization in 0.7 seconds, while the LADRC strategy can adjust grid voltage and frequency within 0.01 seconds, significantly improving synchronization speed and accuracy, reducing current surges, and effectively preventing grid connection switching impacts. Through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, the proposed control method significantly enhances the stability and reliability of the power system, especially in terms of smooth current transition and system response speed.

title:
Influence of Interfacial Oxide on the Polarity-Dependent SET Process in In
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Sb
                    <sub>1</sub>
                    Te
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Based PCRAM devices
authors: Salman Khan, Sakthikumaran Pannerselvam, Anbarasu Manivannan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work investigates the impact of the interfacial oxide layer between the metal contact and In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Sb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>Te<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (IST) phase-change material on switching dynamics. Quasi-DC measurements fail to detect the oxide, but pulse I-V characteristics reveal its significant influence. The oxide layer affects both switching speed and power consumption. Its impact depends on the direction of the current flow, governed by thermal boundary resistance (TBR) at the interfaces. We propose an interface-engineering strategy that optimizes current flow from the high-TBR side to the low-TBR side, enabling high-speed, low-power SET operations in IST-based Phase-Change Random Access Memory (PCRAM) devices.

title: Design and Characterization of a Miniaturized Fractal-EBG Flexible Membrane Antenna for Spaceborne Communication Systems
authors: Veera Boopathy. E, Dhanasekar.J, Suganya.S, Rajalakshmi.R, Pavithra.V, Mydhili.S.K
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Membrane antennas represent a paradigm shift to the next generation spaceborne and aerospace communication designs that offer high apertures characterized by ultra-low mass, compact stowage, and mechanical flexibility. A miniaturized flexible fractal-EBG membrane antenna is suggested in the current research and simulated with the CST Microwave Studio 2024 and ANSYS HFSS 2023 R2. The structure is based on a third-order Hilbert fractal patch etched on a Kapton HN substrate (εr = 3.4, tan δ = 0.0015, depth = 0.12 mm) with a layer of mushroom-shaped electromagnetic bandgap (EBG) layer that is designed to support a broadband operation in the S-, C-, and X-bands. The simulation findings show that the physical dimensions are reduced by 60%, the impedance bandwidth is improved by 24.6% and the gain is increased by 44% compared to traditional microstrip antennas. Surface waves are successfully suppressed by the EBG layer and the fractal geometry guarantees the stability of the impedance matching and multi-resonant behavior. Mechanical bending tests verify that gain loss is under 0.4 dB and retention of more than 95% efficiency thus confirming mechanical resilience. Simulations and measured results show an excellent level of agreement (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.985), indicating high reliability of the model. The suggested antenna provides lightweight, conformal, and high-gain solution to be utilized in CubeSat, radar telemetry, and deep-space communication space.

title: Federated Learning with Containerized Memory Sharing: An Approach to Privacy and Memory Efficiency
authors: Chitranjan Singh, Ankur Agnihotri, Shubham Mehta, Sanjay Wandhekar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is appropriate for edge and distributed environments because it allows decentralized model training while maintaining data privacy. However, memory inefficiency, high communication overhead, and poor scalability across heterogeneous nodes are common problems with traditional FL frameworks. Containerization is repositioned from a deployment tool to a fundamental architectural component of FL in this paper’s introduction of Containerized Shared Memory Architecture (CSMA). Our method turns every Docker container into an active, memory-aware FL agent, in contrast to previous FL systems that treat containers as passive encapsulation tools. By interacting with edge devices, forming a graph-based topology, and exchanging model updates via a centralized shared memory layer, these containers reduce redundant data transfers and enhance synchronization.In addition to presenting a binary nonlinear optimization (BNLP) model to direct container placement and selection under privacy, memory, and performance constraints, we suggest a signal-based aggregation mechanism for effective convergence. This model provides an accurate and scalable substitute for the simplistic linear approximations used in earlier studies. Docker Swarm is used to deploy the system across a high performance computing (HPC) cluster, and a real-world clinical dataset is used for evaluation. The results show improved scalability with increasing container count, 90% model accuracy, and up to 40% memory reduction. Our findings demonstrate that CSMA makes it possible for a modular, repeatable, and privacy-preserving FL architecture that is ideal for contemporary edge-HPC deployments and paves the way for the development of next-generation container-native federated learning systems.

title:
15.4 A 16nm 168Mb Embedded STT-MRAM with 0.0249µm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Bit-Cell, Dual-Port Access, and 51.2Gb/s Read Throughput for Automotive and Edge AI Applications
authors: Po-Hao Lee, Chia-Fu Lee, Hon-Jarn Lin, Cheng-Han Lu, Yen-An Chang, Pranata W. Sanjaya, Chia-Jung Tsen, Kuan-Chun Chen, Ming-Chieh Lin, Chao-Jung Hung, Tan-Li Chou, Chih-Hui Weng, Chia-Yu Wang, J.J. Wu, Harry Chuang, Yih Wang, Yu-Der Chih, Tsung-Yung Jonathan Chang
abstract: A 16nm 168Mb embedded STT-MRAM with a 0.0249μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> bit cell that overcomes density and performance challenges of competing NVM technologies is presented. The design features a modular architecture supporting customizable macros, a dual-port access scheme, and an interleaving read architecture achieving a 51.2Gb/s throughput. Fabricated in a 16nm FinFET process, the chip demonstrates a 20-year data retention at 150°C, 1M-cycle write endurance, and is suitable for automotive and edge AI applications.

title: 32.2 A PVT-Robust Frequency-Scalable Fully Dynamic ΔΣ ADC with Bottom-Plate Level Shift
authors: Tohru Kaneko, Hideaki Ishida, Yuzuru Yamaki, Satoshi Takehara, Un-Ku Moon
abstract: A fully dynamic calibration-free <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \Sigma$</tex> ADC using capacitive-degeneration integrators with bottom-plate level shift (BPLS) is presented. BPLS samples negative charge to reduce threshold-voltage dependence, improve settling, and cancel kT/C noise, enabling 0.5pF sampling capacitance. The ADC achieves 91.7dB peak SNDR and 95.6dB DR with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.1 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> at 10kHz bandwidth (BW). Up to 40kHz BW, the worst-case DR degradation is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 2 \text{dB}$</tex> under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \% \mathrm{V}_{\text{DD}}$</tex> or −40 to 100°C. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FoM}_{\mathrm{S}, \text{DR}}$</tex> is 185.6 dB (10 kHz BW) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>184 \text{dB}$</tex> (1-to-40kHz BW).

title: 4.1 A 0.64mA, -108.2dB THD+N Class-D Amplifier with Neural-Assisted Pre-Reconfiguration for Smart Power Optimization
authors: Kaiwen Zhou, Yuxiang Tang, Zhongqiu Shen, Xuecheng Yang, Tianxiang Qu, Yiming Wang, Shen Ye, Zhiliang Hong, Jiawei Xu
abstract: This work presents a high-precision, power-efficient Class-D audio amplifier (CDA) that addresses switching losses and reconfiguration-induced nonlinearity through neural-network-assisted pre-reconfiguration. Consequently, this CDA achieves the lowest THD+N of −108.2 dB and a very high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FoM}_{\text{THD+N }}$</tex> of 3747, corresponding to a 48% improvement over the state-of-the-art in the accompanying comparison table. This CDA also attains an ultralow quiescent current of 0.64 mA and highest efficiency of 66% at 10 mW.

title: Research on Order Reduction Method of Thermal Simulation Model of Power Converters Based on Feedforward Neural Network
authors: Peng Chen, Peng-Fei Yu, Li-Juan Deng, Qiang-Ming Cai
abstract: With the rapid development of digitalization of electronic devices and digital twin technology, the thermal simulation of electronic devices becomes more and more important. Digital twins can be seen as the synchronous operation and coupling of a large number of basic models. The basic simulation model usually has a high degree of freedom and exhibits complex physical nonlinearities. Simulations using high-fidelity finite element methods require significant computational resources and simulation time. The model order reduction technology can realize the connection of one-dimensional system-level simulation model and three-dimensional professional simulation model to construct a unified digital prototype model in multiple fields, and ensure the real-time operation by simplifying the model, thus solving the problems of long simulation time and large computing power requirements. In this paper, a thermal simulation model reduction method based on feedforward neural network is proposed for the power converter model. The finite element method (FEM) is used to simulate the power converter in batch to obtain the full-order thermal model of the device, and then the required key information is extracted from the thermal model. Finally, the multi-layer feed-forward neural network (FNN) algorithm is used to train the key information data set, and the one-dimensional reduced-order model with high accuracy and fast calculation speed is constructed. The experimental results show that the proposed neural network model significantly accelerates the simulation speed while keeping the error accuracy within 6%, which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed method in the thermal simulation of power converters.

title: A 480×320 CMOS LiDAR Sensor with Tapering 1-Step Histogramming TDCs and Sub-Pixel Echo Resolvers
authors: Wonjong Roh, Canxing Piao, Taehoon Jeon, Junsang Bae, Jung-Hoon Chun, Seong-Jin Kim, Jaehyuk Choi
abstract: A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$480 \times 320$</tex> CMOS LiDAR sensor using tapered 1 -step hTDCs and SPER for memory-efficient operation is presented. The hTDC accumulates histograms with 1ns resolution over the full range and applies 500ps resolution only at the echoes, while SPER maps echoes to individual SPADs, enabling <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times$</tex> image resolution. Implemented with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> SPAD macro-pixels and shared AFEs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 ~\mathrm{T} / \text{SPAD}$</tex>) in a 90 nm BSI CIS process, the sensor achieves 3.8 cm precision at 100 m and 4.1 cm accuracy under 110 klx sunlight at 25fps.

title: Sweep-Free Beamforming via Optimal Angle of Arrival Estimation for 6G Ultra-Dense Networks
authors: Hyung Joon Cho, Byonghyo Shim
abstract: In this paper, we propose a sweep-free beamforming approach for 6G wireless systems by accurately estimating the angle of arrival (AoA) corresponding to the lowest path loss in multipath propagation environments. The proposed method directly estimates optimal azimuth and elevation angles using a lightweight ConvNeXt model trained on angular channel power matrices (ACPMs). The estimated AoA is reused as the angle of departure (AoD) for beamforming, enabling robust downlink transmission without exhaustive beam sweeping. Leveraging the sparse and dominant structure of ACPMs, the method achieves accurate AoA estimation with low model complexity and demonstrates improved beamforming gain compared to the quantized 5G DFT-codebook-based approach, even in non-line-of-sight scenarios. The results show that the proposed method enables high-precision beamforming suitable for 6G systems.

title: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: A K / Ka -Band Transmit/Receive Front-End With Triple-Coupled Transformer Technique in 65-nm Bulk CMOS
authors: Nayu Li, Ziyao Xiong, Botao Yang, Hang Lu, Yiwei Liu, Xiaokang Qi, Gaopeng Chen, Na Yan, Chunyi Song, Zhiwei Xu
abstract: This article presents a K/Ka-band transmit/receive (T/R) front-end for jointed sensing and communication (JSAC) applications. A reconfigurable matching network for both signal reception and transmission is realized using the proposed triple-coupled transformer (TCT) technique, achieving low power loss and a compact footprint. The T/R switch at the center tap of the low-noise amplifier (LNA) coil enhances the T/R isolation by cutting off the coupled leakage power from the power amplifier (PA) coil. The chip was fabricated in 65-nm bulk CMOS and occupies a 1.18-mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> core area. In the RX mode, it achieves a power gain of 24.7–27.6 dB and an NF less than 5 dB at 17.7–29.5 GHz. In the TX mode, it achieves a maximum saturated output power (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_{\text {sat}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 20.5 dBm with a power added efficiency (PAE) of 21.8% and an output-referred 1-dB gain compression point (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {OP}_{\text {1dB}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 20.2 dBm with a PAE of 20.6% at 24 GHz. The measured <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {OP}_{\text {1dB}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is larger than 16.7 dBm over the 20–31-GHz frequency range. The chip demonstrates low noise figure (NF) and high output power among K/Ka-band silicon-based T/R front-ends.

title: Supervised Small-baseline and Large-baseline Homography Learning with Diffusion-based Data Generation
authors: Hai Jiang, Haipeng Li, Songchen Han, Bing Zeng, Shuaicheng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: In this paper, we propose an iterative framework, which consists of two phases: a generation phase and a training phase, to generate realistic training data for supervised small-baseline and large-baseline homography learning and yield a state-of-the-art homography estimation network. In the generation phase, given an unlabeled image pair, we utilize the pre-estimated dominant plane masks and homography of the pair, along with another sampled homography that serves as ground truth to generate a new labeled training pair with realistic motion. In the training phase, the generated data is used to train the supervised homography network, in which the training data is refined via a content refinement diffusion model. Once an iteration is finished, the trained network is used in the next data generation phase to update the pre-estimated homography. Through such an iterative strategy, the quality of the dataset and the performance of the network can be gradually and simultaneously improved. Experimental results show that our method outperforms existing competitors and previous supervised methods can also be improved based on the generated dataset. The code and dataset are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JianghaiSCU/RealSH</uri>.

title: Trustworthy Federated Learning with Authenticated ZKPs in Mobile Edge Intelligence
authors: Zejun Li, Xiaomin Chen, Qiang Sun, Yunlong Lu, Dong Li, Jiayi Zhang, Bo Ai
abstract: Privacy disclosure from model parameters and malicious attacks are critical issues in federated learning (FL). Existing research has yet to effectively address the simultaneous need for efficient communication design, privacy protection, and attack detection, which impedes the widespread adoption of FL in mobile edge networks over 6G wireless communication. In this paper, we propose a trustworthy FL framework that can ensure privacy, robustness, accountability, fairness, and explainability in mobile edge networks. Specifically, we integrate authenticated zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) and Pedersen commitments into the FL process. Despite the lack of direct access between servers and mobile devices, the servers can still identify trustworthy clients for specific tasks. Clients can verify the authenticity of the received global model based on the provided proofs and commitments. Furthermore, we leverage Ethereum to act as the verifier and authenticator of models. This verification and authentication process enables the servers to detect abnormal local models and perform trust-based aggregations. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed trustworthy FL framework significantly improves the global model's in terms of accuracy, convergence rate, and security.

title: Analytical Modeling of Resonance Frequency Shifts Induced by Mechanical Strain in Stretchable Rectangular Patch Antennas_supp1-3667386.pdf
authors: Weibing Lu
abstract: The increasing demand for wearable electronics has emphasized the need for flexible antennas capable of maintaining stable electromagnetic performance under mechanical deformation. This paper presents a universal theoretical framework for predicting resonance frequency shifts in stretchable rectangular patch antennas subjected to uniaxial tensile stress. By extending the cavity model theory to accommodate mechanically deformed configurations, closed-form expressions are derived to explicitly correlate the strain magnitude and Poisson’s ratio with intrinsic resonance frequency deviations. The proposed model reveals, for the first time, the electromechanical coupling mechanism that governs patch antenna performance degradation. A robustness metric (Ξf) is introduced to quantitatively evaluate the resonance frequency stability of the antenna under strain. The model is systematically validated through eigenmode simulations, full-wave electromagnetic analysis, and prototype measurements, demonstrating its broad applicability to various material platforms. This study provides fundamental insights into strain-antenna coupling phenomena, facilitating more efficient design processes by reducing unnecessary computational costs.

title: ReHeartNet: Reconstruct Electrocardiogram From Photoplethysmography by Using Dense Connected Deep Learning Model
authors: Shuenn-Yuh Lee, Kai-Ze Lei, Ju-Yi Chen, Chun-Rong Huang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Goal: To enable comfortable and non-invasive heart rhythm monitoring, this work aims to reconstruct electrocardiogram (ECG) signals from photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals, eliminating the need for multiple electrode attachments, which are often inconvenient and may cause skin irritation. Method: To achieve high-fidelity ECG reconstruction from PPG inputs, we propose ReHeartNet, a novel neural network that formulates the task as a regression problem. To capture the multi-scale temporal and frequency relationships between PPG and ECG signals, the model employs densely connected bidirectional long short-term memory (DC-BiLSTM) blocks. To enhance reconstruction accuracy, hierarchical features from different BiLSTM layers are fused within the network architecture. Results: To validate the proposed method, experiments were conducted on four datasets: MIMIC-III, BIDMC, TBME-RR, and CBIC-Heart. ReHeartNet consistently outperforms baselines based on generative adversarial networks (GAN), recurrent neural networks (RNN), and transformers. Conclusions: To support reliable cardiac monitoring in various populations, ReHeartNet demonstrates strong generalization and robustness in ECG reconstruction for healthy individuals and patients with circulatory diseases and arrhythmias, using only wearable PPG signals.

title: A Predictive Load Balancing Optimization Framework for Open Radio Access Networks via Xn Protocol
authors: Wisam Hasan Ali, Mohd Nazri Mahmud, Raad S. Alhumaima
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Open radio access networks (ORANs) implement a new paradigm based on the Xn protocol (high-level C/U split) that improves interoperability between open distributed units (O-DUs), open central units (O-CUs) and open radio units (O-RUs). In this paper, some of the most critical issues such as heterogeneous user (UE) requirements and preferences, limited resources, dynamic variation in the conditions of the network, and inherent interference in the ORAN are solved by optimizing load balancing and resource allocation. We focus in this paper on using Xn interface for load balancing without relying on external algorithms and techniques for efficient load sharing and coordination among next generation node base stations (gNBs) and to promote multi-vendor intercommunication, not to mention reducing the overhead costs. The predictive load balancing was represented in the form of a weighted metric via several scenarios of multi-objectives optimization problems to maximize network efficiency while improving energy efficiency (EE), minimizing delay, and maximize bandwidth utilization. Numerical simulations based on UEs and gNBs validate the efficiency of these models, demonstrating the enhanced quality of service (QoS). Lagrange multiplier method with standard constrained numerical optimization solvers including sequential quadratic programming (SQP), active set and interior point methods has been used to solve the formulated optimization problems. These solvers are used to validate the effectiveness of the proposed frameworks on scenarios with different allocation approaches (i.e., static, proportional, priority-based, and priority-weighted proportional). The analysis of these allocations highlights the balance between resource fairness and user satisfaction by achieving load distribution among O-DUs and virtual machines (VMs), resulting in reduced load variance and enhanced fairness. Subsequently, applicatio-level QoS measurements, such as throughput, latency, and EE shows that UEs are satisfied with fluctuating networks. Last, the proposed Xn-based models are compared with a classical load balancing model, namely autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) model.

title: Pinching Antenna-Enabled URLLC Downlink
authors: Yanqing Xu, Yang Lu, Donghong Cai, Ruihong Jiang, Fang Fang
abstract: This letter investigates power-efficient downlink transmission for a pinching-antenna system under ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) requirements. Unlike existing works based on Shannon capacity, we explicitly adopt the finite-blocklength coding model and capture the coupling among signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), bandwidth, latency, and reliability. We first derive, for each user, the minimum received SNR that meets a given payload and block-error target, and show that the optimal transmit power then admits a closed-form expression as a function of the pinching-antenna position and per-user bandwidth. This yields an equivalent problem in only these two variable blocks. Leveraging this structure, we develop a low-complexity block coordinate descent algorithm: for fixed bandwidths, the optimal antenna position is a closed-form FBL-weighted centroid of user locations; for a fixed position, the bandwidth allocation subproblem is strictly convex and solved optimally via a dual-based bisection method. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed pinching-antenna design significantly reduces the total transmit power compared with a fixed-antenna baseline, with robust gains across different payloads, reliability targets, and system geometries.

title: Causality-Inspired Cross-Satellite Continual Learning for Tropical Cyclone Intensity Prediction
authors: Luhui Yue, Renlong Hang, Rui Zhang, Jiamu Ding, Qingshan Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep Learning (DL) has been widely used to predict the Tropical Cyclone (TC) intensity from satellite images in recent years. It can achieve satis factory performance when the testing images follow the same distribution as the training images. How ever, due to variations in sensors and observation conditions, the images across different satellites of ten exhibit significantly different distributions, making DL models trained on one satellite difficult to generalize to another one. In this paper, we propose a causality-inspired Cross-Satellite Continual Learning (CSCL) model to address this issue. Specifically, we firstly analyze the cross-satellite generalization challenge. Then, we develop a causality-inspired continual learning architecture, which mainly consists of Princi pal Component Learning Module (PCLM) and Feature Distillation Module (FDM). PCLMaimsatintroducing extra parameters into the model trained on the original satellite for learning new knowledge from the new satellite, while FDM attempts at preserving the old knowledge learned from the original satellite. To verify the effectiveness of CSCL model, we conduct experiments using three widely used geostationary satellites in the Northwest Pacific: Himawari-8 (H8), Fengyun-2 (FY2), and Fengyun-4A (FY4A). Experimental results show that CSCL achieves superior generalization performance across all three satellites, with Root Mean Squared Errors (RMSEs) of 8.45/8.00/7.70 m/s and Mean Absolute Errors (MAEs) of 6.28/6.07/5.92 m/s for H8/FY2/FY4A respectively in 24-hour predictions for 2022, comparable with the official operational forecast methods employed worldwide.

title: Small-Signal Security Region of Floating Offshore Wind Turbine under Wind and Wave Disturbances
authors: Yitian Ren, Zhaojian Wang, Feng Liu
abstract: The wind and wave disturbances of floating offshore wind turbine (FOWT) present significant challenges for small-signal security analysis, due to the nonlinear aero-hydro dynamics and variable working points. This paper proposes a mechanical-electrical coupling FOWT model and analyzes its stability under wind and wave disturbances, deriving the stable operating area. The model incorporates both the mechanical torque applied to the generator and the electrical torque of the generator affects the mechanical dynamics. Compared with a fixed-bottom wind turbine (FWT), new oscillation modes are revealed for the linearized FOWT model to analyze its stability. Based on the FOWT model, the small-signal security region (SSSR) is formulated, offering a geometric representation of the stability boundaries under wind and wave injection space. Due to the coupling characteristics of FOWTs, new dominant oscillation modes leading to Hopf bifurcation on the SSSR boundary are observed, compared with FWT model. Simulations of the proposed model on the single machine infinite system (SMIB) and the four-area system derive the SSSR boundary under wind and wave disturbances, validating the proposed model and SSSR derivation method.

title: An Improved GLMB-SMC Tracking Algorithm for High-Maneuver Targets
authors: Zhenguang Zhao, Yulan Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: To address the challenge of balancing tracking accuracy and computational efficiency in highly maneuvering scenarios for the sequential Monte Carlo implementation of traditional generalized label multi-Bernoulli filters (GLMB-SMC), this paper proposes an improved GLMB-SMC algorithm based on an adaptive grid-based enhanced best model algorithm (AG-BMA) with variable multi-models. Simulation results demonstrate that this algorithm effectively enhances tracking accuracy for multiple targets under high-maneuver conditions while maintaining high computational efficiency.

title: A High-Throughput Multi-Batch GRU Accelerator with Fine-Grained Structured Sparsity on FPGA
authors: Haiqian Yu, Zhuoli Cheng, Jintao Xiang, Yingquan Du, Guosheng Tao, Hongsheng Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper proposes a GRU hardware accelerator tailored for multi-batch parallel computation. The design integrates an efficient dataflow, pipelined input streaming, and high weight reuse, while supporting fine-grained structured sparsity to significantly reduce computational complexity and minimize memory access overhead. The PE array architecture allows continuous, high-throughput multiply-accumulate operations, enabling large-scale batch processing and effective hardware utilization. Implemented on a Xilinx FPGA, the accelerator achieves up to 172.8 GOPS with substantial energy efficiency. The proposed approach demonstrates that structured sparsity and multi-batch parallelism can substantially enhance GRU performance for real-time sequential modeling.

title: SMARTMED: A Blockchain Solution for Privacy-Centric Healthcare and Insurance Integration
authors: E. Sujatha., S. Palpandi, Vinothkumar Kolluru, M. Jayanthi, G. Porkodi
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Blockchain technology offers powerful advantages when it comes to protecting data privacy and ensuring data integrity, making it an ideal solution to many of the security challenges faced by today's centralized systems-such as single points of failure and restricted data sharing. As digital health services continue to evolve, there's a growing need for seamless, secure, and continuous medical support, and overcoming the issue of isolated data has become more important than ever. Blockchain's key features-like its distributed ledger, strong encryption, immutable audit trail, and traceable data history-make it exceptionally wellsuited for solving data sharing challenges across healthcare organizations. In line with these capabilities and the unique demands of the healthcare sector, we propose a blockchainbased, multi-role healthcare data sharing system. This system is designed with security, tamper resistance, and traceability at its core. It also incorporates a smart contract mechanism to streamline and secure the insurance claim process, and includes two tailored data-sharing protocolsone for medical institutions and one for individualsensuring users have a simple, transparent, and efficient way to manage their personal health data while maintaining full control and trust in the system.

title: High-Stability Position Sensor-Less Control Method for Brushless DC Motors at Low Speed
authors: R. Sumathi, S.V. Sweatha, M. Shameem Fathima, K. Nitish Kumar, R.S. Versha
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This venture centers on creating a high-stability, position-sensor-less control system for motors working at moo speeds for Brushless DC (BLDC) motors. Unlike ordinary systems that depend on criticism instruments for exact control, this inventive strategy accomplishes exact speed direction without input, utilizing a Pic microcontroller for streamlined options. A potentiometer is utilized to set the wanted speed as a rate, which the system forms to control the BLDC motor driver. The motor is coupled with a dynamo that changes mechanical vitality into electrical control, which is put away in a battery. This double usefulness improves the system's utility by empowering vitality reception and capacity. The proposed approach offers a cost-effective and proficient arrangement for controlling BLDC motors and creating control in low-speed applications, with potential employment in vitality collecting and small-scale renewable vitality systems.

title: Optimized Resource Allocation for Homeless Shelter Management
authors: A Wasim Raja, Rakisha R, Ranga Nithilesh R, Saran V
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Homelessness represents a critical societal challenge requiring efficient resource management and strategic allocation to maximize impact. Traditional shelter management systems often operate with limited resources, reactive approaches, and inadequate predictive capabilities, resulting in suboptimal utilization of beds, supplies, and services. This paper proposes an Optimized Resource Allocation System (ORAS) powered by machine learning and data analytics that integrates demand forecasting, dynamic bed allocation, inventory management, and service coordination. Unlike existing shelter management approaches, the ORAS emphasizes predictive modeling, real-time optimization, and equitable resource distribution by leveraging historical occupancy patterns, weather data, socioeconomic indicators, and collaborative filtering techniques. The framework ensures efficient operations through automated decision support, demand-supply matching, and multi-objective optimization strategies. The system not only enhances operational efficiency but also improves service quality, reduces resource wastage, and supports evidence-based policy making for homeless services.

title: AI Based Real-Time Dermatological Screening Using Custom Drive Controlled Lens Systems
authors: Porkumaran Karantharaj, Prabakar Srinivasan, Subramaniam Ganesan, Samson Isaac J, Ramani Kannan
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Skin cancer is a major health concern often caused by excessive UV exposure and genetic mutations. Melanoma, the most serious type of skin cancer, arises from abnormal melanin-producing cells and can develop anywhere on the body. It is especially prevalent in Western regions due to environmental factors and is classified into four equally critical categories. Early detection of melanoma is challenging because it often lacks distinctive symptoms. Additionally, normal moles can evolve into melanomic lesions due to various factors. Dermatoscopy, a conventional non-invasive technique, is typically used to diagnose melanoma. Despite potentially being concealed among normal moles, melanoma must be detected early to save lives. Our research introduces a custom drive controlled lens system designed to enhance the visualization of lesion phenotypes, improving the accuracy of subsequent deep learning neural network model based classification of moles on specific features. The smart lens system, integrated with a mobile app, uses a MobileNet convolutional neural network to predict the likelihood and severity of moles as lesions. This offers a cost-effective alternative to traditional dermatoscopes, enabling earlier melanoma detection. The system's adaptability, image quality, and diagnostic accuracy are key performance metrics. Our results indicate that incorporating Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for dataset augmentation could further enhance accuracy.

title: NeuroMesh: Decentralized Edge Mesh for Scalable IoT with Future LoRa and Autonomous Control Integration
authors: Ramesh K, Durgaprasad R, Ashwin Varma T, Harish S, Jayapradha A, Kalaivani K
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem continues to expand rapidly across domains such as smart agriculture, environmental monitoring, and urban infrastructure. Despite its potential, conventional cloud-centric IoT architectures remain constrained by scalability, latency, and resilience limitations, particularly in rural or resource-constrained regions. This paper presents NeuroMesh, a decentralized edge mesh framework built upon ESP32 devices, incorporating optional LoRa overlays and a planned autonomous control layer. NeuroMesh leverages a WiFi mesh backbone for low-latency multi-hop communication and extends coverage with LoRa-based long-range links. Experimental evaluation using a 6-node laboratory deployment demonstrates high packet delivery ratios (PDR), predictable latency growth with hop count, and energy efficiency under duty-cycled operation. Furthermore, a roadmap is provided for integrating Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL), enabling adaptive routing, load balancing, and energy-aware decisions at the edge. NeuroMesh thus offers a resilient and extensible foundation for nextgeneration distributed IoT networks.

title: Application of Portable Chromatograph in On-Site Rapid Analysis of Odorous Gases
authors: Jianhai Sun, Xiangqian Kong, Wenming Wang, Tianye Zhou, Youyin Huang, Jun Wang, Wei Yang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Addressing the limitations of mainstream malodorous gas monitoring methods, this study designed and developed a modular portable chromatograph using purified air as the carrier gas, based on microchromatography technology and a high-sensitivity photoionization detector (Mini-PID). The core structure of the instrument comprises an environmental sample collection module, a purified air carrier gas device, a sample enricher, a 3-meter-long micro chromatographic column, a MiniPID detector, and a chromatographic signal processing & data output module. It enables full-process automation of the "sampling-enrichment-separation-detection-output" workflow. Compared with traditional portable chromatographs, this instrument has achieved significant breakthroughs in volume, power consumption, and analysis speed. Experimental verification demonstrates that it can complete the separation and detection of 10 typical malodorous components within 10 minutes, with a resolution ≥ 1.5 and symmetrical peak shapes. Its detection limit is as low as the 5–10 ppb level, which meets the requirements of trace monitoring; the detection error is ≤1%, and the relative standard deviation (RSD) for repeatability is ≤0.8%, ensuring high quantitative accuracy.In on-site tests conducted in practical scenarios such as garbage landfills and sewage treatment plants, the relative error between this instrument and laboratory-based analysis methods is ≤2%. Furthermore, its analysis time is reduced by more than 70% compared with traditional laboratory methods, enabling effective capture of changes in malodorous gas concentrations. This portable chromatograph can be widely applied in scenarios including environmental law enforcement, enterprise self-inspection, and emergency monitoring. It provides real-time and accurate scientific data support for malodorous pollution source tracing and concentration control, effectively solving the problems of "inaccurate detection" and "slow detection" of traditional methods. It contributes to the governance of environmental malodorous pollution and holds high practical value and broad promotion prospects.
