title: Communication-Efficient Decentralized Contextual X -Armed Bandit Learning in Multi-agent Stochastic Networks
authors: Youqi Li, Fan Li, Pan Zhou, Yu Wang
abstract: Bandit with infinitely many arms (i.e., X-armed bandit) is an important variant of multi-armed stochastic bandits, which is useful to model different networking problems under both wired and wireless settings, e.g., online caching, dynamic channel/power allocation, rate adaption. However, the problem becomes challenging when the characteristic of the networking setting is affected by the side information (i.e., context) and distributed behavior. In this paper, we identify and study a novel problem, decentralized contextual X-armed bandit, where N agents collaboratively solve the problem within time span T. The problem is nontrivial because the infinite arms challenge and statistical information consensus issue make our setting go beyond a simple combination of X-armed bandits and multi-agent bandits. We develop a decentralized arm selection algorithm, called MACXUCB, by elaborating the contextual covering tree technique with a novel gossip communication protocol, which allows each agent to communicate efficiently with her neighbors. We prove that MACXUCB achieves a sublinear regret upper bound Õ(D1/2dX+2dY+4 N dX+dY+1.5/dX+dY+2 T dX+dY+1/dX+dY+2) given aggregation period D, and the covering dimensions dX and dY of arm and context spaces, which asymptomatically matches the lower bound Ω((DN)1/dX+dY+2 T dX+dY+1/dX+dY+2) up to a time-dependent factor. Moreover, MACXUCB enjoys a sublinear communication complexity Õ(NDT0.5/(1–p)) when tuning parameter p ∈ (0, 1/2). Finally, we carry out experiments to verify the performance of our MACXUCB. The results show the effectiveness and efficiency of our MACXUCB.

title: Large Language Model-Driven Framework for Dynamic Stakeholder-Associated issues Analysis in Mega Infrastructure Projects
authors: Ruiyang Ma, Geoffrey Qiping Shen, Jin Xue
abstract: The complexity and dynamic nature of megaprojects necessitate effective stakeholder engagement strategies to ensure the successful achievement of project objectives. However, the lack of an effective framework to uncover interactions and trends between stakeholders and associated issues within the vast, encapsulated project data is a key factor hindering dynamic stakeholder engagement. Traditional methods are constrained by time-consuming, limited semantic interpretability, accuracy issues, and weak reasoning capabilities, making them insufficient for addressing dynamic stakeholder engagement challenges. To address this gap, this research introduced an integrated LLM-TOT (Large Language Model-Topic over Time) dynamic analysis method that enables the automated identification of stakeholders and the analysis of their interactions with relevant issues over time, prioritizing stakeholders directly linked to relevant issues at each project phase. Dynamic Stakeholder Engagement Strategies Framework was proposed to provide efficient and adaptive stakeholder engagement strategies for mega infrastructure projects (MIPs). Using a 14-year case study of the Hong Kong International Airport Third Runway System (HKIA 3RS) project, this study validated the proposed framework by analyzing 1,323 official project documents. The analysis identified 16 critical stakeholder-associated issues, 43 key stakeholders, and their relevance, which were systematically scored. Two validation experiments were conducted to rigorously evaluate the accuracy and reliability of the results derived from the LLM-TOT dynamic analysis method. Based on these findings, a stakeholder engagement framework tailored to the HKIA 3RS was developed and verified against real-world practices, offering strategies for phase-specific, issue-level, and dynamic engagement. This study introduced an integrated LLM-TOT dynamic analysis method as an innovative application that connects a standard TOT model with a commercially available LLM to analyze stakeholder-issue dynamics in MIPs. In addition, it presented a Dynamic Stakeholder Engagement Framework that equips decision-makers with targeted, systematic, and actionable strategies to address the “how-to” challenges of dynamic stakeholder engagement throughout the lifecycle of MIPs.

title:
Effects of SF
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    Plasma Treatment Time on the Electrical Performance and Bias Stability of Dual-Gate ITZO Thin-Film Transistors
authors: Tan Zhang, Yuxiong Li, Zhongming Zeng, Shaocong Lv, Qian Xin, Yuxiang Li, Jidong Jin, Aimin Song, Yu Lin, Xiaochen Ma, Jiawei Zhang
abstract: Hydrogen impurities inevitably incorporated during fabrication deteriorate the performance and bias stability of indium-tin-zinc-oxide (ITZO) thin-film transistors (TFTs). This study addresses this challenge by employing SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> plasma treatment to passivate these defects in dual-gate (DG) ITZO TFTs. We systematically investigate the effect of treatment time on electrical properties and stability. The optimal 30 min treatment successfully suppresses hydrogen-induced degradation. While mobility slightly decreases from 38.56 to 32.87 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s, key stability metrics are dramatically improved: the subthreshold swing (SS) is reduced to 0.153 V/dec, and threshold voltage shifts under positive bias temperature stress (PBTS), negative bias temperature stress (NBTS), and negative bias temperature illumination stress (NBTIS) are minimized to −0.35, −0.06, and −0.59 V, respectively. This work demonstrates that SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> plasma treatment is a highly effective strategy for enhancing the reliability of metal oxide TFTs for next-generation displays.

title: A 1400THz/s Ultra-Fast-Scan 14GHz EPR-on-a-Chip Based on Injection-Locked Phase Detection Featuring 120μM Concentration Sensitivity
authors: Jean-Baptiste David, Alexandre Siligaris, Cédric Dehos, José Luis Gonzalez Jimenez, Jean-François Jacquot, Christian Lombard, Kevin Chighine, Vincent Maurel, Serge Gambarelli
abstract: A battery-operated low power (96mW), high sensitivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(120 \mu \mathrm{M}$</tex>), wide span (100Gauss) Ultra-fast scan (1400THz/s) EPR-on-a-Chip based on injection-locked phase detection, implemented in CMOS 22nm-FDSOI technology is presented. 14GHz coherent frequency generation and sensing signal down-conversion is used to implement a stable and low noise EPR readout at around 500MHz without requiring lock-in based demodulation. A single inductor is used as paramagnetic sensor.

title: A 5G-based Resource Allocation Mechanism between Multi-Access Edge Computing Servers in the Internet of Vehicles
authors: Liqing Gao, Rui Zhou, Jiabao Wen, Wenjie Pang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: 5G is driving the construction of smart cities based on the IoT. MEC based on 5G technology supports the flexible use of computing and storage resources at the mobile edge, aiming to reduce the delay of D2D mobile services and explore the inherent capabilities of wireless networks. However, with the continuous increase of user data, the access of burst information in the short term causes computing resource waste and calculation time extension of MEC server nodes. In order to improve the efficiency of MEC nodes and process multi-user information, this paper takes the Internet of Vehicles composed of road MEC nodes as the analysis object. We propose a resource allocation mechanism between MEC servers in the IoV (MEC-RAM-IoV), which uses decision trees to divide the attributes of receiving tasks. Then, RSUs allocate computing resources based tasks attributes and MEC working status, and finally build an empirical model based on reinforcement learning and deep learning to solve the problems of edge nodes resource waste and tasks processing time delay. In addition, we discussed some future challenges regarding MEC deployment on IoT based on 5G.

title: Research on Dynamic Task Allocation Algorithm Based on Multi-Agent Mechanism Design
authors: Xiaohui Zhang, Linlin Zhang, Lihai Wang, Jie Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Multi-agent task allocation in dynamic environments is a core challenge in the field of distributed artificial intelligence. Traditional optimization algorithms often assume a static environment or altruistic agents, making it difficult to guarantee overall system efficiency in real-world scenarios where tasks arrive dynamically and agents are self-interested. To address this problem, this paper proposes a dynamic task allocation framework based on mechanism design. This framework models the task allocation process as a repeated game. By designing a joint mechanism that includes task bidding, profit distribution, and a reputation penalty system, it guides self-interested agents to spontaneously achieve an approximate optimization of the system's social welfare while pursuing individual profits. In our method design, we incorporate the concept of the Vickrey-Clarke-Groves (VCG) auction for instant task allocation and integrate a reputation system based on historical performance to suppress speculative behavior by agents, ensuring long-term incentive compatibility. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to traditional greedy algorithms and random allocation algorithms, the proposed algorithm significantly improves total system utility, task completion rate, and fairness metrics, with advantages being more pronounced especially in highly dynamic and high-load environments.

title: IoT-Enabled Affective Computing for Teenager Mental Health Monitoring
authors: Xiujie Zhao, Yaqin Zhang, Haixiao Zhang, Yubo Wang, Changyun Ge
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Teenager mental health is a critical component of smart city public services. We present an IoT-enabled affective computing framework that integrates edge sensing (wearables, smartphones, campus gateways) with deep learning models (TextCNN, LSTM, ResNet, DenseNet) for city-scale, privacy-preserving monitoring and early warning. The system adopts an edge cloud architecture with MQTT/CoAP messaging, on-device inference, and federated learning to protect sensitive data while enabling continuous multimodal emotion assessment (valence, arousal, discrete categories). On a smart-campus testbed, the framework achieves competitive emotion classification performance (multi-label accuracy 72.4%, risk F1 90.1%) while meeting urban deployment requirements (median end-to-end latency 85 ms, ¿99.2% message reliability, and 4.6 W mean wearable power). The proposed approach aligns affective analytics with smart city infrastructure, supporting proactive intervention, transparent feedback, and scalable integration into municipal health services.

title: Adaptive PID Coupling Tracking Control for Double-Pendulum Crane Based on Digital Twin Technology
authors: Tengfei Zhang, Yana Yang, Heng Zhang, Yan Zhang, Weili Ding, Junpeng Li
abstract: This article presents an advanced digital twin system constructed for a double-pendulum crane (DPC) with complex dynamic characteristics, aiming to address the pressing need for intelligent control of DPC. The high complexity and underactuated nature of the DPC system make virtual-physical mapping, tracking control, and antiswing control highly challenging. To tackle these issues, this study designs a novel adaptive proportional-integral-derivative (PID) coupling tracking control method with an automatic gain adjustment mechanism, achieving real-time and accurate motion mapping from the physical to the virtual DPC and effectively suppressing the double-pendulum swing, which is a key goal. Particularly, to effectively compensate for different lumped disturbances in both the physical and virtual DPC and improve tracking accuracy, a time-delay control strategy is integrated into the underactuated DPC system, and an adaptive method is employed to estimate the influence of state-dependent time-delay errors online, thereby relaxing conventional boundedness assumptions. Finally, synchronous motion experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed virtual-physical tracking control method.

title: Graph-Informed and FiLM-Enhanced Multimodal Fusion for Myocardial Infarction Prediction
authors: Xiantong Xiang, Longxiao Gao, Yuansheng Liu, Ningji Gong, Yongshun Gong
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate and timely diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases, particularly myocardial infarction (MI), remains a critical clinical challenge. Existing electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis methods often rely solely on a single data modality, such as raw signals or waveform images, which limits their ability to capture the broader physiological context. To address this limitation, we propose GFM-MIP, a Graph-informed and FiLM-enhanced Multimodal Fusion framework for myocardial infarction prediction. GFM-MIP integrates 12-lead ECG time-series signals, ECG images, and laboratory test results through a unified architecture. Specifically, it employs a Graphormer encoder to model inter-lead dependencies in ECG signals and a Vision Transformer to extract morphological patterns from ECG images, both modulated by patient-specific laboratory features using Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM). A Transformer-based fusion module captures cross-modal interactions, while a contrastive learning objective encourages alignment between signal and image modalities. Experimental results on a real-world clinical dataset and three public benchmarks demonstrate that GFM-MIP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines across multiple evaluation metrics. Ablation studies further validate the contribution of each modality and architectural component. The proposed framework offers a clinically meaningful and scalable solution for robust, multimodal cardiovascular diagnosis.

title: Vertex-Guided Redundant Constraints Identification for Unit Commitment
authors: Xuan He, Yuxin Pan, Yize Chen, Danny H.K. Tsang
abstract: Power systems Unit Commitment (UC) problem determines the generator commitment schedule and dispatch decisions to realize the reliable and economic operation of power networks. The growing penetration of stochastic renewables and demand behaviors makes it necessary to solve the UC problem timely. It is possible to derive lightweight, faster-to-solve UC models via constraint screening to eliminate redundant constraints. However, the screening process remains computationally cumbersome due to the need of solving numerous linear programming (LP) problems. To reduce the number of LPs to solve, we introduce a novel perspective on such classic LP-based screening. Our key insights lie in the principle that redundant constraints will be satisfied by all vertices of the screened feasible region. Using the UC decision variables' bounds tightened by solving much fewer LPs, we build an outer approximation for the UC feasible region as the screened region. A matrix operation is then designed and applied to the outer approximation's vertices to identify all redundant constraints on-the-fly. Adjustments for the outer approximation are further explored to improve screening efficiency by considering the load operating range and cutting planes derived from UC cost and discrete unit status prediction. Extensive simulations are performed on a set of testbeds up to 2,383 buses to substantiate the effectiveness of the proposed schemes. Compared to classic LP-based screening, our schemes can achieve up to 8.8x acceleration while finding the same redundant constraints.

title: MFT-NNLS: A Toolbox For Passive Macromodeling From Large Immittance and Scattering Data Sets Using Vector Fitting And Residue Perturbation in Matlab
authors: Bjørn Gustavsen
abstract: This paper describes a Matlab toolbox (MFT-NNLS) for passive macromodeling of large-scale problems defined by admittance, impedance or scattering data in the frequency domain. Vector Fitting (VF) is used for calculating an initial rational model having a common pole set. Passivity can afterwards be enforced by residue perturbation of the associated pole-residue model. Highly efficient implementations are utilized, including the so-called fast implementation of VF, and passivity enforcement with problem compacting via the QR-NNLS method with usage of a fast LU-based non-negative least squares solver. The final result is a rational model satisfying the physical conditions of realness, stability, passivity and reciprocity. The rational model is returned on alternative forms, including a pole-residue model and a state-space model with real-valued or complex-valued parameters. Optional parameters are available for tuning the behavior of the software to the given application, e.g., least squares weighting schemes and alternative methods for passivity assessment. Model export to leading electromagnetic transient programs is included. Several applications are shown, including large-scale problems from high-voltage power systems (high orders, many terminals) and high-speed electronics (many ports). The software consists of a collection of Matlab functions.

title: Intrusion Detection in Industrial Internet of Things Based on Granular-Ball Intuitionistic Fuzzy Sets
authors: Dajiang Chen, Jialiang Xie, Honghui Wang
abstract: With the deep integration of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) into critical domains such as intelligent manufacturing and energy management, its cybersecurity risks are increasing. Traditional intrusion detection algorithms struggle to effectively handle the heterogeneity, sparsity, and complexity of intrusion patterns in IIoT systems, leading to performance limitations and the need for improved detection capabilities through new algorithms. Therefore, this paper proposes an intrusion detection framework based on Granular-Ball Intuitionistic Fuzzy Sets (GBIFS). The proposed framework introduces a novel class-wise granular-ball generation method integrated into intuitionistic fuzzy sets, improving intrusion pattern analysis by combining the adaptive multi-granularity representation of granular-ball theory with the capability of intuitionistic fuzzy sets to handle uncertainty. In this framework, the proposed generation method is used to construct Granular-Ball Intuitionistic Fuzzy Patterns (GBIFP) that conform to the feature distribution of IIoT data, and then an improved intuitionistic fuzzy distance metric is introduced to achieve precise classification between normal traffic and attack behavior. Extensive experiments on IIoT intrusion detection datasets (e.g., X-IIoTID, TON-IOT, WUSTL-IIOT) and classical network intrusion detection datasets (e.g., KDDCUP99, NSL-KDD, UNSW-NB15) demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework under heterogeneous and sparse data conditions. The GBIFS framework proposed in this paper significantly enhances the accuracy and efficiency of intrusion detection, providing a scalable and robust solution for IIoT cybersecurity. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/QzEylsia7/Intrusion-Detection-using-Granular-Ball-Intuitionistic-Fuzzy-Sets</uri>

title: Design of Self-Checking Discrete Devices Based on Boolean Signals Correction and Composition of Constant-Weight Codes “1-out-of-4” and “3-out-of-4”
authors: Dmitry V. Efanov, Yeseniya I. Yelina, Sunnatillo T. Boltayev, Nazirjan M. Aripov, Asadulla R. Azizov, Elnara K. Ametova, Sokhibjamol I. Valiev
abstract: In the design of self-checking discrete devices, the main tasks are to achieve the smallest structural redundancy of the concurrent error-detection circuits and to ensure the checkability of all their components. Practice shows that it is not always possible to achieve this using traditional methods of designing concurrent error-detection circuits, based on duplication and control of calculations using code methods. The authors of the paper propose a method for modifying constant-weight codes, which allows achieving special properties of the designed structures of self-checking devices. In the course of our research, we used methods of discrete mathematics and technical diagnostics for the development and justification of new approaches to designing self-checking discrete devices. We have proposed methods for designing concurrent error-detection circuits based on Boolean signals correction and the composition of constant-weight codes “1-out-of-4” and “3-out-of-4”. These methods allow designing a large number of concurrent error-detection circuits, among which the most effective ones in terms of structural redundancy can be selected. In addition, by minimizing the number of signal correction elements in the concurrent error-detection circuit, the solution to the problem of ensuring complete self-checking of the concurrent error-detection circuit structures is simplified. Experimental results confirm the theoretical prerequisites for improving the methods of organizing concurrent error-detection circuits based on the methods proposed by the authors of the paper. Our proposed solutions can be effectively used in practice in the design of self-checking discrete devices based on any element base.

title: Data-Driven Spatio-Temporal Filters for Crosstalk Reduction in High-Density Surface EMG
authors: Luca Mesin
abstract: Objective: Spatial filters have been extensively studied to improve selectivity of information extraction from high density surface electromyography (HD-EMG). However, variations in volume conductors and experimental conditions require tailored solutions, that predetermined filters with fixed weights cannot guarantee to be optimal. This work proposes the development of data-driven filters, adapted to the specific subject and experimental conditions, with a focus on crosstalk. Methods: A general framework to develop filters is introduced. The data are partitioned into patches, which the filter linearly combines across both electrode channels and time samples (obtaining a spatio-temporal filter). With the purpose to reduce crosstalk, different objective functions are proposed: maximal local correlation with the input and minimal cross-correlation across the output channels; minimum entropy of the filter output. Both optimization strategies promote sparse and spatially localized outputs, thereby reducing the effect of distant sources generating crosstalk. Results: Simulated monopolar HD-EMGs from two close muscles have been used to test the filters on a benchmark (n =60: 10 simulated subjects, 3 fat layer thicknesses, 2 levels of additive noise). Moreover, experimental data from selective contractions of the flexor carpi radialis and the pronator teres were used as a preliminary validation in the field (8 subjects). All proposed filters reduced the spread of the recorded signal, better focusing on the sources below the detection point. The estimation of the envelopes of the target muscles (i.e., the muscles located under the detection points of the filters) and the signal-to-crosstalk ratio were statistically improved in simulations when using the filters instead of the raw data. These positive outcomes were confirmed by trends observed in the experimental tests; however, the limited size of the dataset resulted in only a small number of statistically significant improvements. Conclusion: Defining a filter by optimizing an objective function allows to target a property of interest while adapting to the specific recording condition. This adaptive, data-driven approach offers a versatile tool for information extraction from HD-EMGs and may open new avenues for the investigation of neuromuscular signals.

title: ComGRL: Comprehensive Graph Representation Learning From Local to Global Bridged by Mixup
authors: Jinlu Wang, Yanfeng Sun, Jiapu Wang, Junbin Gao, Shaofan Wang, Baocai Yin, Jipeng Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have demonstrated remarkable effectiveness in various graph representation learning tasks. However, most of existing methods achieve the information extraction by the simple fusion of local and global information, which is a coarse-grained and static way. This may hinder the establishment of a dynamic and collaborative interaction between global and local information, which is crucial for comprehensively understanding graph data. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework called comprehensive graph representation learning (ComGRL). ComGRL integrates local information into global information to derive powerful representations. It achieves this by implicitly smoothing local information through flexible graph contrastive learning, ensuring reliable representations for subsequent global exploration. Then ComGRL transfers the locally derived representations to a multihead self-attention module, enhancing their discriminative ability by uncovering diverse and rich global correlations. To achieve dynamic transformation between global and local information under self-supervision with pseudo-labels, ComGRL employs a triple sampling strategy to construct mixed node pairs and applies reliable Mixup augmentation across attributes and structure for the main frameworks. This approach broadens the receptive field and facilitates coordination between local and global representation learning, enabling them to reinforce each other. Experimental results across six widely used graph datasets demonstrate that ComGRL achieves excellent performance in node classification tasks. The code could be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JinluWang1002/ComGRL</uri>.

title: Linear Programming–Based BESS Dispatch for PV Hosting Capacity Enhancement Under No-Export Constraints in the West Bank
authors: Tala N. A. Hattab, José Manuel González, José A. Aguado
abstract: High distributed photovoltaic (PV) penetration can induce reverse power flow, steep ramps, and transformer stress in weak distribution feeders. Motivated by the import-only configuration of the West Bank (Palestine), this paper develops a feeder-aware Linear-Programming (LP) dispatcher for Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) that enforces no-export, transformer-capacity, and ramp-rate limits under the actual JDECo tariff. Using hourly measured load, PV, and tariff data, four representative months in 2024 (January, April, July, October) are simulated, sweeping BESS sizes from 2–10 MW and 4.5–30 MWh. The optimized dispatch achieves near-zero reverse power flow and near-zero ramp-rate violations under the studied deterministic scenarios, while reducing peak demand by up to 5.6 MW. Monthly savings reach 131,526–324,184 NIS (5–12%), dominated by peak-reduction and adequacy value rather than energy-only arbitrage. The results highlight the central role of feeder-aware storage operation in weak grids and indicate that additional economic benefit would require moderate tariff reform or multi-service value stacking.

title: Characterization of Solid-State Switch Technologies for Transformer-Rectifier Flux Pumps Supplying High-Current, High-Inductance DC Magnets
authors: Hamza Benrabah, Giacomo Russo, Luca Murari, Emiliano Guerra, Mattia Simonazzi, Sabino Pipolo, Alessandro Lampasi, Antonio Morandi
abstract: Traditional power supplies for superconducting magnets suffer from significant losses mainly due to thermal dissipation through current leads. Superconducting flux pumps offer a contactless, low-voltage, high-current alternative, effectively reducing dissipation in the power supply system. They are broadly classified into traveling-wave and transformer-rectifier topologies. The latter involves a contactless AC source and a cascaded rectifier unit, usually consisting of superconducting switches driven by temperature or magnetic field, or alternative, of solid-state switches. Solid state switches provide fast switching times with nearly zero losses in the open state but produce non-zero loss during on-state, which affects overall loss performance of the flux pump during quasi-persistent mode operation. This study focuses on transformer-rectifier based on solid-state switches, emphasizing the impact of switch selection on efficiency and performance. To this aim, a characterization of the solid-state switches, both in terms of on-state resistance and switching losses, was conducted where different technologies including Si-MOSFET and GaN devices are considered. The presented characterization provides quantitative guidance for selecting solid-state switches in transformer-rectifier flux pumps supplying high-current, high-inductance superconducting magnets. These results will be used to optimize switch selection for a full-scale transformer–rectifier flux pump designed to supply a toroidal field magnet in a fusion machine, rated at 42.5 kA and 2.2 H.

title: Selective Harmonic Mitigation Model Predictive Control for Grid-Connected Converters Working with a Very Low Apparent Switching Frequency
authors: Victor Cabezas, Pablo Acuna, Ricardo P. Aguilera, Pablo Lezana, Cristian Garcia, Edson H. Watanabe
abstract: This article proposes a Selective Harmonic Mitigation Model Predictive Control (SHM-MPC) strategy for grid-connected converters working with a very low apparent switching frequency. A three-level SHM pattern in accordance with grid-code standards, a variable sampling-period hardware implementation, and an improved problem formulation are realized, which respectively comply with the IEEE 519-2022 standard limits, adapt to grid-frequency variations, and minimize real-time processing requirements. The proposed solution is experimentally validated on a scaled-down grid-connected three-level Cascaded H-Bridge (3L-CHB) converter working at 350 Hz and compared against a standard problem formulation. The results indicate that the proposed solution is feasible, the algorithm can be solved in real time, and it can be implemented on a Digital Signal Processor (DSP), while emphasizing the impact of grid frequency variability. These results are further illustrated in a supplementary video containing all the experimental tests, demonstrating that operating with a very low apparent switching frequency, combined with a distorted and variable grid, imposes hardware design restrictions that must be considered in the problem formulation to enable implementation without degrading either the current harmonic spectrum or the THD.

title: A 48-to-1 V, 5-A Low-Frequency Step-Down Converter with Bidirectional Recycle Converter for Ripple Cancellation and Transient Acceleration
authors: Ching-Tao Hsu, Yu-Tse Shih, Meng-Zhen Liu, Wei-Yao Wan, Ming-Che Tu, Ke-Horng Chen, Xi Zhu, Ying-Hsi Lin, Shian-Ru Lin, Tsung-Yen Tsai
abstract: This work proposes a low-frequency double step-down (DSD) converter integrated with a GaN-based high-frequency recycle converter. The proposed architecture ensures a sufficiently long on-time of 400 ns while achieving effective output ripple suppression, reducing ripple to 6 mV—an 11× improvement over conventional designs. Leveraging high switching frequency and enhanced inductor slew capability, the converter delivers fast transient response. For a 1-A to 5-A load step, the output exhibits a 69-mV undershoot with a 907-ns recovery time; conversely, a 5-A to 1-A step results in a 74-mV overshoot and an 848-ns recovery time, corresponding to 8.2 mV/A performance. The proposed system achieves a peak efficiency of 96.51%.

title: High-Voltage Multilevel Step-Up DC/AC Converter Using a Cross-Connected Switched Capacitor with Self-Voltage Balancing Property
authors: Abdul Hadi, Hamid Naseem, Jul-Ki Seok
abstract: This work introduces a hybrid multilevel DC/AC converter for grid-connected and standalone renewable energy systems. The main features of the proposed multilevel topology include reduced voltage stresses across power devices, high voltage gain that enhances energy conversion efficiency, and inherent self-voltage balancing of the capacitors that eliminates the need for auxiliary control circuits. A comprehensive theoretical analysis was conducted for the cross-connected seven-level active neutral-point-clamped converter (CC-7L-ANPCC), which integrates a hybrid architecture comprising a cross-connected switched-capacitor module and a five-level active neutral-point-clamped converter. The proposed topology achieves a seven-level output voltage with a gain of 1.5 fold the DC input voltage, eliminating the need for an additional DC/DC boost stage. The performance of this topology was validated using MATLAB and was further assessed through a comparative analysis against recent multilevel converter topologies. An experimental prototype of the CC-7L-ANPCC was developed and tested to confirm the theoretical findings. The tests validated the system under dynamic operating conditions to ensure practical reliability. This comprehensive evaluation demonstrates outstanding performance in achieving efficient multilevel voltage conversion.

title: Design and Analysis of Wideband LNA in 30-80 GHz Frequency Range Using SiGe Technology
authors: Khadim Aadil Hussain Mir, Inayat Hussain Wani, Rahul Rathore, Sheikh Aamir Ahsan, Soumya Ranjan Panda, Alok Kumar Saxena, Chandan Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This study presents the design and analysis of a wideband low-noise amplifier (LNA) that demonstrates improved noise performance, gain flatness, and an extended bandwidth suitable for millimeter-wave applications. This study utilizes high-performance IHP SG13G2 0.13-μm silicon-germanium (SiGe) technology within the Keysight Advanced Design System (ADS) tool for circuit design and simulation analysis. The proposed architecture employs transformer feedback (TRFB) within a multistage topology to enhance bandwidth and noise figure (NF) efficiently, without increasing power consumption or chip area. We employ a synergistic equalization techniques in the proposed circuit to enhance stability and refine the gain response. The proposed design utilizes resonant feedback compensation equalization (RFCE) techniques to mitigate gain roll-off at elevated frequencies. The circuit simulation results indicate a noise figure (NF) ranging from 3.2 to 3.8 dB, alongside a significant enhancement in gain flatness, achieving a peak gain of 19.1 dB across a 3-dB bandwidth spanning from 30 GHz to 80 GHz. The circuit demonstrates unconditional stable in the 3-dB bandwidth.

title: RaspDash: A Cloud-Internet of Things (IoT) for Smart Parking Solution with Predictive Analytics
authors: Andrei Cervoneac, Rhonny Javier Diaz Marques, Meri Kortsa Or Korca, Felix Rokossa, Dipo Dunsin, Sonjoy Ranjon Das, Bilal Hassan
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: This short paper presents the development and deployment of an Internet of Things (IoT)-based smart parking system that integrates edge computing with Microsoft Azure cloud services. Utilizing a Raspberry Pi and ultrasonic sensor for real-time vehicle detection, the system transmits data to Azure IoT Hub, where it is processed via Azure Stream Analytics and stored in Azure Structured Query Language (SQL) Database. A machine learning model predicts peak parking hours, and the results are visualized through a custom web dashboard, RaspDash. The architecture prioritizes costefficiency, scalability, and performance optimization, providing a viable solution for intelligent parking management in urban environments.

title: Anomaly Detection in Internet of Things IAM Logs via Perplexity Scoring
authors: Sam Lelouey, Yacine Ahmed Yahia, Osman Salem, Ahmed Mehaoua
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: Identity and Access Management (IAM) systems generate vast authentication logs in Internet of Things (IoT) environments, where anomaly detection is essential for security. Traditional machine learning approaches often struggle to handle complex contextual dependencies and require extensive feature engineering. In this paper, we propose an approach that reformulates IAM logs as textual descriptions and exploits a refined causal language model, Facebook OPT-125M, to compute perplexity scores as indicators of anomalous behaviour. We evaluate our method on a realistic IoT IAM dataset and demonstrate that it achieves high detection performance while maintaining a reasonable computational cost. We also compare our approach to a larger model, BLOOM-560M, showing that OPT-125M provides a superior balance between accuracy and efficiency. Our results highlight the potential of our method for robust, real-time anomaly detection in IoT IAM environments.

title: Distributed Air Conditioning System Based on Frequency Modulation and Consensus Algorithms
authors: Haoyang Xu, Yin Chen, Linghao Zhu, Zeyuan Xiong
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: To address grid frequency stability issues arising from renewable energy integration, this paper proposes a distributed air conditioning control system based on frequency adjustment and consistency algorithms. By establishing a variable-frequency air conditioning model, precise power regulation is achieved. Simultaneously, the definition of air conditioning state variables effectively prevents the exposure of users' daily preferences regarding room temperature. The control architecture is hierarchical and distributed, coordinating the power dispatch center, gateway control nodes, and air conditioner (AC) control nodes. Local information exchange and global coordinated control are implemented through a multi-network communication mechanism. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy responds rapidly to grid power imbalances, maintains frequency stability, and reduces communication and computational complexity. This work offers a practical solution for demand-side resources to participate in grid ancillary services.

title: Research on Deep Learning Modeling and Diagnosis Technology for Transmission Network Defect Hazards
authors: Shuai Guo, Xiangyu Zhao, Ruiye Zhou, Shang Cao, Huanming Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This study aims to address key issues in the defect diagnosis of transmission lines, including performance degradation under complex environments, insufficient multimodal data fusion, and low detection accuracy of small targets. We propose a novel defect diagnosis model based on a dual-stream Swin Transformer architecture with adaptive feature fusion. The model employs a weight-sharing dual-branch network to process visible and infrared images separately, integrates a coordinate attention mechanism before each Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) layer to enhance small-defect perception, and designs a gated adaptive fusion module that dynamically computes pixel-wise weights for intelligent feature combination. Experimental results show that the model achieves 81.5% mAP, 83.6% precision, and 80.2% recall on a dataset containing 12,845 images, significantly outperforming comparative models. In the challenging test set for harsh environments, the model experienced only a 7.7% performance decline, much lower than the 20.9% decline observed with traditional methods, demonstrating exceptional robustness. This research provides a high-accuracy and robust defect diagnosis solution for intelligent inspection of transmission networks, with significant engineering application value.

title: Chain-of-Thought Construction and Example Optimization in Prompt Learning for Power Customer Service
authors: Huifang Xu, Congcong Shi, Tongyang Liu, Heqi Zheng, Yixin Zhang
abstract: In response to the challenges of limited reasoning depth and unstable information transmission in large-model prompt learning for intelligent power customer service scenarios, a new prompt learning method (SA-CoT-CG) is proposed, based on a semi-automated chain-of-thought (CoT) construction framework and clustering-enhanced example selection strategy. First, a semi-automated chain of thought construction framework is designed, wherein the large model automatically generates pseudo chains of thought with instruction enhancement. A pruning strategy is then employed to effectively filter high-quality examples, forming an accurate CoT-enhanced example pool. Next, a BertTopic-clustering-based automatic example selection strategy is proposed, which divides the example pool into topics and selects diverse and representative high-quality examples within each topic to optimize prompt learning performance. Additionally, a multi-task CoT reasoning dataset, ELECTRIC-CoT, is constructed specifically for complex tasks in the electricity customer service domain. Experimental results show that the proposed method significantly improves the knowledge reasoning capability and reasoning process stability of large models in the power customer service domain, while greatly enhancing the accuracy and interpretability of automated customer service responses.

title: RescueAI: Real-Time Detection of Stranded Victims and Flood Severity Using Deep Learning
authors: Thummala Ravikumar, S V Padmavathi Devi, V.E. Brindha
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative approach to detect humans stranded in floodwaters and assess flood severity using deep learning techniques [1], specifically the YOLOv5 algorithm. By leveraging a dataset of flood images captured by drones, the system identifies stranded individuals and estimates the flood severity based on varying water levels. The paper integrates YOLOv5 with an ARIMA regression model to predict severity levels, using water levels from 1 to 11. A series of modules were designed to upload, preprocess, and process datasets, generate the YOLOv5 model, and provide real-time predictions for human detection from images and videos. This solution aims to enhance flood rescue operations [1] and provide crucial information about flood severity for effective disaster management. The performance of the YOLOv5 model is visualized through training graphs, showcasing its accuracy and efficiency in detecting flood victims and predicting water levels. The system is capable of running real-time analysis, making it a valuable tool for first responders in flood-affected regions.

title: Multimodal Vehicle Trajectory Prediction Based on Time-Frequency Attention and Local Interaction Aggregation Wave Superposition
authors: YuHang Xie
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Accurate multimodal trajectory prediction is crucial for autonomous driving safety. While existing Transformer models can generate high-quality candidate trajectories, they face significant bottlenecks in selecting optimal paths, resulting in substantial gaps between minFDE (1) and minFDE(k). This paper proposes two complementary innovations: (1)WavePooling is a wave-superposition-based local interaction aggregation technique. It parameterizes each agent as an amplitude-phase signal and employs phase-modulated spatio-temporal convolutions to generate coherent perception of social contexts, thereby enhancing modeling capabilities for higher-order long-range interactions that influence pattern ordering. (2) FTAMB(Frequency-Domain-Aware Time-Frequency Attention Block) extracts DCT frequency features, applies learnable band gating, and fuses temporal and frequency attention with adaptive weights. This enhances recognition of periodic and global motion patterns, which is crucial for accurate intent determination. On the nuScenes dataset, this model reduces minADE(10) by 3.8% while decreasing minFDE(1) by 2.2%, significantly improving selection accuracy.

title: PathBridge 2.0: An AI-driven, GraphQL-enhanced plugin-based academic networking ecosystem for student-alumni collaboration
authors: Vishagan V, M. K. Kirubakaran, Santhosh I
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: PathBridge 2.0 is a next-generation academic net-working ecosystem created to overcome existing university portals and third-party networking platforms, which tend to lack Personalization, extensibility, and integrated AI support. Modern Students need dynamic mentoring and real-time collaboration. AI-driven guidance in ideation, project development, and academic skill-building, needs which the fragmented existing systems fail to satisfy. PathBridge 2.0 offers a unique, integrated, Module-based solution developed with hybrid REST–GraphQL. architecture that assuredly allows for efficient data retrieval. improved scalability and smooth communication among its microservices. Its plug-in framework is extensible, where an Institution can add or build custom academic tools easily. An AI microservices layer powered by Gemini provides real-time ideation guidance, automated content generation, and personalized academic support. This platform has a plug-in marketplace; mentorship interfaces that are interactive, asynchronous task execution using Redis queues, and secure, cloud-native data storage using Supabase. Tests conducted on a cohort of students and alumni The students show a significant improvement in ideation efficiency. Engagement, Portfolio Development, and Mentor Participation as compared to traditional academic networking systems. With its It features a scalable design, strong extensibility, and is ready for deployment. targeted at institutions, PathBridge 2.0 lays a robust foundation for to facilitate academic collaboration, productivity, and personalization Learning in modern learning environments. Index Terms-GraphQL, FastAPI, Gemini AI, Supabase, Plugin Architecture, Mentorship Platforms, Educational Technology.

title: Semi-Automated Milk Collection, Testing and Vending System
authors: Surendran T, Cibivarshan M, Chandru N, Abisheck P, Logesh K, Bharanitharan V
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Semi-automated milk collection, testing, and vending system remarkably enhances efficiency, transparency, and accuracy compared to traditional milk collection and vending methods. The system applies the Siemens S7-1200 PLC for controlling diverse operations such as those involving a vending machine. The system starts when raw milk flows into the milk collection tank (Tank 1). The system incorporates three tanks, three solenoid valves, and two float switches. The float switches are used for recognizing a low milk level as well as a high milk level lower than full in the tank. When raw milk reaches a high level, the Siemens S7-1200 PLC will receive a signal sent by the float switch that triggers Solenoid Valve 1. This enables the milk to enter the milk analyzer (Lactoscan) where the quality will be analyzed based on fat content, SNF (Solid-Not-Fat) level, density, protein, as well as water content. If the quality parameters meet the required standards, Solenoid Valve 2 opens, allowing the milk to flow into the main storage tank (Tank 2). However, if the parameters do not meet the required levels, Solenoid Valve 3 opens, directing the milk into the storage tank (Tank 3) designated for rejected quality. The system also incorporates an RFID (Radio-Frequency Identification) module. RFID cards are issued to both farmers and consumers, allowing the system to track the amount of milk produced by each farmer, how much is collected, and to send relevant messages to mobile devices. Additionally, the system supports various online payment methods. The RFID module operates under an HMI (Human-Machine Interface) that enables the startup and running of the vending system. A DC 12 V motor rated as a 0.1 HP pump retrieves milk from the storage tank (Tank 2) and returns it to the vending outlet in a bid towards energy efficiency. In total, this system operating under a PLC program as well as an HMI greatly improves reliability as well as safety for consumers as well as farmers.

title: A Study on Medical Image Super-Resolution with Wavelet Transform and U-Net
authors: Wenqi Tian, Xiaobo Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Insufficient spatial resolution remains a common challenge in medical imaging, often leading to blurred anatomical structures and reduced diagnostic accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a medical image super-resolution method that integrates the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) into a U-Net architecture. DWT is applied in the encoder for multi-frequency decomposition, and the Inverse Wavelet Transform (IWT) is used in the decoder to progressively reconstruct high-frequency information. This design enhances multi-scale feature representation and improves texture and edge recovery. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior PSNR and SSIM performance for ×2 and ×4 upscaling tasks, effectively improving detail clarity and structural fidelity.

title: Large Language Model-Assisted Security Testing for Embedded Systems
authors: Xing Liu, Jian Lin, Weiyu Dong, Zhizhuo Tang, Yonghao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Fuzzing of embedded systems in a system simulation environment is currently a mainstream security testing method. To address the limitations of traditional embedded system fuzzing, such as random byte perturbation existing in traditional embedded system fuzzing technologies, this paper proposes a large language model-assisted fuzzing method. Firstly, static analysis is used to extract the semantic information of data register-related functions and decompiled code, which serve as the input basis for constructing prompts; Secondly, various types of peripheral protocols are analyzed to generate initial seeds that are more in line with the firmware input format; Finally, dynamic detection of coverage is performed, and the large language model is used to assist in generating test cases in a timely manner, helping to increase the number of paths explored by firmware execution. Experiments show that this method improves the code coverage of the tested firmware by 14.2%~52.9% and effectively enhances the generation quality of test cases.

title: DDFE-HCNN-E: A Dual-Domain Feature Fusion Hybrid CNN Ensemble Model for Automated Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Kistam Gopi, Ramanujam E, Jagadeesh Thati, Gottala Surendra Kumar, Naresh Babu Merugu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: The detection of epileptic seizures based on the electroencephalogram (EEG) signal is extremely critical in clinical diagnosis and long-term monitoring of patients. Nonetheless, the interpretation process of EEG recordings is time-consuming, subjective, and much relies on the expertise, which shows that precise and automated methods of the process must be developed. The paper contains a detailed comparative evaluation of epileptic seizure detection with the help of various pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) models, namely, VGG19, ResNet50, DenseNet121, GoogleNet, MobileNetV2, and Efficient-NetB0, on the CHB-MIT EEG dataset. A new Dual-Domain Feature Extraction Hybrid CNN Ensemble (DDFE-HCNN-E) framework is suggested to improve the performance of detection. The approach uses Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) to transform EEG signals to time-frequency representations, which are then useful in learning deep features. The suggested model combines ResNet50 with DenseNet121 by using feature-level fusion in addition to a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) and a Temporal-Aware Inception Module (TAIM) to identify discriminating spatial, spectral, and temporal features of EEG signals. The experimental findings show that the suggested DDFE-HCNN-E model is better than the traditional pre-trained CNN models with an accuracy of 93.4%, sensitivity of 92.8 %, and specificity of 94.1 %. This result demonstrates that hybrid feature fusion and attention-based temporal modeling are effective in the process of reliable and automated epileptic seizure detection.

title: Research on Building Deep Learning-Based Multimodal Sentiment Analysis Models
authors: Hongli Zhang, Waiyie Leong, Yan Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence in affective computing has driven in-depth exploration of multimodal deep feature modeling. To address the limitations of insufficient single-modal information and weak cross-modal correlations, a multimodal sentiment analysis model based on cross-modal attention mechanisms was constructed. By integrating Transformer representation capabilities with residual-based structural optimization on text, audio, and visual encodings, this model achieves efficient alignment and collaborative learning of multi-source emotional features. Experimental results demonstrate that this model achieves an accuracy of 0.912 in the three-classification task, representing an approximately $4.5 \%$ improvement over traditional fusion models. In the fiveclassification task, Macro-F1 increases by approximately $5.3 \%$, while maintaining higher stability in identifying low-frequency emotion categories, showcasing remarkable robustness and generalization capabilities.

title: A Data-Driven Efficient Solution Method for Continuous-Time Unit Commitment Under Renewable Energy Fluctuation
authors: Guorun Li, Zhichao Zhou, Xutao Han, Renjie Luo, Shuo Gao, Zhiyi Li
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Unit commitment (UC) has always been a primary method for power system dispatch. The conventional UC model is usually regarded as an effective way to cope with the predicted discrete load data. However, the large-scale integration of renewable energy poses challenges for its accurate calculation. In particular, conventional continuous-time UC model fails to meet the real-time calculation due to its heavy computational burden. To this end, a data-driven hybrid acceleration method is proposed. We first design a fuzzy adaptive interval aggregation algorithm through data-driven approach, the fuzzy membership function can be derived based on classified data. Then, we build a variable fixing algorithm which extract typical operating scenarios through K-means clustering, and predict the possibility of the startup based on ridge regression. The result of clustering is employed to correct irrational results. Finally, a numerical experiment is conducted on the modified IEEE 6-bus system to test the accuracy and efficiency of the proposed hybrid acceleration method.

title: Many-fold Performance Enhancement of Flexible Capacitive Force Sensor Response using a Thin Graphene Oxide Layer
authors: Shree Harsha Kn, Manivannan M, Soumya Dutta, Bhaswar Chakrabarti
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication and characterization of flexible capacitive force sensors with spin-coated graphene oxide (GO) layers at various rotation per minutes (rpm). GO-modified sensors, based on interdigitated electrode structures on PDMS, are systematically compared to uncoated sensors. Experimental results show that GO coatings significantly improve resolution, linearity, and repeatability. The sensor with a 2k rpm GO coating achieved a fourfold increase in capacitance change ( 4.13 pF vs. 0.99 pF), superior force resolution (0.52 N), and minimal baseline error (0.06 % to 0.12 %) over 100 cycles. Other coating speeds produced higher capacitance but with greater error and drift. These findings demonstrate that GO coatings, particularly at 2k rpm, effectively enhance the performance of flexible capacitive sensors, making them highly suitable for applications in wearable systems, soft robotics, and biomedical devices

title: Experimental and Computational Analysis of the Effect of Winding Turns on Leakage Reactance in Power Transformers
authors: Kamran Dawood
abstract: Power transformers are integral to the efficient distribution and transmission of electrical energy in power systems. The design and performance of transformers are heavily influenced by key electromagnetic parameters, leakage reactance and losses, which in turn affect system stability, voltage regulation, and overall efficiency. A critical yet often overlooked factor in transformer design is the number of turns in the windings, which significantly impacts no-load losses, leakage reactance, and load losses. This study performs an experimental analysis of the relationship between the number of turns in transformer windings and key performance metrics. Using finite element method (FEM) simulations, leakage reactance is calculated, and the effect of the number of turns on transformer losses is investigated under different operational conditions. Through a systematic approach, the number of turns is varied, the corresponding leakage reactance, no-load losses, and load losses are measured, and their interaction with transformer performance is analyzed. The study provides a comprehensive understanding of the role of winding turns in determining transformer efficiency and reliability, offering practical insights for optimizing transformer designs for both traditional and renewable energy applications. By correlating experimental data with FEM simulations, this work advances the understanding of transformer design and highlights strategies to minimize losses and enhance overall operational performance.

title:
FROQ
                    <sup>1</sup>
                    : Observing Face Recognition Models for Efficient Quality Assessment
authors: Žiga Babnik, Deepak Kumar Jain, Peter Peer, Vitomir Štruc
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Face Recognition (FR) plays a crucial role in many critical (high-stakes) applications, where errors in the recognition process can lead to serious consequences. Face Image Quality Assessment (FIQA) techniques enhance FR systems by providing quality estimates of face samples, enabling the systems to discard samples that are unsuitable for reliable recognition or lead to low-confidence recognition decisions. Most state-of-the-art FIQA techniques rely on extensive supervised training to achieve accurate quality estimation. In contrast, unsupervised techniques eliminate the need for additional training but tend to be slower and typically exhibit lower performance. In this paper, we introduce FROQ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> (Face Recognition Observer of Quality), a semi-supervised, training-free approach that leverages specific intermediate representations within a given FR model to estimate face-image quality, and combines the efficiency of supervised FIQA models with the training-free approach of unsupervised methods. A simple calibration step based on pseudo-quality labels allows FROQ to uncover specific representations, useful for quality assessment, in any modern FR model. To generate these pseudo-labels, we propose a novel unsupervised FIQA technique based on sample perturbations. Comprehensive experiments with four state-of-the-art FR models and eight benchmark datasets show that FROQ leads to highly competitive results compared to the state-of-the-art, achieving both strong performance and efficient runtime, without requiring explicit training. The code for FROQ is available from: https://github.com/LSIbabnikz/FROQ

title: Quantum Secure Biometric Authentication in Decentralised Systems
authors: Tooba Qasim, Vasilios A. Siris, Izak Oosthuizen, Muttukrishnan Rajarajan, Sujit Biswas
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Biometric authentication has become integral to digital identity systems, particularly in smart cities where it enables secure access to services across governance, transportation, and public infrastructure. Centralised architectures, though widely used, pose privacy and scalability challenges due to the aggregation of sensitive biometric data. Decentralised identity frameworks offer better data sovereignty and eliminate single points of failure but introduce new security concerns, particularly around mutual trust among distributed devices. In such environments, biometric sensors and verification agents must authenticate one another before sharing sensitive biometric data. Existing authentication schemes rely on classical public key infrastructure, which is increasingly susceptible to quantum attacks. This work addresses this gap by proposing a quantum-secure communication protocol for decentralised biometric systems, built upon an enhanced Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) system. The protocol incorporates quantum-resilient authentication at both the classical and quantum layers of QKD: post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is used to secure the classical channel, while authentication qubits verify the integrity of the quantum channel. Once trust is established, QKD generates symmetric keys for encrypting biometric data in transit. Qiskit-based simulations show a key generation rate of 15 bits/sec and 89% efficiency. This layered, quantum-resilient approach offers scalable, robust authentication for next-generation smart city infrastructures.

title: HybridTrust: Fusing LP-GRU and Transformer Architectures for Enhanced Misinformation Detection in Social Networks
authors: Saket Maheshwari, Rick Chatterjee, Sayantan Sinha
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The swift transmission of misinformation on online social networks presents serious problems for public trust, social stability, and informed decision-making. In response, we introduce Hybrid Trust, a new hybrid architecture combining Labeled Propagation–based Gated Recurrent Units (LP-GRU) and Transformer networks to improve the detection of misinformation. The new model utilizes LP-GRU to capture the temporal dependencies found in user interactions and Transformer layers to model long-range contextual relationships in the textual information. Experiments carried out on multiple multilingual social media datasets show that Hybrid Trust provides 7.8% more accurate detection than state-of-the-art baseline models. Additionally, because of the label propagation mechanism, Hybrid Trust provides improved understanding of the confusion dynamics of information diffusion, while our dual-attention framework captures local and global piece of semantic meaning. Further, we provide a new virality potential metric to quantify and predict early the spread of false information. In all, Hybrid Trust is a good and interpretable solution for detecting misinformation on a range of social networks.

title: Quantum Computing for Autonomous Driving Systems: A Qiskit-Based Framework and Perspective
authors: Syed Sufiyan Ali, K. Tarakeshwar, Adarsh Ganesan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Autonomous driving systems demand real-time processing of high-dimensional sensor data, large-scale decision making, NP-hard routing optimization under strict latency and computational constraints. Classical approaches using convolutional neural networks face scalability issues and performance limitations in dynamic environments. IBM’s Qiskit framework provides a comprehensive solution with a modular architecture, enabling the construction of quantum circuits that encode multi-sensor data into exponentially large Hilbert spaces. The core components like Terra and Aer support circuit design and noise-aware simulation. Additional libraries extend these capabilities to support quantum machine learning and optimization algorithms. This paper presents a unified Qiskit-based framework incorporating quantum neural networks for trajectory prediction, quantum convolutional neural networks for safety classification in poor conditions, and quantum approximate optimization algorithm for efficient vehicle routing. We showcase how quantum superposition explores multiple routing paths in parallel and how quantum circuits represent grid locations to improve path finding. Our survey reveals measurable performance advantages of quantum-enhanced models over classical methods, forming the basis of quantum-accelerated autonomous driving systems.

title: Integrating Eco-Tourism and Phytoremediation: A Sustainable Framework for Waste Management, Ecological Restoration, and Environmental Conservation
authors: Sanjay V, Santosh Birman, Jitendra K. Pandey, Nitin Muttil, Yaswanth MG, PM Dhakate
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Ecological restoration tightly winds urbanization, industrialization, and unsustainable tourism against the urgency of denoting innovative solutions for the same and converging its only imaginary and possible path along sustainable development. This study thus wants to put forward a new framework for synergizing eco-tourism and phytoremediation for waste management, ecological restorative performance, and conservation. The proposed analysis empirically assesses the effectiveness of plant-based phytoremediation in contaminated sites through machine learning techniques in aiding complementing eco-tourism in the establishment of an ecologically functional yet economically viable green landscape. Real-world datasets were analyzed against soil heavy metal concentrations, water quality indices, and tourism activity metrics through supervised and unsupervised machine learning models such as Random Forest and Support Vector Machines and K-means clustering. The findings conclude that ML models are capable of predicting and optimizing the efficiency of phytoremediation, as well as their potential to accomplish integration with eco-tourism and environmental conservation, driven by data strategy. This contributes to a sustainable framework from the policymakers' view, conservationist, and development of tourism.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: AI-Driven Multi-Scale Vision Transfer Learning Approach for Enhanced Mammogram-Based Breast Cancer Classification through Adaptive Feature Representation
authors: Jatin Choudhary, Kushalpreet Kaur, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Cancer is the globe's 2nd greatest cause of death, and it is classified as a fatal condition. Breast cancer (BC) is responsible for twenty percent of all cancer instances globally, making it a leading driver of illness and death. Mammography is a significant diagnostic method for early identification and therapy of BC. Nevertheless, even professional radiologists find it difficult to identify and diagnose breast abnormalities. The proposed study describes a new strategy that utilizes Transfer Learning (TL) to leverage information gained from pre-trained systems and adapt it to the complex environment of BC mammography. When contrasted to standard approaches, the suggested TL-based coupled framework outperforms them significantly. Using widely used pretrained algorithms like VGG-16, ResNet50, etc., all Convolutional Neural Network architectures optimized for classification activities, this research systematically modifies hyperparameters to enhance system performance. The development of a combined classification algorithm is carefully compared to individual algorithms on memogram data. Interestingly, the combined algorithm has an outstanding training accuracy of over 90%.

title: 8.10 A 180-to-240Gb/s Analog-Intensive PAM-4 Transmitter with 0.70pJ/b Analog Power Efficiency in 65nm CMOS
authors: Ziyi Lin, Haikun Jia, Wei Deng, Shitu He, Chang Liu, Zhihua Wang, Baoyong Chi
abstract: This paper presents a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$180-240 ~\text{Gb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> analog-intensive PAM-4 transmitter in 65 nm CMOS process. A three-stage cascaded 2-to-1 analog MUX (AMUX) is employed to reduce the complexity and therefore the parasitic capacitance of both output and internal high-speed nodes. Reconfigurable 4-tap FFE, current injection, and dual T-coil techniques are proposed to enhance the output swing and the bandwidth. The transmitter achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.70 \text{pJ} / \mathrm{b}$</tex> analog energy efficiency at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$240 ~\text{Gb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> data rate.

title: Pore-DMNet: Joint Pore Descriptor and Metric Learning for High-Resolution Fingerprint Recognition
authors: Shuqi Li, Haixia Wang, Zilan Pan, Yilong Zhang, Haohao Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Recent pore-based fingerprint recognition methods have garnered significant attention due to their uniqueness and resistance to forgery, and have been successfully applied in Automated Fingerprint Identification Systems (AFIS). Existing high-resolution fingerprint recognition methods primarily focus on constructing individual pore descriptors while neglecting the modeling of the similarity between pores. Metric learning can capture the relationships between pores in complex feature spaces and learn their similarities, which could be beneficial for pore matching and directly impact recognition accuracy. In this paper, we propose the joint pore descriptor and metric learning network (Pore-DMNet) for high-resolution fingerprint recognition. In order to train the network, a Descriptor-Guided Metric Optimization (DGMO) strategy is adopted to optimize metric branch learning and uncertainty learning is used to deal with the problem of inaccurate automatic pore annotation. Experimental results on the PolyU HRF dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving Equal Error Rates (EERs) of 1.05% on partial fingerprints (DBI) and 0 on full fingerprints (DBII), surpassing several advanced recognition methods.

title: Modeling of Silicon Oxide Growth in Diverse Thermal Oxidation Conditions
authors: Pragya Kushwaha, Sunil Babu Eadi, Teena Jangid, Nandlal Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work presents a mathematical model that captures the silicon oxidation process at 1050°C under varying conditions. By integrating experimental data with advanced TCAD simulations, the model effectively addresses the complexities associated with dry, wet, and dry–wet–dry oxidation environments, which critically influence the growth characteristics of the silicon dioxide (SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) layer. The proposed approach utilizes first-order rate equations customized for each oxidation regime, enabling accurate prediction of oxide thickness as a function of time. The model’s predictions show strong agreement with experimental results, validating its applicability for real-world semiconductor fabrication processes.

title: Performance Enhancement for FE-on-Top Negative Capacitance CFET via Aspect Ratio Engineering
authors: Sandeep Semwal, Pin Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Using numerical simulations, this work highlights the impact of the aspect ratio (AR) of stacked ferroelectric on achieving optimal capacitance matching in FE-on-Top negative capacitance CFETs (NC-CFETs). The results indicate that reducing AR improves capacitance matching, enabling a low subthreshold swing (~47 mV/dec), ~68% higher ON current, ~30× lower OFF current, and suppressed DIBL. Moreover, the pronounced performance enhancement from AR-enabled NC effects at scaled gate lengths (12 nm) underscores its potential for future low-technology nodes. These findings establish AR engineering in NC-CFETs as a promising pathway toward future energy efficient electronics.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Legal Empowerment: Bridging the Gap in Access to Justice for Transgender Individuals
authors: Jessy K Jayanth, Asha Sundaram, JAnice Vinolia, Shruthi R, Sabitha.S.S, Nivetha P K
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Transgender faces multiple challenges in their day-to-day life globally. Added to those barriers in access to justice based on socioeconomic factors, legal invisibility and practises based on discrimination. The needs of the transgender individuals or not met in the conventional system of judiciary which results in inequality in protection under the judiciary system. Advanced technologies like artificial intelligence can support them with transformation injustice process by bridging the with inclusive justice delivery and legal empowerment. The research study explores the role of artificial intelligence including predictive analytics natural language processing in bridging the gap for access to justice. The legal information platform is driven by artificial intelligence nowadays which simplify the complex legal practises for enhanced case outcomes. The community of transgender are about to get awareness in right time for low-cost legal assistance and effort to process in bureaucratic. The findings highlight challenges like algorithmic bias due to ai enabled legal system and it seeks for human oversight sometimes. Thus, artificial intelligence has been integrated in the study for legal empowerment to achieve inclusivity, equity and dignity in the justice framework.
