title: A Novel Study on the Effect of Temperature Cycle Testing on the Transmission Performance of Sub-2 Micron Fine-Wiring for Glass Substrates
authors: Masaya Tanaka, Satoru Kuramochi
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: We have developed various package substrates primarily composed of glass material, including RDL interposer, glass interposer and glass core substrate. Furthermore, we have presented the results of these developments at several academic conferences. It is important to clarify the transmission performance and its reliability of the fine wiring, which is a common element in these structures. In this paper, we report on the changes observed in the transmission wiring through longterm reliability test. We employed a temperature cycling test for this evaluation. We subjected them to the temperature range of 150 degrees Celsius to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 5 5}$</tex> degrees Celsius in accordance with JEDEC class <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H$</tex> standards. Insertion loss characteristics were measured for each cycle using a network analyzer. We summarize the key features of our fine wiring structure based on the result of this evaluation.

title: 8.8 A 0.292pJ/b 56Gb/s/wire Capacitively Driven Simultaneous Bidirectional Transceiver with PVT/Mismatch Tracking for XSR and D2D Interfaces in 28nm CMOS
authors: Kahyun Kim, Yoona Lee, Daehoon Na, Ha-Jung Park, Jeongeun Song, Woo-Seok Choi
abstract: A low-power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.292 \text{pJ} / \mathrm{b})$</tex>, high-bandwidth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(56 ~\text{Gb} / \mathrm{s} /$</tex> wire) single-ended capacitively driven simultaneous bi-directional (CD-SBD) TRX with PVT tolerance is proposed. Capacitive driving reduces power and self-interference in SBD. AC/DC replica and PVT/mismatch tracking provides PVT robustness without extra hardware. Per-lane CDR and a quadrature generator with QEC/DCC, secure clock margin. The TRX sustains <1e-12 BER across -6.5 to -11.5 dB loss, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.93-1.2 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-80^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> temperature variation.

title: Enhancing Intelligent Transportation Systems: A Comprehensive Review of VLC-Based V2V and V2I Communication
authors: Etienne Alain Feukeu
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication plays a crucial role in shaping the future of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), where real-time data exchange between vehicles is essential for enhanced safety, efficient traffic flow, and autonomous driving. Traditional Radio Frequency (RF)-based communication technologies, such as DSRC and 5G, have made notable advancements but continue to face limitations in congested environments, electromagnetic interference, and limited spectrum availability. Visible Light Communication (VLC), leveraging the already existing LED-based vehicular lighting systems, offers a complementary or alternative communication medium with inherent benefits including high bandwidth, low interference, energy efficiency, and directional transmission. This literature review presents a comprehensive analysis of recent advancements in VLC-based V2V systems. It evaluates the methodologies, implementation complexities, performance outcomes, limitations, and practical applicability of various approaches, while identifying current research gaps and proposing future directions for large-scale adoption.

title: Status Monitoring and Full Lifecycle Assessment of Power Equipment Based on the Integration of Edge Intelligence and Cloud Computing
authors: Gong Lu, Fuliang Ma, Yuzhen Dang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: Modern power grids are expanding and becoming more heterogeneous, making legacy centralized condition monitoring and lifecycle assessment difficult to satisfy real-time and intelligent operation-and-maintenance requirements. This paper proposes ECCIM, a full-lifecycle intelligent assessment model that couples edge intelligence with cloud computing. On the edge, a Multi-source Temporal Feature Extractor (MTFE) and an Adaptive Graph Attention Network (AGAT) perform lightweight, event-driven state recognition and transmit only salient changes. In the cloud, a Cross-Modal Health State Encoder (CMHSE) and a Lifecycle Risk Assessment Module (LRAM) fuse sensor time series, maintenance logs, and physics-informed priors to model long-term degradation with a Transformer-enhanced digital-twin simulator. The physical priors refer to degradation monotonicity and hazard positivity constraints, insulation thermal-aging laws and maintenance-effect priors, and grid-consistent operating constraints that regularize state evolution. On the SoCal 28-Bus dataset, ECCIM improves lifecycle prediction accuracy by 12.4% over mainstream baselines, while reducing end-to-end alarm latency from 1.6s to 0.31s and lowering uplink traffic by 38.7%.

title: A 112Gb/s PAM-4 SBD Transceiver with Mismatch-Compensated 2×VDD Hybrid and Two-Step Echo Canceller in 28nm CMOS
authors: Huanfa Sun, Shangjie Wei, Yu Su, Chenyao Cao, Yujie Zeng, Yukun He, Zhouchi Duan, Guohe Zhang, Xiaoyan Gui
abstract: A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$112 \text{Gb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> PAM-4 simultaneous bidirectional (SBD) transceiver in 28 nm CMOS is presented. It features a hybrid with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> VDD stacked driver to restore signal swing, a joint delay and slew-rate matching scheme to eliminate dynamic glitches, and a two-step echo canceller to mitigate reflections. The transceiver achieves BER <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1 \mathrm{E}$</tex>-10 over a 12.7 dB loss channel (equivalent to 24.4 dB at Nyquist), with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.73 \text{pJ} / \mathrm{b}$</tex> energy efficiency and an FoM of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.14 \text{pJ} / \mathrm{b} / \text{dB}$</tex>.

title: Development and Application of an IoT-based Intelligent Compaction System for High Concrete-faced Rockfill Dam Embankment
authors: Longfei Zhang, Xiaozhongmei Tang, Kui Wen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: High concrete-faced rockfill dams (CFRDs), as prevalent hydraulic structures, exhibit structural safety and long-term performance directly determined by roller compaction quality during placement. Conventional quality control methods rely primarily on process-based compaction parameter monitoring during construction, followed by ex-post test pit verification. These approaches fail to accurately characterize full-section compaction uniformity and fall short of modern digital engineering requirements for granular management. To achieve lifecycle-refined management of dam filling operations and mitigate post-construction settlement, this research develops an intelligent compaction quality control system integrating Building Information Modeling (BIM), BeiDou Navigation Satellite System (BDS) positioning, and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The system enables real-time, full-coverage compaction monitoring and closed-loop quality control for high CFRDs.

title: Research on Trust and Privacy of Industrial Embodied Intelligence Based on Blockchain and ZKP
authors: Jian Wang, Wenjing Gao, Weiwei Ma, Hao Xu, Jiawei Li, Lianxin Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: As the application of Embodied Intelligence deepens within the Industrial Internet of Things (IoT) domain, traditional centralized trust schemes are increasingly unable to meet the demand for establishing efficient trust among heterogeneous devices, due to risks like single points of failure, auditing difficulties, and privacy leakage. To address these issues, this paper proposes a trust and privacy-preserving framework based on blockchain and Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP). The framework establishes a decentralized trust foundation using Hyperledger Fabric. On this foundation, a Decentralized Identity (DID) system is implemented through smart contracts, assigning a unique and verifiable identity anchor to each Embodied Intelligence device. Furthermore, to reconcile auditability and data privacy, the framework integrates ZKP technology. This technology enables edge devices to locally generate and submit on-chain proofs of operational compliance, facilitating transparent auditing without disclosing sensitive data. Finally, to transform trustworthy behavior records into a quantifiable metric, the framework designs a dynamic reputation assessment mechanism. This mechanism uses smart contracts to automatically analyze the verified on-chain behavioral history, continuously updating the reputation score for each Embodied Intelligence device. A smart factory case study demonstrates the framework's practical application, while performance evaluation on a physical testbed confirms its efficiency and scalability for real-time industrial control.

title:
A 0.65-to-1V-V
                    <sub>DD</sub>
                    10.5-to-11.85GHz Fractional-N Sampling PLL Achieving 71.47fs Integrated Jitter and <-60dBc Near-Integer Fractional Spur in 40nm CMOS
authors: Yixi Li, Junjie Chen, Xinyu Shen, Jie Yang, Jian Liu, Nanjian Wu, Zhao Zhang, Liyuan Liu
abstract: This paper presents a 0.65-to-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mathrm{V}, 10.5$</tex>-to-11.85 GHz wide-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{DD}}$</tex>-range (WV) fractional-N sampling PLL. The QE-reduction WV SPD, high-linearity 2-stage DTC, and DCC-aided QE dithering method are proposed to address the issue of poor phase noise at low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{DD}}$</tex>, reliability at regular <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{DD}}$</tex>, nonlinearity, and convergence issues at small fractional FCWs. The 40 nm prototype achieves 71.47fs jitter, −251.8 dB FoM, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-60 \text{dBc}$</tex> near-integer spur.

title:
33.4 A 4.5-to-7.2GHz Beyond Rail-to-Rail Output SCPA with 27.9dBm P
                    <sub>out</sub>
                    and 46.2% DE at 5.1GHz Using Periodic Voltage-Pacing Network
authors: Bingzheng Yang, Jie Zhou, Junfa Mao, Xun Luo
abstract: This work presents a switched-capacitor power amplifier (SCPA) using periodic voltagepacing network, capable of generating output voltage beyond conventional rail-to-rail limit, which enhances both the output power (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</inf>) and efficiency. Fabricated in a 40 nm CMOS process, it shows a competitive 27.92 dBm peak P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</inf> and 46.2% peak drain efficiency covering a wide 1 dB bandwidth from 4.5 to 7.2 GHz. Furthermore, it supports 200 MHz 64QAM and 80 MHz 256 QAM, confirming its suitability for 5 G/6 G and WiFi systems.

title: A Key Feature Screening Method for Human Activity Recognition Based on Multi-head Attention Mechanism
authors: Hao Wang, Fangyu Liu, Xiang Li, Ye Li, Fangmin Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Human activity recognition using wearable sensors has become a critical task in ubiquitous computing, with applications in healthcare, fitness monitoring, and smart environments. However, sensor-based HAR often involves high-dimensional, multi-channel time series data, where redundant or irrelevant features may degrade both classification performance and model interpretability. In this study, we propose a lightweight feature screening framework guided by a multi-head attention mechanism to address this challenge. The model first applies channel-wise linear transformations to extract localized representations from each sensor axis, and then employs a multi-head attention module to dynamically assess the importance of each feature across all channels. This design enables the model to emphasize the most informative components while suppressing noise and redundancy. Experiments on the KU-HAR dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 96.0% classification accuracy using only 60 selected features. In addition, the selected features provide valuable references for future research in feature selection, model simplification and multimodal sensor fusion.

title: Automatic Facial Emotion Recognition using Deep Learning Scheme: An Analysis
authors: U Arul, M Ramamoorthy, A Manimaran, Carmel Belinda Mary M J, Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: The application of Lightweight Deep-Learning Approaches (LDA) is widely adopted in various image examination tasks due to its efficiency on a number of real-time image datasets. Further, the LDA approaches are less complex compared to the conventional deep-learning techniques due to its less number of parameters. This research proposed a LDA based tool to analyze the human-face data to identify the Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) with better detection accuracy. The various phases involved in the chosen technique include; image collection from the data repository and resizing it to 224x224 pixels, feature extraction and classification using the SoftMax (SM) classifier, and performance verification with 3-fold cross validation. In this work, the NASNet models are considered for the investigation and the experimental outcome is verified using the binary classifiers, like SM and Support Vector Machine (SVM). The experimental outcome of this research confirms that the proposed LDA helps to provide a detection accuracy of >96% on the chosen database with NASNetLarge and SM based approach compared to SVM classifier. This outcome verifies the merit of the developed tool for the FER with the chosen database.

title: 16.1 PV Energy-Harvesting Interface Using Reconfigurable Self-Clamp CSCR Converter Achieving 3.83× High-Efficiency VCR Ratio and Open-Voltage-Sense-Free MPPT
authors: Ziyang Zhong, Rui P. Martins, Mo Huang
abstract: This work presents a PV energy harvesting interface using a self-clamp CSCR SC converter. It reduces V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CF</inf> stress without additional SC stages, enabling low-voltage C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">F</inf>S, 17.1mW/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> power density and 90 % peak PCE. A reconfigurable self-clamp scheme extends the high-PCE VCR ratio to 3.83 using only 13 C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">F</inf>s, outperforming prior works. An open-circuit MPPT scheme reuses the converter as a slope sensor, eliminating the open-voltage sensor, while reconfiguration enhances MPPT accuracy under V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IN</inf> variations.

title: Lexical Baselines to Transformers: Hierarchical Ordinal Classification of Indic Movie Full Scripts
authors: Pratik N. Kalamkar, Yogesh Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Film certification in India, conducted by the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC), remains a manual and subjective process. While automated text classification has advanced significantly in English and other resource-rich languages, research on Indic languages—particularly full-length Hindi and Marathi movie scripts—remains sparse. Existing studies are limited to short texts, small datasets, and flat classification methods that neglect the ordinal nature of CBFC categories.This paper presents the multilingual benchmark for CBFC classification using full Hindi and Marathi scripts. To address the challenges of long documents, we propose hierarchical two-level models that capture both chunk-level and document-level structures without truncation. Further, we employ ordinal regression to model the inherent hierarchy among certification labels U ⊂ UA ⊂ A . Comparative evaluation across TF-IDF, hierarchical LSTM, IndicBERT, and Longformer demonstrates that while lexical models perform well in constrained settings, hierarchical and transformer-based methods show clear potential for capturing narrative context.By uniting full-script datasets, hierarchical architectures, and ordinal-aware evaluation, this study advances automated film certification in Indic languages and provides a foundation for scalable censorship automation.

title: A Transfer Learning Approach for Children’s Story Generation with Genre - Driven Classifier Integration
authors: Dr P Velvizhy, Matan P
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: The automatic generation of children’s story requires not only fluency and coherence but also contextual relevance and appropriateness to the genre. This work presents a novel method for story generation that integrates a genre classification framework with a BERT-driven classifier and a Large Language Model evaluated through multiple performance metrics. The genre classifier, created with 500 stories that were manually labeled across five categories - Adventure, Fantasy, Moral, Comic-style, and Educational, achieved a macro F1-Score of 93.7% and an overall accuracy of 93.9%. The story generation model was evaluated alongside existing methods using ROUGE, METEOR, and BERTScore, demonstrating notable advancements in effectiveness. The proposed system achieved a METEOR score of 40.65 and a BERTScore of 87.68, significantly outperforming baseline models such as SEQ2SEQ and Multi-Modal BERT-inspired generators. These results validate the importance of incorporating genre-awareness and semantic-level measures into the story generation process, enhancing narrative quality and genre precision. The proposed framework offers an innovative method for developing intelligent storytelling systems, especially in educational and language-centered applications designed for children

title: Towards Zero-Shot ISO/ICAO Face Compliance Verification via CLIP-IQA and Natural Language Prompting
authors: Nicolò Di Domenico, Guido Borghi, Annalisa Franco, Davide Maltoni
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Ensuring compliance of face images with ISO/ICAO quality standards is essential for boosting the document enrollment process. Indeed, traditional manual checks are slow, subjective, and difficult to scale. Therefore, we propose a system that aims to fully automate compliance verification by directly analyzing the official requirements without relying on predefined hand-crafted features or manual thresholds. Our method combines a Large Language Model, a novel prompt learning procedure, and a contrastive learning framework to evaluate the adherence of a face image to quality requirements. Tested on a recent dataset, our proposed system achieves high accuracy, surpassing existing academic and commercial solutions. By streamlining the implementation and updates to the compliance rules, our approach represents a significant step toward simple, scalable, and regulation-driven image verification. Code and models are publicly available <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: Anomaly Detection in Graphs via Topology-Aware Attention Mechanisms
authors: Narges Alipourjeddi, Ali Miri
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Anomaly detection in graph-structured data is critical for a wide range of security-sensitive applications, including fraud detection, intrusion monitoring, and system fault analysis. While Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), particularly Graph Attention Networks (GATs), have demonstrated strong capabilities in modeling complex relational structures, they remain vulnerable to adversarial perturbations and often fail to capture global structural patterns, such as symmetries and higher-order connectivity. These limitations reduce their effectiveness in identifying subtle or distributed anomalies that arise beyond local neighborhoods. In this work, we propose a novel framework that enhances the robustness, interpretability, and anomaly detection capabilities of GATs by integrating topological features derived from persistent homology into the attention mechanism. By embedding persistent topological descriptors into the computation of attention scores, our model becomes more sensitive to structural irregularities that signal potential threats or inconsistencies particularly those emerging from global graph geometry. We evaluate our approach on both synthetic and realworld datasets, and demonstrate that our topologically-aware attention mechanism consistently outperforms traditional GNN baselines. These results underscore the value of combining topological data analysis with attention-based graph learning for secure, and structure-aware anomaly detection.

title: 18.2 A 22nm 1.87ms/Frame Streaming Multi-Speaker ASR Accelerator Leveraging Contextual-Aware Redundancy Skipping with 2D-Writable Microscaling Compute-in-Memory and Similarity-Aware TCAM Design
authors: Wenjie Ren, Mingxuan Li, Zhenghao Jin, Yifan Ding, Ruohuang Xu, Xiangjun Ye, Yufei Ma, Tianyu Jia, Le Ye
abstract: This paper presents a DCIM-based accelerator for on-device streaming multi-speaker ASR (MS-ASR), featuring: 1) a context-aware redundancy skipping scheme with online sparse block prediction, 2) a 2D-writable CIM with static pages and dynamic pages, 3) a similarity-aware TCAM for rapid search of similar speakers and N-best semantic results. The test chip achieves a system energy efficiency of 37.50TFLOPS/W in MXFP8 and shows superior performance on MS-ASR tasks with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.87-7.51 \text{ms} /$</tex> frame and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.158-1.26 \text{mJ} /$</tex> frame efficiency.

title: A Sub-Threshold All-NMOS Reconfigurable PUF with Secure Configuration Selection for Stable 6b/Cell
authors: Shufan Xu, Kunyang Liu, Lando Chan, Hironori Tagawa, Hirofumi Shinohara, Kiichi Niitsu
abstract: In this work, a reconfigurable PUF featuring secure configuration selection is presented. Without exposing secret PUF data, it achieves 6 stable bits per cell (b/cell) and “zero” bit error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<6.5 \mathrm{E}-8 \text{BER})$</tex> across 0 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and 0.6 to 0.8 V. The area per effective bit is only 253F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Operating in the sub-threshold region, the PUF achieves low energy consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.8 \text{fJ} / \mathrm{b}$</tex>. The all-NMOS bitcell structure mitigates long-term NBTI aging, which is verified through a 45-hour accelerated stress test.

title: Study on Aging Characteristics of High-Frequency Impedance in Lithium Batteries
authors: Qiang Xie, Hao Li, Dawei Xiang
abstract: As an electrochemical energy storage component, the electrochemical impedance of lithium batteries serves as a critical parameter reflecting their actual operating status. The impedance at different frequencies corresponds to various kinetic characteristics of internal electrode processes, forming the electrochemical impedance spectrum (EIS), which provides a basis for state evaluation and diagnosis of batteries. The high-frequency impedance of lithium batteries offers advantages in temperature monitoring, such as not being constrained by the Arrhenius characteristic and exhibiting high temperature sensitivity. This study establishes a dedicated experimental platform and designs an accelerated fatigue test scheme for lithium batteries. After 24 charge-discharge cycles, the high-frequency resistance and reactance of the batteries increase by 5.79% and 8.59%, respectively. Based on these results, this study investigates the aging characteristics of high-frequency impedance in lithium batteries. Furthermore, it analyzes the impact of aging on online temperature monitoring, providing valuable insights for online State of Health (SOH) and temperature monitoring based on high-frequency impedance.

title: A Real-Time Multi-Model System for Personality and Interview Performance Analysis Using Transformer-Based Feature Fusion
authors: Madesh G, Maharaja K, Manickam A, Mohankumar M, B. Lanitha
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The interview process is inherently subjective. Interviewers are prone to making subjective judgments based on their own cognitive biases, and most automated evaluation systems have traditionally relied on unimodal approaches to assessing candidate responses. To overcome these limitations, this paper presents a multimodal system. The proposed system combines the capabilities of ResNet-18 for emotion recognition; ResNet-50 for head pose estimation; and Vision Transformer for gaze classification. The results of these capabilities are as follows: 68.4% accuracy for ResNet-18, 5.4° Mean Absolute Error for ResNet-50, and 88.7% truth for Vision Transformer. This system processes video streams at a rate of 28 frames per second, and achieves an end-to-end latency of 124ms. A new mid-fusion framework was developed to combine the features extracted from each modality into behavioral metrics for engagement, confidence and attentiveness. The correlation coefficient between human expert ratings and the system’s behavioral metrics was 0.71. This work represents a foundation for future development of verbal analytic thinking capabilities and highlights the potential of AI-based interview analytics to provide fair, objective assessment of candidates for recruitment and educational purposes.

title:
Quantifying Ionic Dynamics in MAPbBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    PeLEDs: Modulus Peaks, Split Arrhenius Kinetics, and CBH Transport
authors: Abinash Baruah, Arun Tej Mallajosyula
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: We present a unified dielectric/impedance study of MAPbBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> perovskite LEDs (ITO/PEDOT:PSS/MAPbBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/TPBi/LiF/Al) spanning 4 Hz–1 MHz and 100–380 K to disentangle interfacial polarization from bulk response and quantify ionic kinetics relevant to device operation. Frequency–temperature maps of permittivity separate a large, temperature-sensitive low-frequency component (ε′ rises from ~17 to ~502; ε″ from ~204 to ~1228 at 4 Hz) from a weakly temperature-dependent high-frequency plateau (ε′ ≈ 0.51→0.27; ε″ ≈ 2.3→1.6 near 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> Hz). In the electric-modulus view (4–2000 Hz) a single relaxation band shifts from 51.13 Hz (τ=3.113 ms, 100 K) to 1216.50 Hz (τ=0.131 ms, 380 K); a two-regime Arrhenius analysis reveals a low-T localized mode (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> ≈ 10.0 meV) and a high-T long-range ionic-hopping mode (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> ≈ 350 meV) with a crossover near ~310 K. AC-conductivity follows Jonscher’s law with s(T) decreasing with T; a CBH fit yields W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</inf> ≈ 0.643 eV. Split Arrhenius fits of In σ_ac at fixed frequency further show very small E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> below 300 K and substantially larger, frequency-dependent E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> above 300 K, consistent with thermally activated hopping. The extracted figures-of-merit (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf>/τ, E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf>/f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>, s(T), W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">M</inf>) translate directly into design levers—interface passivation, ion-blocking interlayers, microstructure/compositional tuning, and drive/thermal co-design—to guide iterative improvement of MAPbBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> and related PeLEDs toward high performance.

title: Noncontact Injection-Based High-Frequency Impedance Monitoring of Lithium-Ion Battery
authors: Huaijian Zhao, Dawei Xiang, Hao Li, Zhiwen Sun, Qiang Xie, Ming Gao
abstract: The AC impedance of lithium-ion (Li-ion) battery provides vital insights into their conditions. Traditional methods mainly focus on low-frequency (LF) and middle-frequency (MF) bands, which may have limited sensitivity and dynamic performance. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an online monitoring method of high-frequency (HF) impedance of Li-ion battery via noncontact injection. The proposed method utilizes the electromagnetic coupling effect to inject HF signals into the battery without electrical contact. Subsequently, the HF impedance at the injection frequency is accurately determined by measuring the voltage and current responses. Experimental results demonstrate the method’s feasibility and exceptional measurement accuracy, achieving a maximum error of less than 0.6%. This approach offers a promising solution for safe and reliable impedance monitoring in Li-ion battery systems.

title: 24.1 A 28nm CMOS 1-Chip In-Pixel Memory Backplane Circuit with Pixel-Level Sensing and Compensation for 6652-PPI MicroLED AR Glasses
authors: Yongil Kwon, Sunkwon Kim, Youngkil Choi, Taehyun Kwon, Seongyoung Ryu, Sewhan Na, Kangjoo Kim, Uijong Song, Hyun-Wook Lim, Jongwoo Lee, Jae-Yeol Lee
abstract: This work presents a 28nm CMOS 1-chip in-pixel memory backplane for a 6652-PPI microLED AR display. Pixel-level sensing with pBIST enables accurate calibration and compensation of current variation. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.7 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>-pitch array integrates an 8b shifter with MSB latch for PWM. System techniques including self-refresh, partial update, and foveated rendering reduce MIPI bandwidth by 59.2% and power by up to 40.1%. Measured DNL <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$< \pm 0.25$</tex> LSB and flicker SNR −72 dB validate circuit and system effectiveness.

title: 22.2 A 16-Channel Low-Power Cryo-CMOS Flux Control Pulse Generator ASIC in 14nm FinFET Technology
authors: Kevin Tien, David J. Frank, John F. Bulzacchelli, Pat Rosno, Daniel Moertl, John Timmerwilke, Ari Noori, Devin Underwood, Ken Inoue, Subhajit Ray, Daniel Ramirez, Timothy J. Schmerbeck, Bryce Snell, Jeremy Ekman, Ryan Black, Mark Yeck, Tom Haselhorst, Emma Erickson, Kevin Demsky, Christian W. Baks, Jonathan Kaus, Andrea Ruffino, Pier Andrea Francese, Andy Davies, Sudipto Chakraborty, Scott Lekuch, Brian P. Gaucher, Bodhisatwa Sadhu, Scott M. Willenborg, Daniel J. Friedman
abstract: This work presents a 16-channel flux pulse generator ASIC for qubit control in 14nm FinFET technology consuming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<7 \text{mW}$</tex> per channel. Multiple 10b sub-DACs operating at different rates (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{GS} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>) are used to realize a pulse generator with 14b level resolution and IIR precompensation. A custom SERDES designed for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1 \text{mW}$</tex> power dissipation enables offchip <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \text{Gb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> (up) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$250 \text{Mb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> (down) communications. Multi-qubit control is demonstrated, and measured error-per-2-qubit-gate is as low as 0.0017.

title: KGCS: Zero-Annotation Expert-Knowledge Injection for Object Detection in Aerial Images
authors: Wei Hu, Suhang Hu, Fei Ma, Qihao Zhao, Fan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Aerial object detection is vital for remote sensing applications, yet remains challenged by the prohibitive costs of large-scale data annotation. While current Zero Shot Detection (ZSD) and Open Vocabulary Detection (OVD) reduce annotation dependency, their reliance on base-class training limits adaptability in complex scenarios. We propose KGCS, a novel zero-annotation framework that achieves accurate detection without any training data through synergistic integration of domain knowledge with foundation models. Our approach introduces two key innovations: a structure-aware dual-path proposal strategy that maintains semantic coherence for composite structures, and a tripartite feature dictionary enabling semantic-level alignment beyond conventional category-based matching. Extensive evaluations on DIOR and DOTA datasets demonstrate KGCS’s superior performance in both precision and recall, highlighting its potential as an efficient and scalable solution for aerial imagery analysis. Code will be available at https://github.com/HSH55/KGCS.

title: HyperCLIP-CD: Hyperbolic Multimodal Representation Enhancement for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Qian Yang, Shujun Zhang, Xiao Li, Yunuo Wang, Yuanfei Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) is an important branch of remote sensing image analysis, aimed at identifying changes in land features from bitemporal images. In recent years, change detection methods have predominantly been based on deep learning approaches, some even integrating foundational vision-language models to provide multimodal data, achieving impressive results. However, existing multimodal methods merely utilize multimodal data superficially, without delving into the representation relationship between vision and language. Recent research has proposed that non-Euclidean spaces, such as hyperbolic space, offer advantages in modeling graph-text relationships. To this end, we introduce HyperCLIP-CD, a multimodal network specifically designed for the RSCD task. It leverages the property of hyperbolic space, which is suitable for embedding tree-like data, and combines CLIP to capture the hierarchical structure and non-Euclidean features of image and text data.Specifically, we have designed a hierarchical semantic textual prompting (HTP) generation method tailored for change regions that meet the requirements. Based on a dual-branch CLIP architecture, we extract multimodal features from bitemporal images and propose a hyperbolic space-based graph-text alignment module (HMSA) to obtain deeper semantic change information. HyperCLIP-CD has achieved higher detection accuracy than other advanced networks on both the binary change detection datasets LEVIR-CD and CDD, as well as the semantic change detection dataset SECOND.

title: Machine learning assists the design of a W-band phase-change tunable wire-grid periodic resonant broadband absorber.
authors: Yiting Liu, Cheng Chen, Wei Zhang, Yuhang Wang, Jiaxuan Xue, Wu Zhao, Johan Stiens
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Millimeter-wave systems require absorbers with high absorption efficiency, broad bandwidth, and multi-functional tunability. However, existing devices based on composite structures and materials face design challenges: traditional methods hardly achieve all three indicators simultaneously, often sacrificing one performance for another, making "high absorption, broad bandwidth, multi-function" integration highly difficult. To overcome this fundamental trade-off, this study designs a W-band (75-110 GHz) composite absorber, integrating VO2 and graphene, and combining periodic resonant structures with a backplane-free phase-change wire-grid architecture. When integrated into the periodic resonant structure, the VO2 wire-grid provides thermal ‘on-off’ switching of absorption, whereas graphene offers electrical tunability— both achieved by modulating the effective surface conductivity and thus the impedance matching condition. Guided by an XGBoost-based closed-loop inverse design framework, the XGBoost model optimizes parameters, with convergence achieved after four closed-loop simulation iterations. Results show the VO2 wire-grid absorber performs excellently at 90° incidence: it achieves near-perfect absorption (> 99%) over a 21 GHz sub-band (82-103 GHz), showing an 80% improvement in average absorption compared to copper(Cu) backplanes, under identical unit-cell geometry and simulation conditions, with each configuration independently optimized for peak performance. Graphene provides a maximum modulation amplitude of depth of 68%. Innovations of this study include constructing a periodic resonant wire-grid structure that abandons traditional backplanes and uses wire-grids to expand loss areas, enabling dual-mode multi-physics control through co-integration of VO2 and graphene, and establishing a machine learning closed-loop framework for parameter optimization. It explores a new paradigm for multi-functional tunable absorbers, providing high-performance solutions for MMW systems such as radar, communications, and biomedical imaging.

title: Advancing Efficient Vision Foundation Models for Analysis of Multispectral Imagery
authors: Wei Liu, Saurabh Prasad, Melba Crawford
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Traditional supervised learning methods are typically task-specific and highly dependent on labeled data, which limits their scalability and generalization ability in remote sensing applications. Vision foundation models (VFMs) based on self-supervised learning are highly valued in the analysis of RGB imagery for their ability to generalize well without labeled data. Foundation models are particularly suited for remote sensing applications, where datasets are large and high quality labels are limited. Despite their potential, several challenges remain: 1) Traditional masked autoencoder (MAE) techniques in multispectral data may focus primarily on reconstructing multispectral imagery information, potentially limiting their effectiveness in learning meaningful latent representations. 2) There is a lack of in-depth analysis of feature representations during self-supervised training, with evaluations often dependent on performance after fine-tuning. 3) The typically practice of full parameter fine-tuning in downstream tasks is computationally expensive. To address these challenges, a novel self-supervised VFM specifically designed for multispectral image analysis is proposed. The proposed approach utilizes latent representations from the encoder module to selectively reconstruct masked patches, reducing pretraining parameters and enhancing feature representation capabilities from the encoder. Additionally, during the pretraining phase, weighted attention distance and feature map frequency are employed to explicitly analyze feature learning across both spatial and frequency domains. Leveraging the strong generalization ability gained from pretraining on a large-scale in-domain dataset, the model requires only a lightweight fine-tuning strategy to adapt to different downstream tasks, significantly reducing computational costs. by utilizing only 5.2% of the trainable parameters in the backbone. Experiments on three downstream datasets show that the proposed model achieves competitive results in classification, with a Top-1 accuracy of 99.44% and a multi-label mAP of 88.2, as well as in segmentation, with a mIoU of 50.1, while requiring significantly fewer trainable parameters in both the pretraining and fine-tuning stages.

title: DMHA-Transformer with Joint Convolution and Global Attention Enhancement for Hyperspectral Image and LiDAR Classification
authors: Chunyan Chen, Guosheng Han, Chengjun Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) categorization plays a pivotal role in various Earth observation applications. Single-modal HSI is constrained by insufficient spatial resolution, spectral redundancy, and noise contamination, making it inadequate for high-accuracy land-cover classification and complex scene interpretation; thus, complementary multi-source information is urgently needed. Existing CNN-based approaches fail to adequately exploit the global sequential nature of spectral features, while Vision Transformers and related architectures incorporate a randomly initialized external CLS tokens, whose inductive bias often results in limited generalization. Given these limitations, we therefore develop JGAM-DMHA-Net, a new network architecture dedicated to joint classification by HSI and LiDAR data. The framework employs an enhanced dual attention for efficaciously combining supplementary information from HSI with LiDAR data. A joint network incorporating hybrid 3D-CNN, 2D-CNN and global attention mechanism (J-NHGAM) preserves both channel-wise and spatial-wise information by simultaneously modeling the interactions across channel, height, and width dimensions. It dynamically reweights the importance of different locations or channels on a global scale, enabling more effective information exchange and representation learning. In the DMHA-Transformer block, depth-wise (DW) convolution not only excavates deeper-level features but also establishes global dependencies among features at disparate spatial locations by leveraging complementary information sources, thereby endowing the model with superior generalization capability. The experimental results redon three public datasets indicate that the employed approach outperforms several state-of-the-art methods, with improvements of 0.95 %, 0.09 %, and 2.89 % respectively. The results provide clear evidence validating the advantages of our model redin achieving efficient multi-source data fusion, offering a practical solution for complex scenarios.

title: Not the Right Cue for Me: Investigating VR Cue Design and Biofeedback Integration in Drug Psychotherapy

title: Design and Implementation of an Uncrewed Aerial-Aquatic Vehicle With Antenna Retractor
authors: Zikun Zhen, Yunhua Jiang, Zhihui Zou, Zhenglin Li
abstract: Uncrewed aerial-aquatic vehicles (UAAVs) can operate in both aerial and aquatic domains and repeatedly cross domains, thereby presenting considerable application prospects. This letter introduces ‘Diving Hawk-2 (DH-2),’ a forward-tilting coaxial three-axis six-rotor uncrewed aerial-aquatic vehicle (UAAV) that solves the persistent problem of real-time communication for submerged vehicles via an antenna retractor. We detail the vehicle's mechanical and control co-design, which ensures multi-domain agility without actuator redundancy. Experimental results from field trials in polar, lake, and high visibility pool environments confirm both the system's capability for real-time information transmission and control during underwater locomotion, and the vehicle's ability to perform repeatable, cross-domain transitions with stable multi-mode locomotion. The DH-2 platform, with its high modularity, offers a practical and replicable model for future research in real-time control of UAAVs.

title: Millimeter-wave Radar-based Ego-Trajectory Estimation of a Vehicle Using Multi-Target Tracking Filter
authors: Ryuto Terawake, Keiji Jimi, Kenshi Saho
abstract: We present a method for ego-trajectory estimation of a vehicle using only a millimeter-wave radar. The proposed method introduces a Kalman-filter-based multi-target tracking to accurately estimate the relative positions of surrounding static objects as landmarks for an ICP (Iterative Closest Point) matching method. On measured data, a maximum error for a turning trajectory estimation decreased from 1.8 m (ICP-only) to 0.18 m when the proposed tracking method was applied. These results demonstrate that explicit tracking is an effective complement to ICP for accurate, robust ego-localization.

title: Single Stage DC-AC Boost Inverter-Based Perturbation Technique for Online Impedance Spectroscopy Measurements of Photovoltaic Panels
authors: Awingot Akparibo, Paul Barendse
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Traditional online impedance spectroscopy measurements of solar PV cells are achieved through two-stage DC-DC-AC power conversion topologies where the input DC-DC boost converter stage is utilized for power regulation and injecting small signal AC perturbations for Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) measurements. It is easily achieved because the DC DC boost converter can be simply controlled by small signal linear models. However, due to increased power losses, high weight and cost in the two-stage topologies, single stage boost DC-AC power conversion has become an increased subject of research area. Although the single stage topologies have gained popularity for their compactness, high voltage gain and efficiency, it has not been extended to include online EIS diagnostic functionality for the PV device as done in the two stage DC-DC-AC topology. This could be due to high input current and voltage variations/ripples in DC-AC inverters which makes it difficult to establish steady state conditions for EIS measurements. In this research, we adopted a type of single stage inverter, called the simplified split-source boost inverter which presents the ability to produce continuous input waveforms desirable to support EIS measurements. EIS excitation techniques for the proposed topology to ensure simple and effective online impedance spectroscopy estimation of PV cells are discussed. Simulation and experimental results validate the proposed method.

title: Multi-objective Layered Optimization Analysis of Non-Contact Integrated Permanent Magnet Vernier Motor for Deep-sea Joint Motor
authors: Qiushuo Chen, Wenjie Su, Junlei Chen, Ying Fan, Yiming Fang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: This paper proposes a non-contact integrated permanent magnet vernier motor (NCIPMVM) to address the challenges of implementing a high-performance direct-drive actuator within the extremely constrained and pressure-sealed environment of deep-sea joint motors. The core concept involves forming an axially rigid structure with a common inner rotor through the integration of a vernier motor and a magnetic gear (MG). To improve the effectiveness of the integrated design, an optimized layering strategy is introduced, which considers the demagnetization characteristics of permanent magnets (PMs). Sensitivity analysis and hierarchical parameter optimization are conducted using cross-parameter analysis in combination with the Pearson correlation coefficient. Design of experiment (DOE) models are then established across different levels, while NSGA-III and PSO algorithms are employed to perform parameter optimization. Layered optimization is further refined through iterative conditional constraints, with the goal of satisfying output performance requirements. Based on the optimized parameters, a prototype is fabricated. Finally, the validity and accuracy of both the proposed NCIPMVM and the optimization strategy are verified through finite element analysis (FEA) and experimental testing.

title: E-OptEEG: A Hybrid Ensemble Metaheuristic Feature Optimization for EEG-Based Sentiment Analysis on Resource-Constrained Edge Devices
authors: Jayakrishnan Anandakrishnan, Kuei-Chung Chang, Alkha Mohan, Chuan-Yu Chang, Keping Yu, Arun Kumar Sangaiah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Electroencephalogram (EEG) signal processing is essential for achieving accurate and efficient real-time edge computing, particularly in resource-constrained smart wearables. They demand lightweight and optimized solutions for rapid analysis and decision-making. However, the higher dimensionality of EEG signals introduces challenges of latency and computational cost on edge devices. This article presents E-OptEEG, a hybrid ensemble metaheuristic framework designed to bring edge-optimized EEG processing and feature selection specifically for low-powered devices. The E-OptEEG framework integrates the strengths of multiple evolutionary feature selection algorithms, leveraging an ensemble threshold voting scheme to combine their outputs and identify the most relevant features. Further, E-OptEEG employs a fruit-fly optimization algorithm with deep metric feature transformation based on cosine similarity to transform the refined feature set to a minimal latent space. E-OptEEG demonstrates its ability to identify and select the most relevant features, enabling effective emotion and sentiment analysis from EEG signals captured through edge-powered wearable devices. Experimental evaluations against state-of-the-art feature selection techniques on three different EEG-based behavior modeling datasets highlight the framework’s effectiveness, achieving an average accuracy exceeding 98% with an average accuracy improvement of 2.55%. The E-OptEEG framework exemplifies the potential for lightweight artificial intelligence solutions to enable real-time, resource-efficient decision-making in wearable electronics.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning in Healthcare: A Review of Adaptive Treatment Strategies, Clinical Decision Support, and Personalized Medicine
authors: Rajesh D., Ms. Geetha Arjunan
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning are advancing healthcare with personalized medicine, on-demand treatment protocols, and clinical decision-support systems. This review synthesizes the findings from 36 research studies, published between 2020 and 2025, on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and related technologies. The analysis highlights novel methods such as transformer-based assessments of Electronic Health Records (AUC 0.88-0.91 for predicting diabetes) and DRL systems that improved cancer therapy effectiveness by 20 %. While AI technologies demonstrate superior performance over non-AI methods—exemplified by a 94 % sensitivity for detecting pulmonary fibrosis—significant barriers impede their adoption. These include algorithmic biases in datasets (shown by a 24 % accuracy drop for populous ethnic groups compared to white skin tones), mistrust of AI recommendations for important decisions (62% reluctance among clinicians), and workflow integration issues that slowed down innovation in 78 % of trials. Furthermore, liability governance, privacy, and fairness present serious ethical challenges for organizational decision-makers. To address these challenges, this review identifies several critical priorities for future research and development. First, there is an urgent need for (1) robust multi-modal data integration strategies that effectively combine imaging, genomics, and clinical notes to create a holistic patient profile. Second, (2) the development and standardization of real-world performance validation frameworks beyond retrospective studies is essential to prove efficacy in dynamic clinical environments. Recommended priorities also include: (3) hybrid neuro-symbolic frameworks that improve both machine learning performance and model interpretability; (4) federated learning to build DRL models in data-deprived settings while preserving privacy; and (5) context-specific solutions tailored for low-resourced settings to prevent the widening of global health disparities. Lastly, to guarantee long-term safety and efficacy after deployment, proactive policy development for ongoing model monitoring and adaption is necessary. The review highlights the critical need for cross-disciplinary and interdisciplinary teamwork, flexible organizational policies, and a fundamental shift towards human-AI collaborative care models. It also offers a generalizable assessment of the obstacles to implementing the promise of equitable, patient-centric AI solutions.

title: NutriVision: Gen AI-Powered Personalized Dietary Needs Using ResNet50
authors: S Venkata Lakshmi, K Rishikesh, S Vanisri
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Research indicates that keeping a balanced diet plays a key role in warding off serious health issues like diabetes, obesity, and heart-related diseases [1], [2]. Still, tailoring diets to individual needs poses ongoing difficulties. This stems largely from the absence of automated systems designed for personal use. NutriVision stands out as a system built on generative AI. It draws on ResNet50 to identify foods from images [6]. Along with that, it employs generative AI techniques to create recipes and map out nutritional details [5], [15]. The approach involves examining photos of meals. It also factors in personal health data, including things like a person's age, weight, any allergies, and existing medical issues [4]. Through this, NutriVision generates meal plans suited to each user. Evidence expects the system reaches a 98 percent accuracy rate in classifying foods. That level suggests strong dependability for recognition tasks. In turn, it offers dietary advice tailored to the individual, which could lead to better overall health results. Looking ahead, planned updates might expand coverage to more global cuisines. They could also incorporate various input types and connect with wearable tech for deeper integration.

title: Multimodal MRI-Based Brain Tumor Segmentation Enhanced Through BAT Algorithm and Conditional Random Fields
authors: V. Sindhu, A. Raihana, Pradeep Sudhakaran, M. Jayanthi
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Brain tumours such as glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) present significant challenges for magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) segmentation due to their heterogeneous appearance. This paper proposes a hybrid framework, the GLCM-CRF-Bat Algorithm (GCBA), for robust brain tumour segmentation. Unlike conventional thresholding and statistical methods, GCBA integrates multi-level Gabor wavelet features with Grey Level Cooccurrence Matrix (GLCM) texture descriptors and models spatial dependencies using a Conditional Random Field (CRF) with smoothness priors. To further refine segmentation and minimize labelling noise, structural priors based on tumour symmetry and continuity are incorporated. The Bat Algorithm is employed to optimize feature selection and parameter tuning, improving adaptability across datasets. The proposed method was validated on the BraTS 2018 dataset (2,870 MRI slices) and the Figshare brain tumour dataset (3,064 MR images), achieving an average accuracy of 98.2%, precision of 97.6%, recall of 96.8%, and F1-score of 97.2%. These results outperform baseline CNN, Inception-v3, and VGG16 approaches by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5 \%$</tex>. The findings demonstrate that GCBA offers a scalable and noise-resilient solution for brain tumour segmentation, contributing to improved diagnostic support in clinical neuroimaging.

title: ED-RRT*: Environmental-Metric-Driven Rapidly-exploring Random Tree Star
authors: Yiwen Pang, Leng Han
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: Autonomous mobile robots require reliable global planning and dynamically feasible local trajectories to operate safely in complex environments. Although RRT* provides asymptotic optimality, its performance in real-world scenarios is often hindered by inefficient sampling in narrow passages, excessive tree expansion in open areas, and the generation of paths that are difficult to smooth or enforce with dynamic constraints. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an integrated planning-optimization framework based on an Environment-Density-guided RRT* (ED-RRT*) and the Minimum-Control (MINCO) trajectory formulation. ED-RRT* augments the classical RRT* by incorporating an adaptive sampling strategy driven by local obstacle-density estimation, enabling focused sampling in cluttered environments while preserving the optimality properties of RRT*. The resulting global path is further refined using MINCO, where the trajectory is represented with uniform B-splines and optimized by minimizing high-order control effort under dynamic feasibility and collision-avoidance constraints. Experiments performed on an AutoLab AGV platform qualitatively demonstrate that the proposed ED-RRT* + MINCO framework produces smoother, safer, and more executable trajectories than conventional RRT*-based pipelines. The results indicate that this approach provides an effective solution for real-time robotic navigation in cluttered and semi-structured environments.

title: Research on Inertia Regulation Strategies in Islanded Grids with High Proportion of New Energy Sources
authors: Xiaolong Shi, Dongsheng Yang, Song Huang, Xiaodong Zheng, Chenxu Chao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: The high proportion of renewable energy integration reduces power system inertia and exacerbates the frequency stability risks of islanded grids. This paper analyzes the dynamic response characteristics of renewable energy power plants under unbalanced active power disturbances, establishes a unified frequency response model covering wind power, photovoltaics, energy storage, and synchronous machines, and proposes a two-stage inertia coordination strategy combining wind and energy storage: in the inertia response stage, wind power and energy storage provide virtual inertia support; in the frequency recovery stage, energy storage performs droop control independently, combined with SOC adaptive adjustment to ensure the safe operation of energy storage. Simulation results based on the Chongming power grid show that this strategy can reduce the maximum frequency deviation by more than 24% and RoCoF by more than 22%, significantly enhancing the frequency stability of high-proportion renewable energy grids.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Approaches for Reducing Power Consumption of 200-GHz Passive-Mixer First BiCMOS Receivers
authors: Sarfraz Habibullah Shariff, Goutham Murugesan, Suprovo Ghosh, Suhwan Lee, Frank Zhang, Kenneth K. O
abstract: This paper reports approaches for reducing the power consumption for a passive switching mixer first receiver that are demonstrated in a 200-GHz low-IF (3.75 GHz) BiCMOS receiver prototype. The receiver employs a 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup> order sub-harmonic mixer that allows the use of a 100-GHz LO signal generated by HBT's to reduce DC power consumption. Additionally, the receiver employs a single mixer instead of I/Q down converter to further lower DC power consumption by reducing the number of LO chains and eliminating the need for the generation of I/Q LO signals. The receiver fabricated in a GF BiCMOS process with 45-nm CMOS and HBT fT/f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf> of 410/610 GHz exhibits a 16-GHz bandwidth, a single side band noise figure of 23 dB, OPldB of 0.5 dBm and an out-of-band gain roll-off of ~10 dB over 15 GHz at RF while consuming 96 mW not including that for an ADC.

title: Economic Evaluation Study of Grid-Connected Renewable Power Direct Connection Mode
authors: Jiheng Jiang, Shiyu Liu, Jianye Rao, Wangcheng Long, Ning Wang, Cong Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Under the requirements of carbon peaking and carbon neutrality goals, the direct connection of green electricity represents a crucial pathway for the clean energy transition in industrial parks. This paper conducts an economic evaluation study on grid-connected green power direct connection projects in industrial parks, establishing an economic assessment framework encompassing capacity planning, operational simulation, and comprehensive evaluation. First, a photovoltaic-storage capacity planning model is proposed based on the power matching characteristics between PV generation and loads. Operational metrics are then derived through production simulation. Finally, a comprehensive evaluation model is constructed incorporating factors such as green power retroactive pricing. Using a mining area electricity project as a case study, the framework identifies the optimal economic solution, validating the effectiveness of the proposed methodology.

title: Robust Physically Realizable Backdoor Attack
authors: Md Jahirul Islam, Kazi Aminul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Adversarial attacks pose a significant threat to the security of deep learning models by introducing meticulously crafted perturbations to input data, thereby misleading model predictions. A backdoor attack can occur during training when an adversary poisons a small part of the training data. This injects malicious behavior into the model, causing it to reliably misclassify inputs with a specific trigger into a chosen target class. In this work, we propose an adversarial patch-based backdoor attack framework that requires minimal poisoned data. Adversarial patches are visually prominent, physically realizable triggers capable of forcing a model to misclassify inputs regardless of the original label or context. We generated optimized adversarial patches using clean deep learning models on benchmark datasets, e.g., CIFAR-10, UCMerced, and AID datasets. Then, we planted backdoor triggers into the deep learning models by training with minimal poisoned data. Our experimental results show that adversarial patch-based backdoored models achieved a 99.79% attack success rate (ASR) with 0.1% poisoned CIFAR-10 dataset, achieved a 99.95% ASR with 0.1% poisoned UCMerced dataset, and achieved a 100.00% ASR with 1.0% poisoned AID dataset. In comparison, to achieve a similar ASR with the traditional backdoor attack, BadNets requires around 10.0% poisoned datasets. These results demonstrate that our proposed adversarial patch-based backdoor attack achieves higher attack success with significantly lower poisoning ratios. The proposed attack is also physically realizable, making it a practical threat for real-world applications including surveillance, autonomous vehicles, and remote sensing. These findings underscore that adversarial patch-based backdoor attacks pose a practical and stealthy threat to deployed machine learning systems, motivating the need for effective and generalizable defenses.

title: A Review of AWS IoT: Architecture, Applications, and Research Directions
authors: Sitharthan Ramachandran, Twinkle Meziah A, Gnana Swathika OV, Hema R, Darshini R, Teerath Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has emerged as one of the most transformative paradigms in modern computing, enabling billions of connected devices to sense, process, and transmit data. This growth poses challenges in scalability, security, and real-time data management. Cloud platforms, particularly Amazon Web Services (AWS), have become central enablers of large-scale IoT deployments due to their comprehensive service ecosystem. This paper presents a review of AWS IoT, focusing on its architecture, applications, and open research directions. We analyze core components such as AWS IoT Core, Lambda, Greengrass, and data services, highlighting their roles in enabling cloud-edge integration and secure device management. A survey of academic literature and industrial case studies demonstrates AWS IoT's practical adoption across healthcare, agriculture, and smart city domains. Comparative analysis with Microsoft Azure IoT and Google Cloud IoT positions AWS as a scalable and secure platform, while identifying limitations in latency, cost, and provisioning complexity. Finally, we outline future research opportunities in federated learning, energy-efficient edge inference, protocol interoperability, and blockchain-based provisioning. This review provides a consolidated reference for researchers and practitioners aiming to leverage AWS IoT for next-generation intelligent systems.

title: Real-Time Human Tracking and Predictive Danger Zone Forecasting for Public Safety
authors: K. Govindarajan, S. Radha Rammohan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Community safety and security in high-density urban environments such as public transport terminals, commercial zones, schools, colleges, and places of public assembly need precautionary monitoring of pedestrian movement to mitigate accidents, risks, and hazards. Existing and available surveillance infrastructure primarily supports incident-based monitoring with limited prediction-driven capability to identify risks and hazards prior to occurrence. This proposed research paper introduces a novel implementation framework titled "Real-Time Human Tracking and Predictive Danger Zone Forecasting for Public Safety". The proposed system processes and analyzes video surveillance recordings captured by the existing CCTV cameras, reduces the resolution from 720p to 480p using gray-scale conversion to reduce computational complexity, applies YOLO variants based on human object detection, and utilizes light-weight AI-powered tracking to investigate the trajectories of persons in the crowd environment. Explicitly defined high-risk and hazard zones are marked, and the proposed system focuses on single or multiple persons entering from multiple trajectories who may move towards danger or hazard-prone areas. Real-time alerts are produced as output to support early-stage intervention to reduce risks and hazards. Primary analysis demonstrates that the proposed method delivers high-performance, accurate human detection and predictive accuracy without using GPU accelerations, while maintaining computational viability for real-time visualization.

title: Covariance Matrix Estimation Method for FDAMIMO Radar
authors: Xuchen Gao, Junwei Xie, Zihang Ding, Bo Wang, Haolong Zhai, Weihang Han, Yuhang Zhang, Jianwei Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: As a new type of array radar, frequency diverse array and multiple-input and multiple-output (FDA-MIMO) radar can achieve better performance than traditional arrays in signal processing field. The covariance matrix is an important type of prior information. Most array signal processing algorithms, such as adaptive beamforming and target parameter estimation, all depend on an accurately estimated signal covariance matrix. Based on the block Toeplitz structure of FDA-MIMO radar covariance matrix, this paper proposes an improved shrinkage-totapering covariance matrix estimation method based on block Toeplitz modification, which can solve the problem of low estimation accuracy of the sample covariance matrix caused by a limited number of samples. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed FDA-MIMO radar covariance matrix estimation method.

title: SPCNC 2025 Breaker Page
authors: N/A
