abstract: Eye movement biometric has recently reached a meaningful performance threshold within a gaze estimation pipeline. Prior research claimed that good biometric performance can be achieved from a relatively large set of weakly intercorrelated features with high temporal persistence (indexed by the measurement of reliability). In this study, we revisit this hypothesis in the context of a modern deep learning (DL)-based eye movement biometric system, using a publicly available eye-movement dataset. Specifically, we investigate whether the measurement of reliability of learned embeddings continues to predict biometric performance in DL-based biometrics. Our results confirm that temporal persistence—quantified by measurement of reliability—is a significant predictor of performance in DL-based biometric systems, extending prior findings into the DL-based biometric. We also examine how manipulating eye-tracking signal quality descriptors impacts the temporal persistence of embeddings, finding that degradation of any kind undermines their temporal persistence. As a general matter, we found that measurement of reliability is an important predictor of DL-based biometric performance, and also that DL-learned embeddings are generally weakly intercorrelated.

title: FPGA Implementation of Bilateral Filter for MRI and CT Scan Image Denoising
authors: Navaneethan S, Arunkumar K, Sowjanya A, Karthick Kannan S P
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The proposed project introduces a hardware implementation of MRI and CT image denoising with the use of a bilateral filter on a Xilinx Spartan-6 FPGA in real time. The proposed design achieves high throughput (1 frame/s), which is the speed of software-based methods, and therefore, real-time streaming is possible in the proposed design due to the use of power-saving technologies. A symmetrical pipelined bilateral filtering structure optimizes the operations to a point that minimizes the working set of multipliers (by a factor of ~45 per cent) and the count of DSP used in executing the architecture (reduces 120 to 64 blocks). According to power analysis, the optimized design consumes less power than the equivalent designs, with the power dropped down to 730 mW as opposed to the 980 mW theoretical power consumption. The results of the experiment show an increase in image quality and PSNR of 29.3 dB in MRI and 31.0 dB in CT and SSIM of 0.88 indicate that noise is removed successfully when the image boundaries are observed. The proposed FPGA-based bilateral filter outperforms in terms of resource-managing, design and fortunately FPGA-based designs used in the past to do denoising and achieve competitive speed and quality. The characteristics of the design make it a good candidate to be integrated into embedded medical imaging system, which allows real-time, low-power as well as resource-efficient medical imaging denoising.

title: Transferable XAI: Relating Understanding Across Domains with Explanation Transfer
authors: Fei Wang, Yifan Zhang, Brian Y Lim
publish: Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Intelligent User Interfaces
abstract: None

title: Synthesis Methodology for Sensors Based on Nonlinear Resonators
authors: Camilo Moncada, Franco Ramírez, Almudena Suárez
abstract: This work presents a synthesis methodology for sensors based on nonlinear resonators. The new procedure enables high sensitivity near a specific value of the sensing parameter by tracing turning-point loci and identifying cusp bifurcations. As a key advantage, the method enables reconfigurability, since the sensitivity may be optimized around different target values. The sensor can also detect variations in the sensed parameter through discontinuous jumps observed when sweeping either the input amplitude or frequency. The concept has been validated using a prototype that was experimentally characterized.

title: Splice Site Prediction Network Based on Position Aware Attention Mechanism
authors: Zeyao Chen, Shixian Wang, Hongdong Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Alternative splicing affects approximately 95% of human multi-exon genes, with dysregulation contributing to roughly 35% of genetic disease-associated mutations, underscoring the critical importance of accurate splice site prediction. Traditional computational approaches rely on short sequence windows (9-23 nucleotides) that fail to capture longrange regulatory elements. Although recent deep learning models can process extended sequences (up to 10,000 nucleotides), they employ uniform positional treatment, thereby limiting biological interpretability. We propose a deep learning architecture PAMSSplice that integrates global-local context with positional encoding for adaptive feature weighting, and employs hierarchical dilation to capture sequence features at different scales. Testing on real mutated gene datasets confirmed strong generalization, with a remarkable 8.3% relative improvement in AUC on the FAS mutated gene dataset compared to SpliceAI. This approach improves both prediction accuracy and biological interpretability of splicing regulatory mechanisms.

title: Performance Analysis of Frequency Hopping Communication’s Resistance to Partial Band Interference Based on Forward Error Correction Coding
authors: Rui Wang, Chongkai Wu, Bingsong Xiao, Xing Xiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Partial band interference (PBI) is a “killer” interference in practical communications, which seriously impairs the reliability of frequency hopping (FH) communication systems. Aiming at the limited ability of pure frequency hopping communication systems to resist partial band interference, this paper proposes an anti-interference strategy based on forward error correction (FEC) coding. It combines classic Reed-Solomon (RS) coding and Turbo coding with frequency hopping communication systems respectively, and designs simulation experiments to compare their bit error rates (BER) under different interference factors $\rho$, thereby analyzing the performance of resisting partial band interference. When the interference factor $\rho$ is fixed, the influence of different coding parameters on the system’s resistance to partial band interference is analyzed. In addition, under the premise of keeping the interference factor $\rho$ and coding parameters constant, the frequency hopping parameters are changed to analyze their impact on system performance. Simulation results show that the partial band interference resistance performance of frequency hopping communication based on Turbo coding is superior to that based on RS coding, and both are better than that of pure frequency hopping. To facilitate operators to better select frequency hopping waveforms in different scenarios, guidelines and selection methods are provided to improve the application capability of frequency hopping communication based on forward error correction coding in resisting partial band interference.

title: A Synergistic Model for Prostate Cancer Detection Combining Deep Learning and Traditional Techniques
authors: R Gayathri, Denisha T, Barathsaki S, Abenaya S, Eji P
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: In order to give accurate medical diagnosis, this work presents a prostate cancer classification system that combines traditional machine learning algorithms with deep learning approaches. We preprocess, display, and feature scale the dataset before using it to train models such as Random Forest, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression. Additionally, to improve prediction performance and capture complex feature patterns, a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is employed. Optimized with early stopping to improve generalization, the CNN architecture consists of completely linked dense layers, convolution, pooling, and batch normalization. A comparison of the CNN model's performance with conventional methods utilizing ROC curves, accuracy, and confusion matrix highlights the promise of deep learning to enhance prostate cancer detection.

title: An Interactive System for Embedding and Extraction of Dual-Mode Watermarking
authors: Jinghui Huang, Yujuan Si, Mengting Liu, Ying Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: With the growing demand for digital image copyright protection and content traceability, dual-mode watermarking technology has emerged as a key research focus due to its ability to balance “visible identification” and “invisible authentication”. However, existing systems mostly prioritize single-mode functions or theoretical algorithm optimization, lacking integrated interactive dual-mode capabilities and intuitive visual presentation-leading to a high threshold for users to comprehend the logic behind watermark embedding and extraction. This paper develops an interactive system for dualmode watermark embedding and extraction. With a focus on the semi-transparent superposition algorithm for visible watermarks and the LSB (Least Significant Bit) spatial-domain replacement algorithm for invisible watermarks, the system is built based on Java Swing. It supports multi-format image processing and end-to-end operations of “image loading - parameter configuration watermark embedding - watermark extraction - result saving”. This system innovatively transforms the abstract algorithm logic into an intuitive interactive process. Through functions such as dragging the watermark position, configuring visual parameters, and comparing results in dual modes, users can clearly understand the technical differences and implementation principles of the two types of watermarks. Specifically, it provides a practical and operable dual-mode solution for digital image security, while its interactive design and algorithmic architecture offer transferable value for migration to other multimedia security domains.

title: Digital-Assisted-Analog hybrid LDO with High PSR and Transient Enhancement
authors: Chao Tian, Zhangyong Li, Yuanfa Wang, Yu Pang, Yong Wang, Weiwei Sun, Jiasai Luo, Lingrui Xing, Guanjie Zeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This work proposes a hybrid Low-Dropout Regulator (LDO) structure with high power supply rejection (PSR) and fast transient response. By adopting NMOS pre-filter circuit, this LDO achieves excellent Full-Spectrum PSR performance without large off-chip capacitor. Meanwhile, a Digital-Assisted-Analog hybrid LDO structure is employed to reduce the voltage dropout and increase the maximum load current. In response to the special structure of this hybrid LDO, two transient transient enhancement circuits have been designed to reduce voltage fluctuations and accelerate the recovery time. This work uses $\mathbf{1 8 0 - n m}$ CMOS process. The simulation results show that, with an on-chip output capacitor of 250 pF, the voltage dropout of this LDO is $\mathbf{3 0 0 ~ m V}$, worst-case PSR is - 28 $\mathbf{d B}$ and the undershoot voltage is 87 mV with a load steps from 1 mA to 50 mA within 100-ns edge times.

title: Buffer Overflow Fuzzing Guided by Directed Identification Metric
authors: Xiamin Guo, Mingke Zhang, Wei Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Fuzzing serves as a prevalent automated approach for uncovering software vulnerabilities. However, traditional fuzzing often exhibit a lack of directional focus when applied to particular vulnerability types, leading to inefficient triggering performance and suboptimal allocation of testing resources. This paper introduces a directed identification metric guided fuzzing aimed specifically at detecting buffer overflow vulnerabilities. We add source-level instrumentation that computes a runtime identification metric and feed that information back into the fuzzing loop to bias seed selection and mutation toward code areas that appear close to unsafe memory accesses. Based on the AFL++ framework, experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed technique accelerates crash detection markedly compared to existing fuzzing, confirming the practicality and efficacy of the directed identification metric in enhancing fuzzing.

title: Heterogeneous network multi-target tracking algorithm based on PHD filter
authors: Kuiwu Wang, Qin Zhang, Pengfei Wan, Xiaolong Hu, Zhenlu Jin, Xiaohan Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-target tracking method based on heterogeneous fusion of radar and infrared sensors, aiming to enhance tracking accuracy and robustness by integrating complementary information from both sensors. First, angle filtering is independently performed using radar and infrared sensors to obtain their respective angle-prior probability hypothesis densities (PHDs) and angle-posterior PHDs for multi-target scenarios. Subsequently, an infrared-assisted angle correction strategy is employed to refine the radar angle estimates using high-precision angle measurements from infrared sensors. Furthermore, an angle association algorithm is utilized to achieve cross-sensor angle information matching, forming joint angle association pairs. Finally, the corrected angle information is incorporated to update multi-target states, followed by Gaussian component pruning-merging to eliminate low-weight components, yielding the final multi-target state estimates. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively fuses radar and infrared sensor data, significantly improving both tracking accuracy and system robustness in multi-target environments.

title: Multitemporal Vegetation Analysis for Renewable Energy Planning in Colombia's Amazon River Ports and the Areas Surrounding the Bogotá River
authors: Laura Mercedes Arteaga Rojas, Edgar Alirio Aguirre Buenaventura, Sergio González Gil
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This research evaluates the territorial suitability for renewable energy planning in four contexts in Colombia: the Amazonian ports of Puerto Leticia, Puerto Nariño and Puerto Nazareth, and the urban wetlands of the Bogotá River (Meander del Say and Jaboque) with their peri-urban and agricultural areas. The study responds to a converging problem: the deterioration due to water and atmospheric pollution, the effects of climate change on flows and cover, the pressure on the territories of riverside indigenous communities, restrictions on river traffic and the vulnerability of the supply of electricity in remote areas. Consequently, Google Earth Engine, Python, and QGIS were implemented in a reproducible flow of three phases: (i) definition of ROI and data acquisition; (ii) radiometric/atmospheric preprocessing and correlation of vegetation indices (e.g., NDVI) with CHIRPS precipitation, ERA5-Land temperature, and SRTM slope; and (iii) time series analysis, Pearson correlations, and AHP-weighted aptitude index. As a result, Puerto Leticia presented <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>70 \%$</tex> favorable area (93.56 ha high; 84.32 ha average), Puerto Nazareth 76.2% high (15.8% average), Puerto Nariño 65% high (32% average) and the wetlands of Bogotá an index focused on medium values (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.45-0.60$</tex>) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 300$</tex> ha of high suitability. In addition, projections suggest slight weakening of vegetation in Leticia (NDVI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 0.099 \rightarrow 0.091$</tex> by 2046; NDWI almost stable) and decrease of NDVI in Nazareth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.389 \rightarrow 0.339)$</tex> with NDWI trending towards zero, which guides low-impact locations, supports comparison between sites and prioritizes renewable solutions that reduce pollution, improve navigability and strengthen reliable access to electricity for local communities with the use of Floating Photovoltaic Sources.

title: High-Performance Interconnect Interface Design Based on Wafer-Level Systems
authors: Jiahui Wang, Zhaohui Wang, Wei Guo, Chang Liu, Ruoyu Liang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Addressing the trade-off challenge of achieving “high speed, reliability, and low latency” in wafer-level system interconnects, this paper focuses on designing high-performance interconnects between chiplets. It customizes and optimizes the Aurora interface, proposing a non-intrusive external BCH $(128,113,2)$ forward error correction architecture to enable burst error correction on top of the native Aurora protocol. Through comparative testing on a Xilinx VCU128 evaluation board against native Aurora and a trimmed PCIe 4.0 implementation, the approach demonstrates comprehensive advantages in resource utilization, bit error rate, and end-to-end latency. This provides wafer-level systems with a high-performance interconnect solution that balances high reliability and low latency.

title: Vector Array Shortwave Signal Enhancement Technology Based on Neural Networks
authors: Qinghao Zhu, Ai Yan, Zhiyuan Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: In view of the stratified, inhomogeneous, and timevarying characteristics of the ionosphere-which cause distorted effects such as multipath fading, polarization rotation, Doppler frequency shift, and frequency spreading on shortwave signal transmission-this paper proposes a novel method. Specifically, a large-aperture vector array is adopted at the front end for signal reception to enhance the multi-domain information perception capability of shortwave signals, while a signal synthesis and enhancement method based on a multi-layer feedforward neural network is employed at the back end. Compared with traditional channel equalization anti-fading methods, this proposed method achieves better performance in terms of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvement and reduction of signal demodulation bit error rate (BER), and can effectively enhance the anti-fading reception capability of shortwave signals.

title: Microwave-Induced Thermoacoustic Imaging Based on Field Distribution Calibration
authors: Xiaohui Wu, Zihui Chi, Zhengyu Zhang, Huabei Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: Conventional microwave-induced thermoacoustic imaging (MITAI) suffers from nonuniform electric (E)-field distributions, reducing image contrast and edge-detection capability. This nonuniformity mainly arises from the radiation properties of the excitation antenna and the participation/modulation of biological tissues during microwave propagation. This work proposes an E-field normalization calibration incorporating tissue participation. Reference fields are derived from both a tumor-containing tissue model and a simplified homogeneous medium model without tumors, and normalization factors are constructed to correct the original power-loss density (PLD). A finite-element-based forward model is then established from the PLD distribution to solve for the initial acoustic pressure and acoustic signal propagation. Using detected acoustic pressure signals, MITAI images are reconstructed with the delay-and-sum algorithm for comparative analysis—covering uncalibrated, homogeneous-medium field calibrated, and tumor-inclusive field calibrated versions—and image quality under these conditions is evaluated qualitatively and quantitatively. Results show that both calibration approaches enhance image contrast and improve the delineation of tissue boundaries, while calibration employing the tumor-inclusive reference E-field yields more accurate tumor features and closer agreement with the simulation setup. In addition, a saline-tube phantom experiment further verifies the effectiveness of the proposed electric-field normalization calibration. These findings suggest that subsequent calibration methods should account for tissue-electromagnetic interactions to obtain more realistic E-field distributions within tissues. The proposed method holds considerable promise for practical application, providing a viable strategy and new perspective for high-accuracy MITAI in complex tissue environments.

title: Radar Narrow-band Target Recognition Method Based on Image Processing
authors: Jingming Sun, Qiang Zhang, Zhong Wang, Yuhao Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Fast and accurate identification of target type is a key problem that must be overcome to improve the detection and perception performance of radar. However, the existing methods face application problems such as low recognition accuracy or failure to meet real-time processing requirements under narrowband conditions. Aiming at the urgent need of radar narrow-band target recognition, based on radar narrow-band echo data, image processing method is adopted, convolutional neural network is used to extract image correlation structure features from pulserange two-dimensional data, data preprocessing and data augmentation are used to improve network generalization, and sequential fusion is used to improve comprehensive recognition performance. The experimental results show that the proposed method is evaluated comprehensively from three aspects: operating efficiency, recognition accuracy and engineering application, and has achieved good single frame recognition and sequential recognition effects, which can make narrow-band radar have the function of fast and accurate target type recognition.

title: A Wideband CMOS DSA with Low Phase Shift Variation Based on a Compact Co-Design Method
authors: Zhiqing Liu, Jinyi Hua, Jianpeng Sun, Xianrong Zhang, Minghua Zhao, Zhijiang Dai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper presents a DC-to-32GHz CMOS digital step attenuator (DSA) with low phase shift variation based on a compact co-design method for muti-channel phased array systems. The proposed DSA adopts three attenuation unit structures (T-type, simplified T-type, and Pi-type) to realize a maximum attenuation range of 15.5 dB with a step of 0.5 dB. In this design, the additional shunt capacitors are utilized in T-type and Pi-type units for improving the phase shift variation characteristics at high frequencies. Furthermore, by using a compact co-design method, which involves the co-optimization of passive inductor geometries and active transistor layout, the overall circuit layout is effective minimized. Fabricated in 65 nm CMOS technology, the DSA achieves a compact core size of $310 \mu \mathrm{~m} \times 140 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ with an extra calibration bit. From DC to 32 GHz, the DSA exhibits an RMS amplitude/phase error below 0.4 $\mathrm{dB} / 1.9^{\circ}$ under full temperature range, an insertion loss below 4.12 dB while having a $S 11 / S 22$ better than -15 dB, and an $\mathrm{IP}_{1 \mathrm{~dB}}$ of 9dBm, respectively.

title: Semantic Intent Weaver: A Context-Aware Search Algorithm for Ambiguous Query Resolution
authors: Hemanth Manchabale Papachappa
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Traditional search algorithms struggle with ambiguous queries where context determines meaning, such as distinguishing between beverage, adhesive, and fashion contexts in queries like "scotch." This paper introduces Semantic Intent Weaver (SIW), a search algorithm that employs multidimensional intent modeling and dynamic context weaving to resolve query ambiguity. SIW integrates temporal, personal, cross-modal, and emotional context dimensions through a five-layer architecture that deconstructs user intent, weaves contextual understanding, performs multi-dimensional ranking, adapts result presentation, and continuously learns from interactions. To ensure reproducibility, we evaluate our model on the TREC Web Track’s ambiguity sub-task. The revised experiments, which include a new baseline aware of the session, demonstrate that SIW’s dynamic weaving mechanism achieves a 6.8% improvement in nDCG@10 over strong ColBERT models augmented with identical session data. The primary novelty of our work lies in the unified framework for dynamically weighting and integrating disparate contextual signals, providing a robust solution to a longstanding challenge in information retrieval.

title: Face-Based Kinship Verification Using Deep Embeddings for Low-Cost Health Record Linkage
authors: Rahul Balamurugan, Kency Taniya Antony Sekar, V. Nandalal, Congyu Peter Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Precise linkage of health records is essential for continuity of care, reducing duplicate health records, and accurately documenting family medical histories. Genomic testing offers the evidence-based biological ‘gold standard' for verifying kinship; however, access to testing is either impossible or unavailable in most low-resourced environments due to prohibitive costs, long timelines, and/or lack of infrastructure. This study provides a low cost and interpretable pipeline for kinship verification in the form of Siamese deep embeddings. The processed facial image embeddings produced by a ResNet-18 backbone using 256dimensional and L2-normalized embeddings, are then compared using cosine similarity. A validation-based calibration process selects the logit polarity and decision threshold that support stable deployment decisions. GradCAM visualizations can be interpreted frame-by-frame and allow for pair-specific attributions of faces that were more relevant or important in decisions of similarity. In experiments on the Families in the Wild (FIW) dataset (family-disjoint splits), we report ROC-AUC of 0.834, target balanced accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.88$</tex>, with similar precision, recall, and specificity. The confusion matrices also illustrate a near symmetric distribution of errors by family and both Grad-CAM explanations highlight how the model came to a decision for true cases and hard cases. The above results illustrate how we can deploy a lightweight, explainable, and face-based kinship verification pipeline on a CPU-only system. Our study therefore provides a feasible assistive tool for health record linkage where genomic validation is not possible.

title: A Low-Latency Hybrid Cryptographic Framework for Secure Spiking Neural Network Inference
authors: Xiaoliang Zhang, Jundong Feng, Junchao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: The growing adoption of Machine Learning as a Service (MLaaS) has raised critical privacy concerns regarding both client data and proprietary model parameters during cloud-based inference. While Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) offer energy-efficient, event-driven computation, their privacypreserving inference remains challenging. Existing pure Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) approaches suffer from prohibitive latency due to expensive polynomial approximations for nonlinear dynamics, while conventional hybrid frameworks designed for Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) fail to exploit the temporal sparsity inherent in SNNs. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel hybrid-cryptographic framework that strategically combines a sparsity-aware homomorphic encryption kernel for efficient linear layer computation with a low-overhead garbled circuit protocol for exact Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (LIF) activation evaluations. The framework introduces an efficient HE-GC domain-switching mechanism that fully leverages SNNs’ temporal dynamics and event-driven sparsity in encrypted domains. Experiments on MNIST and Fashion-MNIST datasets demonstrate that our approach maintains high inference accuracy while achieving up to $48 \times$ reduction in end-to-end latency and significantly reduced communication costs compared to pure-FHE baselines. These results confirm our framework’s potential for enabling practical and scalable privacy-preserving SNN inference in MLaaS environments.

title: Advances in Convolutional Neural Networks for Image Classification : Architecture Evolution, Transformer Fusion, and Application Expansion
authors: Jian Tang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Convolutional neural network (CNN) is a core component of deep learning, especially in image classification. In terms of results, it can completely transform the face of computer vision. Over time, the architecture of CNN has evolved from a basic shallow design to an increasingly deep and complex one. Against this backdrop, the integration of the Transformer architecture has pushed visual information processing into a more theoretically and mathematically rigorous framework. This article mainly reviews the latest progress of CNN in image classification from multiple aspects: Firstly, the evolution of cnn from basic models such as LeNet and AlexNet to complex networks including VGG and ResNet, while introducing supplementary components such as the attention mechanism; Secondly, the wider application of various CNN-based models was explored; Finally, it is worth noting that the Vision Transformer (ViT) proposed by Dosovitskiy et al. (2020, ICLR) achieved a Top-1 accuracy of $88.5 \%$ on ImageNet after large-scale pre-training. It has demonstrated the great potential of Transformer-based methods in visual tasks.

title: Research and Implementation of Short Video Content Sentiment Analysis Based on LSTM Neural Networks
authors: Jinyuan Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Based on the LSTM neural network, a short video comment sentiment analysis model was designed to enhance the accuracy and robustness of sentiment classification. Building upon traditional sentiment analysis methods, this model leverages the time series modeling advantages of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. By incorporating sentiment feature extraction and a multi-layer classification mechanism, it achieves precise identification of positive, negative, and neutral sentiments in video comments. Experiments employed 300 short video contents and their 112,000 comment data for training and testing. Results demonstrate that the LSTM model achieves $88.4 \%$ accuracy in sentiment classification tasks, with $89.7 \%$ precision for positive sentiment and $82.1 \%$ recall for negative sentiment. Compared to other benchmark models, it exhibits significant performance improvements, particularly in complex emotion recognition and sentiment shift detection.

title: SPCNC 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)

title: A Fuzzy-Adaptive Potential Field Method for Multi-AGV Formation with Dynamic Maintenance and Flexible Obstacle Avoidance
authors: Peiyu Chen, Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: To address the challenges of formation maintenance and cooperative obstacle avoidance for multi-AGV systems in complex warehouse environments, this paper proposes a fuzzyadaptive artificial potential field (Fuzzy-APF) method. Built upon the leader-follower framework, the method introduces dual fuzzy controllers for repulsion weight tuning and offset compensation. By leveraging real-time states including obstacle distance, intervehicle spacing, formation error, and leader trajectory curvature, the controllers adaptively adjust repulsion weights and generate feedforward compensation forces, achieving a dynamic balance between “rigid maintenance” and “flexible response.” Simulation results show that, compared with the traditional APF method, the proposed approach significantly suppresses position errors and preserves formation stability in high-speed turning scenarios. In both static and dynamic cooperative obstacle avoidance cases, it produces smoother trajectories and maintains larger internal safety margins. The proposed dual fuzzy-controller framework proves to be a highly effective strategy for dynamically balancing the conflicting objectives of trajectory tracking and safe navigation in complex multi-agent systems.

title: Analysis of Nanomaterial Based Biosensor Health Monitoring Systems with Artificial Intelligence for Smart Healthcare
authors: Periyanayagi S, Vairachilai S, Keerti Vijay Ananth
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Wearable health monitoring has been greatly improved by sensors based on nanomaterials, which have brought about a revolution in bioelectronics nanoengineering and a leap forward in continuous and personalised healthcare. Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) are a great and potentially beneficial choice for biomedical applications since they have greater solubility, reduced toxicity, and simpler synthesis than regular QDs, in addition to all the benefits of common QDs. Hence, this paper, Nanomaterial-based Biosensor Health Monitoring Systems (NM-BHMS) with Artificial Technologies, has proposed the utilization of nanomaterials in wearable health monitoring sensors and achieves a high-performance ratio of 91.7%. The unique nanoscale features of nanomaterials such as quantum dots, graphene, carbon nanotubes, nanowires, and nanocomposites allow for detecting biomarkers and physiological parameters in bodily fluids at low concentrations, greatly improving sensitivity. The miniaturisation and flexibility of nanomaterial sensors make them ideal for incorporation into wearable technology, which improves their accuracy and user comfort. Among the topics covered in this article are nanotechnology, biosensors, nanosensors, and the Internet of Nanotechnology (IoNT). This article aims to give a general outline of machine learning-based biosensing and detail the various nanostructures utilised for biosensing, including some particular uses in cancer detection and the IoT.

title: Research on Vision-Based Underwater Polarization Navigation
authors: Haiyun Gan, Xirui He, Xinyu Wen, Penghui Liu, Jiasai Luo, Yue Fei, Hao Jiang, Xingyu Niu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This study investigates an vision-based polarization navigation acquisition structure and its corresponding imaging polarization navigation algorithm, aiming to enhance the accuracy of bionic polarization navigation, reduce the navigation failure rate, and simplify the structure of the polarization collector. A rotational simple polarizer structure is proposed herein, and an imaging polarization collector is designed using a multi-camera lens system. The solar azimuth angle in the carrier coordinate system is obtained based on dynamic threshold segmentation of polarization degree valleys and the K-Means clustering algorithm, while the RANSAC robust regression method is employed for angle sequence fitting. Additionally, this study extends the research on the propagation characteristics of polarized light in underwater environments and proposes a correction algorithm suitable for underwater polarized image processing. Finally, by utilizing the current year, date, and realtime, the solar azimuth angle in the navigation coordinate system at the current moment is calculated. Using the solar azimuth angle as an intermediate bridge, the relative relationship between the carrier coordinate system and the navigation coordinate system is determined, thereby completing the navigation and orientation of the carrier. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm exhibits excellent orientation performance both in clear weather and underwater environments.

title: Inkjet-printed flexible photodiode with synaptic characteristics
authors: Manvendra Singh, Mohammed Hadhi Pazhaya Puthanveettil, Subho Dasgupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: The ultraviolet (UV) light has been extensively used in medical settings, optical communication, radar detection, currency detection and resin curing. The global SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) pandemic accelerated the integration of UV light into daily life, notably through handheld sanitisation devices, due to its ability to disinfect surfaces and inactivate airborne pathogens by destroying viral genetic material. The high energy associated with these wavelengths also poses risks of penetrating and damaging human skin cells, raising safety concerns. To mitigate these risks, UV detectors based on oxides are gaining significant interests due to their wide band gap and ease of fabrication using various solution processing techniques, such as printing. In this paper, we report an inkjet-printed, flexible photodiode for detecting UV light with wavelength of 365 nm or below. To form an active semiconductor layer of a photodiode, on a polyethene terephthalate (PET) substrate, we employ a wide-bandgap indium gallium oxide nanoparticle ink that can be cured at room temperature. By exploiting persistent photoconductivity (PPC) in oxides, we demonstrate the synaptic response of a printed photodiode to more than 1000 UV pulses of wavelength 365 nm and a period of 1 s. Moreover, the photodiode exhibits a stable response for 1000 cycles against a bending strain of 1.25%. Such characteristics make these devices suitable for developing smart UV detectors, pulse counters, and neuromorphic vision systems.

title:
Development of CMOS compatible Gd
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    wet etching process for SOI/GeOI applications
authors: Abhishek Mallik, Amita Rawat, Hemant Kumar, Debankita De, Manoj Yadav, Udayan Ganguly
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Single crystalline Gadolinium oxide (Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf>) is an attractive material for next-generation semiconductor technologies due to its potential use as a buried oxide (BOX) layer in silicon-on-insulator (SOI) and germanium-on-insulator (GeOI) applications, and as a high-k dielectric in Complementary Metal Oxide Semiconductor (CMOS) processes. For successful integration of foreign oxides such as Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> into CMOS technology, wet etching is highly preferred in the semiconductor industry for its scalability and chemical selectivity. In this work, we demonstrate a selective wet-etching process for Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> that preserves the integrity of underlying Si and Ge substrates. Structural analysis confirmed the presence of both monoclinic (–402) and cubic (–222) orientations in sputtered Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> films. Etch-rate studies revealed average rates of 0.67 nm/s for monoclinic Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> and 0.33 nm/s for cubic Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf>. XPS analysis verified the full elimination of Gd and associated oxides, leaving chemically clean Si and Ge surfaces. These results establish a reproducible, substrate-safe wet-etching route for Gd<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf>, enabling its reliable integration into SOI, GeOI, and CMOS technologies.

title: Mind the Gap: Bridging Occlusion in Gait Recognition via Residual Gap Correction
authors: Ayush Gupta, Siyuan Huang, Rama Chellappa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Gait is becoming popular as a method of person reidentification because of its ability to identify people at a distance. However, most current works in gait recognition do not address the practical problem of occlusions. Among those which do, some require paired tuples of occluded and holistic sequences, which are impractical to collect in the real world. Further, these approaches work on occlusions but fail to retain performance on holistic inputs. To address these challenges, we propose RG-Gait, a method for residual correction for occluded gait recognition with holistic retention. We model the problem as a residual learning task, conceptualizing the occluded gait signature as a residual deviation from the holistic gait representation. Our proposed network adaptively integrates the learned residual, significantly improving performance on occluded gait sequences without compromising the holistic recognition accuracy. We evaluate our approach on the challenging Gait3D, GREW and BRIAR datasets and show that learning the residual can be an effective technique to tackle occluded gait recognition with holistic retention. We release our code publicly at https://github.com/Ayush-00/rg-gait.

title: 33.3 A 6GHz Quadrature Digital Transmitter Supporting a 1GHz Signal Bandwidth with <-40dB EVM Floor and >55dB Dynamic Range in 28nm CMOS
authors: Yicheng Li, Feng Xie, Yun Yin, Lixuan Cao, Tianze Yang, Jiaxiang Li, Hongtao Xu
abstract: A 6GHz quadrature DTX supporting a 1 GHz signal bandwidth is presented in 28 nm CMOS, where static and dynamic nonlinearities are carefully optimized. Occupying a core area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.67 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> and packaged in a fanout SiP, the DTX delivers 23.1 dBm peak output power with a 30.3% peak PAE at 6.0 GHz, and the 1 dB RF bandwidth is from 5.7 to 6.5 GHz. For 1 GHz 64-QAM signals, it achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18.0 \text{dBm} \mathrm{P}_{\text{avg }}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.2 \% \text{PAE}_{\text{avg }}$</tex> with −25.5 dB EVM. The dynamic power range is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>55 \text{dB}$</tex>, and its EVM floor is −40.8 dB.

title:
Filament Conductivity Modelling of Atomistic Au/MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /Au Memristors
authors: Mohit Tewari, Ashutosh Krishna Amaram, Tarun Kumar Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Conductivity modelling of atomic-scale memristors require ab-initio Quantum Transport (QT) framework that poses significant computational limitations to understand the experiments. In this work, we present an analytical conductivity model for the filament in Au/MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Au memristors with inputs from Reactive Molecular Dynamics (MD) and Quantum Transport (QT). The conductivity model combines the conductivity contributions from filament and disordered MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> around the filament. The resulting current-density (J-V) characteristics show a good agreement with the direct QT results passed on Reactive MD atomic structures. We then employ the developed model to study the effect of higher voltage ramp rates on J-V characteristics, where increased switching voltages and thicker filaments are observed. These results demonstrate the strong correlation between filament structure and conductivity, showing the model’s capability in bridging atomistic dynamics and device-level behavior.

title: 9.4 A Multi-Coil Scalable Energy-Shared Wireless Power Receiver Network for Distributed Time-Division-Multiplexing Somatosensory Cortex Stimulation
authors: Kai Cui, Yan Lu
abstract: This work presents a system-level scalable wireless power receiver (RX) network for miniaturized distributed somatosensory cortex stimulation. By interconnecting the power outputs of all RX cells in the network, the stimulator in each cell can access the total received power of the whole network, with a time-division-multiplexing stimulation manner. Thus, each cell owns a multiple-input single-output wireless power RX network without additional RX coils, with improved efficiency and robustness.

title: Learning from Literature: A Retraining-Free Framework for LLM Jailbreak Defense via NLP-Based Adversarial Literature Analysis
authors: Sheikh Samit Muhaimin, Spyridon Mastorakis
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Recent surge in adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) have made them vulnerable to sophisticated adversarial attacks, manipulative prompts, and encoded malicious inputs. Existing countermeasures often require retraining models, a process that is computationally expensive and impractical for deployment. This paper introduces a novel defense framework that enables LLMs to autonomously detect, filter, and fend off malicious or adversarial inputs without requiring retraining or fine-tuning. The proposed framework consists of two core components: (1) A summarization module that processes and summarizes adversarial research literature to provide the LLM with context-aware defense knowledge and (2) A prompt filtering module that detects, decodes, and classifies harmful inputs through advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques, such as zero-shot classification, keyword analysis, and encoded content detection. By combining text extraction, summarization, and malicious prompt analysis, this approach enhances the robustness of LLMs against adversarial misuse. Experimental results demonstrate that this integrated approach successfully identifies encoded prompts, malicious patterns, and manipulative language structures with a success rate of 98.7%. The framework also enables the model to respond appropriately to malicious prompts with a higher percentage of jailbreak resistance and refusal rate, using minimal quantity of adversarial research literature as context. The framework significantly reduces LLM Attack Success Rate (ASR) as a lightweight, efficient alternative to retraining-based time and resource-consuming defenses, while upholding LLM response quality.

title: NIPIN Diode with Designable Asymmetry for Low Voltage and Low Capacitance System Level ESD Protection
authors: Navin Maheshwari, Krish Patel, Kshitij Agarwal, Hasan Ali, Ritesh Kumar, Sandip Lashkare
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Scaling of CMOS technologies below 20nm and supply voltages under 1V intensifies the need of low voltage ESD protection. Conventional ESD diodes requires higher breakdown voltage (>>1V) to trigger Avalanche or Zener tunneling mechanism. Recently, symmetric NIPIN structure was shown having low breakdown voltage utilizing the sub-bandgap impact ionization for bi-directional ESD protection. Here, NIPIN strcuture with designbale asymmetry is demonstrated for low voltage unidirectional ESD protection. Further, a reduction in capacitance is demonstrated which is needed for high speed interfaces. The designed NIPIN diode achieves ∼25% less capacitance than symmetric diode with V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BD</inf>∼0.66V. The design provides an efficient, scalable solution for protecting high-speed, low-voltage I/O interfaces for system level ESD protection.

title: VulnDetective: Using LLM Agents to Analyze Common Weaknesses and Identify Smart Contract Vulnerabilities
authors: Thanmai Mandala, Cora Zeger, Tessa E. Andersen, Gaby G. Dagher, Jun Zhuang
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Blockchain technology is a promising innovation to store information online while being secure and transparent at the same time. Despite the benefits, the immutable nature of blockchain makes it prone to vulnerabilities as any smart contract uploaded onto the blockchain cannot be modified later. Thus, it is imperative to design secure smart contracts during development stages and incorporate effective vulnerability detection mechanisms. Large-Language-Models (LLMs) are a propitious approach to automate vulnerability detection. However, recent attempts at using LLMs to detect vulnerabilities have resulted in high false positive rates and seem prone to obsolescence due to evolving Solidity code. Moreover, existing research often conflate weaknesses with vulnerabilities, despite their apparent differences, and all prior works only focus on vulnerability detection based on broad categories rather than their exact vulnerability identifiers (VIDs). To address these gaps, we propose a novel framework, VulnDetective, of fine-tuned LLM agents and a CWE-VID database to map weaknesses to their corresponding vulnerability, thereby increasing detection accuracy. Our findings show that pre-trained base models struggle significantly with detecting exact vulnerabilities, while VulnDetective shows significant improvement over base models.

title: OnePV: A Novel One-Stage Palm Vein Recognition Method Based on Oriented Object Detection
authors: Haoheng Lin, Runzhang Chen, Dacan Luo, Wenxiong Kang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: In the conventional two-stage palm vein (PV) recognition the feature extraction is followed by region of interest (ROI) extraction, thus the recognition performance heavily depends on the robustness of the ROI extraction method, and the feature extractor lacks angular modeling capabilities. To address this, the relationship between oriented object detection and palm vein recognition is extensively studied in this paper. We propose a rotation-aware, one-stage PV recognition algorithm, where the rotated bounding box (RBB) of ROI is introduced as supervision information to guide the feature extractor and we annotate the ROI RBB of five public PV datasets. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that applies oriented object detection to one-stage PV recognition. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method can achieve one-stage, high-accuracy PV recognition system and get remarkable results on five annotated datasets.

title: An OCR-Driven Filtering Approach for Improving VIC System Efficiency
authors: Rohini Jadhav, Rajesh Prasad, Aditya Kashyap, Milind Gayakwad, Vinay Mali, Sanmati Jain
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Optical Character Recognition, also known as OCR, converts scanned documents like printed, handwritten, typed text to text readable by machines, enhancing accessibility and streamlining administrative tasks. Despite its potential, traditional OCR systems have limitations in terms of accuracy and efficiency. Real intake often contains no-text images and low-DPI/glared uploads. Standard OCR incurs request overhead and layout/detection time even to return "no text". To improve the Veritas Information Classifier (VIC), this paper presents a Selective Image Extraction and Verification Engine, also known as SIEVE, a deep learning-based OCR filter. SIEVE adds a fast pre-OCR gate to block such cases before OCR is invoked, reducing compute/cost at batch scale while leaving the OCR engine unchanged. To make SIEVE more efficient, it employs color and gradient analysis to perform pre-processing of the data, along with a convolutional neural network to classify the presence of text and optimize resource utilization. SIEVE, a deep learning-based OCR filter for VIC, has 45% less processing time, 35% less CPU consumption during peak processing, and a 98% accuracy rate in detecting imagecontaining text. This model facilitates the handling of large-scale image data. Thorough testing confirms SIEVE's resilience in a variety of situations, ensuring efficient and accurate OCR processing.

title: Session 18 Overview: Technology and Circuits for Domain-Specific Accelerators
authors: N/A

title:
Self-powered Ultrabroadband Photodetection in MoO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -MoSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    heterostructure
authors: Dipanjana Mondal, Vinayak B. Kamble
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: An ultra-broadband photodetector based on a vertically stacked MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-MoSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> heterostructure synthesized by a single-step CVD process is reported. The device exhibits strong photoresponse from deep-UV (280 nm) to near-IR (850 nm). Under 100 mV bias, the detector achieves significant photocurrent across this range, and it remains active even at zero bias, owing to the built-in electric field at the MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-MoSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> metal-semiconductor interface. Material characterizations like Raman, photoluminescence, atomic force microscopy and scanning electron microscopy confirms a few-layer MoSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> formation on top of the metallic MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, establishing the metal–semiconductor stack heterojunction. In the best case, peak responsivity occurs in the near-IR regime, with an External Quantum Efficiency consistent with the response. The self-powered operation yields a finite photocurrent under illumination, indicating photovoltaic action. Further, the photodetection mechanism is discussed with the help of an energy-band diagram of the MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-MoSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> junction, explaining carrier separation and the spectral trends. These results suggest that MoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-MoSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> heterostructures are promising for scalable, self-bias and low-bias broadband photodetectors.

title: Effect of Silicon Anisotropy and Wafer Orientation on Extensional Modes
authors: Manish Kumar, Nivedita Rai, Amitava DasGupta, Deleep R. Nair, Abhijit Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This paper examines the impact of crystal anisotropy and wafer orientation on the extensional modes of silicon structures. The mode analysis of a cuboidal block was simulated in COMSOL using three material models: isotropic silicon, anisotropic silicon in the cubic crystal frame, and anisotropic silicon aligned with the wafer orientation. Two different conditions, namely idealized (wherein only the longitudinal displacements are allowed) and real (wherein no such constraints are imposed), were studied. Results show that isotropic modeling consistently overestimates resonance frequencies, while the anisotropic model yields lower values. The discrepancy becomes more pronounced at higher-order modes. Mode shapes are compared using the Modal Assurance Criterion (MAC). This indicates that the wafer-oriented anisotropy model achieves near-perfect correlation with the idealized mode shape, whereas isotropic and cubic anisotropy models show only moderate agreement. These findings demonstrate that accurate treatment of anisotropy in the wafer reference frame is essential for reliable finite-element simulations. Incorporating this detail improves frequency prediction and mode-shape fidelity, which are critical for the design and optimization of MEMS and resonant devices.

title: Research on Intelligent Management and Control Technology of Electrical Main Equipment Installation Oriented to Accident Prevention and Stability Enhancement
authors: Kewei Wu, Xiao Tian, Li Chen, Guansheng Yao, Wei Wu, Yazhou Cao
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Under the carbon peaking and carbon neutrality goals, the new electricity system is developing rapidly. High-proportion grid connection of new energy imposes higher requirements on the installation of electrical main equipment. This study innovatively proposes an integrated solution featuring quantitative modeling, standard management, data-driven and intelligent empowerment. It constructs a human-machine-environment-management coupled risk model and a full-process quantitative management list. By integrating digital twin and blockchain traceability technologies, the solution achieves precise prevention of installation accidents and improvement of operation stability. Engineering verification shows that the coincidence rate of cause analysis of this system is generally higher than 85%, the installation accident rate is reduced by 68%, and the adaptability to new energy is significantly improved. It provides solid support for the green and safe development of power grids.

title: Enhancing Fall Detection Accuracy with Bagging Ensembles on Kalman-Filtered Smartphone Accelerometer Signals.
authors: Madikana Sydney Sediela, Noluntu Mpekoa, Sheethal Tom
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Falls remain a serious health concern that can result in severe injuries, loss of independence, and diminished quality of life, especially in older people. Prompt and correct fall detection is crucial to enable timely intervention and potentially prevent adverse consequences. For effective fall detection and reduced false alerts, this study proposes the use of Bagging, an ensemble learning technique. Raw accelerometer measurements from the UMAFall datasets, recorded from a smartphone during daily activities and simulated fall activities, are initially smoothed using Kalman filtering to minimize sensor noise and enhance signal reliability. The filtered signals are subsequently partitioned into bootstrap subsets and classified using a Bagging ensemble with Random Forest, Multi-Layer Perceptron(MLP), and k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) models. The proposed model is evaluated against individual models using detection accuracy, precision, and recall as benchmarks. The results show that Kalman filtering improved kNN accuracy from 73.87 % to 94.73 %, MLP from 68.03 % to 73.99 %, and Decision Trees from 66.16 % to 89.86 %. In a Bagging ensemble configuration, the MLP improved to 75.65 %, kNN maintained a stable performance at 94.58 %, and the Decision Trees aggregated as Random Forest was the bestperforming model with an accuracy of 95.47 %. Although the dataset only includes accelerometer readings and simulated falls from participants aged 18 to 55 years, the results highlight the potential of ensemble-based learning in promoting reliable and efficient fall detection solutions for elderly care and intelligent health monitoring systems.

title: An Inverted-V Channel P-type NsFET for Improved Performance per Device Footprint
authors: Siddharth Mohanty, Nihar Ranjan Mohapatra
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: In this work, a novel p-type Nanosheet FET, featuring an unconventional inverted-V channel is presented. The unique configuration not only increases effective device width per footprint area but also exposes the (111) planes as major surfaces. The (111) planes with higher out-of-plane effective mass improve effective hole mobility and device performance. Through detailed device simulation, it is shown that the proposed p-type NsFET offers sheet thickness independent effective hole mobility (thus enabling sheet thickness scaling without performance degradation) and ~5X device performance at same device footprint.

title: 20-H Rule of Power Ground Plane of Printed Circuit Board and Clustering of Electric Near-Field Distribution
authors: Shigeo Nara
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: In this study, we classified the electric near-field distributions of the power supply ground plane of the printed circuit board using AI clustering and verified the classification results for problematic feature patterns. We improved the learning method and confirmed that the training and testing results using AI-based machine learning were satisfactory. In addition, as a novel method, we confirmed that machine learning could classify problematic electric field distribution patterns with high accuracy by augmenting the dataset of electric field distributions around problematic frequencies and converting the data into grayscale.

title: Research on Residential Air Conditioning Load Regulation and Subsidy Mechanism Based on Power Supply Security
authors: Yiwen Chen, Rui Miao, Hongtao Gao, Yangzhi Chen, Ruizhi Li, Zhiyong Xu, Hongsheng Tian
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: To expedite the development of a new power system, ensure the equilibrium of power and electricity within the system, and stimulate user demand response, this study investigates air conditioning load regulation and its associated subsidy mechanism grounded in power supply assurance. Through an analysis of the current electricity pricing structure and the clustering of air conditioning loads, we conceptualize demand response and collaborative regulation forms alongside their subsidies. A mathematical model for subsidy calculation was developed, and a case analysis of peak shaving subsidies was performed based on various scenarios. Furthermore, we explore a subsidy mechanism for the collaborative regulation of air conditioning loads that is predicated on power supply assurance.

title: Clone What You Can't Steal: Black-Box LLM Replication via Logit Leakage and Distillation
authors: Kanchon Gharami, Hansaka Aluvihare, Shafika Showkat Moni, Berker Peköz
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in mission-critical systems, facilitating tasks such as satellite operations, command-and-control, military decision support, and cyber defense. Many of these systems are accessed through application programming interfaces (APIs). When such APIs lack robust access controls, they can expose full or top <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> logits, creating a significant and often overlooked attack surface. Prior art has mainly focused on reconstructing the output projection layer or distilling surface-level behaviors. However, regenerating a black-box model under tight query constraints remains underexplored. We address that gap by introducing a constrained replication pipeline that transforms partial logit leakage into a functional deployable substitute model clone. Our two-stage approach (i) reconstructs the output projection matrix by collecting top-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> logits from under 10k black-box queries via singular value decomposition (SVD) over the logits, then (ii) distills the remaining architecture into compact student models with varying transformer depths, trained on an open source dataset. A 6-layer student recreates 97.6% of the 6layer teacher model's hidden-state geometry, with only a 7.31 % perplexity increase, and a 7.58 Negative Log-Likelihood (NLL). A 4-layer variant achieves 17.1% faster inference and 18.1% parameter reduction with comparable performance. The entire attack completes in under 24 graphics processing unit (GPU) hours and avoids triggering API rate-limit defenses. These results demonstrate how quickly a cost-limited adversary can clone an LLM, underscoring the urgent need for hardened inference APIs and secure on-premise defense deployments.

title: Convergence of Operational Technology/Industrial Control Systems/Internet of Medical Things: Internet-Exposed Medical Device Threats
authors: J. Malakai Bailey, William Yurcik, O. Sami Saydjari, Rodolfo da Silva Avelino, João Luisi Vieira, Pedro Umbelino, Gregory Pluta
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Convergence in healthcare is integrating information technology (IT) systems with operational technology (OT) systems to improve patient care and operational efficiency. This integration allows for real-time data exchange between medical devices, enhances data analysis for better patient outcomes, and streamlines workflows for healthcare professionals. While the increased networked connectivity of medical devices offers numerous benefits, it also introduces significant cybersecurity threats. We survey and characterize medical devices and then perform a small number of empirical experiments to investigate advertised commercial claims about exposed medical devices unprotected on the Internet. In the first non-commercial, peer-reviewed research paper on this important topic, we validate previous claims and state clearly that a significant problem exists. We conclude that the unique new threats created by IT/OT convergence represent threats to healthcare as a national infrastructure that cannot be solved solely by established IT cybersecurity best practice techniques.

title: Online Measurement of Active Frequency Support Capability for Wind-Storage combined System Based on Data-Model Hybrid Driving
authors: Penghan Li, Chao Huo, Huimei Sun, Ying Wang, Xiaowei Ma, Dan Sun, Heng Nian
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: The high integration of renewable energy poses a central challenge to frequency stability of power system. As a promising solution, wind-storage combined system requires accurate quantitative assessment of the active frequency support capability. This paper proposes a hybrid data-model-driven method based on the Koopman operator for online evaluation of the active frequency support capability. Firstly, the complex nonlinear wind-storage combined system is transformed into a lifted linear representation using Koopman operator theory. Secondly, by embedding the physical constraints of wind-storage frequency regulation into the Koopman lifting functions, a linear prediction model that ensures both data adaptability and physical consistency is constructed. Afterwards, based on historical data, the system's frequency response characteristics are predicted, enabling real-time online assessment of the active frequency support capability. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high predictive accuracy and physical reliability.

title: Examining The CoVCues Dataset: Supporting COVID Infodemic Research Through a Novel User Assessment Study
authors: Shreetika Poudel, Ankur Chattopadhyay
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: The public confidence and trust in online healthcare information have been greatly dented following the COVID-19 pandemic, which triggered a significant rise in online health misinformation. The World Health Organization (WHO) has called this problem the COVID infodemic. Existing literature shows that different datasets have been created to aid with detecting false information associated with this COVID infodemic. However, most of these datasets contain mostly unimodal data, which comprise primarily textual cues, and not visual cues, like images, infographics, and other graphic data components. Prior works point to the fact that there are only a handful of multimodal datasets that support COVID misinformation identification, and they lack an organized, processed and analyzed repository of visual cues. The novel CoVCues dataset, which represents a varied set of image artifacts, addresses this gap and advocates for the use of visual cues towards detecting online health misinformation. CoVCues is a uniquely exclusive collection of images for which we make use of categorization and sub-categorization of images from publicly available data. We also utilize AI models to show the capability of visual cues in enhancing misinformation detection. As part of validating the contents and utility of our CoVCues dataset, we have conducted a preliminary user assessment study, where different participants have been surveyed through a set of questionnaires to determine how effectively these dataset images contribute to the user perceived information reliability. The survey participants were shown sample images from this dataset and were asked to differentiate between reliable and unreliable content. These survey responses helped provide early insights into how different stakeholder groups interpret visual cues in the context of online health information and communication. The findings from this novel user assessment study offer valuable feedback for refining our CoVCues dataset and for supporting our claim that visual cues are underutilized but useful in combating the COVID infodemic. Overall, our CoVCues dataset paves the path towards a new image-centric approach to empower COVID misinformation detection and presents a valuable resource for both researchers and professionals in fighting against the COVID infodemic. To our knowledge, this user assessment research study, as described in this paper, is the first of its kind work, involving COVID visual cues, that demonstrates the important role that our CoVCues dataset can potentially play in aiding COVID infodemic related future research work.

title: EDL: Efficient Data-Oblivious Loops
authors: Biniyam Tiruye, Lauren Biernacki, Todd Austin
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Data-oblivious programs are essential solutions for maintaining privacy in computation, crucial amidst rising concerns regarding privacy breaches and data misuse. These programs safeguard sensitive information in today's data-driven landscape. However, their limited practicality due to massive performance overheads poses an obstacle to their widespread adoption. Resolving these inefficiencies not only ensures the viability of data oblivious techniques but also fosters trust in privacy-preserving technologies. Previous work showed that one of the contributors to these significant overheads is loop conversion techniques. Principles of data-oblivious programming demand that decision-making should not depend on sensitive data in order to maintain obliviousness. Due to this requirement, data-oblivious programs are stripped of any heuristics that could terminate loops early, thereby forcing loops to always execute the worst-case number of times. In response to these challenges, this paper introduces an innovative approach, termed Efficient Data Oblivious Loop (EDL), aimed at reducing the impact of loops in data-oblivious program performance while maintaining complete obliviousness. EDL presents a technique that lowers the trip count of these loops by leveraging information derived from safely profiling the algorithms. The algorithm profiling is used to determine the most common trip count of a particular loop across many non-data-oblivious executions of the program, this information is in turn used to determine a new upper bound for the data-oblivious version of the program. This ensures most executions of the algorithm will produce correct results even with the new lowered trip count. For the minority of executions that fail to produce correct results due to a higher trip count than the new trip count, EDL can detect the inputs and prompt a rerun of these executions with a higher trip count. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach, with EDL yielding an average speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0 \times$</tex>, and up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.5 \times$</tex>. While this approach does not work for all algorithms, it resulted in a significant enhancement in the efficiency of many data-oblivious programs. This paper describes this technique in detail and provides a guideline to identify programs that could benefit from this optimization.

title: Inverter Based Resource With a Long Feeder: Modeling and Experimental Validation
authors: Antonio C. S. Lima, Anny A. S. Santos, Maria Teresa Correia de Barros, Luís G. B. Rolim, Alberto H. Otero Llosa, José C. L. V. Silva
abstract: This work presents a comprehensive analysis of Inverter-Based Resource (IBR) performance with long feeders. It is a configuration that has been increasingly common in several power system applications, ranging from renewable interconnections such as offshore wind farms to oil exploitation pumping systems. This work shows that simplified modeling approaches yield significant inaccuracies in predicting system behavior. Analysis reveals that an ideal IBR representation coupled with detailed feeder modeling fails to predict responses at the receiving end accurately. Similarly, detailed IBR modeling with simplified feeder representation also produces inadequate results. In the latter, the feeder is treated as a single-phase equivalent to frequency-dependent and distributed characteristics. Accurate system response prediction requires simultaneous consideration of the actual IBR voltage waveform and detailed feeder representation equivalent to those used in wideband transient testing. This integrated modeling approach captures the complex interactions between harmonic-rich IBR outputs and the distributed impedance characteristics of long feeders. The experimental results were carried out at an operational oil company facility housing the long-feeder. The feeder is a 12/20 kV, 2.5 km umbilical cable wound on a drum. Due to space restrictions in the facility, only a low-voltage IBR was used. It is connected to the feeder without an output filter to create a harmonic-rich environment. All the simulations were carried out using the Numerical Laplace Transform.

title: Reprogramming Automatic Speech Recognition Models for Neonatal Chest Sound Separation
authors: Yang Yi Poh, Ethan Grooby, Kenneth Tan, Atul Malhotra, Mehrtash Harandi, Faezeh Marzbanrad
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Stethoscope-recorded chest sounds are invaluable for providing a non-invasive, real-time assessment of heart and lung sounds. However, noisy chest sounds can affect the dependability of various algorithms that rely on clean chest sounds, making additional preprocessing necessary to isolate the desired sources and remove noise, interference, and artifacts. This paper is the first to explore the reprogramming of automatic speech recognition (ASR) models to perform neonatal chest sound separation. In particular, we reprogrammed the Whisper ASR model to perform chest sound separation. We proposed two approaches: reprogramming just Whisper's audio encoder and reprogramming the full Whisper model. Using only simple linear layers and learnable parameters, we showed that this parameter-efficient method of reprogramming Whisper effectively separates heart and lung sounds from noise on the artificial dataset. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of using the proposed method as a preprocessing step for various heart and lung sound algorithms, yielding results comparable to state-of-the-art performance. Applying the pre-trained ASR model to perform sound separation demonstrates the feasibility of efficient cross-domain model reprogramming, demonstrating the feasibility of using frozen cross-domain foundational models from a different domain on biomedical data.

title: Avatar Standardization Efforts for Interoperable Metaverse Services: Towards a Seamless Avatar-As-a-Service Ecosystem_supp1-3668856.pdf
authors: Anthony Trioux
abstract: The metaverse has emerged as a transformative platform that blends physical and digital realities, supporting immersive communication, interaction, and service delivery. At the core of these experiences are avatars, digital embodiments of users, that serve as interactive interfaces across a wide range of metaverse services, including virtual collaboration, education, healthcare, entertainment, e-commerce, etc. However, the fragmented ecosystem of tools, platforms, and proprietary formats has led to significant challenges in avatar interoperability, preventing seamless user identity and experience across applications. In response, numerous international standardization bodies have launched initiatives to define interoperable and modular avatar frameworks.
