This survey represents the first comprehensive review of these initiatives with a focus on service-oriented requirements, revealing the concept of Avatar-as-a-Service (AaaS). It examines both historical foundations and recent advances, with a particular attention given to the emerging ISO/IEC 23090-39 standard (MPEG-I Part 39 Avatar Representation Format, ARF). By synthesizing technical approaches, standardization efforts, and open challenges, this work presents a roadmap for the evolution of AaaS and highlights emerging opportunities for delivering personalized, high-fidelity, and cross-compatible avatar experiences to support the next generation of metaverse services.

title: Thermodynamic Modeling and Kinetic Estimates of the Destruction of Polyol Ester Oils Contaminated With the Hydrofluorocarbon R-134a via Liquid-Injection Incineration Catalyzed by Nonthermal Gliding Arc Plasma
authors: Nicholas Stine, Jitlekha Tubsuri, Mobish Abraham Shaji, Alexander Rabinovich, Christopher M. Sales, Jinjie He, Gregory Fridman, Mira S. Olson, Alexander Fridman
abstract: Hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) can become contaminated with oil from refrigerator compressors during operation, which renders them ineffective. While HFCs can be reclaimed via distillation, oil contaminated with HFCs is produced as waste. This waste mixture must be destroyed due to its potential to release potent greenhouse gases (GHGs). Liquid-injection incineration (LII) is one method approved by United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for destroying HFC waste. When LII is operated far from thermodynamic equilibrium, the incineration of HFCs, toxic fluorophosgene by-products (COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and COHF) are generated. This study aims to analyze the products of HFC-contaminated polyol ester oil (POE) after incineration assisted by nonthermal gliding arc plasma. The thermodynamic equilibrium of HFC, POE, and air mixtures across different oxidation regimes was analyzed using HSC Chemistry 6.0. Kinetic assessments were made using modeling data to identify reactor limitations (i.e.,assuming a plugflow type reactor). We found that excess O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and supplemental H are needed to suppress fluorophosgene production during the destruction of HFC-POE mixtures. Experimental results were also obtained for the combustion of POE in a laboratory-scale gliding-arc system. As predicted by the thermodynamic model, the ratio of CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O concentration increased with the equivalence ratio during the experiments.

title: Near-Perfect Clustering Based on Recursive Binary Splitting Using Max-MMD
authors: Sourav Chakrabarty, Anirvan Chakraborty, Shyamal K. De
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: We develop novel clustering algorithms for functional data when the number of clusters <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> is unspecified and also when it is specified. These algorithms are developed based on the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) measure between the empirical distributions associated with two sets of observations. The algorithms recursively use a binary splitting strategy to partition the dataset into two subgroups such that they are maximally separated in terms of an appropriate weighted MMD measure. When <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> is unspecified, the proposed clustering algorithm has an additional step to check whether a group of observations obtained by the binary splitting technique consists of observations from a single population. We also learn <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> directly from the data using this algorithm. When <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> is specified, a modification of the previous algorithm is proposed which consists of an additional step of merging subgroups which are similar in terms of the weighted MMD distance. The theoretical properties of the proposed algorithms are investigated in an oracle scenario that requires the knowledge of the empirical distributions of the observations from different populations involved. In this setting, we prove that the algorithm proposed when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> is unspecified achieves perfect clustering while the algorithm proposed when <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math></inline-formula> is specified has the perfect order preserving (POP) property. Extensive real and simulated data analyses using a variety of models having location difference as well as scale difference show near-perfect clustering performance of both the algorithms which improve upon the state-of-the-art clustering methods for functional data.

title: A Novel ICESat-2 Bathymetry Model Guided by Photon Density Profiles and Synergy with Sentinel-2 for Automated Seafloor Mapping Without In-Situ Data
authors: Xiaoming Qin, Ziyin Wu, Xiaowen Luo, Jiabiao Li, Peng Chen, Jihong Shang, Dineng Zhao, Jieqiong Zhou, Hongyang Wan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Nearshore shallow-water bathymetry is critical for coastal management, with important scientific and engineering value. Compared to traditional bathymetric approaches, Satellite-Derived Bathymetry (SDB) is more efficient and cost-effective. Especially, the Ice, Cloud and land Elevation Satellite 2 (ICESat-2) carries a photon-counting 532-nm laser capable of penetrating optically shallow waters for bathymetry without requiring in-situ measurements. In practical application, heterogeneous benthic substrates and variable water optical properties pose significant Out-Of-Distribution (OOD) challenges to SDB. However, current filtering or clustering models for ICESat-2 SDB, relying on constant configuration and narrow applicability ranges, are unadapted to complex geomorphological conditions. Here we propose the Photon Density Profile Guided Cross-Modality Fusion Point Cloud Segmentation (DECOMPOSE) model for ICESat-2 SDB mission. Unlike spatially limited conventional approaches, DECOMPOSE achieves comprehensive seafloor characterization by fusing global density profile distributions with microstructural point cloud details. It is resilient against OOD challenges and generalizable to a variety of environments. On geographically isolated testing dataset, DECOMPOSE outperformed baseline models, with a Mean Intersection-over-Union of 0.9559, 15.2% higher than the suboptimal comparative model. On real-world OOD dataset, the DECOMPOSE bathymetric accuracy was excellent, with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.4938 m, a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.2760 m, and a goodness-of-fit R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> exceeding 0.98. To further improve the usefulness of the proposed paradigm, we integrated DECOMPOSE with a multispectral imagery based SDB model to establish the Data-driven Active–Passive Fusion SDB (DAPF-SDB) model, designed to provide full-coverage underwater topography mapping without in-situ data. Quantitative and qualitative analyses demonstrated that, relative to the baseline models, DAPF-SDB achieves accurate and robust seafloor topography mapping under OOD real-world conditions, reducing RMSE and MAE by more than 16% and 14%, respectively. Code for DECOMPOSE and DAPF-SDB is available at https://github.com/qxm1995716/decompose.

title: ADRC-Based Voltage Regulation and Energy Dispatch in DC Microgrids for In-Situ Wave Energy Converters_supp1-3665977.docx
authors: Weixing Chen
abstract: The in-situ wave energy converter (WEC) transforms wave energy into electricity and can be integrated into unmanned marine systems for continuous power supply. Due to its compact structure, it generally lacks mechanical rectification, and the stochastic nature of wave excitation results in frequent voltage fluctuations and zero-crossing intervals, which hinder stable power utilization and efficient energy storage. To address these issues, a DC microgrid-based energy management scheme is proposed. A complete circuit topology is developed, integrating an in-situ WEC unit, an energy storage module, and a load, in which a bidirectional DC/DC converter regulates energy flow between the battery and the bus. Full-process modeling is conducted for both load and battery sides. An active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) strategy with an extended state observer (ESO) is implemented to stabilize load voltage and enable fast energy dispatch. Based on the power profile of a double-float in-situ WEC, the generator speed range is identified. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that, compared with dual-loop PI control, the proposed method extends charging duration. Under two typical operating conditions, it reduces the average voltage error by 28.45% and 45.11%, and the voltage standard deviation by 44.04% and 50.68%, respectively.

title: I See You: Using RF Signals to See Inside Manufacturing Processes
authors: Oscar R. Hernandez, Bethanie Williams, Candan Martin, Clifton Mulkey, Gabriela F. Ciocarlie
abstract: We present a passive anomaly-detection framework for computer numerical control milling systems that captures naturally radiated radio frequency (RF) emissions from stepper and spindle motors. Results show RF spectral imaging enables scalable, cyber-resilient, trustworthy anomaly detection for Industry 4.0 manufacturing systems.

title: KG-LLM: A Framework for Intrusion Detection in 6G Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Gaofeng Wang, Xinyu Hong, Xiaoming He, Yinqiu Liu, Yan Hong, Mingkai Chen, Yunzhe Jiang, Hadeel Alsolai, Shahid Mumtaz
abstract: With the deep integration of Sixth-generation (6G) networks and the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), security threats are becoming increasingly sophisticated. Recently, Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated unique potential in handling massive heterogeneous data and unknown attack patterns within 6G-enabled IIoT environments. However, challenges remain in terms of addressing fragmented information and complicated and high-dimensional spatio-temporal correlation in industrial contexts. To this end, this article proposes an intrusion detection framework that integrates Knowledge Graphs (KGs) with LLMs. We first systematically review the key technologies of 6G IIoT and LLMs, and analyze in depth the application of LLMs to intrusion detection in 6G IIoT as well as the associated challenges. The framework encodes domain knowledge into structured KGs and fuses them with LLMs, thereby enhancing the model’s capability to understand and reason about attack behaviors in complex industrial scenarios. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed KG-LLM achieves superior performance in intrusion detection, outperforming traditional machine learning models such as XGBoost, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The model particularly excels in detecting attacks, showing robust performance even under noisy conditions. Finally, we discuss promising future research directions.

title: Deformation Behavior of Clay Soil Foundation during Pile Driving
authors: Ran Sun, Shuanglong Li, Yang Zhang, Yanhong Zhou, Weili Wang, Pengzhan Man, Haoze Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: The intense soil compression generated by pile driving poses a severe threat to adjacent structures. The resulting lateral pressure can cause horizontal displacement or even overturning of nearby building foundations, retaining walls, or pipelines. The upward soil heave it induces can also damage surfaces, roads, and shallow foundation structures. After the dissipation of excess pore water pressure generated by soil compression, soil remolding settlement occurs, leading to uneven building settlement and cracking. However, due to the intense soil failure, degradation, and large deformation involved in the piling process, classical finite element models struggle to accurately simulate these phenomena due to mesh distortion. Therefore, this study employs the CEL finite element model to investigate the deformation behavior of clay foundations during pile driving, with particular emphasis on the effectiveness of boreholes in controlling soil deformation. The findings provide valuable insights and guidance for researching and managing soil deformation during pile driving processes.

title: MAGIC: Multimodal Adaptive Graph Interaction Network for Conversation
authors: Le Wang, Jiongxun Lin, Shengtao Liao, Teng Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Emotion recognition in conversations presents significant challenges due to the complex interplay of multimodal cues and dynamic contextual dependencies. While graph-based approaches have demonstrated promise in modeling conversational structure, existing methods often rely on static graph constructions with predefined relational weights, limiting their adaptability to evolving dialogue dynamics. To address these limitations, we propose MAGIC, a novel Multimodal Adaptive Graph Interaction Network that introduces dynamic attention mechanisms for conversational emotion recognition. Our framework constructs a unified multimodal graph where relational importance is autonomously learned through adaptive attention mechanisms, enabling context-aware integration of textual, acoustic, and visual modalities. The core innovation lies in its ability to dynamically recalibrate interaction weights based on conversational context, capturing both intra-modal and cross-modal dependencies throughout the dialogue flow. Comprehensive evaluations on IEMOCAP and MELD datasets demonstrate that MAGIC achieves competitive performance, outperforming several strong baselines. Furthermore, the learned attention weights provide inherent interpretability, offering insights into how different modalities and contextual factors influence emotion recognition. Our findings underscore the value of adaptive interaction mechanisms in conversational affect understanding and establish a new direction for dynamic multimodal fusion in affective computing.

title: 5.1 A Formation Flight Phased-Array Transceiver for Spatial Power Combining and Distributing Architectures in Direct-to-Device-Communication Satellite Constellations
authors: Keito Yuasa, Yuya Takahashi, Shunya Watanabe, Sena Kato, So Ema, Genma Hattori, Atsushiro Naka, Sumio Morioka, Takahiro Inagawa, Kentaro Murata, Naoki Honma, Jill Mayeda, Atsushi Shirane
abstract: Conventional satellites are costly and unreliable. We propose a pico-satellite formation as a virtual phased array to reduce costs and improve reliability. A low-power FDD transceiver wirelessly receives an LO signal from a gateway, eliminating power-hungry PLLs. Precise beamforming is achieved by calibrating path differences and using SRFA technology. This work enables future low-cost, highly reliable satellite communications.

title: Electromagnetic Field Exposure Assessment and Mitigation Strategies for Wireless Power Transfer Systems: A Review and Future Perspectives
authors: Akimasa Hirata, Teruo Onishi, Naoki Shinohara, Valerio De Santis, Yinliang Diao, Mauro Feliziani, Takashi Hikage, Junqing Lan, Francesca Maradei, Keishi Miwa, Alexander Prokop, Yujun Shin, Seungyoung Ahn
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) technologies are increasingly being applied in fields ranging from consumer electronics and electric vehicles to space-based energy systems and medical implants. While WPT offers contactless power delivery, it introduces electromagnetic field (EMF) exposures, necessitating careful assessment to address safety and public health concerns. Exposure guidelines developed by ICNIRP and IEEE define frequency-dependent limits based on internal quantities, such as electric field strength and specific absorption rate, intended to prevent tissue nerve stimulation < 100 kHz and heating > 100 kHz, respectively. Complementing these guidelines, assessment standards such as the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)/IEEE 63184 and IEC Technical Report 63377, provide practical procedures for evaluating the EMF exposure from WPT systems. This review provides a comparative overview of major WPT modalities, focusing on recent developments in computational dosimetry and standardized assessment techniques for the complex, non-uniform fields inherent in WPT environments. It also addresses electromagnetic interference with medical devices and exposure scenarios involving partial-body proximity and various postures. A notable observation across modalities is the considerable variability, often spanning an order of magnitude, in the allowable transfer power, depending on the field distribution and assessment approach. Remaining challenges include the lack of harmonized guidance for intermediate frequencies and localized exposure, underscoring the importance of further coordination in international standardization efforts. Addressing these issues is critical to ensuring the safe and widespread deployment of WPT technologies.

title: StableMorph: High-Quality Face Morph Generation with Stable Diffusion
authors: Wassim Kabbani, Kiran Raja, Raghavendra Ramachandra, Christoph Busch
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Face morphing attacks threaten the integrity of biometric identity systems by enabling multiple individuals to share a single identity. To develop and evaluate effective morphing attack detection (MAD) systems, we need access to high-quality, realistic morphed images that reflect the challenges posed in real-world scenarios. However, existing morph generation methods often produce images that are blurry, riddled with artifacts, or poorly constructed—making them easy to detect and not representative of the most dangerous attacks. In this work, we introduce StableMorph, a novel approach that generates highly realistic, artifact-free morphed face images using modern diffusion-based image synthesis. Unlike prior methods, StableMorph produces full-head images with sharp details, avoids common visual flaws, and offers unmatched control over visual attributes. Through extensive evaluation, we show that StableMorph images not only rival or exceed the quality of genuine face images, but also maintain a strong ability to fool face recognition systems—posing a greater challenge to existing MAD solutions and setting a new standard for morph quality in research and operational testing. StableMorph improves the evaluation of biometric security by creating more realistic and effective attacks and supports the development of more robust detection systems.

title: 4.4 A 2.1-to-3.7ppm/°C Bandgap Voltage Reference with a Current-Domain TC Compensation and ±0.06% Inaccuracy from −40°C to 125°C in 130nm CMOS
authors: Zhong Tang, Sining Pan, Xiaopeng Yu, Nick Nianxiong Tan, Zhangming Zhu
abstract: This paper presents a CMOS bandgap reference (BGR) with a current-domain high-order TC compensation by using a capacitively-biased-diode-based super-PTAT current bias. It achieves a TC of 2.1 to 7.1ppm/°C from −40 to 125°C after a batch trim, which is on par with state-of-the-art BGRs with a 1-point trim. After a 2-point trim, it achieves a TC that ranges from 2.1 to 3.7ppm/°C and an inaccuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.06 \%(3 \sigma)$</tex> from −40 to 125°C. Its simple architecture also results in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> higher energy efficiency.

title:
MADiC: A 3nm 7.4TOPS/mm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    , 17.4TOPS/W Generative Diffusion Accelerator Enabled by Hardware-Compiler Co-Optimization of Memory Hierarchy and Operator Parallelism
authors: Shih-Wei Hsieh, Yi-Syuan Chen, Ping-Yuan Tsai, Ming-Hung Lin, Chia-Yuan Cheng, Lien-Feng Hsu, Po-Hao Huang, Hung-Wei Chih, Po-Han Chiang, Chia-Ming Chang, Ming-Hsuan Chiang, Chia-Hung Yuan, Sheng-Po Kuo, Viswanath Uggu, Chun-Kun Chan, Ming-En David Shih, Yu-Cheng Tseng, Hsin-Ping Cheng, Stan Huang, Chia-Ping Chen, ShenKai Chang, Chih-Ming Wang, Po-Yu Yeh, Jett Liu, Yung-Chang Chang, Chi-Cheng Ju, Yucheun Kevin Jou
abstract: This work presents MADiC, a 3nm 0.338mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> diffusion accelerator for generative image editing on edge devices. MADiC features design-time L1/L2 memory optimization, cross-loop feature map allocation with write-over-read relaxation, and activation-aware input preloading with concurrent resizing. Operating at 0.575V and 546MHz, it delivers 7.4TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and 17.4TOPS/W when running the Diffusion ConvNet model.

title: Optimization of Data Collection in Facial Recognition Models through Subsampling Strategies
authors: Silvana Tayler, Marcelo Fiori, Javier Preciozzi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: This paper investigates subsampling strategies aimed at optimizing facial recognition datasets under ethical and resource constraints. Through 41 controlled experiments, we evaluate both image-level and class-level sampling methods on benchmark datasets (LFW, CFP-FP, AgeDB-30), using the ArcFace model as the performance baseline. Our findings show that for image-level sampling, k-means-based methods consistently outperform random and stratified approaches. However, at class level sampling, no specific subsampling strategy consistently outperforms the rest. We found that simple strategies can perform just as well as more complex methods-even those that rely on oracle-type information-meaning that there may be no need for elaborate data collection mechanisms.

title: Comparison of PM Machines with Different Winding Type for All-Electric Aircraft Propulsion
authors: Ke Lin, Congli Mei, Yan Zhu, Chuanhui Zhu, Chenyi Xu, Lanlan Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: The development of all-electric aircraft (AEA) imposes stringent requirements on propulsion motors, including high power density, high efficiency, and excellent thermal management. Existing motor designs often face challenges in balancing electromagnetic performance with manufacturability and cost, particularly when comparing traditional round-wire windings with emerging flat-wire (hairpin) technologies. Previous comparative studies have not systematically quantified the tradeoffs between these winding types under identical electromagnetic and geometric constraints for AEA applications. This paper addresses this gap by proposing and optimizing two radial flux permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) with identical stators, rotors, and magnets – one employing distributed roundwire windings and the other concentrated flat-wire windings. Computer-Aided Design (CAD) software, specifically MotorCAD, was employed to construct accurate surrogate models, enabling efficient optimization and systematic quantitative comparison. Our results demonstrate that the concentrated flatwire winding PMSM offers superior performance for AEA propulsion: it achieves a higher average torque (162.86 Nm vs. 160.11 Nm), significantly lower copper loss (1071 W vs. 1772 W), and a smoother back-electromotive force (back-EMF) waveform, albeit with a slight trade-off in torque ripple (5.26% vs. 4.52%) and total losses under the specific load condition tested. This work provides critical design insights and quantitative performance data for selecting winding technologies in highperformance AEA propulsion systems, contributing directly to the advancement of power-dense and efficient electric propulsion technologies.

title: An Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis Method for Six-Phase Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on q-Axis Fundamental Current Plane
authors: Pengyu Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Systems, Smart Grid, and Artificial Intelligence (PSGAI)
abstract: Multiphase permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are increasingly widely used in ship propulsion systems due to their high torque density, high efficiency, and high reliability. However, the increase in the number of motor phases raises the probability of faults. Among these, open-circuit faults in multiphase PMSMs are common occurrences. If not detected and isolated promptly, they can lead to degradation of system performance or even secondary faults, thereby affecting the safe and stable operation of the entire equipment. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes an open-circuit fault diagnosis strategy for six-phase permanent magnet synchronous motors based on the q-axis fundamental current plane. First, a sliding mode observer (SMO) based on the dq-axis fundamental plane is designed, employing a nonlinear saturation function to minimize system chattering. Second, an second-order generalized integrator (SOGI) is utilized to separate and extract the Alternating Current (AC) components of the q-axis current, and fault diagnosis is achieved by designing a threshold. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through simulation experiments of open-circuit faults in a six-phase PMSM.

title: A 26/28/37/39GHz Reconfigurable Fully Connected MIMO Receiver Front-End with On-Chip Diplexer Achieving 52-to-70dB Blocker Rejection
authors: Xuhao Jiang, Qin Chen, Ziang Zhang, Haiyang Xia, Shun Zhang, Yang Han, Xin Chen, Depeng Cheng, Long He, Xu Wu, Lianming Li, Xiaohu You
abstract: A 24.25-to-29.5 / 37-to-40GHz fully connected MIMO receiver (RX) front-end for 5G FR2 that enables 52-to-70 dB calibration-free inter-band blocker rejection and up to −5 dBm blocker tolerance is introduced. A compact on-chip diplexer and band-selective network are integrated in the RF chain to reject undesired signals before the mixer. The RX can support concurrent dual-band or multi-stream single-band operation, and achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.8 \text{Gb} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> 64-QAM non-contiguous inter-band carrier aggregation operation.

title: AI-Enabled Next-Gen Waste Segregation and Awareness System
authors: Rithika G, Swathi S, R.Sudarmani, N. Sathish Kumar, S. Elakkiya
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: In the wake of continuous population growth and urban expansion, we are seeing municipal solid waste production become a very serious issue for environmental sustainability. Global studies predict that by 2050, total waste output will surpass 34 billion tons which in turn is a call to action for the implementation of very efficient technology-based management solutions. We still largely rely on manual waste sorting, which is at best inefficient at worst very error prone, often what is put forward as recyclable material is in fact contaminated this research puts forth an intelligent automatic sorting and awareness framework which puts AI and IoT together. To classification and management of waste in a much better way the model uses an android platform, which in turn takes in waste images that it in turn analyzes via a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) which would be trained on both public and custom data sets which cover main waste types of biodegradable, recyclable, non-recyclable, hazardous and electronic waste. Also, when the waste material is identified then the servo motors which are controlled by an ESP8266 microcontroller will allow the waste material in their proper bin at the same time, the mobile app gives out voice alerts notifications and gives out eco tips to in turn educate the users on better waste disposal. The developed system attains over 95 accuracies, which in turn greatly reduces manual work and reduces what goes into the land fill also this approach supports smart city projects puts us toward the circular economy and is a base which would build upon with cloud-based analysis and adaptive models for wide scale municipal use. Future scalability includes cloud-based analytics for municipal waste tracking and expansion to six or more waste categories.

title: Cost-Effective Freight Management with IOT Integration for Railway Wagon Load Optimization
authors: I Aravindaguru, P Veeramani, C Mathan, M Reshika, J Dhivya Nethra, K P Dharshini
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: A few of the most common problems faced by railways during freight transportation include overloading, improper loading, and underutilization of cargo space. Besides, reduced safety, increased fuel consumption, and delays are some of the problems railway logistics operations might face. This work proposes an IoT-based real-time wagon load monitoring and smart cargo management system that is cost-effective and efficient. The system integrates a load cell, ultrasonic sensor, GPS module, and ESP8266 microcontroller for accurate measurement of weight distribution and location throughout transportation. The gathered information is transmitted to the Blynk Cloud, enabling remote monitoring, automatic alert systems, and informed decision-making by the operator. Overloading or underloading and improper placement can be detected to optimize wagon capacity and enhance operational safety. Experimental evaluation shows measurement accuracy of ±2 percentage, latency ranging from 120 to 150ms, and reliable wireless transmission under varying environmental conditions. Sensitivity analysis confirms minimal performance deviation under slight temperature or positional variations. The proposed IoT architecture offers scalability, energy efficiency, and improved reliability for railway freight logistics.

title: An Integrated Method for Transmission Tower Laser Point Cloud Segmentation Based on Bounding-Box Growth and Symmetry Principle
authors: Xie Wen-Ping, Mao Yi-Jie, Zhang Jing-Jing, Nie Ming, Yu Jiang-Shun, Liu Wei
abstract: To address challenges like vegetation removal and power line separation in traditional point cloud segmentation for transmission corridor data, this study proposes an integrated method for transmission tower laser point clouds. The method involves preprocessing with elevation-based segmentation and reorientation, a bounding-box growth algorithm to separate tower structures from conductors and ground vegetation, and symmetry-based post-processing for tower body point cloud denoising and completion. Case studies on Yangjiao and drum-shaped towers show the method eliminates non-structural interference, reduces noise by 69.2%, improves tower point cloud quality significantly, and provides an efficient, reliable solution for tower extraction in complex environments as well as a solid data foundation for subsequent 3D modeling and structural analysis.

title: Bandgap Engineered Tunnel Design to Overcome WL Pitch Scaling Challenges for 3D NAND Flash
authors: Hirokazu Ishigaki, Swarnadip Mukherjee, Zhixin Xu, Shuai Zhao, Arvind Soundarapandian, Yoshiaki Fukuzumi
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: The new proposal of SiON3 and Si–Si bond control is a groundbreaking technological innovation that breaks the limits of WL pitch scaling in 3D NAND flash technology. A novel SiON3 method suppresses shallow traps without increasing Equivalent Oxide Thickness (EOT), achieving Read Disturb (RD) improvement that was difficult to achieve with conventional oxidation technologies. Meanwhile, precise control of the Si–Si bond amount in the SiON2 layer enables improvement of Quick Charge Loss (QCL) without sacrificing erase voltage, opening a new path that breaks down the traditional trade-off barrier. Furthermore, we discovered that the O-rich composition of SiON1 improves data retention (DR). These synergistic advances deliver a significant improvement in Read Window Budget (RWB), extending WL pitch scaling limits and paving the way for reliable ultra-high-density 3D NAND architecture.

title: Effect of Quantum Wells in DC Characteristics of Stacked Gate Oxide AlGaN/GaN MOS-HEMT for High-Power Electronics
authors: Nitish Kumar, Padmakshya Kar, Niranjan Jyoti Borah, Trupti R. Lenka, G. Purnachandra Rao, Hieu P. T. Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: AlGaN/GaN high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) are promising candidates for next-generation high-power and high-frequency electronics due to their wide bandgap, high carrier mobility, and excellent thermal stability. However, conventional HEMTs with multiple quantum wells (MQWs) often face limitations such as excessive gate leakage, mobility degradation, and unstable threshold voltage, which restrict their reliability. In this research, we present a comparative study of AlGaN/GaN MOS-HEMTs employing a stacked gate dielectric (Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) with different numbers of quantum wells (1–3 QWs). Device simulations are carried out to evaluate transfer and output characteristics, transconductance, and threshold voltage variation. The results demonstrate that the stacked oxide significantly improves gate control and suppresses leakage, while quantum wells enhance current conduction through stronger carrier confinement. Among the studied structures, the three-QWs MOS-HEMT achieves the highest drain current and improved device stability. This work highlights the importance of dielectric engineering and QW optimization in advancing GaN-based power and RF technologies.

title: Comparative Analysis of FinFET-LDMOS Device Types for wireless RF communications and high voltage applications
authors: Amit Sarkar, Pulak Ghoshal, P. Srinivasan, Abhisek Dixit
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Reliability of FinFET-based LDMOS is characterized, merging FinFET scalability with LDMOS high-voltage handling for power RF and CMOS applications. Two key designs are compared: STI FinFET LDMOS and Extended Drain FinFET. Effect of Temperature on STI n-FinFET is also analyzed. It was found that with increasing temperature, device on-resistance R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> and interface traps increases, threshold voltage, mobility and drift velocity decreases, while BVDSS improves. Additionally, the role of gate bias (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf>) in modulating breakdown characteristics is explored, with negative V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf> shown to improve BVDSS voltage while positive V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf> degrades it.

title: Optimizing Free Form Parametric Sources via Source Mask Optimization for Advanced Memory Lithography
authors: Lal Krishna A. S, Raghavendra Botta, Vishal Metri, Evgeny Malankin
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: As next-generation memory technologies like high-bandwidth memory (HBM), 3D-stacked DRAM, and advanced logic circuits become more complex, conventional sources for lithography fall short both in terms of resolution and litho metrics. Conventional source profiles like simple dipole or annular illumination offer minimal scope in fine tuning, especially in printing increasingly intricate layout patterns demanded by modern memory chips. Source Mask Optimization (SMO) is one such Resolution Enhancement Technique (RET) and has evolved far beyond its original scope. SMO offers a robust platform to design various type of sources including parametric and free form (pixel-based) sources. In this work, we propose a practical framework for freeform parametric SMO (FFP-SMO) that targets the unique challenges of memory design for smaller nodes like 22 nm. FFP-SMO employs parameterized di(multi)poles and flexible illuminator shapes to directly control the diffraction patterns needed for efficient imaging.

title: Detecting Fraudulent Job Postings Using Bi-LSTM-Based Text Classification
authors: William Tichaona Vambe, Sibukele Gumbo, Tinashe Crispen Garidzira
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Online job scams are becoming increasingly common in South Africa, putting job seekers at risk of falling for deceiving recruitment posts. In this study, we explore how deep learning can help identify and filter out these fraudulent job postings. Using the Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining (CRISP-DM) methodology as a guide, we trained a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model on the text of job requirements to learn patterns associated with scam behaviour. To ensure fairness in training, the dataset was balanced by reducing the number of legitimate posts. The model was then evaluated on testing data and achieved an accuracy of 75.48%, with a test loss of 0.538. These results show that even without large, complex models, a well-designed Bi-LSTM can effectively detect online job scam posts. Our work highlights the potential of text-based machine learning to support safer job hunting, especially in digital spaces where fraud is hard to spot manually.

title: Research Progress on Molybdate-Based Electrode Materials for Supercapacitor Energy Storage Applications
authors: Yining Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Under the backdrop of global energy structure transformation and the "dual carbon" strategy, it is of utmost importance to develop efficient, safe and economical energy storage technologies. Supercapacitors, as a new type of electrochemical energy storage device, possess advantages such as high power density, fast charging and discharging speed, and long cycle life. They play an irreplaceable role in fields that require instantaneous high power output and frequent charging and discharging. However, the energy density of supercapacitors is relatively low, which becomes the main bottleneck for their large-scale application. The currently commercialized supercapacitors are mainly based on carbon materials such as activated carbon, and their energy density is typically only 5-10 Wh/kg, which is much lower than that of lithium-ion batteries (>150 Wh/kg).Therefore, enhancing the specific capacitance of electrode materials and expanding the operating voltage range of the device have become the key approaches to improving the energy density of supercapacitors. In recent years, transition metal compounds have been extensively studied as electrode materials for supercapacitors due to their abundant redox reactions, which offer high theoretical specific capacitance. Among them, transition metal molybdate (AMoO4, where A represents metals such as Ni, Co, Mn, Fe, Zn, etc.) has received particular attention as an emerging electrode material. Molybdate materials possess the characteristics of diverse structures, good conductivity and excellent cycling stability, and are regarded as potential high-quality electrode materials for enhancing the energy density and performance of supercapacitors. This article reviews the research progress of molybdate-based electrode materials in supercapacitor energy storage, focusing on the energy storage principle of supercapacitors, the performance advantages of molybdate materials, the main preparation methods and their advantages and disadvantages, and provides an outlook on future development trends.

title: (POSTER) Rule List Optimization Method via Dependency Removal
authors: Takashi Fuchino, Ken Tanaka, Kenji Mikawa, Takashi Harada
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: Packet classification is the function that eliminates or forwards arriving packets at a network device according to packet classification policies. Packet classification using rule lists is widely used because of its low memory requirements and ease of implementation. However, as the number of rules increases, the classification time increases, and the packet communication latency increases. In this paper, we propose a method that rewrites rules to eliminate the precedence constraint and places the rule with high matching frequency at a higher position. This method of expanding and restructuring rules, which makes rule replacement easier, can reduce latency quickly without disrupting network equipment operation. Computer experiments using ClassBench [1], a benchmark for packet classification algorithm, confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Optimal Design of Amorphous Alloy Dry-Type Transformer Based on INSGA-SA
authors: Daosheng Liu, Guoxuan Huang, Fuming Yu, Fei Xie
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: To address the challenges in current transformer optimization design, such as lengthy design cycles, low optimization efficiency, and difficulties in balancing overall cost and energy efficiency indicators, this paper proposes an Improved Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm with Simulated Annealing (INSGA-SA) for transformer multi-objective optimization. By enhancing the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) through adaptive crossover/mutation probabilities and hybridizing it with Simulated Annealing (SA), the proposed method effectively overcomes NSGA-II's tendencies toward premature convergence and weak local search capability. Using the SCBH15-315/10 amorphous alloy dry-type transformer as a case study, optimization targeting minimum total cost and minimum total loss was conducted, with comparative experiments performed using the original NSGA-II. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved algorithm successfully reduces both total cost and total loss while satisfying all transformer performance constraints. Furthermore, it achieves a Pareto front with superior distribution and coverage characteristics.

title: Experimental Hardware Emulation of Spiking Neural Networks Using Ultra-Low-Voltage 2D SnS Memristors
authors: Anshul Awasthi, Banala Rajesh, Surya Shankar Dan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work reports an experimental hardware demonstration of neuronal integrate-and-fire (I&F) behavior using a two-dimensional (2D) tin(II) sulfide (SnS) memristor, together with its circuit-level realization for spiking neural networks (SNNs). The fabricated Cu/SnS/Cu device exhibits an ultra-low switching voltage of 0.3 V with a high R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf> : R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> ratio of 103, enabled by a dual ionic conduction mechanism involving both cations and anions. Ultraviolet Photoelectron Spectroscopy (UPS) measurements were employed to extract the real-time band diagram of SnS, providing physical insight into the origin of the ultra-low switching threshold. Beyond material and electrical characterization, we demonstrate that the device inherently emulates somatic neuronal dynamics such as input accumulation, threshold firing, and self-reset under pulsed voltage stimuli. Building on this, we design the equivalent spike generator circuits, including an ultra-low-power variant that operates in the nA regime, directly linking device-level phenomena to practical neuromorphic hardware implementations. To extend the scope of deterministic device demonstrations, we introduce a generative AI framework based on Variational Auto-Encoders (VAE), trained on large experimental datasets of current–time spiking sequences. The VAE captures intrinsic device-to-device and cycle-to-cycle variability, reconstructs realistic spike trains with preserved biological-like stochasticity, and generates new firing patterns beyond the measured dataset. This hybrid hardware-AI approach offers a powerful methodology for modeling and predicting memristive neuron behavior under diverse conditions, while inherently reducing experimental noise and maintaining natural dynamics. By integrating 2D-material memristors, neuromorphic circuit realization, and AI-driven generative modeling, this work provides a holistic demonstration of SnS memristors as a versatile platform for scalable, low-power, and biologically realistic spiking neural networks, opening new directions for brain-inspired computing.

title: Enhancing the modulation bandwidth of III-Nitride blue LED by reducing barrier width
authors: Samadrita Das, Sudharsanan Srinivasan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: One of the most significant light sources is the light-emitting diode (LED), which has a long lifespan and low power consumption. Here, we have simulated the transient characteristics of GaN-based blue LED with varying barrier thickness in the active region, and the radiative lifetime limited bandwidth of the LEDs has been systematically studied. The barrier thickness is significantly reduced (from 10 to 5 nm), which simultaneously improves the modulation speed, optical power, and efficiency of the LEDs operating at ambient temperature. Compared with the conventional LED, the light output power and -3 dB bandwidth of our proposed device with 60 μm diameter and 5 nm barrier thickness increase. This innovative device structure is a possible solution for LED applications in illumination and visible light communication systems.

title: Session 28 Overview: Innovations from Outside the (ISSCC) Box
authors: N/A

title: Towards Robust Multi-Object Pose Estimation Under Sparse and Noisy Data in Robotic Systems
authors: Heoncheol Lee
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of estimating the poses of multiple objects under sparse and noisy data in robotics systems in unknown environments. Due to the data sparsity caused by limited data acquisition policies for saving resources and the data noises caused by environmental disturbance, the robustness of the conventional methods for the estimation degenerates. This paper proposes a robust multiobject pose estimation method to solve the problem, which can be applicable to not only ground environments but also space environments. First, a robot builds a map as a model its surrounding environments by simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM). Then, multiple objects are detected by a visual sensor with a deep learning-based algorithm. Finally, the poses of multiple objects are robustly estimated by probabilistic filters with range data and localize them in the built map. The performance of the proposed method was tested with a real robotic system with visual and range sensors. Experimental results showed that the proposed method can robustly estimate the poses of multiple objects under sparse and noisy data.

title: The Role of Perceived Social Identity in Human-AI Collaboration
authors: Jessica K. Barfield
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Research has shown that a number of factors may determine the quality and extent of collaboration occurring between humans and AI agents. To investigate factors which may influence the process of human-AI collaboration this position paper discusses: (i) how the perceived social identity of an AI agent may influence whether collaboration occurs between humans working with an AI agent, and (ii) whether trust formed during human-AI collaboration is dependent on the perceived social identity of the AI agent. The social identities of interest include the perceived gender, race, or ethnicity of an AI agent and whether these social identities influence the evaluation of the agent during human-AI interaction. In addition, two theoretical frameworks, Social Identity Theory and the Stereotype Content Model, are discussed within the context of achieving trust during the process of collaboration between human-AI partners.

title: A Quantitative Data Risk Assessment Model Based on Improved AHP
authors: Huajie Zhu, Zhenyan Liu, Xiaolin Zhao, Yuming Xiao, Ying Jiang, Jiaxin Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of big data technologies, data has emerged as a core factor of production; however, the associated risks of privacy leakage are becoming increasingly severe. Accurate and real-time assessment of data security risks is the prerequisite to implement effective privacy protection. Traditional data risk assessment methods, such as the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP), primarily rely on expert experience to construct judgment matrices. Consequently, these methods suffer from high subjectivity, low evaluation efficiency, and an inability to adapt to massive, dynamic data streams. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a dynamic data security risk assessment model based on an improved AHP driven by objective indicators. The improvements focus on three aspects: (1) establishing a six-dimensional evaluation system covering data sensitivity and distribution features; (2) replacing the subjective scoring at the alternative layer with quantitative mappings based on Tsallis entropy and logarithmic transformations; and (3) implementing a threshold-based absolute classification mechanism to supersede traditional relative ranking, thereby achieving automated decision-making. Furthermore, a Privacy Classification Protection (PCP) Platform is designed and implemented based on this model. Application verification on a realworld fitness dataset demonstrates that the model can accurately identify high-risk data features (e.g., abnormal distributions and high re-identification risks) and automatically output reasonable risk levels, significantly enhancing the efficiency and objectivity of data governance.

title: Low Phase Noise Millimeter-Wave Voltage Controlled Oscillators Based on Electromagnetic Bandgap Material Resonators
authors: Indra S. Ghosh, Enrico Lia, Horst Fischer, Stephen M. Hanham, Rüdiger Follmann
abstract: This paper introduces novel oscillators for direct frequency synthesis at millimeter-wave frequencies. The first approach targeting ultra-low phase noise adopts a reduced filling factor electromagnetic bandgap (EBG) resonator fabricated from high-resistivity silicon (HRS). This was combined with a SiGe-BiCMOS chip comprising a two-stage loop amplifier, coupler, electronic phase shifter and an output amplifier. Using a phased-locked loop (PLL) circuit incorporating a commercial sampling phase detector (SPD) and a low-noise reference source operating at 520 MHz, phase noise values of −81, −98 and −119 dBc/Hz were measured at offset frequencies of 0.1, 1 and 10 kHz, respectively, at a carrier frequency of 45.8 GHz. Additionally, we present approaches to improve the temperature drift of the oscillator by using EBG-resonators fabricated from temperature-stabilized microwave ceramics. The impact on tuning range and oscillator phase noise are addressed.

title: Predictive Neuro – Electrical Modelling for Epileptic Seizures using Machine and Deep Learning
authors: Sanagavarapu Sunitha, Umadevi Ramamoorthy
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Conference on Advances in Electrical and Power Engineering (ACEPE)
abstract: Epileptic seizure forecasting remains essential to allow for early clinical intervention and patient safety. This paper reports on the predictive neuro-electrical modeling framework that makes use of wavelet-based EEG feature extraction, hybrid machine- and deep-learning models, and adaptive control principles inspired by smart-grid fault regulation. Utilizing the BEED EEG dataset that consists of 8 000 recordings from 16 channels across four seizure classes, signals were pre-processed, decomposed by discrete wavelet transform and STL time-series analysis, and fed into a hybrid ensemble of Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and ANN models. The multiclass configuration yielded macro-AUC values as high as 0.999 with ANN and 0.998 with Random Forest, whereas the binary seizure-alarm stream reached AUC = 1.000 and F1-score = 0.99, thus confirming near-perfect discrimination between seizure and non-seizure states. The adaptive controller further suppressed transient false alarms through exponential smoothing and persistence logic, yielding a stable real-time alarm response. The results prove that the proposed framework presents a clinically reliable and computationally efficient approach to continuous EEG-based seizure prediction and intelligent neuro-electrical monitoring.

title: Ai-Driven Detection and Predictive Modeling of Invasive Species Using Deep Learning Models
authors: Antonitta Eileen Pious, Shridula CN, Ramamoorthy K, Sriridhanya B, Shridhan S
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Invasive species pose ecological challenges, requiring intensive monitoring. Tracking methods like trail cameras and human census exist, but predicting species behavior remains complex due to factors like spatial data, weather, and known behaviors. While inaccuracies exist, AIdriven modeling can analyze vast datasets faster and more consistently than humans, reducing workload and improving fieldwork efficiency and help track which fish species are invasive. A continuously evolving framework integrating taxonomy, behavior, and movement patterns will enhance predictions, making long-term surveying more effective.Using computer vision and interpreter models to detect fish species through attributes and predict their risk factor in the detected environment and guess movement patterns across local areas.

title: 3-ASKNet: Resolving Scale-Orientation Dilemma in Aerial Images with Selective Receptive Field Adaptation
authors: JiaLiang Long, Xinghui Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)
abstract: The detection of remote sensing targets in aerial imagery faces dual challenges: large-scale targets (e.g., ships, factories) require precise orientation modeling due to significant scale variations and severe geometric distortions, while small targets (e.g., vehicles, containers) suffer from texture scarcity and directional ambiguity. Traditional fixed kernel convolution algorithms struggle to meet these conflicting demands, resulting in suboptimal feature representation. To address this, we propose Adaptive Selective Kernel Convolution (ASKConv), a novel computational unit with a three-branch architecture enabling context-aware adaptive receptive fields. The framework comprises: (1) Three parallel branches extracting features at different scales: standard 3×3 convolution (small receptive field), expanded convolution (expansion factor=2, medium receptive field), and depth-separable convolution (expansion factor=3, large receptive field); (2) An attention-driven fusion mechanism through global average pooling→dense compression (32D)→Softmax-weighted channel aggregation, dynamically selecting optimal spatial context. After replacing the bottleneck with ResNeXt-50 embeddings, the final 3-ASKNet generates minimal overhead (17% parameter increase, 4.7G FLOPs consumption) while integrating target alignment technology for directional detection. In the DOTA v1.0 benchmark (2,806 images, 188,000 instances), 3-ASKNet achieved an advanced average accuracy of 81.12%. Notably, it demonstrated significant improvements in both rotation-sensitive large objects (BD: 86.12% average accuracy, GTF: 83.49%) and texture-weak small targets (HC: 79.14%, SP: 80.18%).

title: A Multiband CPW-Fed Monopole Rectenna Using SMS7630 SC-79LF Schottky Diode for Powering Low-Power Wireless Sensor Networks and IoT Devices
authors: Akhil T V, Bhavya Kamal K Menon, Aswin Shiju, Ananthasankar U A, Vanilakshmi V, Thomaskutty Mathew
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This study introduces the development of a compact multiband CPW-fed monopole antenna integrated with a rectifier circuit utilizing the SMS7630 SC-79LF Schottky diode, intended for powering lowpower wireless sensor networks and IoT devices through ambient RF energy harvesting. The antenna, fabricated on an FR4 substrate with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$43.2 \times 35 \times 1.6 \text{mm}^{3}$</tex>, is designed to resonate efficiently at three frequencies: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5 \text{GHz}, 5.59 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 7.58 GHz, achieving return losses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-26.54 ~\text{dB},-20 ~\text{dB}$</tex>, and -15 dB, respectively. Measured gains at these frequencies are 2.32 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{dBi}, 3.7 \text{dBi}$</tex>, and 4.84 dBi. The antenna is connected to the rectifier through an impedance-matching network to maximize power transfer efficiency. The rectified and filtered DC output voltages measured at two Wi-Fi frequency bands were 51 mV and 88.9 mV. This result confirms the capability of the proposed rectenna system to harvest ambient RF energy effectively. It emphasizes its potential in self-sustaining, low-power wireless sensor networks and IoT devices.

title: A Path-Subgraph Enhanced Attention Network for Fault Diagnosis of Power Communication Equipment
authors: Jilong Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of power communication networks, fault diagnosis faces challenges such as complex causal chain propagation, insufficient long-range dependency capture, and shallow feature interaction. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Path-Subgraph Enhanced Attention Network (PS-Enhanced HAM) based on the original Path-Subgraph Hierarchical Attention Model (PS-HAM). Three key improvements are integrated: replacing BiLSTM with a Transformer Encoder to strengthen path sequence representation; adopting GATv2 combined with multi-scale subgraph sampling to enrich topological feature extraction; and designing a fusion module with cross-attention and contrastive learning to deepen feature interaction. Experiments on a real-world power communication equipment fault knowledge graph demonstrate that the proposed model achieves $\mathbf{5 3. 2 \%}$ MRR, 45.8% Hit@1, 52.7% Hit@3, and 56.3% Hit@10, outperforming mainstream baseline models and the original PS-HAM. The results verify that the model effectively improves the accuracy, generalization, and interpretability of fault diagnosis.

title: Optimized Solar Energy Utilization for Continuous Power Supply Using Dual-Battery System
authors: Gerald Christopher Raj Irudayaraj, R. Senthil Kumar, K.P. Suresh, J. Velmurugan
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Solar energy powers important ecological processes such as the water cycle and photosynthesis. Despite humanity's long-term efforts, just a small portion of this plentiful energy is harnessed. In this proposed work, solar cells are put in permanent, open places to capture sun energy. The proposed work attempts to offer an uninterrupted power supply to loads by utilizing two battery sources: a primary battery and a backup battery. The mechanism compares the batteries' voltage levels, loading the higher voltage battery and charging the lower voltage one. Both EB (Electricity Board) supply and solar power are used, with solar energy being stored in the battery during the day. The DC load receives power from either the EB supply or the solar power source, ensuring uninterrupted power to DC loads. If EB is failed it can source from solar power and next sources of solar charges a battery.

title: Data-Driven Machine Learning and Visualization for Solar PV System Fault Detection and Power Forecasting
authors: Gagana S, Geetha A, Chandini P, Harshavardhan K, Nithya Shree A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: With the continuous increase in the relevance of solar energy in the world renewable energy portfolio, the concern of ensuring that solar photovoltaic (PV) systems remain dependable and effective has acquired a paramount importance. The paper implements the use of sophisticated data analytics and visualization tools to examine the trends in the generation of renewable energy and find out the origin of the faults in the system. Two sets of primary data, namely, weather sensor data (ambient temperature, module temperature, irradiation) and inverter generation data (DC power, AC power, daily yield, total yield), are the basis of the analysis. After thorough preprocessing, a new labeled fault column was introduced to help to conduct supervised classification and support fault detection. All kinds of visual tools were introduced to explore the relationships between features and the results of their performance, including correlation heatmaps, violin plots, confusion matrices, sunburst charts, etc. The final classification model achieved a high accuracy of 99.26%, and the values of precision, recall, and F1, scores, the final classification model was well balanced between normal and faulty classes. These results indicate that information-motivated solutions can be the path toward a more intelligent solar PV management, increased energy generation control, and better system diagnosis.

title: Simulation of Dual-band Sequential Load Modulated Balanced Power Amplifier Using Reciprocal Gate Bias with Extended Dynamic Power Range
authors: Yujie Han, Jingzhou Pang, Ruibin Gao, Shuang Liu, Yi Zhang, Yinting Liu, Ke Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This work presents the design and simulation of a dual-mode sequential load modulated balanced power amplifier (DM-SLMBA) over a wide bandwidth. By substituting the control power amplifier at the isolation port of the SLMBA with a dual-mode Doherty power amplifier (DM-DPA) and integrating it with a balanced power amplifier (BPA), the proposed DMSLMBA achieves an extended dynamic power range and supports two distinct operational bands. Through proper design of the impedance transformation network between the dual-mode DPA and the isolated port, along with the matching network between the BPA and the coupler, active load modulation of the DMSLMBA can be achieved. This active load modulation includes the load modulation process inherent in the dual-mode DPA and expands the high efficiency range. It is demonstrated in this work that the proposed DM-SLMBA can simultaneously achieve a wider operating bandwidth, higher efficiency and larger dynamic range in two operating frequency bands. The proposed DM-SLMBA exhibits favorable performance and achieves dualband Doherty operation, specifically $1.2-2.0 \mathrm{GHz}$ in Mode I and $\mathbf{0. 6 5 - 1. 0 5 ~ G H z}$ in Mode II. The simulation results show that the saturation drain efficiency (DE) achieves levels of $40.7 \% -63.5 \% $ in Mode I and 52.5% $\mathbf{- 5 8. 3 \% }$ in Mode II. And a $\mathbf{1 0 ~ d B}$ back-off DE achieves levels of $\mathbf{3 8. 2 \% } \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{4 9. 0 \% }$ in Mode I and $\mathbf{3 8. 0 \% } \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{5 0. 5 \% }$ in Mode II. Furthermore, the designed DM-SLMBA exhibits a saturated output power of 46.6 dBm in Mode I and 46.4 dBm in Mode II.

title: A Novel Flexible Resistive Based Pulse Wave Measurement Method
authors: Qian Zhang, Wen Jie Wang, Qi Zong, Jie Ni, Yong Wang, Yu Pang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: At present, most pulse rate measurement devices are outstanding in portability and popularity. However, the accuracy of the measurement is still insufficient, this paper proposes a flexible resistor-based radial artery pulse wave monitoring method. Firstly, the baseline drift of the pulse waveform signal obtained from the flexible resistor is eliminated by fourth-order polynomial fitting. Then, the bandpass filtering with a passband of $[0.45-5] \mathrm{Hz}$ and singular spectrum analysis algorithm are applied to decompose and reconstruct the signal, filtering out the high-frequency noise of the signal and retaining the main frequency components of the signal. Thirdly, the envelope entropy-correlation coefficient scanning process is carried out on the preprocessed signal, and the parameters k and $\alpha$ with the smallest difference between the envelope entropy and the average correlation coefficient are the optimal parameters. The robust variational mode decomposition algorithm is further proposed, and a series of intrinsic mode signals and an outlier signal are obtained. Finally, the comprehensive score is obtained through the energy proportion, energy concentration, and frequency matching factor of each modal component. Thus the pulse wave signal is selected. The experiments are carried out to calidate the effectiveness of the method and the average absolute error of the pulse rate measurement was 2.035 beats per minutes.

title: Performance Study of Ultraviolet Detector Based on Porous Electrode Structure of GaN Material
authors: Lijun He, Kang Ma, Liyan Wang, Liang She, Cheng Mi, Mi Yu, Qijun Huang, Yuhang Qin, Peixuan Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: In this study, a porous electrode structure UV detector was formed by improving a conventional non-porous GaN UV detector. The simulations were performed using pore depth as the control variable and then the number of pores as the variable. The dark current value of the UV detector with a porous electrode structure is reduced but the photocurrent is increased by two orders of magnitude compared to the conventional non-porous electrode structure. The results show that the responsivity of the conventional non-porous GaN UV detector is $0.014 \mathrm{~A} / \mathrm{W}$, while the responsivity of the UV detector with a porous electrode structure with a pore depth of $0.1 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ is as high as $4.63 \mathrm{~A} / \mathrm{W}$, which is a great improvement of the responsivity.

title: Research and Validation of Time-Interleaved ADC Mismatch Correction Algorithm
authors: Qiao Cui, Mingliang Zhang, Zhiyang Qin, U-Fat Chio, Ting Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper introduces a channel-correlation based all-digital background clock skew calibration algorithm designed to mitigate inter-channel clock skew in time interleaved analog-to-digital converters(TI-ADCs). Simulation results on a 16-channel, 5GS/s TI-SAR-ADC platform demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves excellent calibration performance for input frequencies below the Nyquist rate, effectively enhancing the overall effective number of bits (ENOB) to the average ENOB level of the individual sub channels. Compared with existing state-of-the-art techniques, the proposed method maintains comparable calibration accuracy while eliminating derivative filters and reducing the number of multipliers and moving-average filters by 25%, thereby significantly lowering hardware implementation complexity. This work provides an efficient real-time calibration solution for high-speed, high-precision data conversion systems and carries substantial theoretical and practical significance for advancing TI-ADC technology in next generation communication equipment and test instruments.

title: Vision-based flat landing field recognition for the autonomous landing of Micro Aerial Vehicle (MAV)
authors: S Akash, B. Anbarasu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: The use of image processing algorithm for the detection of a suitable landing zone on a road for the landing of a Micro Aerial Vehicle (MAV) has been done. The input is a visual feed from a MAV and the output suggests suitable landing sites for the MAV. The image processing algorithm is created using MATLAB software. Based on the output, this algorithm is analyzed and the values of Precision, Recall and F1 Scores are calculated and tabulated.

title: Defect Detection in PE Gas Pipeline Thermo-fusion Welding Based on Improved YOLOv7
authors: Yunxiang Tian, Xiaoming Zhang, Qiang Gao
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Defect detection in polyethylene (PE) gas pipeline hot melt welds is essential for energy transmission safety. Traditional manual X-ray inspection suffers from inefficiency and inconsistent detection of small defects, often resulting in high false negative rates and safety risks. To address these issues, this paper proposes an improved DM-YOLO model incorporating a Residual Dilated Variable Module (R-DVM), which enhances multi-scale feature extraction and improves recognition of small defects in complex weld backgrounds.Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a mean average precision (mAP) of 94.2% and an inference speed of 48.4 frames per second (FPS), exceeding the YOLOv7 benchmark (91.3% mAP, 26.5 FPS) by 2.9% in accuracy and 21.9 FPS in speed. The model also demonstrates strong robustness under varying conditions, confirming its suitability for real-time industrial use. Overall, this work provides an accurate, efficient, and generalizable solution for automated weld quality inspection in PE pipelines, contributing to safer energy infrastructure.

title: Optimization of CNTFET Ternary Multiplier for Digital Signal Processors
authors: Sindhuja K, Balakumaresan R, Balaji S
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This article presents a Forced Stack Technique (FST) based CNTFET Ternary Multiplier designed for high speed and energy efficient arithmetic computation. By combining the superior electrical properties of CNTFET with the Forced Stack Technique which has leakage reduction capability, thus the design significantly reduces power consumption and power delay product. Unbalanced Ternary Logic is employed to minimize partial products and interconnect complexity. HSPICE Simulation results demonstrate 41-fold reduction in power and 17.36% improvements in power delay product (PDP) and efficiency compared to conventional ternary multipliers, making the proposed architecture highly suitable for next- generation lowpower processors, AI accelerators, Embedded Systems and Digital Signal Processors.

title: Towards Better Few-Shot Learning Embeddings via Full-Pipeline Augmentation
authors: Zhiyuan Song, Kezhao Liu, Yiming Liu, Yuchen Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Few-Shot Learning (FSL) is an emerging machine learning paradigm designed to help models to effectively learn from limited training data, inspiring innovations across different research directions. Recent advances in pre-trained models have highlighted the critical role of feature representation in model performance. In this paper, we first explore the influence of better feature embeddings on FSL tasks through comprehensive analysis. Motivated by the significant impact of high-quality embeddings on FSL performance, we propose the Full-Pipeline Enhanced (FPE) method. This approach is designed to improve feature representations within the constraints of limited training data. The FPE method introduces two strategic modifications: (1) consistent rotation augmentation across all phases of the FSL process (pre-training, fine-tuning, and testing), and (2) the incorporation of an auxiliary rotation prediction task during the pre-training phase. These modifications work synergistically to improve the model’s ability to learn and use robust feature representations. Through extensive experimental evaluations across multiple benchmarks, we demonstrate that the FPE method achieves significant performance improvements over state-of-theart approaches in FSL tasks.

title: Biometric Spoofing Detection with Post-Quantum Cryptography Integration
authors: Gowri Ganesh N S, Jesila Mol J, Manoj Kumar S, Vikamuhan Reddy, Meyyappan T
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Biometric authentication systems increasingly face challenges from presentation attacks, including digitally generated forgeries, while the long-term security of stored biometric templates is threatened by the emergence of quantum computing. This paper presents an early stage, simulation based, proof of concept framework that explores the feasibility of integrating biometric presentation attack detection (PAD) with post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) protection mechanisms. The proposed framework conceptually combines a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based detection module with a lattice-based key encapsulation scheme, specifically ML-KEM , to illustrate how such integration could be organized within a unified pipeline. Rather than focusing on state of the art detection performance, this study emphasizes architectural integration, system interoperability, and design considerations relevant to future quantum-resilient biometric systems. The work also discusses current limitations and outlines directions for extending the framework toward realistic biometric datasets and stronger privacy preserving mechanisms. The results are intended to demonstrate pipeline feasibility rather than biometric accuracy or cryptographic guarantees.

title: Research on Single Event Effects of Sensor Data Acquisition System Based on FPGA
authors: Panfeng Zhang, Xingyu Qu, Chao Ma, Xuecheng Du, Zhenya Li, Hao Yun
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: This paper focuses on the issue of single event effect (SEE) in data acquisition system based on Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) operating in radiation environments. A temperature and humidity sensor data acquisition system has been designed and implemented. The single event effect experiment has been conducted employing α particles radiation source (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">241</sup>Am). During the experiment, data acquisition anomalies have been observed, including garbled data output and interrupted output.These results have demonstrated that the single event effect has serious hazards to the stability of FPGA-based sensor data acquisition system.

title: MOOD MISSION: An Application to Monitor Emotional States in Nonverbal Individuals
authors: Raj Anand Sundaramoorthy, Aarthee Selvaraj, Ganesh Karthikeyan, Jothika S
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Psychiatric illnesses are common today, and affect the lives of most people across the world, including Indians. Nonverbal individuals have strange challenges developing their thoughts and feelings since their customary mode of functioning is lacking, being based on speech. This work presents a new idea premised on exploiting the utilization of technology to fill communication gaps among non-verbal individuals that enables sign language recognition to translate gestures and movement, the proposed research work allows the translation of redeeming sign language to text that gives wonderful insights to caretakers and professionals' mental health. In contrast to other methods that may ignore nonverbal signals or limit functionality, this work provides an integral solution that enables non-verbal persons to speak and communicate openly with convolutional neural networks (CNN) and advanced sentiment analysis classifiers. The sentimental analysis result achieves the compound scores ranging from -0.598 to 0.815 across test samples, effectively identifying emotional states. We successfully identify subtle movements in American Sign Language (ASL) utilizing a CNN and the Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) activation function. The proposed work attained an accuracy of 98.88%, outperforming Method A by 10.3% and Method B by 7.7%. The multilingual translation and speech synthesis functionality provides support for users belonging to various linguistic profiles, changing the face of the treatment of mental well-being of nonverbal individuals.

title: Adaptive Fine-Tuning Attacks for Watermark Removal in Deep Models
authors: Deqing Yang, Aoting Hu, Chengcheng Wei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: In recent years, fine-tuning techniques have garnered increasing attention as a means of removing model watermarks. However, existing fine-tuning methods tend to lose their effectiveness across different model scenarios, indicating that they still exhibit certain limitations in watermark removal tasks. In this work, we conduct fine-tuning attacks on models trained on multiple datasets.
