title: M3DHMR: Monocular 3D Hand Mesh Recovery
authors: Yihong Lin, Xianjia Wu, Xilai Wang, Jianqiao Hu, Songju Lei, Xiandong Li, Wenxiong Kang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Monocular 3D hand mesh recovery is challenging due to high degrees of freedom of hands, 2D-to-3D ambiguity and self-occlusion. Most existing methods are either inefficient or less straightforward for predicting the position of 3D mesh vertices. Thus, we propose a new pipeline called Monocular 3D Hand Mesh Recovery (M3DHMR) to directly estimate the positions of hand mesh vertices. M3DHMR provides 2D cues for 3D tasks from a single image and uses a new spiral decoder consist of several Dynamic Spiral Convolution (DSC) Layers and a Region of Interest (ROI) Layer. On the one hand, DSC Layers adaptively adjust the weights based on the vertex positions and extract the vertex features in both spatial and channel dimensions. On the other hand, ROI Layer utilizes the physical information and refines mesh vertices in each predefined hand region separately. Extensive experiments on popular dataset FreiHAND demonstrate that M3DHMR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art real-time methods. The code is available at https://github.com/Jackson-coder/M3DHMR.

title: Real-Time Plant Health Monitoring via Yolov11 and Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Kavitha M, Jameer Hussain A, Inbathamil S, Kavinkumar M, Naveen A K
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Effective management of plant diseases, which remain a major cause of global crop and economic losses, depends heavily on timely and accurate detection. In this work, we introduce a real-time detection framework built on a hybrid deep learning approach. The system operates in two stages: a YOLO-based model identifies diseased regions on plant leaves, and a MobileNetV3-CNN classifies the specific condition within each region. This approach balances detection accuracy with efficiency, achieving real-time performance at more than 30 frames per second (FPS). Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the hybrid model outperforms existing singlemethod approaches in both precision and recall, making it a practical solution for large-scale field deployment. Furthermore, the system is robust across different plant species and environmental conditions, offering a reliable tool for early disease identification and management in precision agriculture.

title: Sovereign Routing: An AI-Driven Framework for Disaster Resilient Communication Networks
authors: D Sharmila, L V Dhayanidhi, P C Digeesh, J Joe Anni Jeffia
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: During disasters, communication networks often collapse due to damaged infrastructure, cyber attacks, or government restrictions, delaying rescue operations. Existing solutions like path-diverse topologies and redundancy frameworks improve reliability but still depend on centralized systems. To overcome this, the proposed AI-Enhanced Sovereign Emergency Communication Network (AI-SECN) uses artificial intelligence, edge computing, and graph-based resilience metrics to ensure independence and resilience. It introduces a Composite Resilience Score combining path diversity, latency, and localization accuracy. Simulations show AI-SECN increases path diversity by 35%, reduces latency by 22%, and improves overall resilience by 45%, offering a more sovereign and dependable emergency communication system.

title: Improvements to Small Object Detection Algorithms for Urban Battlefields
authors: Chen Li, Na Sheng, Xiangjin Zhang, Jipeng Zha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Detecting objects in UAV images is difficult. Problems like small object size, limited features, busy backgrounds, and crowded scenes often lead to missed or incorrect detections. To improve accuracy and strength, this paper introduces a lightweight detection method based on YOLOv8. It is designed for urban environments and makes several key improvements. First, the original backbone network is replaced with MobileNetV3. This change greatly reduces the model’s size and computation needs. Next, the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) and ASPP are added. It helps the model focus on important areas in both the channel and spatial dimensions. This makes it easier to find small objects, even in complex scenes. Furthermore, the EIoU loss function is used instead of the original one. It improves how the model predicts object location. Tests were run on a custom dataset of urban military targets. Compared to the original YOLOv8, the new model reaches $88.24 \% \mathrm{mAP}$, has about 23% fewer parameters, and still runs at a real-time speed of around 30 FPS. It offers a good balance of accuracy and efficiency. Among other typical models, our method shows the best overall performance. It is both practical and has strong potential for wider use.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning and Machine Learning Approach for Text-Based Stress Classification
authors: Karunamoorthi R, Akshay K, Dhayabaran J, Dilip N, Akshaya R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Since stress is becoming more and more common in today's culture, there is interest in automating stress monitoring and detection from all digital communications, especially social media. The suggested project is a machine learning-based stress detection system that can distinguish between two types of text data: those in distress and those that are not. Based on a sizable dataset of 8,900 distinct social media posts, the method eliminates extraneous elements such as URLs, user mentions, emojis, and special characters. Two discrete machine learning models—the more conventional Multinomial Naive Bayes (MNB) model, which makes use of TF-IDF features, and a more recent model that employs BERT as a feature extractor and Logistic Regression (BERT+LR) as the classifier—are put into practice and assessed. The cleaned dataset was used to train and test the models, and model performance is assessed using measures like accuracy and F1-score. The results show that our BERT + L2 model outperforms the MNB model in terms of predicted accuracy and resilience. Lastly, the authors used Streamlit to implement their machine learning model into an interactive web application that included two distress classification features: (1) the ability to classify a single text input and (2) the ability to classify an entire set of text inputs from a CSV batch upload. After selecting a preferred model and entering text or uploading numerous texts, users can receive predictions along with confidence scores. After that, the tool lets users download the data and displays summary statistics for the batch forecasts. In order to help people, organizations, and mental health professionals identify stress patterns and take the proper action, the project successfully combines machine learning and natural language processing techniques to detect stress patterns in real time. This could be a scalable solution.

title: A Global Semantic Guided Dual-Path Network for Intraoperative STAS Detection in Lung Adenocarcinoma Frozen Sections
authors: Ze Yang, Huikang Xie, Lingxiao Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: To address the poor image quality of intraoperative frozen sections and the difficulty in distinguishing genuine spread through air spaces (STAS) from artifacts, this study proposes a global semantic-guided dual-path discrimination network. The network mimics expert diagnostic reasoning through a dual-path module that separately learns features of genuine STAS and artifacts. A global semantic-guided local focusing module uses tumor segmentation masks as spatial priors to direct attention to regions outside the tumor boundary. Experiments on patch-level STAS identification show that the proposed method consistently outperforms baseline models in both accuracy and specificity.

title: Partial Discharge Pattern Recognition Using Hybrid Network Modelling Technology
authors: Yong Zhang, Ruixiang Zhang, Xuemei Zhang, Weihua Xiong, Liangyao Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The phase resolved partial discharge (PRPD) pattern characterizes the distribution characteristics of discharge pulse amplitude and count based on phase statistics of partial discharge signals. This paper presents a PRPD pattern recognition model using a hybrid network combining Depthwise Separable Convolution (DSConv), self-attention mechanism and Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM). The proposed method employs DSConv and sparse attention mechanisms to achieve lightweight processing of the model while LSTM is utilized for discharge type classification and identification. Further, the research studies the impact of using different intermediate parameters on the performance of the model based on the balance between the accuracy and inference speed of the model to obtain the optimized parameter combination. Simulation tests have verified the effectiveness and PD recognition capability of the designed deep learning model.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Flame Temperature Field Reconstruction via Differentiable Physics Proxies
authors: Yifan Li, Jingtao Cheng, Yue Zhao, Ping Song
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Accurate reconstruction of flame temperature fields is critical for combustion diagnostics, industrial safety monitoring, and propulsion system analysis. Traditional methods, such as twocolor pyrometry and hyperspectral imaging, often suffer from sensitivity to noise, occlusion, and limited spatial resolution. While deep learning approaches like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have shown promise in regression tasks, they lack the generative capability to handle uncertainty and recover incomplete data. In this work, we propose a novel Flame Physics-Informed Diffusion Model (Flame-PIDM) framework for reconstructing flame temperature fields. Unlike standard data-driven generative models which may violate physical laws, our framework integrates a Differentiable Physics Proxy (DPP)—a neural network trained to approximate the complex mapping between radiative emission and temperature. This proxy is embedded directly into the diffusion training loop, imposing a strict consistency constraint between the generated visual appearance and the thermal field. We validate our approach on a synthetic flame dataset governed by non-linear thermal radiation laws. Experimental results demonstrate that our model generates high-fidelity temperature fields with a Pearson correlation coefficient greater than 0.99 against ground truth, maintaining strict adherence to physical constraints and significantly outperforming purely data-driven baselines.

title: Leveraging Big Data for Measuring and Improving Financial Inclusion in India
authors: S. Malathy, M. Shanthi, M. Gowri, Gnana Sugirtham
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: India's financial inclusion (FI) landscape has transformed over the last decade, propelled by digital public infrastructure such as the JAM trinity (Jan Dhan–Aadhaar–Mobile), real-time rails (UPI), and consent-based data sharing (Account Aggregator). Big data analytics can convert this digital exhaust into actionable intelligence that improves access, usage, and quality of financial services– the three pillars tracked by the Reserve Bank of India's Financial Inclusion Index (FI-Index). This paper presents an analytical framework to measure, explain, and enhance FI outcomes using high-frequency indicators (UPI volume/value, PMJDY penetration, e-KYC adoption, Account Aggregator consents) and socio-economic controls (income, connectivity, urbanization). We describe a pipeline combining descriptive dashboards, fixed-effects panel regressions, and machine-learning prediction with explainability to assess the contribution of data-driven channels to FI-Index movements. Using 2024–2025 statistics, we document continued gains–FI-Index rose from 64.2 (Mar 2024) to 67.0 (Mar 2025), UPI reached 19.47 billion transactions in July 2025, and PMJDY accounts surpassed 56 crore by July 2025. We also outline risks–privacy leaks, consent fatigue, authentication failures, and algorithmic bias–and propose governance mechanisms (privacy-by-design, auditable consent, fairness testing) to avoid exclusion harms. Policy recommendations focus on granular FI-Index publication, expanded open dashboards, and incentivized use of consented alternative data for affordable credit.

title: Evaluating the Performance of Diffusive Cooperative Relay System for Molecular Communications
authors: Jiaxing Wang, Di Wu, Yanfeng Li, Wanjun Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) is a communication technology that utilizes biochemical molecules as information carriers. Cooperative relay strategy can be used to extend communication distance and achieve reliable remote communications. In this paper, a two-hop diffusive decode-and-forward (DF) cooperative relay is considered, in which the equal weight merging method is used at the receiver to detect signals. The paper derives a closed-form expression for the bit error rate of DF cooperative relay system. It investigates the impact of the relay's position and signal detection threshold on system performance, while also obtaining a closed-form solution for the optimal detection threshold. The simulation results show that adding relays significantly improves system performance, and the relay position has a significant impact on system performance.

title: Research on WSN Network Coverage Based on an Improved Grey Goose Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yiran Tian, Hao Tian
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: To solve the concerns of inadequate coverage and node redundancy caused by random node deployment in wireless sensor networks, this research proposes an improved Grey Goose Optimization method (IGGO). The technique begins by including a differential evolution mutation strategy to boost global search capacity, with random differential vectors used to effectively escape local optima. Then, an elite spiral search approach is used, in which spirals are produced from randomly selected individuals, allowing for parallel, in-depth investigation over various locations to identify ideal solutions. In experiments on WSN coverage optimization, the suggested IGGO algorithm outperformed the Whale Optimization method and the Artificial Bee Colony algorithm, demonstrating its robustness and efficiency in complicated circumstances.)

title: Time-Series Forecasting of Agricultural Market Prices Using Attention-Enhanced LSTM with Weather-Informed Crop Recommendation via XGBoost
authors: Prajwal BG, Karthik Hugar, Manoj Kumar BC, Smitha N
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Fluctuations in prices in agricultural markets offer farmers a challenge in determining when and where to market their products, and in most cases, their incomes become uncertain. These price variabilities arise from a combination of seasonal effects, regional demand–supply imbalances, short-term weather conditions, and policy-influenced factors. Current forecasting techniques that rely heavily on pre-existing statistical models or manual methods are often unable to represent such complex and dynamic interactions with the required accuracy.This paper introduces an artificial intelligence–based model for agricultural crop price prediction that combines an Attention-Enhanced Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network with the XGBoost machine learning model. The models are trained using historical market price data acquired from the Agmarknet portal, enabling them to learn both temporal patterns and non-linear correlations among commodity prices. To strengthen contextual awareness, limited yet significant weather forecasting data are incorporated using a free five-day weather API, considering real-world data availability constraints.The proposed system provides localized price forecasts through a web-based dashboard supported by visual trend analysis and automated price notifications, assisting farmers in making informed selling decisions. Although the current implementation does not include mandi selection recommendations, the framework is extensible and can be refined by integrating richer meteorological data and additional market intelligence. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved predictive performance compared to conventional forecasting methods, as indicated by lower prediction error metrics.

title: PCB surface defect detection algorithm base on DMC-YOLO
authors: Xingchen Zhang, Yikui Liao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Surface defect detection on printed circuit boards is a critical step in ensuring the quality of electronic products. To address the limitations in accuracy of existing detection algorithms, this paper proposes a PCB surface defect detection algorithm based on an improved DMC-YOLO model. First, the downsampling modules in the backbone and neck networks of YOLO11 are replaced with the V7Downsampling module from YOLOv7. Second, drawing inspiration from the Mixed Aggregation Network (MANet), the C3k2 modules in the backbone and neck networks are replaced with a combined MANet_Faster module, integrating Faster Block to enhance detection accuracy. Third, a feature fusion module named CAFMFusion is designed to effectively integrate multi-scale contextual information. Finally, the MPDIoU loss function is employed to optimize bounding box regression and improve localization accuracy. Experimental results show that compared to the original YOLO11 model, the improved algorithm achieves an F1-Score of 93.6%, with mAP50 and mAP50-95 increasing by 2.9% and 2.3%, respectively. These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed improvements, offering a more reliable detection technology for intelligent PCB manufacturing.

title: Neighborhood-Comparison Charge Sharing Correction for Photon-Counting ASICs
authors: Jing Yang, Miao Li, U-Fat Chio, Xi Wang, Jun Liu, Hongsheng Zhang, Hong Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper presents a charge-sharing correction algorithm and its circuit implementation for high-count-rate CdZnTe photon-counting detectors. Targeting the inherent tradeoff between processing speed and circuit complexity in conventional Charge Summing and C8P1 architectures, a lowcomplexity neighborhood-comparison-based correction scheme is proposed. The algorithm performs localized charge reconstruction within the pixel array through rapid triggering and limitedneighborhood activation, enabling fast identification and allocation of the pixel with maximum collected charge. The circuit architecture adopts a mixed-signal design, in which the analog front-end consists of a Krummenacher-type charge-sensitive amplifier (CSA) followed by dual shaping channels—a fast channel for timing discrimination and a slow channel for amplitude comparison. Three comparators execute inter-pixel signal evaluation with the north, northwest, and west neighboring pixels. The digital processing stage introduces an optimized ORAND neighborhood activation logic that reduces the number of simultaneously active pixels from nine to four, thereby minimizing redundant communication and power consumption while significantly improving data throughput. Cadence simulations of a $\mathbf{4} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{4}$ pixel array implemented in a $\mathbf{1 1 0 n m}$ SMIC CMOS process demonstrate that, when the charge fraction of the central pixel exceeds 50%, the proposed algorithm accurately identifies and records the hit pixel. Across various charge-sharing scenarios, the correction remains stable and robust. These results confirm the feasibility and efficiency of the proposed architecture in highthroughput photon-counting ASICs, offering a compact, highspeed, and low-complexity on-chip correction solution for nextgeneration spectroscopic X-ray imaging detectors.

title: GAP-RNet: Towards Robust and Efficient Multimodal Small UAV Detection
authors: Jiahui Li, Runjie Liu, Yihui Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: The growing security threat posed by small Unmanned Aerial Vehicles necessitates the development of highly accurate, robust, and real-time detection systems. In this paper, we propose the Gated-Alignment and P2-Refinement Network (GAP-RNet), an efficient framework for robust small UAV detection. First, the Dual-Gated Cross-Modal Alignment Module ensures highly reliable fusion by coupling an Information Entropy Gate for dynamic, quality-aware weighting with Bidirectional Alignment Attention which explicitly compensates for cross-modal spatial offsets to achieve feature coherence. Second, the P2 Enhanced Feature Pyramid Network integrates the high-resolution P2 layer to maximize sensitivity and recall for ultra-small targets. Experiments on challenging aerial surveillance datasets demonstrate that GAP-RNet achieves superior detection accuracy and efficiency.

title: Analyzing Cost Optimization in Multi-Phase Batch Arrival Retrial G-Queue with Non-Persistent Customers
authors: Sangeetha N, Loganayaki P, Ebenesar Anna Bagyam J, Vivesini D
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This work presents a generalized multi-phase batch arrival retrial queueing model that incorporates several realistic customer behaviors, including impatience, optional service choices, and non-persistent retrial attempts. The system allows customers to arrive in groups, receive an essential first-phase service, and then proceed through multiple optional stages based on probabilistic selection. The server is assumed to be vulnerable to interruptions triggered by negative arrivals, and repair activities follow general time distributions. Analytical expressions for key performance indices–such as orbit size, system population, and server activity levels–are obtained using the Supplementary Variable Technique. To determine cost-efficient operating conditions, Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is applied to estimate service parameters that minimize total system cost. The proposed model is evaluated using real callcenter observations, and the results show close agreement between analytical predictions and empirical values, indicating the reliability of the framework. Overall, the study highlights the applicability of the model to service environments including customer support centers, telecommunication operations, and healthcare scheduling systems.

title: Next-Generation 3D Object Scanner With Intelligent Depth Mapping and Point-Cloud Processing
authors: Nithya Priya S, Sriram K S, Naveenraj M, Vignesh S, Vignesh R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a portable, low-cost 3D object scanner using infrared (IR) distance sensing technology. The system uses a Sharp GP2Y0A51SK sensor to capture depth data, stepper motors with A4988 drivers for precise rotation and elevation control, and an Arduino microcontroller for data acquisition. The scanned data is converted into 3D point cloud and mesh models using MeshLab/MATLAB, allowing further applications such as 3D printing, reverse engineering, and augmented reality (AR) visualization. The proposed system provides an affordable solution compared to commercial 3D scanners, achieving good accuracy for small objects while being compact and easy to use.

title: The Synergy of Machine Learning and Medical Imaging for Early Cancer Biomarker Detection: a Comprehensive Review
authors: Sathia Priya M, Gowthami S
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The most crucial factor in patient survival is the early detection of cancer and the five-year survival rate following localized cancer is more than 90 percent compared to fewer than 30 percent survival rates in metastatic disease. This theoretical analysis considers the revolutionary adoption of machine learning (ML) approach to the current medical imaging technology endeavor to enhance early biomarker discoveries of cancer. We conduct a systematic examination of the state-of-the-art diagnostic pipelines that involve standardization of image acquisition, deep learning-based segmentation with the help of advanced U-Net architectures, hybrid feature representations that include both hand-designed radiomic descriptors and an automated representation through CNN and elegant ensemble strategies of classification. The review also discusses the most important issues that hinders translation of clinically useful implementation, such as extreme class imbalance in screening data, where positive cases make up less than 1 percent of sample data, and inherent need to have models that can provide interpretation medical contexts where high stakes are involved. We investigate state-of-the-art mitigation strategies promoted by SMOTE variants, costsensitive learning, and the latest explainable AI approaches like SHAP and LIME that can indicate clinically meaningful explanations of what an algorithm does. By analyzing lung cancer CT screening applications in detail we show that ensemble classifiers that combine more than one algorithmic strategy offer both a high diagnostic accuracy and a high interpretability in clinical terms. Current models obtain Dice score higher than 0.85-0.90 of segmentation of nodules and AUC higher than 0.94 of malignancy classification. The synthesis shows the emergence of new paradigms such as multi-modal radiogenomics integration, federated learning or privacyprotected collaborative research, and delta-radiomics of longitudinal analysis. Such an end-to-end system moves on the path to scalable, understandable, and implementable AI systems that combine computational creativity with functional oncologic practice, culminating in the gradual shift to precision medicine and patient-centered outcomes.

title: Smarter Medic Monitoring System with Pill Dispenser for Patients Receiving Medication
authors: Vijayalakshmi V, Sudhagar N, Hershini N, Jayalakshmi P
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Real-time health monitoring technologies enabled by the Internet of Things (IoT) have significantly enhanced human well-being in both urban and rural areas. Many of these methods are ineffective in developing countries such as Bangladesh due to the lack of reliable communication infrastructure. In this work, we provide a real-time, Internet of Things (IoT)-based health monitoring system that enables users to test, track, and report their health status online and from any location. Our proposed Internet of Things-based solution may rapidly transmit sensitive health data to caregivers and medical facilities. The Global System for Mobile Communication (GSM), Nodemcu, Arduino UNO, and other modules were used to build the proposed system for detecting room temperature, air quality, oxygen saturation, body temperature, and pulse rate in a smart home setting. The system may access the patient's historical health data. We tested our approach with a few tests, and it works smoothly and accurately. The proposed strategy has great promise in the towns and villages of emerging countries. This contains a pill dispenser.

title: Real-Time Emission Prediction and Monitoring Using CatBoost and MQTT Protocol
authors: Nagendra Rakesh N, Ganeash Vasanth, Sreevarsan M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Due to public concern over pollution, there is an increased demand for monitoring systems that will detect emissions in real time and will quantify the changes in the local emissions environment qualitatively and quantitatively. Traditional monitoring systems have been viewed as inefficient in data collection, and data processing, which has delayed response time and mitigation measures. This study proposes an artificial intelligence integrated methodology for emissions analysis and future prediction with the CatBoost machine learning model. This methodology uses data flowing in real time, using message queuing telemetry transport (MQTT), to process emission data, project pollution outbreaks, and display usable data in the web-based interface.The CatBoost model used historic emissions data to predict anticipated quantity of pollutants in specific areas and with the general area.By facilitating instantaneous transmissions of data from numerous Pollution Monitors to AI Models to User Interfaces, MQTT provides a significant increase in the Utility of All Three Types of Technology. This work contributes to the understanding of how Artificial Intelligence can be used in real-time emissions monitoring Systems. The Theory through which this framework was proposed also provides a demonstration of how this newly formed framework may apply to Environmental Management Monitoring of Pollutants and may therefore be of value in Managing Pollution both at an Industrial and Urban level.

title: Heat Dissipation Optimization Analysis of IGBT Module of Energy Storage Converter
authors: Fusheng Wang, Wei Lai, Hui Li, Xuewei Xiang, Ran Yao, Peng Yang
abstract: With the gradual improvement of the integration of electronic equipment, high power and miniaturization are important trends in the development of power electronic equipment. IGBT is the core device inside the energy storage converter, and its heat dissipation management affects the reliable operation of the entire equipment. In this paper, based on the finite element simulation method, the temperature distribution of the whole machine under the high power loss condition of IGBT is studied for the traditional energy storage converter cabinet. The temperature distribution of the key areas such as power devices and heat dissipation structure is analyzed when the steady state is reached. On this basis, a heat dissipation scheme of adding a fan to the key parts of the cabinet to achieve forced air cooling is proposed, and the heat flow distribution and temperature distribution inside the whole machine under forced convection are further simulated. The research results show that the internal temperature of the converter cabinet is at a high level under high power operating conditions, and the heat generated by the chip is mainly concentrated on the chip and its surroundings. The traditional heat dissipation design leads to heat accumulation, which affects the reliable operation of the whole machine. By adopting the design scheme of forced air cooling instead of natural cooling, the temperature inside the rectifier cabinet can be effectively reduced.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Distributed New Energy Access on the Stability of Provincial Power Grid
authors: Guanyu Zhang, Mingyang Liu, Ze Gao, Xueqing Pan, Di Zhang, Chenghao Li
abstract: New energy is undergoing rapid development, with distributed new energy growing particularly fast in the Henan power grid. The impact of distributed new energy on system stability cannot be ignored. To delve deeper into this issue, typical station models were constructed based on the types of distributed new energy and their integration points into the grid. Using this model, 371 220kV substations in Henan Province were simulated. The PSASP software was employed to simulate the transient stability level of the Henan power grid under two fault scenarios: line fault and DC blocking. The analysis reveals that the influence of new energy on system stability varies depending on operational modes and fault types.

title: Coordinated Active and Reactive Power Optimization of Distributed Generations Based on Network Loss Minimization
authors: Yuliang Jiang, Wei Ni, Guoliang Zhang, Siyu Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This paper proposes a coordinated optimization framework for distributed generation (DG) active and reactive power control based on network loss minimization. This framwork uses a spanning tree generation algorithm (STGA) based exhaustive search (ES). The approach integrates detailed power flow analysis, loss decomposition, and intelligent control to improve energy efficiency in active distribution systems. The proposed model quantifies the influence of both active and reactive power on line losses, introduces a dynamic loss model for real-time optimization, and incorporates adaptive control method. The simulation studies using the IEEE 16-node system demonstrate that the proposed optimization method significantly reduces network losses, improves voltage profiles, enhances power transfer capability, and achieves both economic and carbon-reduction benefits.

title: Noise-Annealed Deep Recurrent Architecture for Day-Ahead Electricity Price Forecasting
authors: Huazheng Zhang, Peng Ma, Yanjie Li, Qiang Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Short-term electricity price forecasting is a core enabler for risk-aware bidding, contract settlement, and system-wide dispatch planning in modern power markets. Price trajectories are notoriously volatile and exhibit seasonal recurrence, structural drift, and abrupt shocks, which makes naive statistical models unstable in practice. This paper develops a noise-annealed deep recurrent forecasting framework that combines (i) a multi-layer Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) backbone with dropout-based regularization and (ii) a staged training strategy in which exogenous stochastic disturbance is gradually reduced ("noise annealing") to improve robustness and generalization. To emulate realistic market behavior, we synthesize price series that superimpose seasonal demand cycles, slow price drift, and Gaussian disturbance, and then convert the series into supervised learning samples through a sliding temporal window. The model is trained with the Adam optimizer under a mean-squared-error objective. Empirical analysis shows that the proposed framework tracks actual price levels and volatility patterns with high fidelity, and its prediction curve aligns closely with the true series on the test horizon. These results indicate that structured recurrent modeling plus noise-annealed training can provide stable and accurate price forecasts for electricity markets.

title: Power Transfer Limits and Stability Enhancement in Wind Farms by Using Grid-Forming BESS With the Three-Windings Transformer
authors: Shuai Xiao, Jiawei Wang, Canlin Wang, Yuzheng Xie, Mengjiao Li
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Under weak grid conditions, the power transmission capacity of wind farms is greatly constrained. Against this backdrop, this paper proposes a scheme by using grid-forming battery energy storage system (BESS) with the three-windings transformer to enhance the power transfer limit (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</inf>) of wind farms. First, an analysis is conducted on how grid-forming BESS connected through a three-winding transformer affects the power transmission capacity of wind farms, finding that the voltage stability limit (∂Q/∂V) is a better reflection of the system's power transmission limit compared to the short-circuit ratio (SCR). Second, it is discovered that grid-forming BESS can increase the maximum power transmission capacity, and that using a three-winding transformer for grid connection has a better effect on enhancing transmission power compared to a two-winding transformer. Finally, simulation results verify the correctness and efficacy of the theoretical analysis.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)

title: Laser-Induced Graphene/PDMS for Flexible Strain Sensing Applications
authors: Soha Maqbool Bhat, Fazian Tariq Beigh, Dhiman Mallick
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Flexible strain sensors are essential for the next generation of wearable and stretchable electronics, offering real-time tracking of physiological signals. In this work, a highly sensitive and stretchable strain sensor was developed based on Laser-Induced Graphene (LIG) and Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS). To overcome the limitations of conventional, LIG-based Polyimide (PI), LIG was transferred onto PDMS for improved stretchability, flexibility, and skin compatibility. The sensor exhibits excellent gauge factors (GF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> ~ 385 at low strain and GF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> ~ 661 at higher strain levels) along with strong durability under cyclic loading. Moreover, high-quality LIG with low defects has been formed later confirmed by Raman spectroscopy (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~ 0.26 and I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2D</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> ~ 0.75). These results validate the potential of the fabricated LIG/PDMS strain sensor as a multifunctional platform for wearable, rehabilitative, and voice-interactive applications.

title: Random Re-Masking Reconstruction for Mining Protein-Protein Interactions
authors: Hu Yuan, Lu Yao, Xiangyu Zeng, Xiang Li, Yan Kang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Protein-protein interactions (PPIs) play a crucial role in biological processes such as signal transduction, cellcycle regulation, and metabolism. Although recent PPI prediction methods leveraging protein structural data have achieved notable performance gains, effectively modeling long-range dependencies and maintaining reliable generalization to unseen proteins remain major challenges. To address this issue, we propose RMR-PPI, a residue-level self-supervised framework that learns structure-aware protein representations directly from PPI-related structural graphs. RMR-PPI integrates a multi-view random remasking strategy with a latent target-guided semantic consistency loss, enabling robust recovery of masked residue features through a Protein encoder across multiple perturbed views. Experiments on real PPI datasets demonstrate that RMR-PPI outperforms or matches state-of-the-art methods, while exhibiting superior robustness and cross-dataset generalization capabilities.

title: An Enhanced AI Framework for Predicting Type II Diabetes – Insights from 100000 Health Records
authors: Khushi Shah, Vishwa Chokshi, Jasvi Desai, Divya Bhavani Mohan, Prakash Arumugam, S.S. Abinayaa
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Diagnosis of Type 2 diabetes at an early stage is very essential towards effective prevention and treatment of the disease particularly among high-risk groups. This study leverages the state of the art Machine Learning (ML) models and Deep Learning (DL) models to come up with an effective and accurate diabetes risk prediction framework based on the Diabetes Risk Prediction dataset available on Kaggle, which contains 100000 samples with a rich combination of health indicators. Eight types of classifiers i.e. Logistic Regression (LR), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest (RF), XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and TabNet were evaluated used and the results were compared. The executed models were evaluated on various key performance metrics. TabNet model beat the rest of the models (91.84% accuracy and 0.962 AU-ROC) closely followed by CatBoost (91.66% accuracy) and XGBoost (91.54% accuracy). LightGBM is a little bit less accurate (91.10%), but showed relatively good results and improved compared to traditional solutions, including Random Forest (91.08%) and Logistic Regression (78.88%). These findings prove the high predictive accuracy of TabNet, has great potential as a healthcare solution in the real world in the case of Type 2 diabetes detection.

title: High-Accuracy Underwater Acoustic Channel Estimation Method Based on Delay Compensation and Response Generation Model
authors: Xiaoguang Zheng, Lei Yang, Zicheng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: To improve the accuracy of channel estimation in underwater acoustic communication scenarios, this paper proposes a novel estimation method based on a response generation network. First, an underwater acoustic impulse response model is constructed according to the characteristics of the MIMO-OTFS channel. On this basis, a localized analysis algorithm incorporating delay compensation is developed to achieve three-dimensional reconstruction of underwater acoustic signals, thereby reducing feature deviations caused by channel dynamics. Experimental results demonstrate that, under a signal-to-noise ratio of 5 dB, the proposed approach significantly decreases the normalized mean squared error of channel estimation and effectively suppresses signal distortion.

title: A Multi-Band Si-InGaAs/InP Avalanche Photodetector
authors: Jingcheng Gong, Yu Zhang, Wei Dong, Yuanfa Wang, Yu Pang, Yong Wang, Weiwei Sun, Jiasai Luo, Hong Liu, Lingrui Xing, Guanjie Zeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of electronic and optoelectronic technologies, there is an increasing demand for high-performance photodetectors in communication, sensing, and imaging applications. However, conventional detectors often face inherent trade-offs among broadband response, high gain, and low noise, thereby limiting their overall performance. To address these challenges, this work proposes a back-to-back integrated avalanche photodiode (APD) structure, where the cathodes of a Si APD and an InGaAs/InP APD are interconnected. By employing selective bias control, only one APD operates under reverse bias at a time. The electrical and optical characteristics of this integrated device are investigated through numerical simulations using Silvaco TCAD. Simulation results reveal avalanche breakdown voltages of $\mathbf{6 8. 6 ~ V}$ and $\mathbf{5 1. 4}$ V in the visible (0.4-1.0 $\mu \mathrm{m}$) and near-infrared (0.6-1.8 $\mu \mathrm{m}$) regions, respectively. The proposed device demonstrates a dark current on the order of 1 pA, along with responsivity and quantum efficiency levels adequate for multiband detection. These results demonstrate that the dual-band APD structure offers an effective solution for high-performance and broadband optoelectronic detection.

title: Cost-Effective Implementation of PLC Systems for Training_supp1-3666812.pdf
authors: Zaira Pineda-Rico
abstract: This work presents a cost-effective approach to implementing programmable logic controllers (PLCs) for educational use, using commercial microcontrollers (Arduino UNO, ESP8266 NodeMCU) and a Raspberry Pi configured with open-source software. The objective is to provide accessible training resources for automation and control in settings with limited resources, where conventional PLCs are often unaffordable. The proposed architecture integrates with Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) platforms, enabling students to gain practical experience in PLC programming, human–machine interface (HMI) development, and cloud-based monitoring. A case study of an automatic water tank filling system demonstrates the system’s ability to perform real-time control, communication via Modbus, and data storage through Node-RED and InfluxDB Cloud. Educational validation was carried out with a small group of engineering students (n=6), who highlighted the system’s usefulness, accessibility, and realism for learning industrial automation. While preliminary, the findings support the platform’s potential as a hands-on teaching tool. The results indicate that low-cost, open-source PLC configurations can effectively bridge the gap between theory and practice, fostering inclusive training opportunities in automation and IIoT.

title: Rethinking the Utilization of Individual Rewards in Multiagent Reinforcement Learning With Sparse Team Rewards
authors: Yang Zhang, Yunjian Xu, Chengwei Zhang, Chao Wang, Zhihe Yang, Bo Tang, Edward Chung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: The sparsity of team rewards significantly hinders the learning of optimal team policies in cooperative multiagent reinforcement learning (MARL). While augmenting sparse team rewards with individual rewards is a common solution, existing methods face three critical challenges: 1) inconsistency between the learned policy and the optimal team policy due to reward function modification; 2) incompatibility with different individual reward settings; and 3) suboptimal balance between individual and team reward-oriented policy optimization. To address these challenges, we propose CLOT, a novel policy consistency constrained multiagent policy optimization approach that leverages individual rewards in a reward setting-agnostic manner. Specifically, we first present a constrained policy optimization problem formulated by a consistency constraint between the team returns of the learned policy and those of the optimal team policy. Then, we develop a Lagrangian dual-based iterative policy optimization procedure to solve the formulated problem, deriving exact optimization objectives for policy training. Throughout this process, a dynamic Lagrangian multiplier update mechanism is proposed to automatically balance individual and team reward-oriented policy optimization. Extensive experimental evaluations across the StarCraft II Multiagent Challenge (SMAC), multiagent particle environment (MPE), and Google research football (GRF) environments demonstrate that our approach effectively addresses all three identified challenges, significantly enhancing performance in cooperative multiagent scenarios with sparse team rewards.

title: Cost-Efficient Public-Cloud BBU Deployment via Peak-Hitching Mapping for 5G C-RAN
authors: Yue Yang, Hongyun Zheng, Ruiliang Song, Naibo Zhang, Haipeng Zhang
abstract: This letter presents the Peak-Hitching VM framework for 5G C-RAN vBBU cost optimization. Targeting the core cost driver of overflow penalty, its key PHvM algorithm exploits RRH burst asynchrony to realize offline joint optimization of vBBU count, capacity, and mapping with O (N log N) complexity. Extended online, it uses historical traffic for near-optimal provisioning. Evaluated on real-world traces, offline PHvM cuts costs by up to 69% vs. baselines, with Online-PHvM only 5% away from offline truth. It validates the first framework bridging spatial RRH grouping with temporal cloud billing.

title: IVD-NET: An Adaptive Dual-Branch Network for Small Object Detection in Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Images
authors: Haibin Wu, Pengfei Yuan, Wenbai Liu, Aili Wang, Liang Yu, Gabor Molnar
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: To address challenges such as drastic illumination changes, dense small objects, and frequent occlusions in remote sensing and UAV Images, this paper proposes an Infrared-Visible Dual-branch Detection Network (IVD-NET). Existing fusion methods often overlook cross-modal feature alignment and insufficient representation of small objects. To tackle these issues, IVD-NET employs a dual-stream architecture with three key innovations. First, an Adaptive Modality Fusion Module (AMM) comprising Cross-modal Correlation Attention (CCA) and Cross-modal Guided Pixel Attention (CGPA) is proposed to dynamically integrate complementary information from infrared and visible modalities, effectively handling lighting interference. Second, a Visual Parallel Aggregation (ViPA) module is designed to enhance feature representation for small objects through parallel multi-scale branches and frequency-domain attention. Finally, a Dual-Path Adaptive Detection Head integrating the Spatial Enhanced Attention Module (SEAM) is introduced to improve localization and classification accuracy for occluded targets. Extensive experiments on DroneVehicle and LLVIP datasets demonstrate that IVD-NET achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance. Specifically, on the DroneVehicle dataset, it achieves an mAP50 of 83.08%, outperforming methods like YOLOv10 and MMDetection.

title: High THz Average Power Generation from Photoconductive Connected Array Antenna Excited by High-Power Laser
authors: Martijn D. Huiskes, Mohsen Khalili, Juan Bueno, Nuria Llombart, Paolo M. Sberna, Clara J. Saraceno, Andrea Neto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: We report on high power THz emission from LT-GaAs photoconductive emitters, excited with a frequency-doubled, high power ultrafast Yb laser emitting 100 fs pulses at 515 nm (green) at a high repetition rate of 91 MHz. The device presented in this work incorporates a dedicated connected array. First, we describe the general methodology for designing such impedance matched photoconductive connected array devices, then we describe how this methodology was applied for the design of a 361-element photoconductive connected array specifically optimized for the high optical power (20 W) at 515 nm laser wavelength and 91 MHz repetition rate. The combination of these optimized devices and high power laser at very high repetition rate allow us to demonstrate a high THz power of 10.1 mW during the on-cycle of a chopper with 50% duty cycle.

title: Hierarchical Feature-Modulated Transformer with Introspective Interactions for Visual Grounding in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Yongfeng Li, Ying Wei, Jiapeng Zhang, Jiaming Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Visual grounding in remote sensing images aims to localize objects referred to by a query text in complex aerial scenes. However, existing methods still suffer from limitations in multimodal interaction and alignment. Specifically, multi-scale visual feature fusion often lacks effective textual semantic filtering, introducing redundant background information that degrades cross-modal alignment. Moreover, although bidirectional cross-attention enhances inter-modal information exchange, it tends to neglect intra-modal semantic consistency, resulting in unstable and less discriminative representations. In addition, language queries obtained after cross-modal interaction are vulnerable to linguistic noise during decoding, which further impairs localization robustness. To address these challenges in a unified and progressive manner, we propose a hierarchical vision–language alignment framework, termed Hierarchical Feature-Modulated Transformer with Introspective Interaction (HMIT). Specifically, TMCFF performs early-stage text-guided modulation on multi-scale visual features to suppress text-irrelevant background information. CMICI refines cross-modal representations through contextual introspection while preserving intra-modal semantic consistency. VGTC calibrates high-level language queries using global visual priors prior to decoding, producing more robust and discriminative query embeddings. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that HMIT consistently outperforms existing methods across multiple metrics, achieving new state-of-the-art performance.

title: A Dual-Band Reconfigurable Filtering Power Amplifier for 5G mmWave Applications
authors: Weisen Zeng, Li Gao, Zi-Kang Hu, Hongtao Xu, Xiu Yin Zhang
abstract: This article presents a frequency reconfigurable power amplifier (PA) for fifth-generation (5G) millimeter-wave dual-side local oscillator (LO) injection systems, which effectively reduces the LO design complexity. A tricoil transformer is proposed to design a high <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula> broadband output matching network (OMN), thus enhancing power-efficiency at high-frequency band. Besides, two distinct reconfigurable resonator topologies are proposed for the design of both the input and interstage matching networks. These resonators effectively realize filtering functionality by utilizing the switch-off capacitance in conjunction with inductive resonance, while maintaining low-circuit losses. Importantly, this design methodology inherently embeds filtering capabilities within the matching network without introducing additional area overhead. The proposed PA is implemented in 28-nm bulk CMOS and realizes 18.4–20.2 dBm <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_{\text {sat}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, 13.7–19 dBm OP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1dB</sub> and 24.5%–34.7% PAE over n257–n261 bands. The PA exhibits maximum image/LO rejection (LOR) of 38.5/28.5 dBc. For 5G new radio (NR) frequency range 2 (FR2) 200 MHz 64-QAM signals (9.7 dB PAPR), the PA achieves average <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_{\text {out}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> /PAE of >7.3 dBm and >5.2% with −25 dB EVM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub> over n257–n261. The PA occupies a core area of only 0.109 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, making it suitable for cost-effective, high-yield 5G user equipment (UE) and large-scale phased-array applications.

title: Unified Time–Frequency–Phase Analysis of Speech Using Quaternion Linear Canonical Transform
authors: Mohan Bansal, Kartheek Budime, Varun Gupta
abstract: The Quaternion Linear Canonical Transform (QLCT) provides a unified time-frequency-phase representation for multidimensional signals. However, its applications have largely been limited to theoretical studies and image processing, leaving speech an inherently one-dimensional non-stationary signal unexplored. This paper presents a Discrete QLCT (DQLCT) implementation that operates directly on real-valued temporal signals by mapping them into the quaternion domain via analytic signal representation. The transform is derived from first principles, with invertibility proven through discrete exponential orthogonality and validated experimentally on a 9.41 second speech signal. Reconstruction error remains at machine precision (mean <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8.36 \times 10^{-13}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and Itakura-Saito COSH spectral distances stay below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-12}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, confirming lossless forward-inverse transform consistency. This establishes DQLCT as a numerically stable, practically implementable tool for quaternion-based speech analysis.

title: Current Density Distribution Estimation of REBCO Coated Conductors Using Machine Learning
authors: Junichiro Takei, Takanobu Mato, Atsushi Ishiyama, So Noguchi
abstract: High-temperature superconducting (HTS) rare-earth barium copper oxide (REBCO) coated conductors (CCs) are promising for high-field applications; however, their critical current often exhibits non-uniformity along the tape length due to fabrication-induced defects such as edge cracks or low critical current region. To ensure the reliability of REBCO magnets, non-destructive and high-resolution evaluation of the local critical current distribution is essential. The Hall array measurement (HAM) method enables two-dimensional mapping of the critical current density distribution through the inverse analysis using the measured field distribution. The performance of the conventional methods is limited with the number of sample points in HAM. In this study, a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based inverse analysis method is proposed to reconstruct the current density distribution from the measured field. The CNN-based estimator is trained with 5,000 pairs of field and current density distributions artificially generated considering the current conservation law. Experimental validation demonstrated that the CNN-based method can estimate a more realistic current density distribution than the conventional FFT-based approach, even with a limited number of measurement points. Although the CNN results showed slight noise effects, they remained consistent with the expected Bean's model behavior. The proposed approach offers a promising framework for fast and accurate non-destructive evaluation of REBCO tapes, contributing to improved reliability of HTS magnet systems.

title: A QR-Anchored Secure Offloading for Intelligent Transportation Using Planar Graph Modeling
authors: Saad Alalklabi
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The proliferation of connected vehicles intensifies critical challenges in computation offloading, particularly concerning security, communication stability, and scalability within Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). Existing offloading strategies are prone to routing loops, unstable vehicular connectivity, and are vulnerable to impersonation and Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel secure offloading framework that integrates a mobility-aware planar spanner graph for robust routing and a lightweight QR-code-based protocol for decentralized authentication. The graph theoretic component dynamically constructs a stable network topology that eliminates routing loops and provides proven bounded-path stretch which ensure routing optimality under dynamic urban mobility conditions. The QR-anchored mechanism establishes an initial trust between vehicles and Roadside Units (RSUs) through a visual channel which mitigate impersonation attacks without the overhead of traditional Public Key Infrastructure (PKI). Performance of the proposed method is evaluated through extensive simulations on the Veins-OMNeT++/SUMO platform under a dense urban grid scenario. The results demonstrate that our solution significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines like Multi-Layer Task Offloading (MTO), Mx-TORU, and Delay-Sensitive Task Offloading (MAFCPTORA), achieve up to 19% lower latency, a 22% higher task success ratio, and 17% reduced computational overhead on resource-constrained On-Board Unit (OBUs). This work highlights the efficacy of combining graph-theoretic routing with lightweight cryptography to enhance the security and efficiency of ITS offloading platforms.

title: Reflectionless Design in RF Passive and Active Circuits: Recent Advances and Emerging Trends
authors: Roberto Gómez-García, Li Yang, Mohamed Malki, Nasrin Iranpour, Jim Darrel Ang, Zekai Luo, Xi-Bei Zhao, Shiyan Wang, Runze Li, José-María Muñoz-Ferreras, Xi Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: RF-signal power reflections produced by impedance mismatches remain a critical limitation in high-frequency systems, degrading their operational performance, robustness, and RF-signal integrity. Typical RF-protection mechanisms, such as inter-RF-stage attenuators, isolators<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {/}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>circulators, or RF-signal-cancellation loops, often impose penalties in terms of increased in-band insertion-loss levels, size, cost, DC-power consumption, and design complexity. To overcome these challenges, reflectionless or absorptive RF circuits have emerged as an alternative paradigm. They intrinsically absorb undesired stopband<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {/}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>non-transmitted RF signals, thereby providing broad-band impedance matching. This overview paper reviews the latest advances on RF reflectionless circuits, covering their fundamental RF design principles and more-recent developments. Beyond the well-established case of RF reflectionless filters illustrated through several state-of-the-art implementations in 3-D and planar technologies, other emerging designs are also addressed. They include reflectionless beyond-transmission-band-flat-group-delay single-ended bandpass filters and balanced lowpass filters for high-data-rate digital communications in planar and integrated RF platforms, and on-chip two-port-absorptive bandstop filters. Furthermore, as key examples of reflectionless RF active devices, millimeter-wave input-absorptive RF amplifiers for beyond-5G wireless scenarios are presented. Cutting-edge applications in antennas, electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), and quantum engineering are also discussed, highlighting the versatility of the reflectionless design philosophy.

title: TIDE-S: Telemetry Informed Delay Testing With Optimized Sensor Placement
authors: Deepesh Sahoo, Eduardo Ortega, Peter Domanski, Farshad Firouzi, Krishnendu Chakrabarty
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Silent data corruption (SDC) refers to undetected errors that yield incorrect results without triggering system alerts or error logs. Existing test methodologies are inadequate for capturing dynamic voltage fluctuations that occur under realistic workload conditions, thereby limiting their effectiveness for detecting SDCs. We present TIDE-S, a telemetry-informed delay testing (TIDE) framework that integrates presilicon sensor placement strategies to improve telemetry accuracy. By evaluating different sensor allocation schemes—uniform, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-means, and energy-aware clustering—TIDE-S improves the spatial granularity of voltage observation, enabling more accurate correlation between voltage fluctuations and path delay behavior. The combined telemetry- and sensor-aware methodology significantly improves the detection of timing-sensitive SDCs. The proposed framework incurs minimal infrastructure overhead by leveraging standard pad-based voltage observation, making it practical for real system-on-chip (SoC) designs. We demonstrate the effectiveness of TIDE-S across multiple RISC-V-based SoCs and a diverse set of real-world benchmarks, showing quantifiable improvements in voltage estimation, slack prediction, and test coverage.

title: Instantaneous Pulse Pattern Control of Three-Phase Multi-Active Bridge DC-DC Converters
authors: Jonghun Yun, Jaekeun Lee, Giovanni De Carne, Shenghui Cui
abstract: A three-phase multi-active bridge (MAB3) converter is a multiport dc-dc converter for high power applications. Its three-phase multi-winding transformer enables flexible power flow between multiple dc sources, offering superior high power density and efficiency. However, when fixed duty cycles are employed with mismatched dc voltage conditions, the efficiency of the MAB3 deteriorates, particularly in light-load conditions. To address this, modulation schemes with variable duty cycles are needed, along with dynamic control. However, few studies have explored dynamic control methods compatible with such multi-variable modulations. Without proper dynamic control, step changes in voltage patterns can induce transient overcurrents and transformer flux saturation, limiting the achievable power slew rate. Consequently, mitigating this poor dynamic performance may necessitate transformer overdesign or larger DC-link capacitance. To overcome these challenges, this article proposes an instantaneous pulse pattern control (IPPC) to support generic modulations of MAB3 converters. By formulating dynamic control as a multi-flux regulation problem, the IPPC enables to accommodate the generic modulation schemes while achieving consistent and minimized transient times. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed IPPC consistently reduces transient times to a single sampling period, equivalent to one sixth of the switching period.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Selective Prediction of Neovascular Age-Related Macular Degeneration Recurrence Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Won Tae Yoon, Hun-gyeom Kim, Junhyung Moon, Dat Ngo, Jae Hui Kim, Baek Hwan Cho
abstract: Neovascular age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) is a progressive, sight-threatening disease predominantly affecting older adults and a leading cause of global vision loss projected to rise with aging populations. Although existing artificial intelligence (AI) studies have investigated nAMD recurrence detection, predictive modeling remains underexplored, with limited performance in a few studies relying on a single type of retinal image. Therefore, we aimed to comprehensively explore AI-based recurrence prediction incorporating multiple types of retinal images and clinical data. We collected retinal photographs and clinical data from 399 nAMD patients. The retinal photographs are six images, comprising fundus photographs and optical coherence tomography (OCT) horizontal/vertical scans taken both before and after treatment. The clinical data includes demographic information, such as age, sex and medication type. Using our collected dataset, we proposed an uncertainty-aware selective-prediction model for nAMD recurrence. The proposed model, built on popular CNN architectures, filters out highly uncertain predictions to enhance reliability of the recurrence prediction. Results showed that OCT horizontal post-treatment images achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.662, which increased to 0.836 with Monte Carlo (MC) dropout by excluding uncertain predictions, while the double-threshold method yielded an AUC of 0.744. Incorporating clinical parameters further improved performance, raising the AUC to 0.855, and drug-specific modeling produced particularly high predictive performance, with AUCs of 0.934 for ranibizumab and 0.978 for aflibercept. Our experimental results highlight the potential of AI-based multimodal modeling, selective prediction, and treatment-specific analysis in improving prognostic prediction and guiding therapeutic decision-making for patients with nAMD.

title: IZeRO-NF: Intelligent-Controlled Zero-Redundancy Heterogeneous Network Functions for 6GC Endogenous Security
authors: Hongjia Li, Zhihao Wang, Yan Zhang, Nirwan Ansari, Xiangji Li, Weiping Wang, Guangxi Yu
abstract: In this article, we propose an Intelligent-controlled Zero-Redundancy heterOgeneous Network Function (IzeRO-NF) framework, for achieving efficient security-endogenous 6G core networks. To ensure interoperability and standardization, the framework adheres to the 3GPP enhanced Service-Based Architecture (eSBA). Inspired by the hierarchical structure of the human central nervous system—comprising the effectors, the spinal cord and the brain—we design a three-tier intelligent perception-decision-action cycles for the framework. Particularly, the effector tier comprises resource-adaptive heterogeneous execution entities of NFs. The spinal-cord-like tier proactively schedules signaling flows to heterogeneous execution entities to disrupt the attacker’s ability to establish a stable, repeatable attack chain. The brain-like tier intelligently learns the optimal joint policies for heterogeneous execution entity scheduling and resource scaling. After highlighting key design aspects of the IZeRO-NF framework, we present simulation results from a case study that demonstrate its security and efficiency. Finally, we discuss open issues that warrant further exploration to advance and refine this novel framework.

title: Decentralized Joint Access Point Selection and Power Control in Uplink Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Farimehr Zohari, Ali Reza Heidarpour, Masoud Ardakani, Alberto Leon-Garcia, Çağatay Edemen, Murat Uysal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) technology is emerging as a highly promising solution for supporting large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) networks, where a large number of spatially distributed and heterogeneous devices operate under stringent power constraints. In these complex and dynamic environments, achieving an optimal balance between spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE) requires intelligent resource allocation and a flexible, adaptive optimization framework capable of responding to fluctuating network conditions, device demands, and energy limitations. This paper presents a novel fully decentralized deep deterministic policy gradient (FD3PG) algorithm, enabling IoT devices (acting as agents) to autonomously optimize joint access point selection and power control (JAPSPC) in uplink CF-mMIMO systems. Our approach, termed Joint D3PG, aims to collaboratively maximize a performance metric known as resource efficiency (RE) to effectively capture the balance between SE and EE, guiding the system toward configurations that best align with its operational demands and resource constraints. Extensive simulation results demonstrate significant performance gains over existing benchmark schemes. Specifically, our approach achieves up to a 79% improvement compared to conventional CF-mMIMO systems and a 12% gain over state-of-the-art methods, highlighting its effectiveness for complex IoT networks.

title: Beyond Direct Embedding: Secure Separable Latent Space Watermarking for Anti-Screen-Shooting
authors: Jiyou Chen, Xiyang Xie, Dewang Wang, Huihuang Zhao, Gaobo Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Existing anti-screen-shooting watermarking methods embed watermarks on either the server or client side. Server-side embedding incurs high computational and communication overhead under concurrent requests, while client-side methods risk watermark interception during transmission and require additional encrypted channels. To address these limitations, we propose an end-to-end separable watermarking framework (SepWater) that exploits latent space representations. By decoupling server-side watermark embedding from client-side image generation, SepWater enhances security by preventing leaks of both the original content and the watermark, while also reducing transmission costs. On the server side, a dedicated encoder processes the watermark information, while a frozen pre-trained encoder handles the original image. We then fuse their outputs into a compact latent vector for transmission to the client. On the client side, a frozen VQGAN decoder reconstructs the watermarked image directly. In addition, we propose a local residual attention loss, combined with other image quality constraints, to produce watermarked images with high capture resistance and visual fidelity. Furthermore, to improve the robustness of the SepWater, two noise modes are simulated that contain eye protection noise and lightweight edge grayscale deviation noise. Experiments show that SepWater outperforms state-of-the-art methods in withstanding screen-shooting distortions, optimizing communication efficiency, and scaling under high concurrency, making it suitable for practical deployment. The source code is released at https://github.com/CVhnu/SepWater.

title: A Modified Parallel Plate Waveguide for Wide-Angle Characterization of Frequency Selective Surfaces
authors: Patinavalasa Megh Sainadh, Saptarshi Ghosh
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents an innovative parallel plate waveguide (PPW) system for wide-angle characterization of frequency selective surfaces (FSSs). The structure is made of two parallel metallic plates, with increased height at the center to accommodate a 1-D FSS array, and reduced height at the ends to achieve wideband impedance matching. The plates are further tapered toward the edges to suppress the edge radiation and leakage. For validation under normal incidence, the setup is tested using three distinct FSS prototypes: a bandstop filter, a two-layer wideband absorber, and a switchable absorber/transmitter structure. For oblique incidence characterization, two identical PPW sections are joined at an angle 2θ, forming a controlled angular excitation path with the FSS inclined at θ. The measured results of FSS samples for both normal and oblique incidences closely match unit cell and full-wave simulations, thereby confirming the accuracy of the proposed system for wide-angle characterization up to 8 GHz.
