title: Real-Time Wildfire Detection in Power Line Corridors Using YOLOv10 and UAV-Based Cloud-Edge-End Collaboration
authors: Bo Gao, Xiaohua Zhang, Yihui Kong, Yunlong Li, Yuxin Wang, Huanan Liu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Wildfires pose a significant threat to power line infrastructure, potentially causing large-scale power outages and severe economic and environmental damage. Early and accurate detection of wildfires within power line corridors is critical for enabling timely intervention and mitigating risks. In this paper, we propose an intelligent wildfire detection system based on the state-of-the-art YOLOv10 object detection algorithm. Leveraging UAV-acquired imagery and real-time edge processing, the system is designed to detect wildfire occurrences with high speed and precision in complex outdoor environments. Compared to traditional image classification and older detection networks, YOLOv10 offers enhanced performance in terms of detection accuracy, computational efficiency, and robustness under varying lighting and background conditions. We construct a wildfire detection dataset specific to power line corridors and perform extensive experiments to evaluate the model. The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 89.6%, with an inference time of 11.3 ms per frame, significantly outperforming previous YOLO versions and lightweight detection models.

title: A Multi-temporal Coordinated Optimal Operation Method for Basin-scale Hydro-PV Complementary Systems Considering Hydro-Electric Coupling
authors: Rong Wu, Jiajun Zhang, Hanxiang Mo, Xiaobo Hu, Yongqiang Zhang, Yajie Tang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: In basin-scale hydro-PV complementary systems abundant in water and solar resources, the capacity constraints of transmission channels and the limitations of hydropower regulation characteristics frequently lead to PV and hydropower curtailment, which has become a key bottleneck restricting the consumption efficiency of renewable energy in the system. To address the challenges of power fluctuations caused by high-penetration distributed PV integration and the insufficient flexibility of the grid, this paper proposes a Multi-temporal coordinated optimal operation method for basin-scale hydro-PV complementary systems considering hydro-electric coupling, effectively enhancing the economic efficiency and stability of distribution network operation. Firstly, the hydro-PV complementary system containing multiple types of hydropower units and its hydro-electric coupling characteristics are analyzed, revealing the interaction mechanism between the spatiotemporal distribution constraints of basin runoff and the power network topology and power flow constraints. Secondly, under the premise of safe and stable grid operation, a coordinated optimal operation model for the hydro-PV complementary system is constructed with the core objective of maximizing distributed PV consumption. A regulation framework is built using differentiated time windows, and an iterative feedback correction mechanism is designed to achieve rolling optimization of strategies. Finally, a typical concurrent rainy and hot season weekly scenario of an actual basin-scale hydro-PV complementary system in Zhejiang is analyzed as a case study to verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: A Blockchain-Based Information Architecture for Next-Generation Relay Protection in Smart Grids
authors: Huaning Zhang, Hong Zhao, Qiannan Chen, Chong Sun, Xiaojing Guo, Lei Fan, Ruochen Shi, Mengqi Zhou, Wangfei Ge, Peng Xu, Junyan Hu, Yannong Xu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Despite growing blockchain adoption in energy markets, its integration into critical operational functions — particularly relay protection— remains limited. Existing solutions fail to meet the stringent requirements of cryptographic auditability, cross-domain coordination, and configuration integrity in hierarchical grid architectures. To address this gap, we propose a three-tier blockchain-based information architecture for relay protection, featuring hybrid PoA+PBFT consensus (1.8 s finality), zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) for privacy-preserving setting verification, and smart contracts for tamper-evident logging. Evaluated on a Hyperledger Fabric v2.5 testbed across provincial, municipal, and substation tiers, the system achieves 40.2% faster hierarchical synchronization and 100% integrity of operation records. While unsuitable for sub-100 ms tripping, the architecture excels in non-real-time workflows such as setting management, fault archiving, and collaborative diagnostics— laying the foundation for secure, auditable, and interoperable protection systems.

title: AI-Powered Customer Churn Prediction in Banking with R-based Gradient Boosting Models and Power BI Dashboards
authors: R. Suyam Praba, P. Suganthi, J. Harrish Samraj, V. K. Arthi, M. Bhuvaneswari
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: In the 21st century, Customer churn is a significant challenge in the banking sector, directly impacting revenue, operational efficiency, and long-term customer relationships. With increasing digital banking adoption and competition from fintech firms, banks must transition from reactive to proactive customer retention strategies. This study leverages machine learning techniques, specifically gradient boosting models such as XGBoost and LightGBM, to predict customer churn based on key demographic, behavioral, and financial attributes. The dataset undergoes rigorous preprocessing and feature engineering in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> to ensure data quality, eliminate inconsistencies, and optimize feature selection. Additionally, Power BI is integrated to create interactive dashboards that provide dynamic and actionable insights for financial institutions. The LightGBM model achieved an accuracy of 90.04 %, precision of 86.2 %, recall of 61.1 %, and F1-score of 71.4 %, outperforming the XGBoost model (accuracy 89%). By implementing predictive analytics, banks can transition from reactive to proactive churn management, optimizing customer retention strategies and enhancing business sustainability. This research highlights how machine learning-powered predictive modeling can provide a data-driven framework for improving banking sector customer loyalty and mitigating churn risks. The seamless integration of R-driven predictive analytics and Power BI visualization enables banking institutions to convert analytical findings into data-driven strategies, bridging the gap between quantitative modeling and executive decision-making [1].

title: Quantized Bessel Phase and Embedded Gaussian Tapering for Near-Field Wireless Power Transfer Using Low-Cost Metasurface Lenses
authors: Sakib Reza, Azin Hojjati, Ifana Mahbub
abstract: This paper presents the experimental demonstration of a low-cost metasurface lens for near-field wireless power transfer (WPT) at 24 GHz. The lens is horn-fed and engineered to embed quantized spherical-phase control and Gaussian amplitude tapering directly into its physical structure. Both functionalities are realized through geometric variation in cross-shaped unit cells distributed across a 10λ × 10λ aperture. This dual-control strategy enhances near-field focusing by mitigating quantization artifacts and edge diffraction effects. Fabricated using standard PCB processes, the metasurface achieves efficient energy convergence without auxiliary feed shaping. Measurement results confirm a tightly confined beam sustained over a 7.6λ depth and a 5.8 dB enhancement in transmission coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert S_{21}\vert$</tex> relative to free-space propagation. These findings validate the effectiveness of geometry-driven amplitude tapering in improving spatial energy localization for compact WPT systems.

title: Enhancing Resilience in Industrial Control Systems: Rapid Attack Detection, Recovery, and Monotonicity Preservation Through STL-GT Online Monitoring
authors: Chidi Agbo, Hoda Mehrpouyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: This paper addresses the pressing challenges of building secure and resilient Industrial Control Systems (ICS) by focusing on rapid attack detection, recovery, and maintaining monotonicity in online monitoring systems. Traditional methods often fail to quickly identify threats, leaving systems vulnerable to prolonged downtime. Specifically, classical online monitoring systems struggle to preserve monotonicity, as they typically detect only isolated anomalies during operation, which can mask subsequent deviations in system behavior. This research presents an advanced approach to resilient cyber-physical systems (CPS) that utilizes Signal Temporal Logic with Global Time (STL-GT) in combination with an Autotuning mechanism for robust realtime monitoring, multi-point anomaly detection, and recovery. This innovative framework enables prompt detection of attacks and faults, along with rapid system recovery. Our STL-GT online monitoring system incorporates a syntactic separation technique based on a partition rule to address monotonicity issues and improve the system's ability to detect and respond to multiple anomalies during system execution. The autotuning mechanism, based on Internal Model Control (IMC) PID tuning rules, enables swift and adaptive system recovery in the event of disruptions. We validated our methodology using the Tennessee Eastman Plant model, a complex chemical plant simulator designed to study industrial processes and control systems. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach provides effective security and resilience of the CPS by consistently detecting anomalies and restoring the system with zero overshoot and minimal oscillations, eliminating violation masking and missed alarms. These findings highlight the effectiveness of our approach in fortifying critical systems against adversarial attacks and operational faults.

title: Factors Influencing Small-Scale Farmers' Intention to Adopt the Internet of Things
authors: Olorato Heavenly Jobeta, Thabang Edwin Maruping, Olebogeng Ambrocius Baikgaki
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: The South African government places great importance on livestock as a key agricultural initiative to address poverty among rural residents, considering high unemployment rates. This research aims to investigate factors influencing small-scale farmers' intention to adopt the Internet of Things to prevent stock theft. The problem is that small-scale farmers remain at risk of stock theft due to low adoption of IoT technologies, which can harm their economic and social well-being. The research methodology of this study is quantitative and uses a survey research strategy to collect data through a structured, closed-ended questionnaire. A random sampling strategy was employed, where a sample size of 304 potential participants was calculated from a population of 1,449 small-scale farmers in the Mafikeng area, North-West Province. However, despite the low response rate, the findings indicate that effort expectancy (EE) and facilitating conditions (FC) are significant drivers of technology adoption. To improve adoption and support for farmers to utilize IoT for livestock theft, it is important to address the challenges, such as a lack of financial support, poor internet connectivity, and a lack of training.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning for Advanced Network Security in Cybersecurity Systems
authors: V. Sangeetha, M. Nalini, V. Jayashree, G. S. Geethamani, J. Divya Jose, R. Saravanakumar
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The "darknet," a segment of the internet often regarded as inaccessible for communication between computers by users, is sometimes referenced by cyber intelligence agencies. Assessing potential hazards to the network should precede any efforts to strengthen its defenses. This paper presents a novel approach to machine learning (ML) classification known as stacking ensemble learning, designed for the evaluation and classification of darknet data. This research achieved a 98% accuracy rate in distinguishing between Darknet and innocuous traffic by using ensembles of artificial intelligence (AI) techniques on the newly available CIC-Darknet2020 dataset. It had a 97% success rate in precisely identifying the individual software type responsible for the Darknet traffic. To enhance our comprehension of Darknet patterns of traffic, they used a game-theory-based methodology to evaluate the effects of features on ML models. The dataset's creators have verified that our study is unparalleled, to the best of any individual's understanding.

title: Training-free Dimensionality Reduction via Feature Truncation: Enhancing Efficiency in Privacy-preserving Multi-Biometric Systems
authors: Florian Bayer, Maximilian Russo, Christian Rathgeb
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Biometric recognition is widely used, making the privacy and security of extracted templates a critical concern. Biometric Template Protection schemes, especially those utilizing Homomorphic Encryption, introduce significant computational challenges due to increased workload. Recent advances in deep neural networks have enabled state-of-the-art feature extraction for face, fingerprint, and iris modalities. The ubiquity and affordability of biometric sensors further facilitate multi-modal fusion, which can enhance security by combining features from different modalities. This work investigates the biometric performance of reduced multi-biometric template sizes. Experiments are conducted on an in-house virtual multi-biometric database, derived from DNN-extracted features for face, fingerprint, and iris, using the FRGC, MCYT, and CASIA databases. The evaluated approaches are (i) explainable and straightforward to implement under encryption, (ii) training-free, and (iii) capable of generalization. Dimensionality reduction of feature vectors leads to fewer operations in the Homomorphic Encryption (HE) domain, enabling more efficient encrypted processing while maintaining biometric accuracy and security at a level equivalent to or exceeding single-biometric recognition. Our results demonstrate that, by fusing feature vectors from multiple modalities, template size can be reduced by 67 % with no loss in Equal Error Rate (EER) compared to the best-performing single modality.

title: Optimizing Self-Organizing Map Classification with Grey Wolf and Modified Grey Wolf Optimization
authors: Roseline Oluwaseun Ogundokun, Rotimi-Williams Bello, Pius Adewale Owolawi, Etienne A. van Wyk, Chunling Tu
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: The global outbreak of COVID-19 has underscored the urgent need for reliable, efficient, and automated diagnostic tools. Chest radiographs provide a noninvasive and widely available modality for early detection, yet accurate classification remains challenging due to high data dimensionality and overlapping features between normal and infected cases. This study investigates the use of Grey Wolf Optimiser-based Self-Organising Maps (GWOSOM) and its modified variant (MGWOSOM) for COVID-19 chest radiograph classification. A balanced dataset of 7,232 images (3,616 normal and 3,616 COVID-19) was preprocessed into numerical representations and divided using a 70:30 training-testing split. Both models were optimised for feature selection and classification. Experimental results reveal that GWOSOM achieved superior performance, with an overall accuracy of 94%, precision and recall averaging 0.94, and an F 1 -score of 0.94, albeit with longer computation time (16 hours for 5,000 epochs). Conversely, MGWOSOM attained a slightly lower accuracy of 90% but demonstrated faster convergence, reducing training time to 7 hours for the same number of epochs. Confusion matrices further highlight GWOSOM's balanced performance across both classes, while MGWOSOM favoured sensitivity for COVID-19 cases at the cost of reduced precision for normal cases. These findings emphasise the tradeoff between accuracy and computational efficiency in medical AI systems. The study contributes to the growing body of knowledge on metaheuristic optimisation for medical image classification, offering insights into selecting appropriate algorithms under varying clinical and computational constraints.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Phishing Attacks: A Study of Machine Learning Methods and Their Effectiveness in E-Commerce
authors: Happy M Twala, Tonderai Muchenje, Solly P Maswikaneng, Bongisizwe E Buthelezi
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Phishing continues to represent one of the most pervasive and costly forms of cybercrime, exploiting human vulnerabilities to obtain sensitive information such as login credentials, banking details, and credit card numbers. Cybercriminals employ diverse attack vectors, including emails, websites, SMS messages, and voice communication, to deceive individuals and organisations. These threats are not only increasing in frequency but are also becoming more sophisticated, often serving as a gateway to other cyberattacks such as ransomware. This study conducts a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) to provide a comprehensive analysis of existing mitigation strategies, dominant phishing vectors, and emerging research gaps. The review adhered to Kitchenham's guidelines for systematic reviews and followed the PRISMA 2020 framework to ensure transparency and rigour. An extensive search across five major databases IEEE Xplore, ACM Digital Library, SpringerLink, ScienceDirect, and Wiley Online Library, initially yielded 1,240 records, complemented by 30 additional sources identified through manual reference searches. After removing 220 duplicates, 1,050 studies were screened by title and abstract, leading to the exclusion of 720 irrelevant records. A full-text review of 330 articles resulted in the exclusion of 130 that lacked methodological detail, were nonpeer reviewed, or demonstrated limited relevance. Ultimately, 200 high-quality studies met the inclusion criteria and were synthesised for analysis. Findings indicate that mitigation strategies can be categorised into three broad groups: antiphishing concepts, paradigms, and human-centered tactics. Websites and emails emerged as the most prominent phishing vectors. The review further proposes anti-phishing guidelines and highlights unresolved issues, offering a foundation for advancing cybersecurity research and practice.

title: Memory Trace Synthesis via Deep Learning for Memory Controller Simulation
authors: Xiaofen Hua, Yaze Song, Jundi Zou, Shengtao Wu, Yuheng Zhao, Peng Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: As a bridge between processors and memory, the memory controller is crucial for system performance, which is determined by memory access behavior. To guide its design and optimization, the memory controller requires workloads that reflect real access patterns. However, in traditional simulation environments, workloads are often manually designed with simple access patterns (e.g., random or sequential), making it difficult to model the complex behaviors of real workloads effectively. Using real workloads is also impractical due to the challenges of data transfer and large-scale storage. To address these issues, we propose the first deep learning framework for automated memory trace generation in memory controller verification. Our approach introduces two key innovations: 1)Jointly modeling bank switching behavior and row spatial locality to capture fine-grained memory access patterns; 2)Decomposing physical memory traces into 12 timing-sensitive mode streams (e.g., Cross-Bank-Write-After-Read) instead of injecting random read/write operations, which accurately replicates real-world read/write overlap characteristics. Experiments show that synthetic traces closely match real workloads in page hit rate ($99.2 \%$ similarity), read/write distribution (KLD $=0.119$), throughput ($98.7 \%$ similarity), bus utilization ($98.6 \%$ similarity), and latency ($98.8 \%$ similarity). Deployed in an HPC SoC DDR controller verification flow, our framework replaces physical traces while maintaining workload validity and avoiding massive data collection.

title: Impact of Chanting on the Human Brain Using EEG Analysis and Psychological Assessment
authors: Deepali Chauhan, Shelly Sachdeva, Sandep Kumar, Upasna
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The Gayatri Mantra (GM)was first mentioned in the Rig Veda. It holds a central place due to its reputed cognitive and spiritual significance. Many studies explore the impact of Vedic chanting; however, there is a gap in objective neurological measures and robust psychological evaluations. The practice of mantra chanting has been found to promote mental focus and emotional equilibrium, especially due to its vibrational resonance. This pilot study investigates the impact of chanting on the human brain using an electroencephalogram (EEG) study and psychological assessment among meditation-naïve individuals. This study includes 21 college graduates out of them, 10 individuals volunteered for EEG recordings before and aftera10-minute Gayatri Mantra chanting session to evaluate immediate changes in brainwave activity. All 21 participants underwent a 60-day daily chanting routine. Anxiety levels were assessed pre- and post-intervention using Sinha's Comprehensive Anxiety Test (SCAT). EEG data revealed increased beta and gamma band power post-chanting, suggesting heightened cognitive alertness and mental engagement. Concurrently, psychological assessment showed a measurable reduction in anxiety scores following the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0}$</tex>-day chanting period. These results indicate that even a short period of mantra chanting may induce beneficial changes in brain activity and emotional state. Its assessment for sustained results supports its potential as a non-pharmacological practice for enhancing mental wellbeing.

title: Decoding the Decoders: An Empirical Study of Reverse Engineering Questions on Stack Exchange

authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)

title: Fusion Method of Digital Terrain Classification and Deep Learning in Power Transmission Line Path Optimization
authors: Rui He, Gengbin Zhang, Tianyou Yan, Xin Wan, Qi Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This study aims to address the challenge of optimizing power transmission line paths in complex terrain environments by integrating digital terrain classification with deep learning techniques to enhance the economic feasibility and viability of path planning. The method involves combining SRTM digital elevation data with Landsat 8 multispectral imagery to construct a multi-dimensional feature matrix. A conditional generative adversarial network (cGAN) is used to generate terrain traversability cost maps, and an improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is designed for path optimization. Experimental results show that the cost map generated by the proposed method achieves an RMSE of 0.023 and an SSIM of 0.892 on the test set. The optimized path's total length is reduced by 8.3%, the cumulative elevation gain is decreased by 33.7%, the length of path through higher-cost areas is reduced by 65%, and the overall engineering cost is lowered by 21.9%. The results confirm that this method can effectively improve the accuracy of path optimization and the economic feasibility of the project, providing reliable technical support for the intelligent design of power transmission lines.

title: Stability Analysis of Medium Voltage DC integrated Power Systems Based on A Semi-Supervised Learning Framework
authors: Zhou Lepei, Fang Hao, Zhao Zekai, Tian Zhen, Zha Xiaoming
abstract: Shipboard medium-voltage DC integrated power systems (MVDC-IPS) offer advantages such as high efficiency, high power density, and flexible controllability, making them an important development direction for future shipboard power systems. However, their complex structure, strong nonlinear characteristics, and variable operating conditions pose significant challenges for identifying the system's stable state. Traditional physics-based methods rely on precise modeling and are difficult to adapt to the dynamic changes of high-dimensional nonlinear systems. Furthermore, fully supervised learning in deep learning relies on large amounts of labeled data, which is costly. To this end, this paper proposes a framework for intelligent stable-state identification based on semi-supervised learning (SSL). This framework constructs a mean teacher (MT) model that integrates virtual adversarial training (VAT) and spectral normalization (SN). This model generates pseudo-labels through a teacher network and utilizes consistency regularization to mine potential information in unlabeled data, significantly reducing its reliance on labeled samples. VAT is introduced to enhance the model's robustness to input perturbations, and the Lipschitz constant of the SN constraint network is combined to improve training stability. The proposed method was validated on simulation data of a single-source, single-load Boost-Buck cascade circuit. The results show that the proposed method achieves a test accuracy of 97.22% using only a small amount of labeled data, outperforming fully supervised learning methods. Furthermore, VAT and SN exhibit synergistic effects, effectively improving the model's generalization and robustness. This study provides a feasible solution for reducing data annotation costs and improving intelligent discrimination performance in power system simulation analysis.

title: WaveVerif: Acoustic Side-Channel Based Verification of Robotic Workflows
authors: Zeynep Yasemin Erdogan, Shishir Nagaraja, Chuadhry Mujeeb Ahmed, Ryan Shah
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: In this paper, we present a framework that uses acoustic side-channel analysis (ASCA) to monitor and verify whether a robot correctly executes its intended commands. We develop and evaluate a machine-learning-based workflow verification system that uses acoustic emissions generated by robotic movements. The system can determine whether real-time behavior is consistent with expected commands. The evaluation takes into account movement speed, direction, and microphone distance. The results show that individual robot movements can be validated with over 80% accuracy under baseline conditions using four different classifiers: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Deep Neural Network (DNN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Additionally, workflows such as pick-and-place and packing could be identified with similarly high confidence. Our findings demonstrate that acoustic signals can support real-time, low-cost, passive verification in sensitive robotic environments without requiring hardware modifications.

title: Privacy at Scale in Networked Healthcare
authors: M. Amin Rahimian, Benjamin Panny, James B.D. Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Digitized, networked healthcare promises earlier detection, precision therapeutics, and continuous care; yet, it also expands the surface for privacy loss and compliance risk. We argue for a shift from siloed, application-specific protections to privacy-bydesign at scale, centered on decision-theoretic differential privacy (DP) across the full healthcare data lifecycle; network-aware privacy accounting for interdependence in people, sensors, and organizations; and compliance-as-code tooling that lets health systems share evidence while demonstrating regulatory due care. We synthesize the privacy-enhancing technology (PET) landscape in health (federated analytics, DP, cryptographic computation), identify practice gaps, and outline a deployable agenda involving privacy-budget ledgers, a control plane to coordinate PET components across sites, shared testbeds, and PET literacy, to make lawful, trustworthy sharing the default. We illustrate with use cases (multi-site trials, genomics, disease surveillance, mHealth) and highlight distributed inference as a workhorse for multi-institution learning under explicit privacy budgets.

title: Compact 3D-SiP Power IC Packaging with Thermal Performance Improvement
authors: Poyu Tsai, Hsin-Han Lin, I-Cheng Huang, Hung-Hsien Huang, Cheng-Nan Lin, Gavin Kao, Shihwen Lu, Alexcc Wang, Arbie Chuang, Harrison Chang
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate a 3D-SiP Power IC packaging approach that achieves a 20 % reduction in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x-y$</tex> footprint while maintaining electrical performance. The vertical stacking of components allows for greater power density and compact system integration, making it suitable for nextgeneration power modules. Electrical measurements confirm no degradation in efficiency, indicating that the miniaturization does not compromise functional performance. However, thermal challenges emerge due to increased power density and limited heat dissipation paths. To address this, comprehensive thermal simulations were conducted to analyze heat accumulation and identify critical hot spots. Optimized thermal management solutions were implemented, including enhanced heat spreading layers and improved thermal vias. These improvements reduced the junction-to-board thermal resistance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\Psi}_{\text {JB) }}$</tex>) from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$31.62^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{W}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23.35^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{W}$</tex>, effectively mitigating thermal impact and ensuring reliable operation. The proposed solution highlights the trade-offs and benefits of vertical integration in power ICs and provides a guideline for future compact power system designs.

title: Estimation of Power Bus Resonance Suppression by Lossy Resonator Filter in the Wi-Fi Band Using Equivalent Circuit Model
authors: Sho Kanao, Yoshitaka Toyota
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: We investigated the power bus resonance suppression effect obtained by implementing a lossy resonator filter tuned to a resonant frequency of 2.45GHz, which corresponded to the center frequency of the 2.4GHz band used for Wi-fi communication. When we compared the suppression amount values obtained by the estimation formula based on the equivalent circuit model and full-wave analysis, the discrepancy was less than approximately 2dB, and indicates relatively high accuracy due to the absence of higher-order resonances. Therefore, it was found that even when the resonant frequency of the lossy resonator filter does not match the anti-resonant frequency of the power bus, the suppression amount can still be estimated within approximately 2dB. This is useful for deriving a bandwidth estimation formula for the suppression characteristics of the lossy resonator filter.

title: Customer Segmentation in E-Commerce Through Data-Driven Customer Behavior Analysis Employing Unsupervised Machine Learning Models
authors: Tinashe Crispen Garidzira, Courage Matobobo, William Tichaon Vambe
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Understanding and dividing up the consumer base according to their behavior is important for strategic decision-making and tailored marketing in the ever-changing world of e-commerce. By utilizing the Cross-Industry Standard Process for Data Mining methodology, this study uses unsupervised machine learning techniques to present a datadriven approach to customer segmentation. This study uses the publicly available “Shop Customer Data” dataset from Kaggle, comprising 1,965 customers with demographic (age, gender, profession, family size) and behavioral (income, spending score, work experience) features. Unsupervised machine learning algorithms such as K-Means, Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM), Spectral Clustering, and Agglomerative Clustering were applied to segment customers. The Silhouette Scores were used to evaluate the clusters' quality. Agglomerative Clustering achieved the lowest Silhouette Score (0.1042), while Spectral Clustering performed best (0.1297), closely followed by GMM (0.1283) and KMeans (0.1281). These results indicate that unsupervised clustering can uncover meaningful customer segments in e-commerce, even when separation is moderate. This highlights the potential of machine learning in transforming raw data into actionable insights, enabling intelligent retail systems that support targeted marketing strategies and deliver personalized customer experiences.

authors: N/A

title: A Machine Learning Model for Classification in Cybersecurity Networks
authors: Precious. M Leutle, Olalekan Samuel. Ogunleye
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: As organisations across sectors digitise operations and interconnect systems at scale, their exposure to network vulnerabilities grows accordingly. This study addresses that risk by developing an automated, machine-learning-based mechanism for cyber-threat detection tailored to high-volume, heterogeneous cybersecurity data. We design a classification pipeline that learns discriminative patterns from large, streaming network telemetry and flags malicious activity with minimal latency. The approach is evaluated through a quantitative comparison of six widely used classification algorithms. Model selection is driven by a multi-metric assessment-accuracy, precision, and recall-that balances overall correctness with the reliable identification of true threats. The best-performing classifier is then instantiated as the core of the proposed detection model. The results demonstrate that systematically leveraging machine learning on large-scale data network streams can enhance proactive defense by improving threat identification and facilitating timely mitigation. The resulting model is sector-agnostic and scalable, providing a practical path for organisations that process large volumes of data to enhance their cybersecurity posture.

title: Authors' Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)

title: Scalable Vacuum-Deposited Organic Photovoltaics for Powering Miniaturized Electronics
authors: Suresh Madduri, Satish Bonam, Dungaram D, Sai Santosh Kumar Raavi, Shiv Govind Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Thermal vacuum deposition of organic photovoltaic (OPV) cells offers superior batch-to-batch reproducibility compared to conventional solution-processed counterparts, making it a promising fabrication route for reliable device manufacturing. In this work, we demonstrate a scalable methodology for integrating thermally evaporated OPV cells into series and parallel configurations to form functional modules. This integration strategy enables enhanced device performance, achieving open-circuit voltages of up to 2.45 V under AM1.5G (1 sun) illumination and 2.95 V under a 40 W LED source, suitable for indoor lighting conditions. Additionally, the modules exhibit short-circuit currents of 0.65 mA (1 sun) and 1.05 mA (40 W LED), highlighting their potential for low-power electronic applications.

title: Signal Integrity Analysis of High-Speed Chiplet Interconnection Considering Surface Roughness Based on Huray Model
authors: Jonghyeon Lee, Jiwoon Moon, Yuchul Jung, Uichan Kim, Sungbum Kim, Youngwoo Kim
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: In this paper, we conduct a signal integrity (SI) analysis for universal chiplet interconnect express (UCIe)-based high-speed interconnection, considering surface roughness. A simulation-based analysis is performed using the Huray surface model to investigate the effect of surface roughness. As surface roughness increased, voltage transfer function (VTF) exhibited a degradation of up to −2.785 dB at the Nyquist frequency. Consequently, the eye-height decreased by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 3} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> and the jitter increased by 6.1%, leading to a violation of the universal chiplet interconnect express (UCIe) advanced package specification. Our work demonstrates the impact of surface roughness on SI in high-wire-density environments in chiplet interfaces with stringent design requirements and emphasizes the importance of incorporating realistic surface roughness conditions into the analysis for practical designs.

title: A ±60mA-Inaccuracy Low-Side Average Current Sensor with Operating-Conditions-Insensitive Control Supporting 0.1-to-3A Load Range and Sub-100ns Sample Time for Automotive USB Charge Application
authors: Jian-Jun Kuang, Xin Ming, Xin-Ce Gong, Tian-Chen Lang, Xiang Geng, Bo Zhang
abstract: In Paper 21.6, UESTC and SouthChip Semiconductor Technology present a low-side average current sensor for automotive USB charging, which supports 0.1-to-3A load range and sub100ns sample time with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 60 \text{mA}$</tex> inaccuracy. This is achieved by 1) an adaptive settling helper to reduce the settling delay, 2) a DC level-shift bias to improve the light-load current sense accuracy, and 3) an anti-duty resistor to suppress the drift of −3dB bandwidth.

title: Deep Learning Approaches to Contour Extraction in Noisy SEM Images for Advanced OPC Modeling
authors: Raghavendra Botta, Aditya Jain, Prakash Deep
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Accurate extraction of feature contours from low-contrast, noisy scanning electron microscope (SEM) images is critical for advanced optical proximity correction (OPC) in semiconductor manufacturing. Traditional CD-SEM (Critical Dimension SEM) methods provide high-resolution, site-specific measurements but are limited by operational overhead and insufficient 2D pattern coverage. Recent advances leverage deep learning—particularly convolutional neural networks such as HED, RCF, and DexiNed—to enable direct, single-pixel contour extraction from SEM imagery, overcoming the limitations of classic edge detection techniques.This work demonstrates that deep-learning-based pipelines substantially improve contour fidelity, model calibration, and robustness for complex semiconductor layouts, while simultaneously reducing data requirements and operational cost. The methods discussed are validated on multiple industrial datasets and show strong generalization across different process layers and noise conditions. By adopting these machine learning approaches, process engineers can achieve more reliable, automated OPC modeling, better design-to-manufacturing matching, and support the scaling needs of next-generation semiconductor technologies.

title: Bit-Width Agnostic Parallel In-Memory Adder with O(1) Latency Using STT-MRAM
authors: Ravi S. Siddanath, Deepak Joshi, Manish Goswami, Kavindra Kandpal
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel in-memory adder architecture based on Spin-Transfer Torque Magnetic Random Access Memory (STT-MRAM) to perform high-speed parallel addition with bit-width-independent latency. In contrast to traditional adder designs, where latency increases with operand width, the proposed approach achieves a fixed latency (O(1)) of just 4.28 nanoseconds (measured at the SS process corner), using only two Sense Amplifier Enable (SAE) pulses—irrespective of bit length. The architecture exploits the intrinsic parallelism and non-volatility of STT-MRAM to perform in-memory computation (IMC), reducing data movement and enhancing energy efficiency. Analytical and simulation results validate the feasibility and advantages of the proposed adder, making it well-suited for next-generation computing systems that require ultra-fast arithmetic operations and minimal energy overhead.

title: A Survey and Taxonomy of Privacy-Preserving IoT Anomaly Detection Systems Using Federated Learning and Blockchain for Sustainable Next Generation Networks
authors: Keziah kerona J, Nagendrakumar D
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is expanding at an unprecedented pace, making anomaly detection vital for ensuring security, reliability, and efficiency in smart systems. However, existing surveys tend to address Federated Learning (FL) or Blockchain in isolation, without providing an integrated perspective on privacy, anomaly detection, energy efficiency, and sustainable deployment. This paper fills that gap by presenting a systematic review of Blockchain-enabled FL approaches for IoT anomaly detection. We introduce a novel hierarchical taxonomy of privacy-preserving methods that highlights key architectural trends and technological synergies. A critical analysis of state-of-the-art techniques is conducted, focusing on privacy protection, energy efficiency, scalability, and the feasibility of real-world deployment. The study also identifies persistent research gaps, such as interoperability challenges and computational overhead in resource-constrained IoT environments. Finally, we propose a forward-looking roadmap to guide the development of sustainable and secure next-generation IoT ecosystems. This survey uniquely emphasizes originality, practicality, and sustainability relevance, making it highly timely for emerging smart infrastructures.

title: BDAMEA 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)

title: Wireless Sensor Network Architecture Design Method Based on Hybrid Channel Allocation
authors: Gulin Xia, Maoheng Jing, Xiaoxia Li, Shuai Shao, Zhongfeng Wang, Ligang Li, Shijie Cui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The heterogeneous wireless sensor networks (HWSNs) have a wide range of applications, especially for event monitoring and detection when wired communication devices cannot be set up. With the increasing scale of wireless communication networks, communication congestion and data packets are prone to occur in traditional single network architectures. Therefore, researchers urgently need to find new solutions that can improve network throughput and packet transmission rate. This article proposes a wireless sensor network architecture design method based on hybrid channel allocation to achieve efficient path selection. This method integrates time division multiple access (TDMA), frequency division multiple access (FDMA), and self-organizing routing planning protocols, which can effectively reduce the communication delay of various devices in complex communication networks.

title: Research on Mixed-precision Quantization Methods for Transformer Models
authors: Kelin Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: In Transformer models have demonstrated increasingly prominent performance in visual tasks, yet different layers exhibit significant variations in sensitivity to quantization errors. Uniform low-precision quantization leads to severe accuracy degradation, while global high-precision quantization fails to fully leverage hardware acceleration potential. To address this issue, this paper proposes a mixed-precision quantization method based on sensitivity analysis. By comprehensively evaluating gradients and the Hessian matrix, the method accurately identifies the sensitivity of different operators to quantization. High-sensitivity modules are assigned INT16 precision, while low-sensitivity modules are assigned INT8 precision. Experiments conducted on the CwinTransformer_tiny model and the ImageNet_train2012_200 dataset (containing 200 categories, with approximately 650 training samples per category) demonstrate that the mixed-precision quantization method achieves an optimal balance between inference efficiency and accuracy. All experiments were conducted with 100 repeated tests, and quantization accuracy was validated by calculating the cosine similarity between the quantized model outputs and the FP32 baseline. This study provides an interpretable and reproducible quantization solution for the efficient deployment of Transformer models in resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Design of a High-Performance SAR ADC-Based Column-Level Digital Readout Circuit
authors: Xiujuan Bian, Jingguo Li, Yong Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: In response to the challenges of signal transmission interference and limited bandwidth stemming from the trend toward large-scale, high-frame-rate infrared focal plane arrays (IRFPAs), this paper presents the design of a high-performance successive approximation register analog-todigital converter (SAR ADC) suitable for column-level digital readout. The design incorporates a segmented capacitor array structure to reduce area and implements redundancy to mitigate dynamic errors. Power consumption is reduced through a Vcmbased switching scheme, while performance is further optimized by employing an improved double-tail dynamic comparator and asynchronous control to increase speed. Simulated using a 180 nm CMOS process, the ADC operates at a 1.8 V supply voltage and a $\mathbf{1 0 ~ M S} / \mathbf{s}$ sampling rate. Despite adhering to a $\mathbf{3 0} \boldsymbol{\mu}$ m pixel pitch constraint, the design achieves an ENOB of 13.72 bits, a SNDR of 84.38 dB, and the calculated Figure of Merit (FoM) is 6.11 fJ/conversion-step, offering an effective solution for the digital readout of large-scale IRFPAs.

title: A low-cost 3D flexible multifunctional sensor based on composite fiber
authors: Keyun Yi, Shuhuan Deng, Ningjing Wang, Enchong Ning, Yu Pang, Qinping Qiang, Cong Zhao, Bitao Li u, Guoquan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: To address the limitations of existing flexible piezoresistive sensors, developing low-cost, high-performance flexible piezoresistive sensors remains a critical challenge. We successfully fabricated PANI@Paper sensors by removing calcium carbonate filler from carbon paper using hydrochloric acid, followed by impregnation of porous paper. Due to the effective integration of PANI with the multi-layered reticular microstructure of the substrate significantly, electrical testing confirmed compliance with Ohm’s law-exhibiting linear I-V curves under varying pressure conditions across the $\mathbf{0 - 1 2 5 ~ k P a}$ detection range. The sensor delivers stable electronic signals across multiple frequencies ($0.33 \mathrm{~Hz}, 0.46 \mathrm{~Hz}, 0.75 \mathrm{~Hz}, 1.28 \mathrm{~Hz}$) and exhibits no signal attenuation after over 1000 cycles of 30 $\mathbf{k P a}$ pressure testing. It captures faint pulse signals, tracks elbow/finger motion trajectories, and distinguishes finger-press frequencies (high resolution/repeatability). It maintains stable response in 30 %-100 % relative humidity environments and identifies normal/rapid/deep breathing patterns. Combining balanced performance with manufacturing simplicity, low cost, and structural flexibility, this sensor holds broad application prospects in human health monitoring and real-time tracking, poised to accelerate the industrialization of wearable devices.

title: Emotion Aware Chatbot-Echosense Using AI
authors: Sridharan K, Suseendra R, Kaviya S P, Keerthana A, Nithyashree G
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: In today's modern world people are becoming more involved in both personal and professional obligations in technological world, which results in a decrease in emotional connection, a lack of communication, and an increase in loneliness. Many people suffer from low selfesteem, emotional detachment, and vulnerability to emotional distress or cyberbullying as real human contact is replaced by digital interactions. Microaggressionsinadvertent but harmful expressions-are a subtle but significant problem in these interactions. In this paper, we present “EchoSense,” a conversational AI system that enhances emotional intelligence and mental health through empathetic dialogue. The system uses the ideas of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Emotional Intelligence (EI) to identify loneliness, identify microaggressions, and encourage positive behaviors like kindness and gratitude. A custom-labeled text dataset covered the primary emotion categories, including neutral, anger, sadness, and gratitude. EchoSense uses rule-based emotion classification, tokenization, and sentiment scoring to generate contextually relevant responses. Experimental evaluation generated emotion detection accuracy above 85%, confirming the system's reliability for emotionally aware communication.

title: A Comprehensive Survey on 5G Network Slicing: Techniques, Challenges, and Reinforcement Learning Approaches
authors: Rajavel S, G Vairavel, Pradheep Balaji R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The fifth-generation (5G) wireless network brings a significant shift in mobile communication by introducing network slicing, which allows multiple logical networks to operate over a single physical infrastructure. This capability enables service providers to deliver customized network performance for varied application categories such as enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB), Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communication (URLLC), and massive Machine-Type Communication (mMTC), each requiring specific levels of latency, reliability, and bandwidth. Leveraging technologies like Software-Defined Networking (SDN), Network Function Virtualization (NFV), and edge computing, slicing facilitates isolated, on-demand services across diverse use cases. Despite its advantages, the practical deployment of network slicing faces several hurdles, including slice orchestration, scalability, interference management, and optimal resource allocation. Traditional static solutions fall short in dynamic environments, prompting a shift towards intelligent, automated frameworks. In recent years, Machine Learning (ML), particularly Reinforcement Learning (RL), has been increasingly explored to enable adaptive and real-time slicing strategies. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of current developments in 5G network slicing, emphasizing architectural models, key technical barriers, optimization techniques, and RL-based solutions. It also discusses existing gaps, assesses real-world applicability, and outlines directions for future research.

title: Design of an Ultra-Wideband Out-of-Band Modulated Optical Transmitter with Pilot-Based Offset Point Control Circuit
authors: Xinyu Wang, Binbo Wang, Huan Wang, Siqi Gou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue of insufficient control precision in the two commonly employed bias point control methods for externally modulated optical transmitters. It does so by designing a compact, low-power bias point control module for an externally modulated microwave optical transmitter, providing detailed circuit design specifications. A microwave link test system was constructed on the basis of external modulation principles with a view to validating key performance metrics of the externally modulated optical transmitter with bias point control. These metrics included S-scatter parameters, spurious rejection ratio, harmonic rejection ratio and system noise figure. The results of the tests indicate the following: The S-scatter parameter link gain must be no less than $-\mathbf{2 5 ~ d B}$, with gain flatness set to $\mathbf{\pm 2 ~ d B}$. Furthermore, the spurious rejection ratio must exceed 65 dB, while the harmonic rejection ratio must surpass 50 dB. Finally, the noise figure must be controlled within 2 GHz @ 34 dB to 18 GHz @ 37 dB. In comparison with directly modulated optical transmitters, the high-frequency noise figure enhancement achieves approximately $\mathbf{2 0 ~ d B}$.

title: Automatic Generation of Medical Imaging Diagnostic Report using BLIP Model
authors: Ponsubash Raj R, Thenmozhi E, Mirunalini P
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: The generation of radiology reports forms a very important aspect of medical imaging, which furnishes physicians with timely and accurate diagnostic information. However, interpreting medical images and compiling their findings remains cumbersome for radiologists. To address this challenge, we propose an automatic report generation system based on the BLIP (Bootstrapping Language-Image Pre-training) model. By fine-tuning a pre-trained BLIP image captioning framework using paired chest X-ray images and their corresponding diagnostic reports, the model learns to describe visual findings in clear and clinically relevant text. Experimental results show that our model achieves strong performance with BLEU, METEOR, ROUGE, and RadGraph-F1 scores of 0.5859, 0.4021, 0.6780, and 0.6424 respectively, demonstrating its ability to generate meaningful and clinically grounded descriptions. This work highlights the potential of vision–language models in assisting radiologists and improving the efficiency of clinical workflows.

title: The Creation of an Artificial Intelligence Model for the Sports Map in Tang Dynasty Sports Murals
authors: Qian Qiao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: This research is dedicated to the construction of an artificial intelligence model for the sports map in Tang Dynasty sports murals. The historical significance and cultural value of sports murals in the Tang Dynasty were elaborated in detail. By applying advanced artificial intelligence technologies such as computer vision and deep learning, the creation process of sports map models was deeply explored from data acquisition, feature extraction to model training and evaluation. By introducing a variety of complex algorithms and formulas to optimize and verify the model, the aim is to provide innovative technical means for the study of sports culture in the Tang Dynasty and promote the digital protection and inheritance of cultural heritage.

title: P-CAP: A PUF-Based Decentralized Protocol for Trustworthy IoT Operation and Traceability
authors: Yuchen Zhao, Kun Meng, Daxing Chen, Qiyuan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: With the accelerating digitization of physical assets, ensuring trustworthy verification and traceable recording of critical operations has become a crucial security challenge. Traditional solutions relying on centralized identity systems and third-party trust mechanisms struggle to meet high-trust requirements in decentralized scenarios. Physical Unclonable Functions (PUF), as lightweight hardware security primitives, offer a novel approach to physical identity binding and behavioral authentication.This paper presents P-CAP, a decentralized protocol that leverages PUF to establish trustworthy operations and traceability for IoT assets. The protocol operates through three phases - initialization, operational execution, and archiving - forming a complete behavioral cycle. Through the Joint Trust Response Value and bilateral signature mechanisms, it ensures the authenticity and non-repudiation of operational processes. Finally, security analysis and prototype implementation verify the protocol’s correctness and security.

title: Rainfall Prediction in Data-Scarce Regions of India Using Transfer Learning and Apache Spark
authors: E. Maruthavani, P. Jeyabharathi, Lokeshkumar Ramasamy, C. Ramesh
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Rainfall is a critical parameter affecting different situations in many countries. To predict certain natural calamities like floods, the prediction of rainfall is evident. India is economically driven by agriculture, which is why rainfall patterns play an important role and hence, more precise rainfall prediction techniques in India are necessary. In this research paper, we have used various machine learning models to predict the rainfall in different places in India using Spark's machine learning library. The dataset is extracted from a weather data API, which provides the most recent and accurate weather details. After determining the appropriate factors that affect rainfall for each place, we implement three machine learning models: Linear Regression, Decision Tree Regression, and Gradient Boosted Decision Tree Regression. Then, we provide a comparative analysis based on the performance of each of these models using RMSE and R squared values. Decision Tree Regression also allows further analysis because it reflects each feature's importance when predicting rainfall. The results indicate that Gradient Boosted Decision Tree Regression is the best model in the majority of cases because it offers the highest value for R squared estimation tool, and also the lowest value for the RMSE metric. When considering each parameter's importance in predicting rainfall, “Visibility” feature plays the most important role in most of the cases.

title: UAV for Search and Rescue Operation
authors: Madhavan K, Maragathalakshmi S, Rajaswathi G, Mohana Geetha D
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Search and rescue missions in disaster zones call for quick response, high mobility, and effective target detection. This paper reports the design and development of a low-cost Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) drone specifically suited for search and rescue operations. The UAV employs a Vtail design with both fixed-wing and multirotor capabilities to provide greater stability, lower weight, and higher endurance. The drone has a maximum payload capacity of 1 kg and is equipped for long-lasting flight times as well as effective monitoring in hostile environments. The UAV is run by three 1300 KV Emax motors, which are controlled through a KaramAN 4-in-1 ESC (BLHeli_32, 45A) so that thrust can be optimized with efficiency. There is a SpeedyBee F405 Wing flight controller used for flight maneuverability and stability, while flight transitions between modes of hover to forward flight modes are enabled using Emax ES08MA II analog servos (for the V-tail) and Power HD1711MG servos (motor tilting). A Foxeer Razer Mini camera records live video, processed through an onboard deep learning model to identify and classify survivors as human or animal. The SpeedyBee TX800 video transmitter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(25 ~\text{mW}-800 ~\text{mW}$</tex> power output) transmits video to rescuers, who can view it using an EWRF OTG FPV receiver for real-time situational awareness. The FlySky i6X 10-channel transmitter and receiver offer manual control redundancy for operational reliability. When survivors are detected, their GPS location is sent to rescue personnel by the UAV, reducing search time and optimizing efficiency. The system combines VTOL with AIbased survivor detection, creating an efficient disaster response tool. The paper describes the UAVs architecture, hardware integration, and on-field performance, highlighting its potential to transform emergency rescue operations.

title: Performance Evaluation of Mobile Net-Models on Normal/DR OCT Images
authors: Neal Stephen Reon.C, V. Rajinikanth
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Eye is a vital sensory organ and abnormality in eye will lead to various vision issues. Early diagnosis and treatment is crucial to reduce the eye illness. Diabetic-Retinopathy (DR) is a harsh eye illness due to diabetes and it needs appropriate treatment to reduce its complications. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) is one of the common imaging modality to analyze the eye and deep-learning (DL) based examination is recently adopted technique to identify the normal/DR class images. This research aims to develop a tool for automatic examination of DR from the OCT-data. The different phases of this tool include; image collection and resizing, deep-features extraction with the Mobile Net-model, classification with Soft Max and generation of fused-features (FF) using serial concatenation of reduced features with 50% dropout, and classification and verification using various classifiers. This research considered various Mobile Net-models for the examination and the result with FF helps to achieve better detection accuracy on the considered OCT-database.

title: An Improved Coordinated Control Strategy for Circulating Current Suppression in Parallel-Connected VSGs
authors: Qiang Yang, Yujun Shi
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved coordinated control strategy for multiple parallel-connected Virtual Synchronous Generators (VSGs) to address the issues of inaccurate power sharing and significant circulating currents caused by mismatched between power sharing and line impedance in traditional VSG control. The core of the proposed strategy is twofold. First, an improved VSG control, incorporating both voltage feedback and an integral component, is designed to prevent voltage drop at the point of common coupling. Second, a dynamic virtual complex impedance is introduced to dynamically compensate for line impedance mismatch, thereby achieving both accurate power sharing and circulating current suppression. Simulation results indicate that, under the coordinated control of the improved VSG and the dynamic virtual complex impedance, the improved strategy enables accurate power sharing, suppresses circulating currents, prevents voltage droop, and improves voltage quality.

title: GMCRR_DPNet: A Gradient Multi-Level Convolutional and Residual Attention with a Regularized Weight Decay-Based Dengue Outbreak Prediction Network
authors: Vijayakumar P, Karuppusamy S, Natarajan P, Vanithamani S, Prakash V S, Gokul Chandrasekaran Professor
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This paper presents GMCRR_DPNet, a novel gradient multi-level Convolutional and Residual Attention network designed for predicting dengue outbreaks. The model integrates various data sources, including historical incidence rates, weather conditions, and demographic information, to enhance the accuracy of predictions. By employing advanced machine learning techniques, GMCRR_DPNet captures complex patterns and correlations that traditional methods may overlook. The architecture supports parallel processing of temporal and spatial features, allowing for a comprehensive understanding of the factors influencing dengue transmission. The incorporation of a self-adaptive metaheuristic algorithm (SA-BOA) enables real-time adjustments of model parameters, improving adaptability to changing environmental conditions. The paper addresses critical research gaps, such as the need for better data integration frameworks and the inclusion of diverse climatic variables, to enhance the generalizability of prediction models across different geographic contexts. The performance of GMCRR_DPNet is evaluated using key metrics, demonstrating its potential as a robust tool for public health management, facilitating timely interventions and effective resource allocation in response to dengue outbreaks.

title: An Interleaved Buck-Boost-PWM LLC Converter With Wide Input Voltage Range
authors: Yayu Yang, Hu Zhou, Zhaiyi Shen, Chen Yang, Min Huang, Lixun Zhu, Kangan Wang
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Conference on Advances in Electrical and Power Engineering (ACEPE)
abstract: To accommodate the significant fluctuations in photovoltaic output voltage due to variations in illumination and temperature, and to meet the wide voltage range requirements of DC grid interface converters, this paper proposes an Interleaved Buck-Boost-PWM LLC (IB<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-PWM LLC) converter. This converter is cascaded from a Buck-Boost converter and a PWM LLC converter, featuring a constant switching frequency equal to the resonant frequency of the LLC network. It reduces the number of switching devices by sharing switches. All switches and diodes in the converter achieve zero-voltage-switching and zero-current-switching. Voltage gain is adjusted by modifying the duty cycles of the Buck unit switches and the secondary-side switches of the PWM LLC. The paper first introduces the proposed converter’s topology and provides a detailed analysis of its operating principles across different modes. It then examines the converter’s voltage gain range, concluding with simulations that validate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed solution.

title: High Efficiency Broadband Power Amplifier Based on Generalized Chebyshev
authors: Jiayu Zhong, Yong Fang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: To address the issues of small bandwidth and difficult matching of GaN power amplifiers, the input and output network matching design is achieved based on the generalized Chebyshev matching method. This approach optimizes the cumbersome matching of the prototype Chebyshev, broadens the working bandwidth, and maintains the high efficiency of GaN devices, achieving the optimal fundamental impedance. Meanwhile, a comprehensive explanation and summary of the generalized Chebyshev matching method are provided, and further mathematical outputs are conducted using MATLAB software to facilitate the rapid calculation of specific parameters of the matching sections in the future and shorten the matching time. The results of the layout co-simulation and actual measurement show that the relative bandwidth of the power amplifier can reach $\mathbf{6 0 \%}$, with an output power of $\mathbf{3 7 - 4 1. 6 ~ d B m}$, a gain of $13.3-16.5 \mathrm{~dB}$, and a power-added efficiency of 47 to 66.2% within the operating frequency range. This fully demonstrates that the application of the generalized Chebyshev matching method in matching can achieve excellent results.

title: Intensity-based decoding method of multiplexing Bessel Gaussian beams in turbulence
authors: Wanjun Wang, Jiaheng Chen, Jingqi Cui, Zhensen Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The intensity-based decoding method of multiplexing Bessel Gaussian (BG) beams propagation in turbulence was studied. Based on the beam intensity at different radii, the sinusoidal signals carried by the beam amplitude, Bessel width and the Gaussian width were decoded with a good precision based on nonlinear leasts-quare method. And the advantage of the intensity-based decoding method was that this method was more realizable for the beam intensity was easier to measure than the beam field. The selection of the input signal was investigated to increase the communication quality, and the decoding precision could be improved by decreasing the number of the carrier. This paper provides the theoretical algorithm for the multiplexing application of BG beams in free-space laser communication.

title: Research on a Buck Switching Power Supply Chip with Fast Transient Response
authors: Yuxuan Huang, Zhangyong Li, Yuanfa Wang, Yu Pang, Yong Wang, Weiwei Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: DC-DC switching power supplies are preferred for applications with frequently varying loads, yet conventional counterparts lack sufficient transient response under rapid load or supply changes. To address this, this study explores methods to boost DC-DC transient performance. This study adopts the 180 nm BCD process from Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) to design a peak current-mode Pulse-Width Modulation (PWM) buck converter. Two methods and technologies for improving the system’s transient response are proposed, mainly including the Miller capacitance multiplication technology used in compensation and the adaptive slope compensation technology. The current-mode Miller capacitance multiplication technology is applied for compensation at the output terminal of the error amplifier. When the system is in a steady state, this technology is equivalent to a large capacitor, which ensures the stable operation of the system; when the system undergoes large-signal transient changes, it converts into a small capacitor, which can enhance the system’s transient response capability. For slope compensation, the adaptive slope compensation technology is adopted. Compared with the traditional slope compensation technology, this technology avoids the occurrence of over-compensation and improves the transient response of the system. Simulation results show that it exhibits excellent dynamic response performance: when the load current changes, its transient overshoot is less than 55 mV, and the recovery time is less than $50 \mu$ s.

title: A Novel Diffusion-Guided Underwater Image Enhancement Framework for Marine Debris Detection
authors: Chi Zhang, Wenran Zhang, Chen Liang, Qian Qiao, Jianghan Tao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Marine debris detection is vital for the preservation of ecosystems, but conventional object detectors such as the YOLO family suffer from severe underwater image degradation such as scattering blur, color shifts and low contrast. To solve this problem, we propose UDD-YOLO, a new lightweight underwater debris detection framework based on modified YOLOv12n architecture. UDD-YOLO introduces three new features for enhanced robustness and performance. First, Cold Diffusion Model is used as preprocessing step for the deterministic degradations like blur and loss of contrast, providing high-fidelity inputs for downstream recognition. Second, the detector backbone is upgraded with AMC2f module for robust scale-aware feature extraction for targets of different sizes and shapes in dense scenes. Third, we include UIoU loss function for more precise bounding box localization for occluded and irregular debris. Extensive experiments show that UDD-YOLO achieves a mAP@50 of 81.8% and 87.3% precision, significantly surpassing 11 state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, we maintain a light atomic structure (2.56 M parameters), demonstrating its performance for real-time marine monitoring on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: Federated Tree-Ensemble Learning for Crop Yield Prediction in Low-Connectivity Environments
authors: Ummethala Venkata Datha Sai Bharadwaja
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: This work addresses the emerging demands for intelligent, privacy-preserving, and decentralized decision-making in rural and low-connectivity agricultural scenarios. It proposes a federated tree ensemble learning framework for accurate crop yield prediction over large-scale, non-IID agricultural datasets. The approach leverages the robustness of LightGBM within a federated setting that maintains the localization of raw data while allowing models to collaborate on global learning. The dataset used in this paper was based on district-level agricultural statistics of India, aggregated to a total of more than 345,000 records, and was divided into ten regional clients to simulate realistic heterogeneity. Experimental results show that the proposed Fed-LGB ensemble reaches performance close to centralized benchmarks, with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value of 0.8508, while reducing communication overhead by 56% (from 7.03 MB to 3.10 MB). In addition to accuracy, communication costs, convergence under packet loss, mitigation of client drift, and personalization effects are also studied. The manuscript further provides concise algorithmic descriptions to support reproducibility at critical stages of the federated learning pipeline.

title: HGT-Net: A Hybrid Geometry Transformer Network for Efficient 3D Object Retrieval and Recognition
authors: Jianlin Guo, Chengguang Chen, Kun Liu, Pinhe Wang, Wenxiong Kang, Feiqi Deng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Learning discriminative representations from unstructured point clouds is a fundamental challenge in 3D shape retrieval, primarily due to the inherent trade-off between capturing fine-grained geometric details and modeling longrange dependencies. Existing methods often struggle to balance these aspects: point-based CNNs typically lack global topological understanding, while pure Transformers may overlook highfrequency local variations due to a lack of inductive bias. To address this, we propose the Hybrid Geometry-Transformer Network (HGT-Net), an end-to-end framework that synergizes inductive local encoding with global topological reasoning. Specifically, we design a hierarchical local encoder to preserve detailed surface features, which are then processed by a novel Geometric Context Attention (GCA) module to explicitly model high-order correlations between spatially distant parts. By bridging local geometry and global context, HGT-Net generates robust shape descriptors. Extensive experiments on ModelNet40 and ShapeNet Core55 demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance among point-based methods, significantly outperforming strong baselines in retrieval tasks while maintaining high efficiency.

title: Performance Analysis of Contemporary Single-Ended SRAM Bit-Cells Using CMOS 90nm Technology
authors: Hardik Patel, Mitesh Limachia, Purvang Dalal, Keshav Virpariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Static Random Access Memory (SRAM) is a fundamental component of modern System-on-Chips (SoCs), serving as fast cache and register files. This survey analyzes recently proposed single-ended SRAM cell architectures using the Cadence Virtuoso tool at 90 nm CMOS technology. The analysis focuses on key performance metrics such as Static Noise Margin (SNM), static and dynamic power consumption, and read/write delay. By comparing these trade-offs, the paper offers insights to help designers to choose the optimal cell for specific SoC applications. This work synthesizes existing knowledge to guide future research and enhance the efficiency of next-generation memory technologies.

title: Session 4 Overview: Analog Techniques & Amplifiers
authors: N/A

title: Benefits of 3D-ICs in Scaled-Up Event-Based Hybrid Accelerator Architectures
authors: Sylvain Powis de Tenbossche, Refik Bilgic, Kanishkan Vadivel, Pankaj Upadhyay, Lucas Stefanidis, Dwaipayan Biswas, Dragomir Milojevic
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Emerging edge Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications increasingly suffer from the memory wall, where limited on-chip storage constrains data reuse and leads to inefficient compute-memory interaction with tightly coupled logic and memory interfaces. The efficiency of event-driven accelerators relies on optimal balancing of memory and compute elements. However, scaling both the on-chip memory capacity and compute parallelism to meet the demands of emerging large-scale models remains a significant bottleneck for conventional 2D architecture. In this work, we present the System Technology Co-Optimization (STCO) for a Hybrid Event-Based Accelerators (HEBA) capable of supporting both Spiking Neural Network (SNN) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) "hybrid" workloads. We explore 3D technology, focusing on Memory-on-Logic (MoL) configurations, to scale-up Processing Elements (PEs) & local memory. Using GlobalFoundries 12nm (GF12) node, we evaluate the Power, Performance & Area (PPA). Our results show that although higher PEs and memory counts increase power and degrade frequency in 2D designs, tailored 3D stacking mitigates these penalties by reducing wire length and buffering overheads. This leads to higher compute density and improved energy efficiency.

title: EEG-based User Authentication in Realistic Scenarios: From Solo Reading to Dialog Games
authors: Chi Xu, Xiang Li, Shuqiong Wu, Yasushi Yagi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
