title: CoDICE: Roll the DICE for Firmware Attestation
authors: Rakesh Podder, Jason Simental, Elmaddin Azizli, Bharadwaj Mantha, Indrajit Ray
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: The firmware manifest is a critical file used during firmware updates that contains essential instructions and data about the update. This information allows devices to validate, download, and install the firmware update securely and is a critical component for firmware attestation. A compromised firmware manifest can lead to system-wide vulnerabilities and critical operational failure. Although firmware attestation using trusted hardware such as trusted platform modules (TPM) or trusted execution environments is gaining popularity, existing approaches do not provide support for protecting the firmware manifest. However, these approaches are often impractical in resourceconstrained environments. Moreover, the lack of standardized firmware manifest formats across vendors exacerbates interoperability challenges and increases attack surfaces by exposing sensitive information about vendors' firmware. In this paper, we propose CoDICE, a comprehensive attestation framework that integrates Trusted Computing Group's Concise Reference Integrity Manifest (CoRIM) format with the layered Device Identifier Composition Engine (DICE) architecture. CoDICE is platform<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>We use the term “platform” to mean any computer or hardware device and/or associated operating system, or a virtual environment on which software can be installed and run. Source NISTIR 7698, https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/Legacy/IR/nistir7698.pdf agnostic. It defines a uniform way to transform unstructured vendor-specific manifests into IETF's Concise Data Definition Language (CDDL)-validated CoRIM structures enabling layered, runtime attestations. CoDICE ensures verifiable firmware integrity, runtime security, confidentiality, and enhanced cross-vendor interoperability. Our proof-of-concept implementation demonstrates the security and practicality of the approach, paving the way for structured, formally analyzable firmware update mechanisms in constrained and heterogeneous environments.

title: Optimal Control of Reactive Voltage in Distribution Network Areas Based on Adaptive Weight Multi-Objective Gray Wolf-Tabu Hybrid Algorithm
authors: Ziwei Zhang, Diancheng Qiu, Zhefu Sun, Yi Mao, Zaixu An
abstract: Aiming at the problems of voltage exceeding the limit frequency of distribution network under the high penetration of distributed power generation (DG), multi-objective optimization conflict (network loss, voltage deviation, equipment operation cost) and traditional algorithms that are prone to local optimization, an adaptive weighted multi-objective gray wolf-taboo hybrid algorithm (AW-MOGWO-TS) is proposed, and a multi-objective reactive voltage optimization model for the station distribution network is constructed. Firstly, based on the principle of voltage safety first and economic operation second, a four-objective function is established, including the smallest network loss, the smallest voltage deviation, the lowest operating cost of discrete equipment and the lowest DG abandonment rate. Secondly, the AW-MOGWO-TS algorithm is set up: the Kent chaos-Sobol sequence is used to initialize the population to ensure diversity, the adaptive weight mechanism is introduced to dynamically balance the multi-target priority, and the taboo search (TS) is triggered by the variance of population fitness to jump out of the local optimum, and the efficient decision-making of the Pareto solution is realized through fuzzy interception theory. Finally, the IEEE33 node station model is verified by examples. The results show that compared with the MOPSO and MOGWO algorithms, the proposed algorithm reduces the network loss by 62.8%, the number of voltage exceeding the limit to 0, the light curtailment rate to 2.76%, and the solution time by 15.6%~22.4% in the DG penetration scenario of 100% permeability.

title: Impact of Digital Entrepreneurship for Youth Economic Independence in Industrial 4.0
authors: Ugbaga Nkole Ifeanyi, Paul Darlington Ibemezie Ndubuisi
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: The Growing prevalence of digital entrepreneurship presents a valuable opportunity to combat youth unemployment and foster financial independence. In Nigeria, the youth unemployment rate is increasing daily; this has necessitated an increase in crime and insecurity in the whole country. This study investigates the relationship between digital entrepreneurship and access to funding, training, and education, supportive networks, government policies, and technological infrastructure in providing financial autonomy among young digital entrepreneurs in Nigeria. A structured questionnaire was distributed on different WhatsApp platforms involving lecturers, students, graduates, freelancers, and entrepreneurs, and 436 respondents were recorded. The collected data were analyzed using correlation and multiple linear regression techniques. The regression model yielded statistically significant results, demonstrating that these enablers collectively account for 8.4% of the variance in financial independence. Among the predictors, training and education exhibited the strongest standardized effect, followed by access to funding and supportive networks. These findings, although showed little importance of equipping youth with digital and entrepreneurial competencies in Nigeria through education and training, access to funds, encouragement of mentorship, and peer group support. The study concluded by suggesting that the government, private individuals, and companies increase their investments more through capacity-building and development of skill centers. They should ensure that training, funding, education, and other enablers, such as digital training, entrepreneurial expertise, get to the targeted youths, while also providing a monitoring system for the programme to be productive. Thus, in doing this, unemployment and crime will reduce drastically, if not eliminated.

title: Spine Abnormality Detection Using GAN And Deformed CNN
authors: Pooja M, Jayasri K, Radhakrishnan K R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Early diagnosis of spinal abnormalities is an important to prevent serious conditions of patients and facilitates earlier medical intervention. Nevertheless automatic classification of spinal irregularities from X-ray images is a complex task due to uneven class distributions, disparities in vertebral bodies, and a limited availability of annotative medical images. To solve these issues, this work investigates a deep learning framework that combines GANs for synthetic data generation and DCNs for strong feature extraction. Namely, the GAN model creates high-quality, class-balanced synthetic spinal X-ray images, which increases the diversity of less-represented abnormality categories. These improved datasets are then used to train a DCN-based multi-class classifier, in which deformable convolution layers adaptively sample the geometric features. This automatically improves the localization of irregular spinal structures. The experimental results have demonstrated that GAN and DCN considerably improve the performance of traditional CNN methods by a large margin, such as accuracy and sensitivity, and per-class AUC for various types of spinal abnormalities. A combination of generative data creation and flexible feature learning effectively reduces data scarcity and anatomical differences, thus providing promise for the application to support clinical decisions and the automation of spinal health assessment.

title: Predicting Hypertension at an Early Stage Using Machine Learning Methods
authors: Zuhair M. Mohammedsaleh
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Hypertension poses a significant global health issue that frequently develops without symptoms, elevating the risk of severe cardiovascular illnesses. Timely prediction is crucial for prompt intervention and effective management. This research introduces a machine learning framework aimed at forecasting hypertension through the UCI Hypertension Dataset, which encompasses demographic, lifestyle, and clinical data from 1,500 individuals. The data preprocessing phase involved addressing missing values, employing one-hot encoding, normalization, and feature selection via Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE). Three supervised learning algorithms—Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and XGBoost—were utilized. The objective of this framework is to enhance preventive healthcare measures and improve clinical decision-making by delivering accurate predictions regarding hypertension.

title: SPCNC 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)

title: Research and Engineering Implementation of Electronic Product Testing Method Based on State Machine Model
authors: Yi Du, Yutong Zhao, Yani Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Building upon the current state of development in testing technologies globally and relevant domestic engineering applications, this paper leverages Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) theory and synthesizes relevant technologies and standards. Focusing on the application scenario of universal testing for electronic products, it conducts research and engineering implementation of State Machine Model-Based testing for Units Under Test (UUTs). Following the MBSE methodology, the process is as follows: Firstly, requirements for the entire testing process are captured, considering the simulation needs for usage scenarios and environmental characteristics. This involves collecting requirements for simulating the UUT's full range of static/dynamic environments and related general quality attributes (e.g., reliability, maintainability) during testing. Secondly, modeling software based on the state machine model is developed, enabling test modeling and test case generation according to the captured requirements. Finally, test case execution is performed. By constructing a universal, integrated software-hardware testing platform, test cases are executed, enabling the identification, processing, and management of process data and result data from the model-based testing. Ultimately, this approach achieves practical enhancement in testing capabilities, ensuring test traceability, controllable testing processes, and reliable test results.

title: A Multi-Modal Deep Learning Framework for Early Classification of Neurodegenerative Disorders
authors: Kaviya P, Ishaa M, Elavarasan R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Alzheimer Disease and Parkinson Disease are neurodegenerative conditions of great importance to the classification as they have a specific clinical presentation as well as they are based on various biomedical modalities. The heterogeneous nature of the disease is not represented in the traditional unimodal AI systems and thus have restricted reliability in the real-world generalization processes. NeuroFusion suggests a combined multi-modal deep learning structure to early-classes to meet this clinical need. The objective of the approach is to replicate multi-input clinical thinking, by exploiting correlating sources of information rather than the classical methodology of handling inputs separately. It uses MRI scans with EfficientNet to analyze spatial information about the brain region, voice samples developed with LSTM to encode time speech patterns and structured biomarkers that are categorized using XGBoost. Subsequently, there is a fusion layer fusion coupled with modality-specific predictions to receive a single diagnosis. The Experimental evaluation demonstrates that NeuroFusion achieves a classification accuracy of 92.3% on average, the best result in comparison with current unimodal and early- fusion baselines by far, and a new standard on multimodal neurodegenerative disease prediction.

title: Evolving Recovery Trees: A Genetic Approach to Tight Lower Bound Derivation in Multi-Access Coded Caching
authors: Lei Yu, Xueting Zhang, Heng Wang, Chenglong Wang, Yimin Jiang, Xueting Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Existing lower bound analysis for multi-access coded caching (MACC) remains challenging due to the complex overlap in cache accessibility. In particular, the current method based on tree structure often suffers from high construction complexity and limited scalability in large system settings. To address this limitation, we proposed a genetic algorithm (GA)based lower bound estimation method that models the lower bound derivation process as a combinatorial optimization problem over the space of encoding tree structures. By encoding user demand vectors as genetic sequences and evolving recovery trees through crossover and mutation, the method efficiently searches for structures maximizing the number of distinct recovered file fragments L in the information-theoretic inequality. Experimental results showed that the proposed approach achieves bound close to exhaustive tree construction and better than other existing analytical results across diverse MACC configurations.

title: Flexible Capacitive Humidity Sensor Based on MXene/Halloysite Nanotube Composites
authors: Bin Zhou, Qiao Chen, Yu Pang, Cong Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: We report a flexible capacitive humidity sensor based on composites of $\mathrm{Ti}_{3} \mathrm{C}_{2} \mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{x}}$ MXene and halloysite nanotubes (HNTs). MXene establishes a percolated, hydrophilic conductive network within the tubular HNT scaffold, simultaneously boosting charge transport and water adsorption. The optimized device ($2 \mathrm{wt} \%$ MXene) operates across $11.3-97.3 \% \mathrm{RH}$ with a sensitivity of 1953.5 $\mathrm{pF} / \% \mathrm{RH}$, rapid response/recovery ($60 / 3 \mathrm{~s}$), and low hysteresis $(2.25 \% \mathrm{RH})$. Frequency screening identifies 100 Hz as a practical operating point balancing sensitivity and stability. Impedance analyses revealed a humidity-dependent transition from proton hopping on chemisorbed monolayers at low RH to ion conduction through physisorbed water at high RH. The simple, solutionprocessable fabrication on flexible interdigitated electrodes yields uniform films and excellent cycling repeatability. This composite strategy offers a general route to high-performance, low-cost, and mechanically compliant humidity sensors suitable for environmental and wearable applications.

title: An Advanced Electronic Health Record for Prescription Analysis Using YOLO and OCR with Spell Correction
authors: S.Jeevidha, P.Yogeshwaran, K.Kannathasan, A.Prem Anand
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Keeping accurate and well-organized medical records is still quite difficult in today's healthcare setting, particularly when it comes to handwritten prescriptions. In order to solve this problem, RxVision created a sophisticated technology that automatically transforms handwritten prescriptions into organized Electronic Health Records (EHRs). The system uses OCR for text extraction following utilizing the YOLO model to identify important sections of interest, such as medication names, dosing amounts, and usage instructions. A spell correction module improves OCR outputs to increase accuracy and reduce handwritten text interpretation problems. With a mAP@0.5 of 0.93, Precision of 94.1%, Recall of 91.3%, and an F1-score of 0.92, the final integrated model demonstrated its dependability and effectiveness with outstanding performance. Furthermore, the structured prescription data generated by the system enables downstream analytical tasks such as risk pattern identification and trend analysis, supporting clinicians in data-driven decision-making. All things considered, RxVision greatly enhances healthcare data management by making patient record maintenance and utilization quicker, more accurate, and simpler for practitioners and hospitals. This system way more managing the health records easier, more accurate, and faster, reducing the manual work for the hospitals and helping the doctors to make better and data-driven decisions

title: Eco-Efficient Electrodeposited Boride Coatings: Experimental Insights into Cyclic Plasticity and Structural Durability
authors: J. Dhiyaneswaran, P. Ashoka Varthanan, R. Sivasubramanian, B. Ashok Kumar, M.Vimal Raja
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This study examines the cyclic plasticity and compressive stress-induced structural transformations in electrodeposited boride. Bulk samples were prepared via pulsed and direct current electrodeposition and subsequently analyzed using scanning electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, and transmission electron microscopy. Cyclic loading redistributed dislocation networks without significantly altering the average grain size, with smaller grains retaining higher residual stress levels. These findings demonstrate that electrodeposition is an effective method for producing boride structures with adjustable mechanical properties. The study enhances the understanding of grain size-stress interactions in electrodeposited materials and provides insights for designing boride-based coatings and components in aerospace, automotive, and energy applications.

title: Agentic AI for Therapy
authors: Shubham Yadav, Anton K Sam, Joseph Jitto, Jai Vinita L
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Existing Large Language Model (LLM) based systems for mental health support are often unsafe and unreliable, primarily due to the absence of dynamic, user-controlled memory. This limitation leads to inconsistent therapeutic continuity and undermines the user trust necessary for forming meaningful therapeutic alliances. This paper introduces an Agentic Therapeutic Agent designed to address these challenges through a modular architecture that prioritizes safety, transparency, and long-term relationship building. The proposed framework's core feature is a secure Long-Term Memory system with a user-facing control panel and pathways for human therapist oversight. Initial development has produced two foundational, non-integrated modules: a rudimentary conversational AI and an effective crisis detection model intended to function as the first component of the agent's Safety & Guardrail Layer. The central contribution of this project is the design, implementation, and validation of this transparent memory architecture, which will integrate the conversational and safety modules. This approach directly addresses the critical gaps of memory and trust in therapeutic AI, demonstrating a tangible pathway toward a more ethically guided and effective support tool.

title: A CNN-Based Framework for Skin Disease Classification Using EfficientNetB0
authors: Karunamoorthi R, Shanmugi E, Nehasri R S, Priyanka V
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Especially with cancers like melanoma, automatic and accurate skin disease classification using dermatoscopic images can significantly enhance clinical decision-making and early management. In this study we address multiclass skin disease classification by integrating feature extraction with transfer learning using EfficientNetB0. Leveraging state-of-the-art augmentation methodology, a bespoke dataset of nine skin disease classes that were collected from publicly accessible sources such as Kaggle was painstakingly selected and analyzed. Finally, the final model scored 92.6% validation accuracy and outperformed the MobileNetV2 and ResNet50 baselines. This extensive report is a detailed overview of clinical challenges, limitations, and future work, a long with the experimental procedure, data preparation procedure, evaluation measures, and performance-affecting factors.

title: Privacy-Preserving Decentralized Voting System Using Hyperledger Fabric and IOTA
authors: Gowthamani R, Granty Regina Elwin J, Kavin A, Rahul A, Patrick Periyanayagam I, Kavin ES
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Elections are the base for a democratic society, but there are problems like fraud tampering, slow result processing in the current voting systems. To solve these problems, this project introduces a Blockchain-Based Voting System as a scalable solution. This system uses IOTA's Tangle (a Directed Acyclic Graph model) to provide fast, scalable, and feeless transactions, making it suitable for large-scale voting. The personal details are kept secret by using Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs). A Hybrid Cloud Model is used, where sensitive data is stored securely on private servers, while the public network ensures transparency. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of security by blocking unauthorized access. By combining these technologies, the system makes voting tamper-proof, keeps voters anonymous, allows real-time result announcements, and protects against frauds. Overall, this solution shows how blockchain can improve the trust, fairness, and accessibility of democratic participation.

title: Error Recognition System for Technical Action in Sports Training Based on Machine Vision
authors: Zhixin Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: In order to meet the requirements of autonomous recognition and analysis of user training actions in intelligent sports training scenarios, this paper investigates a machine visionbased error action recognition system for sports training. Targeting primarily environments such as training rooms where professional instruction is unavailable, the system automatically captures user training videos through standard camera equipment and enables recognition and analysis of technical movements during exercise. Experimental results demonstrate that the system achieves an average recognition rate of approximately 80.55% for racket states. Among successfully detected serving actions, the accuracy for violation judgments exceeds 97%, confirming the system’s strong classification capability. Additionally, real-time performance tests demonstrate an inference latency of 45 ms per frame (22.2 FPS) on standard hardware, confirming suitability for dynamic training scenarios. Convergence analysis reveals stable training dynamics with loss plateauing at epoch 120 and validation accuracy stabilizing at 82.3%, indicating optimal model convergence. Comparative evaluation against state-of-the-art methods shows our system achieves competitive performance while maintaining lower computational requirements. Further analysis reveals significant performance variations under different conditions: recognition rates decline from $\mathbf{8 9. 2 \%}$ at $\mathbf{0 - 1 5}{ }^{\circ}$ to $\mathbf{5 8. 3 \%}$ beyond 60°, highlighting angular dependency limitations. Environmental factors also substantially influence performance, with recognition rates dropping from 80.55% in natural light to 73.35% in low-light conditions. The primary performance bottleneck lies in the target detection phase, where approximately 20% of serves remain undetected, constraining overall effectiveness. The real-time performance analysis indicates that while throughput is sufficient for practical applications, angle variations significantly impact latency. Future improvements should focus on developing viewpoint-invariant feature learning, enhancing environmental adaptability, and optimizing the classification model through advanced machine learning algorithms to improve robustness across diverse application scenarios.

title: Data-Driven Assessment of Generative Adversarial Networks: A Unified Evaluation Framework for V(G,D)
authors: Ziyuan Cai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Most recent studies have proposed various evaluation standards and improvement strategies for the value function $V(G, D)$ in Generative Adversarial Networks; each evaluation method has its own value under specific conditions or datasets. Current research lacks a comprehensive evaluation criterion that can adapt to diverse application backgrounds. Each evaluation method is constrained by narrow and rigid assumptions, which limit its applicability to certain scenarios. The collective body of these methods indeed covers a wide variety of conditions, but this breadth of applicability remains largely theoretical. In practice, integration is problematic due to technical mismatches and the lack of a common framework. The gap between the collective potential and its practical exploitation reveals the need for a unifying approach that can translate broad theoretical coverage into effective and comprehensive analysis. To address this, this paper proposes a generalized framework for developing a representative methodology toward a meta-evaluation function for $V(G, D)$ metrics. To achieve this goal, the proposed framework consists of two steps. First, a set of representative studies is reviewed to examine the compatibility among their improvements, thereby mapping the methodological landscape and, importantly, culminating in a set of candidate $V(G, D)$ metrics for systematic comparison. Second, a meta-evaluation function is constructed, incorporating characteristics of the datasets to assess the relative effectiveness of the candidate metrics identified in the first stage. The application of this meta-evaluation function constitutes a principled mechanism that automatically selects the appropriate V(G, D) metric from a given candidate library to achieve a valid and context-aware evaluation.

title: An Improved YOLOv11-based Algorithm for Detecting Different Categories of Process Vessels in Complex Environments
authors: Wuqiang Wang, Youzhu Liu, Xuecai Bao, Xiaohong Zha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: To enhance the accuracy of ship object detection in challenging river and lake environments, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv11 object detection algorithm. The algorithm first introduces the SMU activation function into the original convolution module, yielding the improved CBS and CkBS convolution modules, thereby enhancing the nonlinear mapping capability of cross-stage features. Furthermore, an improved Spatial Pyramid Pooling-Fast (SPPF) module is strategically introduced before the three output feature layers of the backbone network to significantly enhance multi-scale feature extraction performance. Finally, Coordinate Attention (CA) modules are embedded after the three backbone output layers and both the upsampling and downsampling layers in the feature fusion network, constructing an improved parallel feature fusion architecture that adaptively emphasizes salient regions. Experimental results on complex scenarios, including dense occlusion, small targets, nighttime infrared and faggy images, demonstrate that the improved YOLOv11 algorithm achieves an average precision (AP) of 94.60%, outperforming the baseline model by 2.02 percentage points. This algorithm provides effective technical support for the intelligent management and real-time monitoring of passing ships in river and lake systems.

title: Medical Image Segmentation Using Triple Cross-Attention Graph Agnostic Multi Scale U-Net
authors: S. J. Kavitha, S. Sridevi, Wilfred Blessing N. R.
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Breast cancer (BC) is one of the most prevalent cancers in women. Breast Cancer Pathological Image Segmentation (BCPIS) is an important process for assisting medical experts in calculating tumor area and providing treatment suggestions. However, achieving accurate semantic segmentation is still a challenging task for this technology. This work introduces Triple Cross-Attention Graph-Agnostic Multi-Scale U-Net (TC-GAMU-Net), a new deep-learning architecture that combines graph-based learning, crossattention mechanisms, and multi-scale feature extraction to improve segmentation performance. The Triple CrossAttention module captures local and global dependencies, while the Graph-Agnostic framework provides robustness across imaging modalities. Moreover, the Multi-Scale U-Net architecture enables segmentation of fine-grained and largescale structures, allowing accurate boundary delineation. Experimental assessments on benchmark BC histopathology datasets reveal TC-GAMU-Net better than other models with accuracy of 0.987. This research demonstrates the significance of cross-attention and graph-based learning in accurate segmentation to support enhanced Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems in BC detection.

title: Autonomous Underwater Waste Removal Robot
authors: R. Lakshmi Devi, K. Sangeetha, G. Saravanan, P.R. Monika, S. Divyashree, M. Jayasharlina
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The escalating problem of marine debris poses a severe threat to aquatic ecosystems, endangering marine life and deteriorating water quality. Conventional waste management methods are often inefficient, labor-intensive, and dependent on fossil fuels, limiting their sustainability and effectiveness. This paper presents the development of an innovative underwater waste removal robot designed to autonomously remove surface debris from water bodies. Unlike existing solutions, this system integrates real-time navigation and an adaptive waste collection mechanism powered entirely. The device features an intelligent filtration and storage system, optimizing debris capture while ensuring minimal environmental disturbance. A key innovation of this project is its autonomous decision-making capability, enabling dynamic route adjustments based on debris density detected through onboard sensors. Additionally, its modular design allows scalability for deployment in diverse aquatic environments, from urban waterways to remote marine areas. Performance evaluations highlight the system's high efficiency in debris removal while maintain in energy consumption, demonstrating its potential sustainable alternative to traditional cleanup methods. By harnessing solar energy and smart automation, this collector offers a long-term, ecofriendly solution to water pollution. Its deployment can significantly reduce floating waste, improving water quality and protecting marine biodiversity without adding to the environmental burden. This research underscores the feasibility of integrating renewable energy with autonomous technologies to create a self-sustaining, scalable solution for global water pollution challenges.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Interpretable Model for Blood Glucose Prediction
authors: Yuvarani P, Arun Karthik S, Dhanush K, Gowshik S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Accurately predicting blood glucose levels is essential not only for day-to-day diabetes management but also for the development of reliable non-invasive monitoring technologies. While continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) devices provide high-quality measurements, their cost and invasive nature prevent many individuals from accessing them. To explore alternatives, this study compares the performance of three deep learning architectures—LSTM, CNN, and a hybrid CNN–LSTM model—across two different data scenarios. The first focuses on glucose-only prediction using the OhioT1DM dataset, whereas the second incorporates additional physiological and wearable- derived features from the D1NAMO dataset. All models are trained and evaluated independently on both datasets to ensure fair comparison. In the Ohio glucose-only setting, the hybrid model achieves the lowest RMSE (22.5147 mg/dL), while the LSTM has the least MAE (15.5314 mg/dL) implying complementary strengths. Under the multimodal D1NAMO setting, once again, the hybrid model proves to be better in performance (MAE = 0.8605, RMSE = 1.1317), which indicates that the combination of the two can be useful. This increases the spatial and temporal features representation, when more plentiful physiological inputs are present, this is increasingly beneficial. The results highlight the extent to which prediction performance is determined by the type of data and emphasize the possible potential of combined designs in the measurement of glucose patterns and sophisticated nonverbal physiological patterns.

title: A current-mode DC-DC converter with on chip current-sensing technique
authors: Zhengxing Qi, Rongjing Zhang, Feng Yang, Qin Zhang, Peng Li, Hongwei He
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: The DC-DC converter in this paper works in current-mode with integrated power switches and have a wide wage of input voltage, it adopts the a novel on-chip current sensor for feedback control. The current senor sensed the inductor current and convert it to a voltage with ramp compensation compared with the voltage sensed from the load, can be used for the construction of the feedback loop. The DCDC converter has been fabricated with a standard $0.35 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ BCD 40 V process. The converter can operate from 500 kHz to 1 MHZ with a wide input voltage from 6 to 40 V, which is suited for LED lighting、 Automotive Lighting and Monitor Backlighting.

title: Quality-Assisted Domain Transfer for Fast Face Super-Resolution
authors: Yi-Hao Cheng, Wan-Chi Siu, Shing-Chow Chan
abstract: Face super-resolution (FSR) is a challenging task, especially when the low-resolution (LR) image does not have sufficient information to build a high-resolution (HR) image. In this paper, we propose a quality-assisted domain transfer framework that uses a two-stage pipeline for efficient face SR. Instead of treating all LR images uniformly, we use a quality evaluator to classify images in different LR domains with distinct quality levels and determine the starting points for domain transfer adaptively. We further design a new residual domain learning strategy that integrates the LR domain with learned residual information. This approach allows us to avoid the correction formulation in diffusion models and the momentum correction in the originally proposed domain transfer approach and enables the model to learn the transformation from the LR domain to the HR domain gradually while preserving content information. The experimental results demonstrate competitive performance with the best inference speed among all approaches.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of TinyML Applications in Environmental Monitoring and Their Technical Challenges
authors: Sara Yarham, Mehran Behjati, Rosdiadee Nordin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Environmental monitoring is essential for safeguarding ecosystems, ensuring public health, and supporting climate resilience. Traditional machine learning methods, while effective, often depend on cloud-based processing, resulting in high latency, increased energy consumption, and privacy concerns—limitations that hinder large-scale, real-time deployment. Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) addresses these issues by enabling on-device intelligence on microcontrollers with only a few kilobytes of RAM, reducing cloud reliance and power usage. This paper presents a detailed review of recent TinyML applications in environmental monitoring, covering wildlife conservation, air and water quality monitoring, smart agriculture, and smart cities. For each application, we analyze model design, deployment strategies, results, key challenges, and limitations. We also identify common barriers such as resource constraints, security risks, and lack of standardized benchmarks, and outline future research directions. This review aims to guide researchers in advancing sustainable, efficient, and scalable TinyML-based environmental monitoring solutions.

title: Single Layer Hierarchical Graph Neuron (SLHGN)-Based RADAR Technology (SLRADT)
authors: Benny Benyamin Nasution, Idham Kamil, Agus Edy Rangkuti, Devanta Abraham Tarigan, Indra Siregar, Rina Walmiaty Mardi, Rini Indahwati
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Military observations on marine objects like ships or other vessels that illegally enters maritime territories require sophisticated equipment. Due to some technical or regulatory restrictions, many military vessels that are normally equipped with marine RADAR are not always capable of watching those suspicious activities on sea surface, such as illegal fishing. Additionally, if the body of the vessel is made of anti-RADAR materials, the vessel cannot be detected by a conventional RADAR either. For those purposes, the research work on the investigation on the Single Layer Hierarchical Graph Neuron (SLGHN)-Based RADAR Technology (SLRADT). The SLRADT does not work on the reflected electromagnetic (EM) signals approach like a RADAR does. Rather, the technology work on the state of the EM signals that can get through or blocked by the vessel. The SLHGN as part of the SLRADT can recognize the full-shaped body of targeting objects. Piece by piece of the small parts will finally represent the whole objects. The coverage of the SLRADT forms the display not as a limited circle like in every RADAR, but it is a rectangle, broader. The technology is very scalable, and the coverage area depends on the number of the deployed neurons. The refresh cycle time is independent on the coverage area, which is constant 5 seconds. The architecture for the coverage area of 50X50 square kilometers can be installed within two days only. Compared to common RADARs, vessels with covered with anti-RADAR materials can be detected. The system is more difficult to paralyze the system, because for that all the neurons must be destroyed. The equipment is also cheaper when deployed.

title: Differential SOT NVSRAM with Shared Diffusion for Low-Power High-Speed Applications
authors: Sangeetha C Soman, Sunanda Ambulker
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This paper presents a Differential Spin–Orbit Torque-based Non-Volatile SRAM (DIFF SOT-NVSRAM) that leverages spin–orbit torque magnetic tunnel junctions (SOT-MTJs) to enhance speed and reliability. The proposed differential architecture significantly improves write performance and energy efficiency, addressing the limitations of conventional STT- and SOT-based NVSRAM designs. Simulation results in 40 nm CMOS technology demonstrate up to 27% improvement in read energy efficiency, 70% reduction in write delay, and over 80% reduction in switching power compared to state-of-the-art NVSRAMs. Furthermore, the design offers enhanced write stability along with robust non-volatile store and restore functionality, making it a promising candidate for future high-performance, energy-efficient memory systems.

title: 7.1 54×42 LiDAR 3D-Stacked System-On-Chip with On-Chip Point Cloud Processing and Hybrid On-Chip/Package-Embedded 25V Boost Generation
authors: Neale A. W. Dutton, Xavier Branca, Mathieu Thivin, Steven Collins, Herve Thuaire, Fabrice Martin, Vincent Clemencon, Mohammed Al-Rawhani, Duncan Hall, Axel Crocherie, Cedric Pastorelli, Sophie Taupin, Severin Trochut, Abhishek Singh, Andreas Assmann, Bruce R. Rae, Pascal Mellot
abstract: We report a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$54 \times 42$</tex>-resolution SPAD-based LIDAR receiver SoC leveraging 3D-stacked <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65 / 40 ~\text{nm}$</tex> technology with high-frequency TDC and 2 counters for ToF and intensity exposure, with peripheral memories used for image signal processing (ISP) frame stores, point cloud generation & HDR management. A hybrid on-chip & package-embedded 25V boost converter is described integrating SPAD supply generation synchronized with sensor frame timing. The sensor is integrated in a LIDAR module for 9.6m ranging with <1cm error.

title: GenAI-Powered Autonomous Test Automation for Mission-Critical Online Booking Systems: A Scalable and Monetizable Framework for Adaptive Quality Assurance
authors: Durga Krishnamoorthy, Ram Ghadiyaram, Laxminarayana Goud Hthalli Rama
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel Generative AI (GenAI)-powered framework for autonomous test automation tailored to mission-critical airline online booking software systems. By leveraging Natural Language Processing (NLP) for codeless test creation, Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive test case generation, and predictive analytics for defect detection, the framework addresses complex airline workflows, frequent updates, and regulatory compliance. Empirical evaluation across aviation case studies demonstrates a 60% improvement in test coverage, 45% reduction in testing time, and 30% increase in defect detection. The paper propose an Airline Workflow Adaptability Score (AWAS) to quantify performance and introduce strategies for growth (e.g., adoption by airlines and vendors) and monetization (e.g., SaaS models, API services). Ethical AI testing mitigates biases in passenger-facing algorithms, ensuring fairness. This scalable, monetizable framework transforms quality assurance in airline ecosystems, enabling rapid, reliable, and sustainable software validation.

title: ASDnB: Merging Face with Body Cues For Robust Active Speaker Detection
authors: Tiago Roxo, Joana C. Costa, Pedro R. M. Inácio, Hugo Proença
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: State-of-the-art Active Speaker Detection (ASD) approaches mainly use audio and facial features as input. However, the main hypothesis in this paper is that body dynamics is also highly correlated to "speaking" (and "listening") actions and should be particularly useful in wild conditions (e.g., surveillance settings), where face cannot be reliably accessed. We propose ASDnB, a model that singularly integrates face with body information by merging the inputs at different steps of feature extraction. Our approach splits 3D convolution into 2D and 1D to reduce computation cost without loss of performance, and is trained with adaptive weight feature importance for improved complement of face with body data. Our experiments show that ASDnB achieves state-of-the-art results in the benchmark dataset (AVA-ActiveSpeaker), in the challenging data of WASD, and in cross-domain settings using Columbia. This way, ASDnB can perform in multiple settings, which is positively regarded as a strong baseline for robust ASD models (code available at https://github.com/Tiago-Roxo/ASDnB).

title: Debias-DPO: Debiasing Diffusion-based Face Image Generation with Direct Preference Optimization
authors: Malsha V. Perera, Vishal M. Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: The exceptional ability of diffusion-based generative models to produce high-quality images has led to their widespread adoption many in real-world applications. However, despite their impressive capabilities, these models often exhibit undesirable characteristics, such as social biases related to attributes like gender and race, which can have significant negative societal implications. In this study, we propose a Direct Preference Optimization (DPO)-based debiasing algorithm to effectively mitigate these biases in diffusion models used for face generation. We achieve this by giving greater preference to generating underrepresented classes, thereby achieving a balanced distribution in terms of the considered social attribute. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method successfully reduces social biases in diffusion-based face generation models across multiple datasets while outperforming state-of-the-art debiasing techniques.

title:
A 1.09e
                    <sup>−</sup>
                    -Random-Noise 1.5μm-Pixel-Pitch 12MP Global-Shutter-Equivalent CMOS Image Sensor with 3μm Digital Pixels Using Quad-Phase-Staggered Zigzag Readout and Motion Compensation
authors: Sanggwon Lee, Sun-Young Yoo, Yeongseok Shim, Yong-Suk Choi, Daehee Bae, Masamichi Ito, Deokha Shin, Si-Gyoung Koo, Sung-Jae Byun, Gihwan Cho, Hyukbin Kwon, Jaehun Jeong, Bumjun Kim, Su-Hyun Han, Youna Lee, Hiroyuki Sugihara, Junghoon Jung, Suksan Kim, Kyungtae Lim, Wonoh Ryu, Yongjun Kim, Seung-Sik Kim, Heesung Shim, Min-Woong Seo, Jae-kyu Lee, Jonghyun Go, Jaihyuk Song
abstract: A quad phase-staggered digital pixel sensor with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.494 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> pixel pitch featuring a 1.09 e-rms RN, delivering a total resolution of 12 MP composed of four 3 MP sub-frames is presented. A single ADC is shared by a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> pixels sequentially digitized, enabling optical flow extraction using two-stage deep burst super-resolution framework that incorporates a U-Net-based Quad-Bayer Re-mosaic module, extending the 12 MP resolution up to 48 MP while providing de-noising, motion compensation.

title: An Ultra Low Power Variable Gain Amplifier Based on Negative Impedance for Low-IF Receiver Applications
authors: Priyanka Chaurasia, Manish Goswami, Prasanna Kumar Misra
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work presents an ultra low power Variable Gain Amplifiers (VGA) based on negative impedance for low-IF reciever applications. VGA is designed in 40nm CMOS technology and the concept of negative impedance is investigated with small signal analysis. The integration of negative impedance in differential outputs of the VGA is proposed, and the performance is estimated and compared with conventional VGAs. The negative impedance function is optimized for VGA. Simulation results show a significant enhancement in voltage gain from 16 dB to 34 dB compared to the conventional VGA architecture. Conventional VGA uses 394 μW and the proposed VGA uses a power of 448 μW. With this concept the dynamic range of VGA can be significantly improved with minimal addition of power (54 μW) which is useful as a power saving tool (low power receiver) in wireless communication systems.

title: Malicious Traffic Detection in IoT Systems Driven by 6G and Edge Intelligence
authors: Tong Niu, Yuguang Chen, Tenghui Ke, Shujing Zhang, Pengcheng Shi, Mai Hao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: With the continuous evolution of communication technologies, future 6G networks will feature intelligence, ultra-low latency, and massive connectivity, driving deep integration between computation and communication in Internet of Things (IoT) systems. However, in such a highly dynamic and heterogeneous environment, traditional centralized malicious traffic detection mechanisms fail to meet the requirements of privacy preservation, real-time processing, and scalability. To address the emerging security challenges of 6G communication environments, this paper proposes a context-aware and Docker-containerized federated malicious traffic detection framework for 6G-enabled IoT systems. Built upon the AI-native architecture of 6G, the framework incorporates intelligent resource allocation and stable binding mechanisms to ensure optimal edge node selection and adaptive collaboration during device onboarding. At the detection level, a confidence-based mechanism is introduced to dynamically adjust model confidence scores, enhancing the system’s ability to recognize unknown attacks and imbalanced traffic. In terms of system deployment, Docker containerization enables lightweight model migration and cross-node rapid updates, ensuring the efficiency and flexibility of distributed training. Experimental results on IoT benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves superior performance, with a detection accuracy of 0.987 and an F1-score of 0.9653, while significantly reducing communication overhead and improving system robustness. This study provides a novel technical paradigm and feasible pathway for building intelligent and privacy-preserving IoT security protection systems in future 6G communication networks.

title: A Hub-Enabled Cloud-Edge Collaboration IoT Model for Resolving Dual Fragmentation in Digital Transformation
authors: Wenjing Gao, Jian Wang, Weiwei Ma, Hao Xu, Xiangpeng Zhu, Lianxin Song, Jiawei Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: The digital transformation is hampered by a structural contradiction of dual fragmentation—fragmented business scenarios and isolated data silos — which creates a core conflict between localized real-time response and global optimized decision-making. To resolve this contradiction, this study proposes a multi-scenario, hub-enabled empowerment model based on cloud-edge collaboration and IoT capabilities. The model employs an integrated industrial edge appliance as both a physical and logical hub, constructing a three-tier architecture for data perception, edge processing, and cloud intelligence. By incorporating key technologies such as ubiquitous access and lightweight edge governance, it provides unified support for diverse business scenarios. To validate its efficacy under realistic conditions, a comparative experiment was conducted on an operational discrete manufacturing assembly line, benchmarking the proposed model against a traditional on-premise deployment. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves millisecond-level real-time performance comparable to the traditional scheme, while exhibiting superior and more stable system resource utilization under sustained load, attributable to its containerized and microservices architecture. Furthermore, it demonstrates significant advantages in data interoperability, cross-scenario collaboration efficiency, and system scalability, effectively addressing the core pain points of dual fragmentation. This high-performance, high-stability characteristic systematically enhances resource efficiency and operational reliability, offering a feasible path and a technical model for digital transformation that integrates real-time performance, stability, and long-term economic viability.

title: Design and Implementation of Reconfigurable PUF for Hardware Security
authors: Thakar Rudra Nilesh, Madhuram Mishra, Devendra Singh Negi, Harshit Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: With the widespread growth of connected devices and embedded systems, significant concerns regarding the hardware security have emerged. Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) are a promising method for creating distinct and tamper-evident identities for integrated circuits (ICs) by taking advantage of inherent manufacturing variations. In this work, we present the design and FPGA-based implementation of a Ring Oscillator (RO) PUF. This design produces unpredictable, device-specific responses by utilizing the small variations in the operating frequencies of supposedly identical ring oscillators, which act as a biometric for the device. We further address practical challenges in PUF stability by evaluating key performance metrics for reliability and uniqueness through implementations on FPGA. To overcome limitations of conventional RO-PUF designs for resource efficiency, we investigate FPGA implementation of Configurable Ring Oscillator (CRO) PUF architecture. This design dynamically changes the oscillation paths using control logic. Experimental results demonstrate that the CRO-PUF achieves improved reliability, uniqueness, and reduced resource utilization compared to traditional RO-PUFs, making it a more suitable candidate for lightweight hardware security.

title: QGait: Toward Accurate Quantization for Gait Recognition
authors: Senmao Tian, Haoyu Gao, Gangyi Hong, Shuyun Wang, JingJie Wang, Xin Yu, Shunli Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Existing deep learning methods have made significant progress in gait recognition. Quantization can facilitate the application of gait models as a model-agnostic general compression technique. Typically, appearance-based models binarize inputs into silhouette sequences. However, mainstream quantization methods prioritize minimizing task loss over quantization error, which is detrimental to gait recognition with binarized inputs. To address this, we propose a differentiable soft quantizer, which better simulates the gradient of the round function during backpropagation. This enables the network to learn from subtle input perturbations. However, our theoretical analysis and empirical studies reveal that directly applying the soft quantizer can hinder network convergence. We addressed this issue by adopting a two-stage training strategy, introducing a soft quantizer during the fine-tuning phase. However, in the first stage of training, we observed a significant change in the output distribution of different samples in the feature space compared to the full-precision network. It is this change that led to a loss in performance. Based on this, we propose an Inter-class Distance-guided Calibration (IDC) strategy to preserve the relative distance between the embeddings of samples with different labels. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of our approach, demonstrating state-of-the-art accuracy across various settings and datasets. The code will be made publicly available.

title: MTCAE-DFER: Multi-Task Cascaded Autoencoder for Dynamic Facial Expression Recognition
authors: Peihao Xiang, Kaida Wu, Ou Bai
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: This paper expands the cascaded network branch of the autoencoder-based multi-task learning (MTL) framework for dynamic facial expression recognition, namely Multi-Task Cascaded Autoencoder for Dynamic Facial Expression Recognition (MTCAE-DFER). MTCAE-DFER builds a plug-and-play cascaded decoder module, which is based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture and employs the decoder concept of Transformer to reconstruct the multihead attention module. The decoder output from the previous task serves as the query (Q), representing local dynamic features, while the Video Masked Autoencoder (VideoMAE) shared encoder output acts as both the key (K) and value (V), representing global dynamic features. This setup facilitates the interaction between global and local dynamic features across related tasks. Additionally, this proposal aims to alleviate overfitting of complex large model. We utilize autoencoder-based multi-task cascaded learning approach to explore the impact of dynamic face detection and dynamic face landmark on dynamic facial expression recognition, which enhances the model’s generalization ability. After we conduct extensive ablation experiments and comparison with state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods on various public datasets for dynamic facial expression recognition, the robustness of the MTCAE-DFER model and the effectiveness of global-local dynamic feature interaction among related tasks have been proven.

title: Collaborative Spatial and Channel Attention for Structural Vibration-based Gait Recognition
authors: Junhao Lu, Haijun Xiong, Junjie Zhou, Ziyu Lin, Bin Feng
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Structural vibration-based gait recognition aims to identify pedestrians through their unique vibration patterns and has gained significant attention for its non-invasive nature. However, existing methods primarily rely on manually crafted features and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to extract local details, without considering global gait features in vibration signals. To address this limitation, we propose CSCA, a novel framework designed to capture discriminative global gait features through collaborative spatial and channel attention. Specifically, CSCA consists of two key components: Mamba-inspired Linear Channel Attention (MLCA) and Strip Pooling-guided Spatial Attention (SPSA). MLCA integrates Mamba with linear attention to enhance discriminative attention in the channel dimension by capturing global gait features. Additionally, SPSA integrates axial strip pooling with multikernel depth-wise convolutions to extract multi-level spatial semantic features. Experimental results on the VIBEID dataset demonstrate that CSCA achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse environmental conditions, with identity recognition accuracy exceeding 96%. Code is available at https://github.com/xiaomush/CSCA.

title: Robust Forecasting Framework for Agricultural Markets Using LSTM and ARIMA Hybridization
authors: Jayanthi R, Rishikeshan C A, Sushilkumar K S, Esha Sachin Potdar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: Price forecasting is vital for agricultural supply chains, yet it remains difficult due to erratic price fluctuations caused by weather, policies, and seasonality. This study focuses on forecasting the retail prices of two key perishable crops bitter gourd and banana using ARIMA and LSTM models. ARIMA is effective for capturing linear and seasonal patterns but struggles with non-linear and long-term dependencies. In contrast, LSTM, a deep learning method, models complex price behavior but requires large datasets and sacrifices interpretability. To overcome individual model limitations, a hybrid ARIMA-LSTM approach is proposed, combining ARIMA’s short-term and seasonal forecasting strengths with LSTM’s capacity for long-term and nonlinear trend modeling. The hybrid model achieved 97.33% accuracy, outperforming standalone ARIMA (96.41%) and LSTM (95.25%) models.

title: A Multi-Scale Explainable Deep Learning Pipeline for Early Skin Cancer Detection with Grad-CAM-Based Visual Explanations and Robust Lesion Segmentation
authors: Priyanshu Singh, Shikha Gupta, Reshma Reshma
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Skin cancer (SC) has a substantial influence on numerous people around the world. As a result, it is critical to detect and diagnose such problems as early as possible, utilizing skin images. Deep learning (DL) algorithms are now gaining prominence as a tool for detecting SC in its early stages. Despite their great performance, depending merely on an image classification algorithm lowers the program's legitimacy and makes it impossible to grasp the reasoning behind the system's predictions, emphasizing the importance of Explainable DL (XDL). Grad-CAM is used to increase system interpretability and promote therapeutic confidence by enabling physicians to see heatmaps of the areas that influence the system's conclusions. This investigation conducts a review of the diagnosis of SC employing DL methods used in investigations from 2017 to 2024. In contrast to previous survey research, the researchers discuss the most recent associated investigations, which encompass many public SC image databases and emphasize segmentation, categorization employing XDL. Grad-CAM is used to increase system interpretation and build medical confidence by enabling medical professionals to visualize heatmaps of the areas that influence the system's conclusions. The examination and contrasting of previous research will help investigators and designers in this field discover the most appropriate strategies for automatic SC image segmentation. As a result, the review data can be employed to create assistance tools that advance the SC diagnostic procedure.

title:
UV-Stimulated Low-Voltage Organic Synaptic Phototransistor Based on WS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Nanosheets-Pentacene for Flexible Neuromorphic Electronics
authors: Rajdeep Banerjee, Priyanka Rani, Sanjoy Dey, Samik Mallik, Riya Sadhukhan, Dipak Kumar Goswami
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: A low-voltage organic synaptic phototransistor has been fabricated based on two-dimensional (2D) tungsten disulfide (WS2) nanosheets and organic semiconductor pentacene. When stimulated by UV light, the devices could mimic the basic biological synaptic functions, including excitatory post-synaptic current (EPSC), paired-pulse facilitation (PPF), and transition from short-term plasticity (STP) to long-term plasticity (LTP). Flexibility measurements demonstrated that the devices are resilient to mechanical bending. This research underscores the strong potential of WS2-pentacene-based synaptic phototransistors for use in flexible neuromorphic electronics.

title: AI-Powered Personalized Curriculum Design to Enhance Educator Creativity, Decision Making and Behavioural Outcomes: A Behavioral Economics Perspective
authors: Shalini Jadiya, Swati Mishra
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The rapid adoption of Artificial Intelligence across education creates new pathways to embrace the use of information to personalize curriculum design. Existing models of curriculum development often neglect to consider the cognitive biases, behavioral incentives, and decision-making heuristics that drive educator creativity and learning outcomes. This article proposes an AI blended personalized curriculum design framework through machine and deep learning models to enhance instructional planning. Using survey responses from 1,300 educators in higher education institutions, we employed: Natural Language Processing-based content clustering, reinforcement learning-based adaptive decision making, and Deep Neural Networks to predict behavioral outcomes. We demonstrate meaningful improvements in educator creativity (23%), confidence in decision making (19%), and positive behavioral outcomes (21%) applying an AI-infused personalized curriculum design approach compared to a control (traditional) approach. Our findings demonstrate that the implementation of principles of behavioral economics (e.g., nudges, framing, loss aversion) to customize the design of the curriculum with the help of AI-infused ideas alters the performance of educators, and produces more efficient learning experiences.

title: Design and Development of a Smart Therapeutic Massage Chair with Dynamic Zone-Based Vibration and Deep Tissue Stimulation
authors: Nilesh Parkash, Padmanaban S, Rajasingam N, Aman Anand, Shiv Kumar Pandit, Pardeep Kumar Sharma
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: In this paper, we present the design and development of a Smart Therapeutic Massage Chair equipped with dynamic zone-based vibration and deep tissue stimulation. The system offers a full-body therapeutic experience through a hardware-centric control approach that eliminates software complexity while maintaining high performance and safety. The chair is ergonomically structured and integrates six DC motors and four vibration units positioned to target major muscle groups, thereby enhancing blood circulation and muscle relaxation. Compared to conventional low-cost massage devices, the proposed system achieves a 30% higher user satisfaction score and 25% greater relaxation efficiency in controlled trials. The modular design supports easy maintenance, low power consumption, and sustainable construction using eco-friendly materials. This work demonstrates a cost-effective, reliable, and user-centered therapeutic system suitable for home and rehabilitation applications, bridging the gap between affordability and advanced wellness technology.

title: Enhancing Maternal E-Health Access for Pregnant Women in Zimbabwe's Underserved Communities: A Review of Literature
authors: Abel Moyo, Fine Masimba, Tranos Zuva
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: This Systematic Literature Review explore the maternal e-health challenges faced by pregnant women in low-resource areas of Zimbabwe, where high maternal morbidity and mortality rates remain pressing concerns. The aim of the study was to explore the maternal e-health challenges experienced in under-served communities in Zimbabwe. The data was collected from sixteen different online databases and analyzed used PRISMA framework. Findings reveal that significant challenges include lack of affordable ICT devices, educational deficiencies, language barriers and financial constraints, all of which impede women's ability to engage with e-health resources effectively. Moreover, the quality of healthcare delivery is compromised by connectivity issues and insufficient support systems. To enhance maternal e-health services, this study recommends improving access to affordable ICT devices, implementing comprehensive digital literacy training, developing culturally relevant communication strategies and investing in infrastructure improvements. Additionally, financial support initiatives and community engagement are essential for fostering acceptance and utilization of e-health services. Addressing these challenges through targeted interventions can significantly improve maternal health outcomes in Zimbabwe's low-resource settings. Future research should focus on longitudinal assessments of the impact of these strategies on maternal health and explore the integration of emerging technologies.

title: 11.6 an 8B 20Gs/S Time-Interleaved ADC with 2.6mW 1GS/s Hybrid Voltage/Time-Domain Sub-ADC in 12Nm FinFET
authors: Daisuke Miyazaki, Yuki Yagishita, Mika Takasaki, Shun Nagata, Takeru Nogamida, Yudai Abe, Kazutoshi Tomita, Kazunori Hasebe, Toshiyuki Kikkawa, Satoshi Yoshizawa, Atsuya Suzuki, Soichi Kato, Takahiro Naito, Keigo Bunsen, Tomohiro Matsumoto, Yasushi Katayama
abstract: A 2.6mW, 1GHz, 42dB-SNDR sub-ADC is presented. A hybrid voltage- and time-domain architecture is employed to avoid the theorical SAR comparator trade-offs and enable power efficiency benefit from the time-domain ADC. Additionally, a hierarchical sample-and-hold circuit with a new common-mode feedback scheme is introduced to enhance signal distribution. A 20GHz time-interleaved ADC, including an input buffer and reference, demonstrates an SNDR of 37dB with a power consumption of 135mW.

title: Evolutionary Ansatz Selection for Post-Variational Quantum Neural Networks
authors: Cao Vu Bui, Van Nam Nguyen, Hoang Anh Vu Nguyen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Post-variational quantum neural networks (PV-QNNs) shift learning from parameterized quantum circuits to a classical head, but their performance hinges on selecting effective fixed ansätze. Manual templates or random circuit picks are brittle and scale poorly. We propose an automated, genetic-algorithm (GA) framework for PV-QNN ansatz ensemble selection. The GA searches a discrete circuit grammar (single-/two-qubit gates under topology and depth constraints) to assemble small ensembles whose quantum features are extracted via classical shadows and consumed by a shallow classical classifier. On Fashion-MNIST and MNIST (2 vs 6), GA-selected ensembles consistently outperform random and heuristic baselines while using about 40% fewer circuits; our efficiency metric, accuracy per circuit, improves by 74%. We also observe stable cross-dataset transfer, indicating that the discovered circuits capture task-relevant structure beyond a single training distribution. These results demonstrate that evolutionary search is a practical route to high-utility, low-footprint PV-QNNs, improving accuracy and sample efficiency without any quantum parameter training, and strengthening PV-QNN viability on near-term hardware.

title: Impact of RF Sputtering Power on ZnO-based Optoelectronic Synapses
authors: Anusha P, Roshni Oommen, Aswathi R. Nair
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: The performance of metal oxide semiconductor (MOx) optoelectronic synapses depends on the extent of persistent photoconductivity (PPC) in the material. The PPC is governed by the amount of oxygen vacancies, which, in turn, depends on the fabrication process conditions. However, there is limited research on how deposition conditions affect synaptic properties. In this study, we developed a two-terminal (2T) Zinc oxide (ZnO)-aluminium (Al) metal-semiconductor-metal (MSM) structure-based optoelectronic synaptic device.A ZnO thin film was deposited under varying radio frequency (RF) powers, and its synaptic characteristics were systematically examined. The RF powers used were 30, 60, and 100 W. From the synaptic properties, we observed improvements in synaptic metrics, including sensory memory (SM), short-term memory (STM), long-term memory (LTM), and paired-pulse facilitation (PPF), as the RF power increased. This enhancement is attributed to the increase in defect formation at higher RF powers.

title: Multi-task Convolution Neural Network towards Detection and Classification of Cardiovascular Disease on Leveraging Color Fundus Retina Images
authors: Nandhini S, Vanitha K
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: cardiovascular disease is one of the most prevalent diseases which causing high mortality rate among various age groups across the world. According to world health organization, addressing cardiovascular diseases in the early stages can highly prevent and reduce the mortality rate. Thus, it becomes mandatory to perform early diagnosis of the cardiovascular disease with respect to heart condition. Various manual and automated researches have been performed to early diagnosis of heart condition through multiple medical examination in form blood test, ECG, X-Ray, CT scan, MRI Scan and Angiography. However, those approaches involve invasive procedure and it is considered as expensive and complexity for early diagnosis. Hence it becomes mandatory to design a simple cost effective non -invasive tool to be employed to diagnosis of the heart condition with high scalability. In this paper, a new early cardiovascular disease diagnosis approach is performed on integration of the artificial intelligences process the color fundus image of eye which acquired through color fundus photograph. A Novel artificial intelligence technique entitled as Multi-Task Convolution Neural Network is designed and implemented to the process the acquired color fundus image of eye is to detect and classify the heart disease. Initially acquired color fundus retinal image is preprocessed using wiener filter to remove the noise and Contrast limited adaptive histogram equalization for image enhancement. Preprocessed image has been projected to dual branch convolution neural network. Dual branch convolution neural network contains U-Net for artery –vein segmentation and ResNet architecture towards extraction of the feature in the AV segments and to classify that feature into different cardiovascular disease classes. U-Net architecture composed of the encoder (contraction path) and decoder (expansion path) sections. Encoder section of U-Net is augmented with ResNet backbone to eliminate the vanishing gradient challenges in deeper layer and to preserve the fine-grained details of the feature. ResNet backbone contains different block with residual connection to capture the hierarchical contextual feature related to blood vessel (Artery vein characteristics) at different resolutions in different regions of the fundus image through process of the down-sampling in the convolution layers and max pooling layer on generating the feature map while decoder section reconstructs the segmentation map through upsampling process (Transposed convolution or bilinear interpolation) in upsampling layers on combining features of the encoder through skip connection. Segmentation map (artery vein map) is projected to the fully connected layer of the Residual Network for classification of the segmented map. Finally, fully connected layer composed of softmax function is used to identify the retinal biomarker to associate the different cardiovascular diseases. Experimental and performance analysis of the proposed model is performed using benchmark Fundus AvSeg15 dataset in the python environment. On experimental analysis, it is proved that proposed model produces 98.2% accuracy in detecting the cardiovascular diseases.

title: Detecting Hyper-Realistic Videos Generated by Diffusion Models via Text-Guided Semantic Enhancement
authors: Shum Hing Ling, Zhenyu Zhou, Ajay Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: This paper addresses two key challenges in detecting diffusion-model generated videos: generalization to unseen but sophisticated video synthesizers and threats from the multimodal manipulations where the deepfakes combine the text prompts and visual synthesis to create realistic forgeries. We propose a novel multimodal framework built on a transformer-based architecture, which was originally designed for image forgery detection. Our framework extends this architecture by integrating two complementary components: (i) spatio-temporal feature extraction and (ii) a text-guided enrichment module which uses a frozen Vision Language Models (VLMs) text encoder when prompts are available and a small set of learnable default embeddings when no prompt is provided. Trained on our self-curated dataset comprising KlingAI and StableDiffusion Samples, we present cross-dataset performance from unseen but hyper-realistic fake video generators comprising Sora, Luma, Pika, and Runway. Our model can achieve high accuracy and outperformance results, which demonstrate cross-model generalization for detecting hyper-realistic AI-generated videos.

title: A Transformer-based Sequence Prediction Architecture for Etch Profile Evolution in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Jianming Guo, Kun Ren, Dawei Gao, Dong Ni
abstract: The precise control of three-dimensional feature profiles during plasma etching is a fundamental challenge in nanoscale device fabrication, directly impacting performance and yield. While conventional physics-based simulations can capture the underlying plasma-surface interactions, their prohibitive computational cost limits their use for large-area analysis or rapid process development. To bridge this gap, we introduce EtchFormer, a Transformer-based deep learning framework engineered as a high-speed virtual metrology engine for 3D etch profile prediction. Crucially, the model is trained on a comprehensive dataset generated by the physically-calibrated, multi-scale TCAD simulations described in our methodology. This dataset, comprising over 50,000 time-resolved frames from thousands of etching sequences, embeds the fundamental physics of ion-enhanced etching, surface passivation, and byproduct transport across a wide spectrum of industrial process conditions and complex nanoscale patterns. The EtchFormer framework demonstrates high-fidelity reconstruction of entire etch sequences (Structural Similarity Index > 0.945) and, critically, exhibits robust long-range forecasting capabilities by accurately extrapolating profile evolution beyond the temporal range of the training data. Validated on complex layouts, EtchFormer reduces the prediction time for a complete sequence from hours to subseconds (~0.3 s). This capability provides a foundation for the virtual metrology, enabling the exploration of complex process-pattern dependencies, the prediction of structural anomalies, and the accelerated design and optimization of etching processes.

title: Spatiotemporal Coordination Optimization in Electric Vehicle Transportation-Energy Networks Based on Virtual Service Nodes
authors: Zhiwei Wang, Wei Jiang, Junjun Xu, Jianguang Yao, Aihua Zhou, Min Xu, Zhiyuan Liu
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are essential for addressing dynamic path planning and charging scheduling problems in transportation-energy coupled networks. However, conventional traffic assignment models struggle to accurately capture EV charging behaviors due to limited temporal resolution, insufficient power-transportation integration, and inadequate representation of queue dynamics. This paper proposes a mixed-integer quadratic programming (MIQP) model based on Virtual Service Nodes (VSN), which decomposes the charging process into distinct waiting and service phases, thereby enabling fine-grained spatiotemporal capture of EV charging behaviors and their queue dynamics. We model each charging station as a logical triplet (entry, service, exit) connected by wait and charge arcs. Within the VSN framework, charging pile states and queue dynamics are characterized using discrete time steps and binary variables with linear constraints, which facilitates precise modeling of operational constraints. Furthermore, a grid-transportation collaborative equilibrium mechanism incorporates time-of-use pricing into the optimization objective, thereby incentivizing peak-valley load balancing. Experimental results demonstrate that the VSN strategy reduces average and maximum waiting times by 91.0% and 84.5%, respectively, while enhancing charging station utilization balance and smoothing grid load profiles, ultimately improving the overall service capacity of the coupled network.

title: Virtual Inertia Limit Estimation Method for Power Systems with High Proportion of Renewable Energy Considering Time Delay
authors: Xuesong Zhang, Minghui Yin, Zaiyu Chen, Qun Li, Qiang Li, Yun Zou
