title: The Role of AI in Accelerating Digitization of Education Systems: A System Dynamics Approach
authors: Babalwa Outshiki, Xolani Yokwana, Mbuyu Sumbwanyambe
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is fundamentally reshaping education by introducing novel, scalable learning methods. However, the long-term systemic implications of AI-driven digitization—particularly concerning educational equity, environmental sustainability, and institutional adaptability—remain inadequately understood, especially in developing nations like South Africa. This research employs a system dynamics approach to model these complex interdependencies. Simulations reveal a reinforcing feedback loop where AI adoption improves learning outcomes, but this progress is ultimately constrained by a balancing loop involving environmental costs and resource inequalities. For instance, under a laissez-faire scenario, AI adoption plateaus at approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 5 \%$</tex>, but this is accompanied by a significant equity gap (access ratio of 0.45) and a sharp 40% increase in e-waste accumulation. The findings indicate that unmitigated AI integration risks significantly increasing the education sector's carbon footprint and widening the performance gap between well-resourced and under-resourced institutions. However, the simulations demonstrate that a proactive “Green AI Policy” can reduce the equity gap by 33% (raising the access ratio to 0.60) and cut e-waste accumulation by 40%, despite a minimal impact on the final adoption level. This study underscores the critical need for policymakers to adopt a systemic perspective, advocating for policies that promote energy-efficient technologies and equitable access to ensure sustainable and ethical AI implementation in education.

title: Smart IoT-Driven Blood Bank: Real-Time Stock Monitoring and Management
authors: Shanmugapriya A, Kalaivani A, Sangeetha Devi A, Jayapriya P
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Blood donation facilities are essential for providing blood in times of need; however, problems, including unavailable donors, inefficient manual processes, and poor inventory control, make management difficult. For the purpose of improving accessibility, dependability, and efficiency in blood bank activities, this study suggests an Internet of Things-based blood stock control system. Real-time blood stock monitoring is made possible by the system's integration of RFID technology, cloud-based data transfer, and Internet of Things sensors (temperature, relative humidity, and infrared). In order to verify appropriate storage, the first module consists of sensors placed in blood storage racks to monitor stock levels and environmental conditions. Monitoring from a distance is made possible by the second module's automated data transmission to administrators via a Wi-Fi module. Blood seekers can quickly access the third module, which uses RFID for real-time inventory tracking and updates the blood stock availability on a web-based platform. According to experimental results, blood type detection was highly accurate (96.5%), while real-time data transfer speeds varied according to network availability (fastest at 1.9s on 4G). Professionals working in blood organizations who participated in a usability assessment reported increases in productivity, dependability, and managerial simplicity. This automated blood bank system guarantees quicker and more effective emergency responses while reducing waste, thereby enhancing the accuracy of blood availability.

title: 25.6 A 17%/27% Area-/Energy-Overhead Glitch-Transition Secure SHA-3 Engine Fusing Dual-Rail Precharge Logic and Asymmetric Masking
authors: Cankun Zhao, Hanyue Shui, Bohan Yang, Wenping Zhu, Yuluan Cao, Zhang Hou, Yuqi Liu, Xiangdong Han, Shuying Yin, Weinan Chen, Hanning Wang, Jinjiang Yang, Min Zhu, Aoyang Zhang, Leibo Liu
abstract: This 0.046 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> SHA-3 engine demonstrates overheads of only 17% area, 27% energy, and 0% latency while achieving provable glitch- and transition-robust security (power/EM TVLA MTD <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>100 \mathrm{M}$</tex>). Glitch-free DPL reduces masked states by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex>, resulting in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$72,950 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> register area reduction and lower logic energy. LMDPL refresh preserves secure asymmetric-share mapping. Masked transitions enable partial replacement of DPL with SRL, saving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50,852 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> in registers, precharge gates, and multiplexers.

title: 25.10 A 65nm 0.066pJ/b Floating-Latch-Based True Random Number Generator Resilient to Power-Noise Injection Attacks
authors: Kai Cheng, Yunbo Huang, Zunsong Yang, Li Wang, Hongyu Ren, Xiaoyu Shan, Pui-In Mak, Yong Chen, Bo Li
abstract: In Paper 25.10 a floating-latch-based true random number generator (FL-TRNG) is presented that achieves an energy efficiency of 0.066pJ/b by leveraging a floating reservoir capacitor as its power supply. This architecture inherently offers strong resilience against power-supply noise injection attacks. Furthermore, current-starved inverters are employed within the latch to perform mismatch compensation and enhance PVT tolerance.

title: Voice-Activated Emergency Surveillance System
authors: Ambili A R, Jeslin P Jo, Adithya Dileep, Arya S, Athira Ramesh, Bhavana V Nath
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Citizen security is a crucial component of community well-being, especially during circumstances where individuals may face harassment or physical threats. Preset keywords like "help me" or "save me," which are used by traditional voice-activated safety systems, might not function in high-stress situations where victims are unable to speak certain words. This study proposes an intelligent speech-activated surveillance system that employs real-time voice pattern analysis to detect potential threats using both automated and human triggers. In order to process continuous audio input from a microphone and identify abusive or threatening speech, a trained Decision Tree classifier examines linguistic and paralinguistic features in a custom Natural Language Processing (NLP) pipeline. An onboard camera is automatically activated, GPS coordinates are acquired, and the detection is communicated to pre-registered emergency contacts. Location, audio, and video data are all securely stored in the cloud for later analysis. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system offers a dependable, privacy-preserving, and non-intrusive solution for early threat detection and prompt emergency response, with an accuracy of more than 90%

title: A 500kGy Radiation-Hardened 2.4GHz Wi-Fi Receiver for Innovative Nuclear Power Plant Decommissioning
authors: Yasuto Narukiyo, Sena Kato, Kotaro Yanaka, Yuya Takahashi, Masaya Miyahara, Jill Mayeda, Atsushi Shirane
abstract: This work presents a 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi receiver in 65 nm CMOS, designed with radiationhardened techniques to sustain operation under total ionizing dose to 500 kGy. Such tolerance addresses the requirements of nuclear power plant decommissioning, where conventional studies have considered doses of only several Gy. Measurement results confirm that even at 500 kGy, the proposed receiver maintains acceptable performance showing the feasibility of wireless systems in extreme radiation environments.

title: Cryogenic Performance of Vertical III-V Nanowire Gate-All-Around Ferroelectric FETs
authors: Mamidala Karthik Ram, Zhongyunshen Zhu, Lars-Erik Wernersson
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work investigates the cryogenic performance of InAs-based vertical nanowire ferroelectric field-effect transistors (VNW-FeFETs) with hafnium zirconium oxide (HZO) as the ferroelectric gate dielectric. Measurements at 300 K and 14 K reveal a 25% increase in memory window, from 560 mV at room temperature to 700 mV at 14 K, indicating enhanced polarization stability at low temperatures while also observing an improvement in the subthreshold swing. These results highlight the potential of VNW-FeFETs as scalable non-volatile memory elements for cryogenic computing applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of Two 16-Term On-Wafer Calibration Methods versus 8-Term SOLT Off-Wafer for RF Transistor Characterization up to 110 GHz
authors: Jojo Varghese, Thomas Zimmer, Tarek Bouzar, Philippine Billy, Sébastien Fregonese
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Accurate calibration is critical in RF and mm-wave device characterization, especially as BiCMOS technologies approach the sub-terahertz regime. In this work, we evaluate and compare two on-wafer 16-term calibration (16T) techniques against the traditional off-wafer 8-term SOLT method. Our analysis is based on measurements performed on a B55X SiGe HBT transistor, with transition frequencies (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>) approaching 400 GHz and maximum oscillation frequencies (f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MAX</inf>) exceeding 500 GHz. The calibration configurations studied include: (1) 16T with Short-Load, Load-Short (SLLS), (2) 16T with OpenLoad, Load-Open (LOOL) and (3) ISS SOLT Calibration. Extraction focuses on f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>, f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">MAX</inf>, and small-signal capacitances (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BE</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BC</inf>, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">CS</inf>). All measurements were acquired using an E8361A vector network analyzer equipped with N5260-60003 mm-wave frequency extenders, spanning 1 to 110 GHz. Our results suggest that on-wafer 16-term calibration provides a significantly more robust and accurate characterization framework compared to off-wafer SOLT-based methods, particularly at higher frequencies.

title: Solanum Lycopersicum Leaf Disease Prediction Using CNN Algorithm with MATLAB – Hybrid CNNAM Technique
authors: T.Bhuvaneswari, V.Janani, M.Harikaran, M.Iniyan, R.Koushikraja
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The Solanum Lycopersicum is among the most extensively cultivated crops and high commercial value in worldwide area. The hybrid Convolutional Neural Networks algorithm in the MATLAB environment used in this paper to organize and carry out a deep learning integrate with MATLAB based system that is reliable and accurate for detecting and classifying the Solanum Lycopersicum leaf petal diseases. The proposed model hybrid CNNAM is trained and validated using an extensive image dataset comprising both healthy and diseased tomato leaves. The hybrid CNNAM techniques to enhance the performance of classification based on comprehensive image preprocessing, including resizing, normalization, and segmentation using K-means clustering, followed by extraction of feature texture via the Gray-Level Co- occurrence Matrix (GLCM). The Graphical User Interface (GUI) displays the identified disease such as Leaf Mold, Late Blight, Early Blight, or Healthy along with extracted statistical and texture features. Experimental results of proposed method CNNAM model achieve high accuracy 97%, F1 score 0.98, Recall 0.98 and Precision 0.97 during both training and testing phases, demonstrating its capability to distinguish between visually similar disease patterns with minimal human intervention. The integration of CNNAM based classification with MATLAB’s advanced computational tools offers a practical and efficient framework for plant disease diagnostics. In future work, the proposed CNNAM model can be extended for real-time applications in agricultural fields. The deployment of the trained model on mobile or embedded devices will help farmers identify diseases instantly. Further research will focus on improving the system’s scalability, handling different lighting and environmental conditions, and integrating cloud or IoT platforms for large-scale monitoring. These improvements will enhance the practical relevance and usability of the proposed method in real-world scenarios.

title: ML-Based Human Gut Microbiome Classifier for Multi-Disease Risk Prediction
authors: Kaveen S M, Roshan Dibhag S, Aswin P, G Priyanka
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: A primary factor in overall health is the composition of the human gut microbiota; an imbalance of this microbiota has been shown to be associated with many types of metabolic disease, including type 2 diabetes (T2D). Although machine learning has shown promise in developing microbiota-based diagnostics, these models are typically hampered by the large number of variables associated with microbial abundance data, and by the inherent noise present in these data, leading to reduced performance when classifying previously unseen samples. In this study, we have developed a novel robust methodology for accurately classifying individuals diagnosed with T2D versus healthy control subjects based upon their gut microbiota profile. Our methodology involves a multi-stage approach to transform the complex multi- disease problem into a single binary classification task with a balanced, high signal-to-noise ratio. The key innovation of our methodology lies in the use of recursive feature elimination with cross-validation (RFECV) to select the optimal subset of features from the available dataset that maximizes the predictive ability of those features. Once the optimal feature subset is identified, it can be used as input to a trained XGBoost classifier whose hyperparameters were determined via Bayesian optimization techniques implemented through Optuna. Overall, we found that our methodology resulted in a highly accurate 76% prediction rate with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.82 on an independent test dataset. These results demonstrate that a well-defined, automated method for selecting features is critical to maximizing the potential for predicting the presence or absence of metabolic disease using microbiota-based data, and that such methodologies provide a solid foundation for developing effective, data-driven diagnostic tools.

title: Rethinking Early-Fusion Strategy for Palmprint and Palm Vein Fusion Recognition
authors: Yi Yu, Penghao Jin, Jun Wang, Zaiyu Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Multimodal biometric recognition has shown great potential in identity authentication tasks. Currently, most existing multimodal biometric recognition methods mainly employ multiple separate feature extraction networks for multimodal biometrics, leading to nearly multiple times the inference time compared to a single-stream feature extraction network. This increased inference time has hindered the widespread employment of multimodal biometric recognition in embedded devices for autonomous systems. To this end, we rethink the early-fusion strategy for palmprint and palm vein fusion recognition. First, an Image-Level Adaptive Fusion module (ILAF) is proposed to achieve the dynamic fusion of palmprint and palm vein images in pixel space, and then the fused images are inputted into the single-stream feature extraction network for identity recognition. Second, Modality-Aware Knowledge Distillation (MAKD) is presented, which includes PalmVein-Aware Knowledge Distillation (PVKD) and PalmPrint-Aware Knowledge Distillation (PPKD). This approach enables the single-stream feature extraction network to learn more modality-specific information of different modalities, thereby enhancing the discriminative ability of multimodal fusion representation. Experimental results on four multimodal biometric benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art multimodal biometric recognition models.

title: BiommWave: A Non-Visual Approach for Biometric Recognition Using Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Mupei Li, Yunlong Wang, Yiwei Ru, Kunbo Zhang, Zhenan Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: This paper explores the application of millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar in biometric recognition. As a non-visual human sensing technology, mmWave radar captures reflection properties and micro-movements, providing a complementary modality to visual appearance. We implemented a complete system pipeline to utilize mmWave sensing for individual recognition. Based on physiological mechanisms, we design preprocessing methods to extract intuitive biometric feature maps, concerning body reflections, cardiopulmonary activity, and micro-motion frequencies. To address the data uncertainty, a dynamic pole-based learning strategy is proposed to construct compact and discriminated feature distributions. In real-world evaluations, the system achieves 95.12% accuracy and an Equal Error Rate (EER) of 1.96%. This work leverages the advantages of mmWave radar for flexible, unconstrained, and private biometric systems. From a non-visual sensing perspective, it explores novel modalities as unique biometric cues, demonstrating significant value of research and applications.

title:
Compact Model for β-Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    MOSFET on Diamond and SiC Substrates
authors: Pragya Kushwaha
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: This work introduces a compact modeling approach for β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MOSFETs using the industry standard BSIMBULK HV framework. 2D numerical simulation of β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> on diamond device is performed to understand the physics of the device in the drift region. By accurately extracting key parameters such as effective gate oxide thickness (TOXE), flatband voltage (VFB), and drift region resistance (RDLCW), we calibrate the BSIM-BULK HV compact model for β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> channel based MOSFET. The proposed approach demonstrates the insights into β-Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> device behavior, offering a significant impact in modeling these promising ultra-wide bandgap semiconductor devices for advanced power electronics and high-frequency applications.

title: Explaining Convolutional Neural Networks via a Concise and Hierarchical Approach
authors: Jiawei Pan, Xiangyu Zhu, Haoyuan Zhang, Stan Z. Li, Zhen Lei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) aims to bring transparency to black-box neural networks. Many innovative explainable methods provide rich and multifaceted explanations. However, these methods often have complex mechanisms or a high complexity of explanations, making them difficult for people to understand. To address this challenge, we introduce a concept-based explainable method which can improve the readability of deep neural network explanations by obtaining a concise set of high-quality explanations. We reduce the explanation redundancy by weighted hierarchical clustering, thereby obtaining a set of explanations that completely describe the input image and are crucial to the network’s decision-making. Compared with existing XAI methods, our approach reduces the complexity while ensuring the integrity and comprehensibility of the explanation. In addition, we show that our approach can be traced back to the neuron-level explanation, which can also provide inspiration for model researchers to interpret the model. We validated the effectiveness of our approach through experiments in bird classification and facial recognition tasks. Specifically, we employed XAI to investigate the mechanisms behind face recognition, identifying critical neurons that correspond to key concepts in the recognition process.

title: Hierarchical Spatio-Temporal Representation Learning for Cross-Modal Gated Fusion in Driver Emotion Recognition
authors: Jing Yi, Xingang Mou, Wei Shen, Hongming Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Driver emotion recognition is essential for enhancing both safety and human–machine interaction in intelligent cockpits. Existing video-based methods primarily rely on static facial representations, overlooking subtle spatio-temporal dynamics in sequences. Moreover, single-modal RGB images are highly sensitive to illumination and occlusion, limiting robustness in in-vehicle scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an end-to-end, hierarchically optimized dual-stream video Transformer framework for driver emotion recognition. At the feature extraction stage, a Spatio-Temporal Attention Patch Embedding module (STAP-Embed) is introduced. It integrates CNNs for spatial encoding, bidirectional LSTMs for local temporal evolution, and multi-head temporal attention to generate dense patch embeddings, thereby improving the representation of micro-expressions and transient emotions. At the fusion stage, a Hybrid Fusion Block (HFB) is designed, which combines bidirectional cross-attention and input-driven gating to achieve adaptive RGB–IR fusion, significantly reducing sensitivity to illumination changes. Experiments on the PPB-Emo dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 81.17% accuracy and 81.25% F1-score, outperforming competitive baselines by 2.6% and 3.15%, respectively. Ablation studies further confirm that STAP-Embed and HFB independently and cumulatively contribute to performance gains. Finally, the paper discusses the model’s generalization ability and outlines potential directions for future extensions.

title: 3.4 an Ultrasound-Powering TX with Standing-Wave Peak Tracking Employing Adiabatic Power Sensing Achieving 82% Power-Tracking Accuracy and <90ms Settling Time for Brain Implants
authors: Marios Gourdouparis, Chengyao Shi, Jiang Liu, Yuming He, Stefano Stanzione, Wouter Serdijn, Yao-Hong Liu
abstract: An ultrasound powering TX ASIC for brain implants with autonomous on-chip standingwave peak tracking for RX power (PDL) regulation is presented. With a proposed adiabatic power-sensing scheme, the TX consumes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$43 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> for power tracking, and the system achieves a settling time of <90ms while using the standing-wave peak-tracking FSM. The TX can achieve PDL improvement of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.7\times$</tex> with a power-tracking accuracy of 82%.

title: 3.2 A Near-Field RF Reflection Transceiver ASIC for Continuous Unobtrusive Blood Pressure Monitoring
authors: Yiming Han, Neelotpala Kumar, Linran Zhao, Jide Yinka Adebiyi, Linrui Jiang, Hao Lu, Gina Perkins, Hirofumi Tanaka, Deji Akinwande, Yaoyao Jia
abstract: This work presents continuous, unobtrusive, and clinically accurate BP monitoring in a fully wearable form factor using an near-field RF reflection TRx ASIC in 65 nm CMOS. The NRR approach enables noncontact monitoring with comfort and long-term wear. The optimized TRx achieves 98.6 nV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</inf> IRN, while SAR-based DC-offset cancellation and closed-loop phase calibration mitigate Rx leakage and phase error for clinical accuracy. The ASIC is thoroughly validated in human studies achieving accurate BP extraction within 6mmHg mean absolute error.

title: Design of a Secured Graphical Master Password for Password Manager
authors: S. Rajarajan, PLK. Priyadarsini, G. Vahini
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Nowadays people manage multiple accounts across various applications, making it challenging to remember each password. It has become a common practice for users to reuse same passwords between multiple accounts. This makes the accounts vulnerable for attacks. Password managers offer an effective solution by securely storing usernames and passwords. But users need to choose a master password to unlock the password manager. Since the security of all the user accounts depends on the master password, it's crucial to choose a strong password and keep it confidential. Forgetting the master password could make it impossible to access the stored credentials. In this paper, we propose a novel graphical password scheme to construct a strong and memorable graphical master password made of images. The proposed scheme transforms the graphical password into a stronger textual password. So the security of master password is improved without burdening the users.

title: Enhancing Skin Cancer Detection with Hybrid Attention Networks and Triple-Force Deep Learning Model
authors: Angel Ida Chellam J, Priyadharshini S, Bharaninayagi S, Radjarejesri S, Rajeswari N P, Deepa T
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: In this research, present a Hybrid Attention and Triple-Force Deep Learning (TFDL) framework for automated skin cancer detection and classification via dermoscopic images. Our proposed framework combines the Hybrid Attention U-Net (HAU-Net) for precise segmentation, combining Channel Attention (CAM), and Spatial Attention (SAM) modules to increase feature localization and delineate lesion boundaries. The Adaptive Ensemble Learning model consisting of three state-of-the-art convolutional networks (VGG16, EfficientNet-B3, and ResNet50) is then used for classification, where feature aggregation is dynamically weighted, referring to the acronym AEL. The design of AEL enables improved learning efficiency, generalization, and interpretability of the model. The experiments using benchmark skin cancer datasets (HAM10000, ISIC, and DermNet) for evaluation purposes and show that we achieve 98.1% accurate diagnostic accuracy, 97.5% sensitivity, and 98.6% specificity when combined. These accuracy metrics are 3–5% better than that of the original CNN or even existing ensemble models in diagnostic accuracy. The findings of this research confirm that the proposed triple force deep learning framework provides a clinically viable, explainable, and high-precision AI system for real-time skin cancer screening and early diagnosis of skin cancer, reducing diagnostic errors, and assisting in the clinical decision-making process for dermatologists.

title: WaveVerify: A Novel Audio Watermarking Framework for Media Authentication and Combatting Deepfakes
authors: Aditya Pujari, Ajita Rattani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: With the rise of voice synthesis technology threatening digital media integrity, audio watermarking has become a crucial defense for content authentication. However, current solutions often lack robustness to effects like high-pass filtering and temporal modifications and suffer from poor watermark localization. We introduce WaveVerify, a watermarking system that addresses these challenges using a Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM)-based generator for resilient multiband watermark embedding and a Mixture-of-Experts detector for accurate extraction and localization. Our unified training framework enhances robustness by applying multiple distortions per backpropagation step via a dynamic effect scheduler. Evaluations across multiple datasets show WaveVerify outperforms SOTA models like AudioSeal and WavMark, achieving zero Bit Error Rate (BER) under common distortions and MIoU scores of 0.98+ under severe temporal modifications. The parallel FiLM-based generator also reduces training time by ∼80% compared to sequential embedding approaches. Code and pretrained models are available at: https://github.com/vcbsl/WaveVerify.

title: UDO: Unsupervised Disparity Optimization with Local Geometric Constraints for Urban Satellite Imagery
authors: Li Zhao, Henan Li, Shifeng Jiang, Qiang Zhi, Bingqian Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Accurate disparity estimation in satellite stereo images is crucial for three-dimensional (3D) urban reconstruction but remains challenging due to large image sizes, occlusions, and illumination variations. Traditional methods falter in textureless areas and under illumination changes, while deep learning approaches, despite improved accuracy, often suffer from limited generalization and stability in complex scenarios. We introduce the Unsupervised Disparity Optimization (UDO) algorithm for urban satellite imagery, which exploits local geometric constraints. UDO integrates superpixel segmentation with homography estimation to capture fine-grained structural variations while preserving global consistency. A belief-guided adaptive update, operating in an unsupervised manner without external ground truth, iteratively refines disparities and yields more accurate and robust depth estimation. Experimental results demonstrate that UDO effectively improves disparity maps of deep learning-based stereo matching models, achieving robustness and precision in high-resolution satellite images of complex urban scenes, with maximum improvements of 4.22% in D1, 2.96% in EPE, and 3.37% in RMSE. The code is available at https://github.com/henanleo/UDO.git.

title: 7.7 A Fully Reconfigurable Hybrid SPAD Vision Sensor with 134dB Dynamic Range Using Time-Coded Dual Exposures
authors: Kieop Hong, Jubin Kang, Jung-Hye Hwang, Insang Son, Seonghyeok Park, Jung-Hoon Chun, Jaehyuk Choi, Seong-Jin Kim
abstract: This paper presents a fully reconfigurable HDR intensity/event hybrid SPAD sensor. The fully reconfigurable scheme enables both pixel types to be freely blended and operated simultaneously. A time-coded dual exposure encodes saturation time to provide an adaptive secondary exposure, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>134 \text{dB}$</tex> DR in both modes while reducing quantization error of the time code. Event detection with pixel-wise shot-noise thresholding keeps uniform event noise < 0.46% across all illumination conditions.

title: A Robust and Compliant Robotic Assembly Control Strategy for Batch Precision Assembly Task_supp1-3669249.docx
authors: Dan WU
abstract: In many high-precision industrial applications, robots are deployed to perform precision peg-in-hole assembly on large batches of manufactured pegs and holes. When the nominal design adopts a transition fit, machining errors may cause each peg–hole pair to exhibit either a clearance fit or a slight interference fit, with an unknown and continuously varying fit amount. This paper addresses robotic batch precision assembly tasks under uncertain fit types and fit amounts, and proposes a novel multi-stage framework for systematically constructing a robust assembly control strategy. The overall batch precision assembly task is first decomposed into multiple deterministic subtasks characterized by different but fixed fit amounts. For these subtasks, a force-vision fusion controller-driven reinforcement learning method combined with a multi-task reinforcement learning training framework (FVFC-MTRL) is developed to jointly learn multiple compliance control strategies. Subsequently, a multi-teacher policy distillation scheme is designed to integrate multiple trained strategies into a single student strategy, yielding a robust control strategy. Real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed method successfully constructs a robust control strategy for high-precision assembly tasks with varying fit types and fit amounts. Moreover, the MTRL framework significantly improves training efficiency by 50%, and the final control strategy achieves superior force compliance and a higher success rate compared with several existing methods.

title: Relay Performance in D2D Communications Overlaying Multi-Antenna Cellular Networks
authors: Sutanu Ghosh, Iacovos I. Ioannou, Santi P. Maity, Prabagarane Nagaradjane, Vasos Vassiliou
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper reports the outage performance of different relaying mechanisms, namely amplify-and-forward (AF), decode-and-forward (DF) and their hybrid mode, in a multi-antenna cooperative cognitive radio network (CCRN). The proposed CCRN model supports two-way device-to-device (D2D) communications through spectrum sharing of the existing cellular network. In this architecture, cellular nodes are considered as primary users (PUs) and the associated devices in D2D communications act as secondary users (SUs). Furthermore, it is also considered that the SU devices are able to harvest energy from PUs’ radio frequency signals, using simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) protocol. Closed form expressions of outage probability for both cellular and D2D communications are derived and the analytical results are validated through simulation results. Numerical values show that performance of the proposed system is about ∼ 92% and ∼ 47% better in terms spectrum efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE), respectively over the existing system using DF relaying mechanism. The study also reports the various relaying options at different SU nodes and their relative performance comparison. Based on the exhaustive simulation results, it is found that hybrid relaying is the best option over all other single relaying techniques. It is also observed that about ∼ 12% SE improvement is achieved using multiple antenna over the single antenna communications.

title: GAPSO-Optimized GRFT Algorithm for Maneuvering Target Detection
authors: Shuoyu Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The Generalized Radon-Fourier Transform (GRFT) suffers from high computational complexity and poor real-time performance due to global parameter search when detecting maneuvering targets. To address this problem, a fast detection method based on the adaptive Particle Swarm OptimizationGenetic Algorithm (PSO-GA) hybrid optimization algorithm is proposed. This algorithm integrates the fast local exploitation capability of Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and the strong global exploration capability of Genetic Algorithm (GA), aiming to significantly improve search efficiency while maintaining the high detection performance of GRFT. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm is analyzed and verified through simulation experiments. On the premise of ensuring no loss of detection performance, fast and accurate detection of maneuvering targets is achieved.

title: DRvalve: A Dual-Layer Resource Scheduling Approach Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhijie Han, Anbang Wang, Ying Du
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Achieving efficient resource utilization while pre-serving end-user experience is a central challenge for cloud operators. Modern applications increasingly adopt microservice architectures, requiring management of both end-to-end latency and per-service resources. Translating resource allocation into performance is difficult because requests traverse heterogeneous services with complex dependencies. We propose DRvalve, a dual-layer framework that decouples global SLO feedback from local resource control via performance targets. A global deep reinforcement learning controller periodically sets CPU-throttling targets, and per-service allocators adjust cgroup quotas accordingly. On two real-world microservices, DRvalve reduces CPU usage by up to $19.9 \%$ relative to the best-performing baseline and, compared to all baseline methods, achieves overall savings of up to $46.2\%$ across diverse workloads.

title:
Attention-Guided Knowledge Distillation for Efficient U
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    Net Based Salient Object Detection
authors: Zheng Liu, Pinhe Wang, Ming Zeng, Kun Liu, Feiqi Deng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Salient Object Detection (SOD) aims to accurately locate and segment visually distinctive regions in an image, which playing a critical role in intelligent perception and visual inspection tasks. Although U<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Net has achieved notable performance in SOD, its complex architecture limits deployment in resource-constrained scenarios. To address this issue, we propose an attention-guided self-distillation framework to enhance the performance of a lightweight U<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Net. Specifically, during the encoding stage, we leverage deep-layer channel attention and Gram-based correlation to distill comprehensive semantic relationships into shallow layers, which improves their inter-channel representation capability. In the decoding stage, multi-scale spatial attention distillation transfers spatial awareness from high-resolution outputs to intermediate layers, facilitating fine-grained reconstruction of salient regions. Comprehensive experiments on public SOD benchmarks demonstrate that our method significantly enhances both accuracy and efficiency compared with the baseline lightweight U<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-Net, achieving competitive results while maintaining a compact model size.

title: FTCS 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)

title: Decoding Sleep Patterns through Explainable AI An Interaction Aware Hive Ensemble Model
authors: R. Bhuvanya, T. Kujani, K. Kishore Kumar, G. Thanigaivel
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Sleep quality, affected by lifestyle, stress, and physiological aspects, is an important indicator of overall well-being. In order to forecast sleep health, this research proposes the, Explainable Interaction Aware Hive Ensemble Framework (EIA-X). The model incorporates SMOTE balance, polynomial feature interactions, and several base learners such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, LightGBM, Logistic Regression, and XGBoost—into a hive ensemble method. Based on experimental findings, baseline models such as LightGBM (0.907), Gradient Boosting (0.893), and Random Forest (0.89) are outperformed by EIA-X in accuracy (0.920). Moreover, EIA-X improves progressively with respect to ROC-AUC and F1-score metrics, indicating generalizability and strength. Integration of Explainable AI offers interpretative insights into feature influence, making the proposed model a clear, effective framework for decision support and individualized sleep health prediction.

title: Dynamic Spectrum Access Using Adaptive Propagation Deep Graph Neural Network in 5G and Reconfigurable Wireless Networks
authors: M. Ramkumar, Dhanya Krishnan, Sincija C, Vidhya S, S Karthikeyani, R. Sarathkumar
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The drastic growth of 5 G and reconfigurable wireless networks (RWN) necessitates effective usage of limited spectrum resources. The traditional fixed spectrum allocation results in frequent underexploitation, requiring the use of dynamic spectrum access (DSA) mechanisms. However, fairness, flexibility, and scalability become hard to achieve in extremely dynamic environments. Interference management, throughput maximization, and computational cost are concerns with existing schemes, pushing the need for designing an effective deep learning-based approach. This paper introduces Dynamic Spectrum Access using Adaptive Propagation Deep Graph Neural Network in 5G and Reconfigurable Wireless Networks (APD-GNN-DSA). It begins with having a system model based on stochastic arrivals and Markov states to describe idle, occupied, and shared spectrums states. Spectrum sensing translates raw signals into organized state vectors. The Scale-aware Modulation Transformer (SaMT) generates adaptive access, share, or wait decisions. Rewards are securely stored with the Hybrid Consensus Blockchain Sharding Protocol (HCBSP). Valid experiences train the Adaptive Propagation Deep Graph Neural Network (APD-GNN) for spatial and temporal relationship capturing and Tasmanian Devil Optimization (TDO) optimizes parameters for non-stationary 5 G system resilience. Simulation results confirm that APD-GNN-DSA outperforms the existing methods with 99.3% sensing accuracy, 3.2 ms idle time, 98.7% channel utilization, and 99.1% access probability. In short, APD-GNN-DSA achieves intelligent, secure, and adaptive spectrum access with scalable and high-performance solutions for future wireless networks of 5 G and beyond.

title: Hierarchical Adaptive Robust Control for Trajectory Tracking of Distributed Drive Electric Vehicles Subject to Parametric Uncertainties
authors: Jigang Huang, Weiwei Li, Lin Li, Tongtong Gu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)
abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical adaptive robust control strategy for trajectory tracking of Distributed Drive Electric Vehicles (DDEVs) under parametric uncertainties and external disturbances. A control-oriented model integrating lateral dynamics and tracking kinematics is established and formulated as an uncertain MIMO system. To address modeling errors and unknown nonlinearities, an adaptive integral sliding mode controller (AISMC) with a radial basis function (RBF) neural network is designed. The adaptive laws and control gains are derived using Lyapunov stability theory and linear matrix inequalities (LMIs), ensuring finite-time convergence and robustness. The upper-level controller generates the front steering angle and additional yaw moment, while the lower-level controller optimally distributes wheel torques via quadratic programming that minimizes tire workload under physical constraints. Simulation results conducted on the CarSim–Simulink platform verify that the proposed hierarchical control strategy achieves accurate trajectory tracking, enhanced lateral stability, and smooth control performance under various uncertainties, outperforming conventional MPC and non-adaptive ISMC methods.

title: FPGA-Based Implementation of LeNet Architecture in Verilog for Disease Prediction
authors: E. Dhiravidachelvi, A. Muthuvel, K.Sabitha Banu, H. Peeroli, M.Thahzeeb Fathima
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Early and accurate disease prediction through medical image analysis is a critical step in modern healthcare systems. This paper presents an FPGA-based implementation of the LeNet convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture for disease classification, designed and simulated using Verilog HDL in Xilinx Vivado. The proposed workflow begins with dataset preprocessing, where medical images are converted into. mem files using Python scripts for compatibility with hardware simulation. The LeNet model is trained in software to obtain optimized weights and biases, which are then mapped into Verilog modules to replicate the network architecture on FPGA. The system is evaluated through functional simulation to verify prediction accuracy and synthesized to analyze hardware performance metrics, including power consumption and resource utilization. Experimental results demonstrate that the hardware realization of LeNet provides efficient disease classification with reduced latency and improved energy efficiency compared to software-only implementations. This work highlights the feasibility of deploying CNN-based disease prediction models on FPGA platforms, offering a scalable and low-power solution for real-time medical diagnostics.

title: Intelligent System -Driven Coal Supply Chain Through IOT, Machine Learning, and Blockchain Augmentation
authors: E. Sujatha, S. Palpandi, S. Sharmila, D. Elangovan, T. Indumathy
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The coal supply chain is traditionally operated by heavy machinery such as stacker reclaimers, conveyor systems and wagon loaders. While these systems form a solid operating foundation, their full capacity can be unlocked through intelligent growth. This paper suggests how the existing coal handling equipment can be increased with IOT sensor to monitor the realtime position, the machine learning algorithm for future maintenance and operating adaptation, and blockchain for safe, tampering-proof data logging. Instead of replacing heritage systems, this study takes a comparative approach - currently uses images and operations as baseline - and proposes smart upgrade that improves security, reduces downtime, and ensures transparency. A special attention is paid to the risk of spontaneous combustion in a pilewong stack, indicating how thermal imaging and IOT-active sprinkler systems can effectively reduce the dangers of fire. By integrating the traditional infrastructure with intelligent technologies, the task underlines a scalable roadmap for more efficient, safe and durable coal supply chain. In addition, the proposed systems provide valuable improvements not only for coal, but also for industries such as defense and pharmaceuticals such as logistics, manufacturing and even sensitive areas.

title: Ceramic Ultrasonic Signal Recognition Model Based on Kolmogorov-Arnold Network
authors: Aiguo Li, Yingjie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Big Data Applications, Mechatronics Engineering and Automation (BDAMEA)
abstract: Time series classification has always been an important research direction and has been widely used in various fields. The existing time series classification models based on deep learning methods have the problems of weak ability to extract nonlinear features and low model classification accuracy in processing time series data. To solve this problem, the CR-KAN model was proposed by combining the Kolmogorov-Arnold network (KAN) and the convolutional neural network (CNN). The model combines the convolutional layer with the KAN layer, the basic convolution obtains the local features of the time series data, and the KAN layer constructs a high-dimensional map through the B-spline function to extract the nonlinear relationship of the time series data, and at the same time adds residual connections to better capture the global features of the time series data. Through the experimental analysis on the one-dimensional time series dataset of 3 UCR public and the Private Ceramic Sonic Time Series Dataset, the results show that the CR-KAN model is superior to the CNN, ResNet, LightGBM, KAN base model and the TimeSformer, LSTM-KAN, ST-T emerging models in most cases.

title: Evaluation of Audio Quality Performance Metrics in LSB-Based Audio Steganography
authors: K Revathi, S. Kaja Mohideen
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Audio steganography secures multimedia communication by embedding data within audio files. The success of such communication depends on how resilient the stego audio file is to distortion and detection. Various research studies have used different metrics to evaluate audio quality. The objective of this study is to analyze and compare multiple metrics, such as Mean Squared Error (MSE), Peak Signal-toNoise Ratio (PSNR), Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR), Audio Fidelity (AF), and Percentage Root Mean Square Difference (PRD), to determine the most suitable metric for measuring audio quality in steganographic communication. The average metric values are MSE: 8.1898E-12, PRD: 6.1240E-05, PSNR: 32.8540 dB, SNR: 28.0120 dB, and AF: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.3700 \mathrm{E}-09$</tex>, indicating a low level of distortion in the stego audio, which supports secure and efficient multimedia communication.

title: Automated Screening and Severity Assessment of Retinopathy of Prematurity Using Vessel Analysis and ANFIS
authors: Porkumaran Karantharaj, Prabakar Srinivasan, Subramaniam Ganesan, Samson Isaac J, Ramani Kannan
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Retinal vessel vasculature occurs in the prematurity baby, who are having crucial risk factors of gestation age period less than or equal to 32 weeks and birth weight of 1500 grms, and causes for the visual impairment or blindness from the childhood if not treated correctly, which is known as Retinopathy of Prematurity(ROP). So it is very essential to measure the vessel's tortuosity, dilation and morphological properties, which affect the vascularised retina for diagnosis, screening and treatment. The main objective is to screen the severity level of ROP with its earliest development and prevent the child from vision loss by providing proper treatment which depends also the severe level. In this paper, ROP images are adopted for three pre-processing techniques such as green colour plane, histogram equalization and contrast limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), and wavelet transform. Then vessel's and ridge parameters are taken from the segmented image. These parameters are given to Adaptive Neuro Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) as a sample data. ANFIS classifier screens the severity level of given image which is very useful for further treatment. Out of 50 images, only 2 images are gotten erroneous results for vessels and out of 38 images, 7 images are gotten erroneous results for ridge. Better accuracy is achieved. This work could be enhanced to screen the severe level based on the vessels growth development in the three zones namely, zone1, zone2 and zone3 which could also be used to identify the plus disease along with the severe stages.

title: Enhancing Smart Grid Performance with LSTM-Based Real-Time State Monitoring and Prediction
authors: S. Simonthomas, S. Balakrishnan, K. Shantha Shalini, S. Alex, J. Jayasuthan, Bandaru Siva Ganesh
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: The modern smart grid calls for innovative and system adaptive data driven intelligence technologies to ensure reliability, stability, and efficiency in real-time. This study develops an enhanced framework called Enhancing Smart Grid Performance with LSTM-Based Real-Time State Monitoring and Prediction that provides effective grid management using deep learning and time-series analytics to perform proactive grid management. To perform a real-time anomaly detection and state forecasting, the proposed system uses the neural Long Short Memory model (LSTM) network to analyze the multidimensional electrical parameters such as load, voltage, generation trend, etc. By constantly learning from actual operations' data, the framework self-adapts to grid fluctuations, overcoming instability and minimizing energy losses. The predictive modeling has been integrated with the benefits of early fault detection, better demand-supply alignment, and efficient power distribution. In addition, the interpretability and scalability of LSTM-based architectures for large-scale smart grids are emphasized in the study. Experimental simulations have been conducted confirming that proposed model allows robust decision-making and improve grid resilience in dynamic environmental and load conditions. The WSN strategy provides a sustainable approach to achieve autonomous, intelligent, and self-healing smart grid systems.

title: ASL-YOLO: A Lightweight and Efficient YOLOv11 Model for Rail Surface Defect Detection
authors: Yiyang Lin, Li Mou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: In response to the high computational cost and poor detection of small, low-contrast defects in existing rail surface inspection methods, this paper proposes ASL-YOLO, a lightweight detector based on the YOLO11n framework. To improve both efficiency and accuracy, an asymmetric downsampling module (ADown) is introduced in the backbone to preserve fine rail-edge structures while reducing computation, a parameter-free SimAM attention block is inserted in deeper layers to highlight defect regions, and a Lightweight Streamlined Convolutional Detection head (LSCD) that reuses a compact convolutional block before the classification and regression branches to reduce redundant parameters in the prediction head. In addition, Wise-IoU v3 replaces the conventional IoU-based regression loss and dynamically focuses on high-quality samples. Experimental results show that, compared with YOLO11n, ASL-YOLO reduces the parameter count from 2.58 M to 2.07 M and the FLOPs from 6.3 GFLOPs to 4.7 GFLOPs, while increasing precision, recall, and $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@}xg1 0.5$ by 6.8,5.9, and 4.6 percentage points, respectively. These results indicate that ASL-YOLO achieves a better balance between accuracy and efficiency and is suitable for real-time rail surface defect detection.

title: A CNN-GRU-Attention Hybrid Model with DPDK Acceleration forHigh-Performance Network Threat Detection
authors: Jinghan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: To address the real-time requirements of highperformance network threat detection in encrypted traffic environments, a hybrid deep learning model called DCGA is proposed. This model integrates dynamic convolution, gated recurrent units, and multi-head attention mechanisms to extract spatial features of traffic, model temporal dependencies, and focus on key information, respectively. Innovatively, neural networks and decision trees are combined, with the former used for feature learning and generating interpretable rules, which are then compiled into access control list rules. High-speed online traffic filtering is achieved using the DPDK data plane development kit. Experimental results demonstrate that this framework achieves high detection accuracy and low inference latency on multiple public datasets, validating the feasibility and superiority of an end-to-end solution from intelligent detection to efficient execution.

title: Design and Fabrication of Fruit Peel Fertilizer Maker
authors: P.M. Arunkumar, S. Sathiyavathi, S.A. Nakul, S.V. Nandhaa, R. Ramdeepak, S.Sakthi Mahendran
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Consumption of fruits has recorded an upsurge in the recent past, and actually, the use and incorporation of fruit peels into different industries has attracted much attention in the same relation to their nutritional value. In this study, we described an efficient processing method for the treatment of fruit peels through heat drying and grinding into fine powder. The system includes a hot air circuit composed of nichrome, mainly for heat drying, and a grinder motor for grinding the dried peels. In order to open and close the lid of the carrying case to the different chambers for the next cycle of the process, a servo motor is used. For effective and controlled peeling of fruits, the nichrome heating coil with high electrical resistance and high rate of heat generation is used to dry fruit peels. In this way, the best conditions for drying can be determined, which will allow maintaining maximal nutritional value and taste of the peels. After drying the fruit peel, they are conveyed to the grinding chamber, where, through the power of the grinder motor, the fruit peels are crushed into a fine powder. This step improves the utility and value addition of the fruit peel powder for use in food, cosmetic, and pharmaceutical applications. The particularity of the servo-controlled lid mechanism is that it is an automatic and fully controlled system to perform the tasks. With the use of a servo motor, the lid is programmed to open and close at a given time and interval to ease the interchange from the drying chamber to the grinding chamber with minimal interference. In general, the presented integrated system can be considered to be a one-step solution for the processing of fruit peels and the recovery of valuable nutrients and bioactive compounds for various purposes. Its modular character, as well as incorporating automation to a certain degree, enables it to provide for both small-scale producers and industrial use.

title: An Extended Standard Cell Library Approach for Voltage and Cell Co-optimization in EnergyEfficient IC Design
authors: Dashan Pan, Rong Yao, Jinming Huang, Yan Li, Yongfei Ma, Shiyun Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: In response to the escalating demand for energy efficiency in processor design, this paper presents a novel circuit energy optimization methodology centered on enhancing the energy efficiency of standard cells. Through a comprehensive analysis of standard cell library performance impacts—focusing on inverters within combinational logic—this work achieves a meticulous balance between power consumption and performance by fine-tuning transistor dimensions and optimizing circuit structures. Theoretical and simulation analyses identify an optimal operating voltage of approximately $\mathbf{0. 6 V}$ for FinFET-based inverters, utilizing the minimum process channel length and moderate transistor widths. Based on these parameters, an energy-efficiency-optimized extended library was developed, accompanied by a methodology compatible with commercial EDA flows. Validation in critical computational control core modules demonstrates an energy efficiency improvement exceeding 10% without compromising the target operating frequency, underscoring the significant practical value of the proposed approach.

title: A Hybrid AI Framework for Predictive and Prescriptive Analytics Using Gradient Boosting, Graph Neural Networks, and Transformers
authors: R. Thiyagarajan, M. Banupriya, S. Pavithra, J. Sumitha, P. Preetha, B. Arya Shree
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Contemporary organizations are increasingly adopting advanced data frameworks in creating decentralized, scalable, and secure data ecosystems. When integrated with cloud infrastructure, edge computing, and AI-driven analytics, these architectures dismantle data silos and enable real-time, insight-oriented decision- making. Leveraging state-of-the-art algorithms, including gradient boosting, graph neural networks, and transformer-based models, predictive and prescriptive analytics attain superior accuracy, adaptability, and contextual relevance.

title: Agentic RAG for Command Generation in Automated Penetration Testing
authors: Zhengkun Chen, Chuanjun Yi, Pan Jia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown great potential in automated penetration testing (PT), but they need to rely on external knowledge to obtain precise output in complex command generation tasks. Although the traditional retrievalaugmented generation (RAG) can supplement external evidence to alleviate the hallucination problem, its static, single-round retrieval mechanism struggles to meet the dynamic knowledge needs of PT: the initial retrieval results may not cover the knowledge gaps generated during the reasoning process. To address it, this paper proposes a Reason-in-Document framework based on Agentic RAG, which transforms a single query into multi-step dynamic retrieval reasoning. Following the ReAct paradigm, the agent identifies knowledge gaps in the reasoning process, constructs targeted queries, and retrieves knowledge using the designated tools. By this mechanism, the system can gradually evolve the initial single query into a semantically progressive sub-query, enabling layer-by-layer knowledge refinement from conceptual-level understanding to implementation-level details.In this paper, a prototype system is implemented on the basis of PentestGPT, a structured knowledge base is constructed, and a variety of retrieval tool sets are designed. In the experiments of 22 real vulnerability scenarios, the Agentic RAG framework improves the accuracy of penetration command generation by 36% compared with the traditional RAG, which significantly enhances the knowledge utilization rate and the ability to generate complex commands. It provides a more efficient and accurate command generation mechanism for intelligent penetration testing.

title: In-Situ PCB Trace Continuity Testing via Near-Field Magnetic Signatures Based on Lock-in Amplifier
authors: Yonghui Liu, Zixin Lin, Wenhua Feng, Shunxiang Wei, Yuying Li, Zixin Wang, Mu-Shui Zhang
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: In this paper, we propose an in-situ non-contact method for PCB trace continuity testing based on a lock-in amplifier (LIA). The technique eliminates the need for expensive external signal generators by leveraging the chip’s own I/O pins to output excitation signals, which drive the target PCB trace and induce time-varying magnetic fields around it. A dedicated near-field magnetic probe captures these magnetic field signatures and converts the field variations into measurable voltage signals. These voltage signals are then fed into the LIA, which uses phase-sensitive detection to simultaneously extract two critical parameters: the amplitude and phase of the target signal. Systematic experiments validate that the proposed method achieves a balanced performance between detection sensitivity and measurement stability, enabling reliable discrimination between continuous traces and open-circuit faults. Furthermore, supplementary experiments that introduce spatial magnetic noise via a switching power supply confirm the method’s strong noise immunity. As such, this approach provides a sensitive and reliable solution for in-situ quality control in the manufacturing of high-density electronics.

title: Development of Multi-Channel MIMO SC-FDE Modem for 700-Mbps-class Immersive Video Transmission using Millimeter-Wave Band
authors: Tomohiro Shimazaki, Nobuaki Tanaka, Junichiro Kawamoto, Yoshifumi Matsusaki, Naohiko Iai, Tsuyoshi Nakatogawa
abstract: We have been conducting research and development on a wireless transmission scheme using the 42-GHz-band in millimeter waves, which enables large-capacity transmission with a wide frequency bandwidth, to realize the wireless transmission required for content production such as 3D and ultra-wide field-of-view video that provides a sense of presence and immersion. This paper reports a prototype modem that implements a multi-channel 2 × 4 MIMO SC-FDE system that is capable of 700-Mbps-class signal transmission and the results of evaluating its basic performance. By comparing the bit error rate characteristics of the AWGN channel between the computer simulation and prototype, it was confirmed that the prototype achieves the desired performance and provides a prospect for realizing 700-Mbps-class immersive video transmission using a 42GHz band multi-channel 2 × 4 MIMO SC-FDE system.

title: Storm Sight: Next-Gen Vision Models for Cyclone Intensity Estimation
authors: Prathik Kumaar. B, A. Deepak
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Accurate cyclone intensity estimation is essential for effective disaster preparedness and mitigation. Deeplearning (DL)-based models have shown potential in improving prediction accuracy; however, selecting the most suitable model remains challenging, particularly with limited datasets. This study explores the use of ConvNeXt Small and ConvNeXt Tiny for cyclone intensity estimation, leveraging infrared satellite imagery to predict cyclone strength. The methodology includes (i) data preprocessing and re-sizing, (ii) deep learningbased feature extraction, (iii) regression-based prediction of cyclone intensity, and (iv) statistical validation of model performance. The evaluation is based on Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE),Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> Score. The results demonstrate that ConvNeXt Small outperforms ConvNeXt Tiny, achieving a lower MAE (9.46 vs. 12.15) and a higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 2 6 5}$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{0. 0 1 6 5}$</tex>). The findings indicate that ConvNeXt Small is better suited for cyclone intensity estimation with limited data, reinforcing the importance of selecting efficient models for real-world applications.

title: A Systolic Array Matrix Multiplier on RF-SoC for Wireless AI Applications
authors: Gayani Rathnasekara, Arjuna Madanayake
abstract: This paper presents a polyphase systolic array for implementing a 512-tap digital FIR filter on the Xilinx RF-SoC ZCU-111 platform using a matrix-vector multiplier core. The design is tailored for high-speed real-time applications and supports dynamic coefficient assignment. The proposed architecture is designed from scratch instead of using Xilinx-provided FIR cores to accommodate the configurability of high tap counts, parallel processing, adaptive coefficient updates in real-time RF applications, and portability for any FPGA or ASIC platforms. In addition, this custom design leverages multiplier symmetry and pipelining. Real-time operations across 8 parallel data paths processing at 256 MHz on RF-SoC digital fabric leading to 1 TeraMAC/s throughput. The digital architecture is verified using a low-pass filter and a bandpass filter. Thus, experimental results demonstrate successful realization of the custom designed FIR filter core in wide band signal processing scenarios in advanced wireless communication, RF AI/ML applications, spectrum sensing, electronic warfare (EW), and signal intelligence applications.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Recognition System for Weld Defects Based on Sparse Dictionary Matrix
authors: Qinyang Zhang, Junjie He, Xiao Yang, Weixin Gao
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: To solve manual reliance, poor noise resistance and low robustness in industrial weld defect detection, an intelligent system based on sparse dictionary matrix is proposed. It optimizes weld images via preprocessing, builds adaptive sparse dictionary with defect features, uses sparse coding and reconstruction error classification for recognition. Experiments show it suppresses noise, adapts to defect types, boosts generality/reliability, offering an intelligent weld quality inspection solution.

title: Session 5 Overview: Sub-THz and mm-Wave Phased Arrays and Beamformers
authors: N/A

Design of a Novel Asymmetric Hybrid Supercapacitor with Na

authors: Yaqi Chen
abstract: Asymmetric hybrid supercapacitors (AHSCs) have drawn considerable attention these days, owing to their superior energy and power density characteristics. This work designed a novel AHSC with an extended 1.9 V operating voltage window by ingeniously pairing a Na<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.6</inf>CoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> anode and a Mo<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1.33</inf>CT<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf> MXene thin film cathode. The Na<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.6</inf>CoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> anode achieved a wide voltage window of 0-1.0V and an exceptional areal capacity of 701.3 F/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at 2 A/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, while the Mo<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1.33</inf>CT<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf> MXene cathode also exhibited excellent performance in −0.9 to 0.0 V potential.

title: FSR-Unet: An Improved U-Net Model with Fourier-Skip Refinement for High-Precision Downhole Perforation Image Segmentation
authors: Musen Yuan, Qinyang Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: With the continuous optimization of China's energy structure, shale oil and gas development has become a key direction in the field of unconventional oil and gas. Horizontal well multi-stage fracturing technology is at the core of this extraction technique, where the precise identification of perforation shapes is crucial for oil and gas production capacity. Traditional methods for identifying perforation shapes rely on manual interpretation or conventional image segmentation algorithms, which struggle with common issues in downhole imaging such as noise interference, uneven lighting, and blurred edge contours. This paper proposes an improved U-Net model, known as FSR-Unet, which enhances the extraction of small target features and adapts better to complex imaging environments by incorporating a Fourier-Skip Refinement (FSR) module at key feature layers of the encoder. Experimental results demonstrate that FSR-Unet achieves superior performance in downhole perforation image segmentation tasks, with mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) and mean Pixel Accuracy (mPA) reaching 90.99% and 97.19%, respectively, significantly outperforming other methods and validating the model's effectiveness and superiority in the field of perforation image segmentation.

title: Enhanced ConvNeXt-V2 Framework with Squeeze-and-Excitation Attention Module for Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy using Fundus Images
authors: T Devishree, K Abdul Rahman, R Sathishkumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a progressive microvascular complication associated with diabetes and is a leading cause of preventable blindness globally. The automated and accurate grading of DR from retinal fundus images is challenging due to high intra-class similarity, variations in illumination, and class imbalance. This study introduces an improved convolutional architecture for classifying DR severity, utilizing the ConvNeXt-V2-Tiny backbone enhanced with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention modules. A customized preprocessing pipeline using green-channel CLAHE and Rayleigh-gamma is applied to enhance lesion contrast, while LDAM loss and class-balanced sampling address skewed label distributions. The model is trained on the APTOS-2019 dataset, which includes 3,662 fundus images across five severity levels, and is evaluated using clinically relevant metrics. The proposed framework achieves an accuracy of 87.2%, a macro-F1 score of 0.78, and a Quadratic Weighted Kappa of 0.70, outperforming the ResNet-50 and Xception baselines. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of ConvNeXt-V2-SE in delivering robust, interpretable, and computationally efficient diagnostic performance for large-scale DR screening.

title: Three-Party Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution Based on GHZ States
authors: Shuquan Ma, Ru Wang, Luhe Zhang, Zhe Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: Device-independent quantum key distribution (DI-QKD) represents the strongest form of physical security in QKD, it successfully wipes out almost all underlying unsafe factors in reality. The security premise of DI-QKD protocols is based barely on general physical laws and dose not require any detailed knowledge or control of the physical devices used in the protocol. In this paper, we devise a simple three-party DI-QKD protocol using three-qubit Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ) states, by which three participants can negotiate a common secure key. We consider our protocol in a noisy quantum channel. Finally, we briefly analyze the security of our protocol and derive the theoretical key rate.

title: Optimization of Electronic Nose Array Based on Information Theory and Fuzzy Rough Sets
authors: Zhifang Liang, Yilin Chen, Liu Yang, Zili Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: The optimization of sensor array has always been a key issue in the development of electronic nose. Sensor array optimization faces two major challenges: redundant sensors and the erroneous removal of critical sensors. To solve the issue, a novel array optimization of electronic nose is proposed which is based on Multi-Layer selection mechanism using Information Theory and Fuzzy Rough Sets (Ml-ITFRS). The method adopts a multi-layer selection mechanism with layer-specific evaluation criteria - based on granularity, fuzzy correlation, fuzzy redundancy, and internal attribute importance - to facilitate layer-bylayer sensors selection. ML-ITFRS can quantify the redundant information and criticality of sensors, helping to select sensors that play a key role in classification task. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve the highest recognition accuracy with the least number of sensors on two public datasets, which proves a better performance than other advanced methods.

title: Iot Based Crop Protection System with Automatic Roof Cover
authors: B. Anishfathima, Affrin Banu S, Durgeswari A, Fazurunisha M
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This project introduces a smart crop protection and water management. This system designed with IoT to automate and monitor tasks automate water use and monitor environmental data. Powering the system is an ESP32 microcontroller that gathers information from a number of sensors, such as a soil moisture sensor for irrigation control, The water level is detected using ultrasonic sensor, and a DHT11 for temperature monitoring. The device starts the water pump automatically, turns on when the soil moisture level falls below a certain limit and turns it off automatically when the preferred moisture level is attained. A servo motor is also activated by a rain sensor to automatically raise a roof cover to shield crops from rain. All of the parts are controlled by two relays, and real-time sensor values are shown on an LCD display. Users may monitor environmental conditions and system performance through a mobile interface thanks to the system's integration. Using the Blynk app, the system can be monitored and controlled from anywhere. This automated system is perfect for sustainable and effective agricultural practices since it maximizes water use, minimizes manual labor, and improves crop protection.

title: Sensorless Control of Starter and Generator Based on High-Frequency Signal Coupling Injection
authors: Xiaoyu Wang, Yong Li, Chun He, Fangrui Wei
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Addressing the challenge of detecting the back-EMF signal at zero and low speeds in traditional sensorless brushless DC motors, this paper proposes a method based on high-frequency signal coupling injection. An external high-frequency sine wave generator is used to inject signals into the motor windings through a coupling capacitor, extracting rotor position information and commutation signals from the high-frequency line voltage response. The line voltage sampling strategy is optimized to eliminate false triggering of the line voltage comparator. To address the phase lag caused by the filter, the commutation timing is advanced and corrected, optimizing the position detection accuracy under low-speed and high-load conditions.

title: Improved YOLOv11-based Wind Turbine Component Detection Method
