authors: Zhiqiang Guo, Tao You, Pengcheng Li
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: Traditional component detection models suffer from large parameter volume and high computational complexity, resulting in low inference efficiency and difficulty in meeting deployment requirements for drones, inspection robots, and mobile devices. Therefore, this paper proposes a wind turbine component detection method based on improved YOLOv11 (You Only Look Once version 11) — — YOLOv11-SGEM. First, a lightweight network StarNet is introduced to replace the original backbone of the model, utilizing star operations to reduce model complexity and improve detection speed. Second, a lightweight neck network GENeck is constructed by combining GSConv and EfficientViMBlock, further reducing the model's parameter volume and computational complexity. Finally, the detection head is optimized using the inverted bottleneck structure of MBConv to reduce model complexity while improving detection accuracy. Training and validation are performed on a wind turbine component dataset from an oil field in western China. Experimental results show that the model's parameter volume and computational complexity are only 42.6% and 32.9% of the baseline model respectively, the model weight is reduced to 7.9MB, detection speed is improved by 47 frames/s, and mAP50 and mAP50-95 reach 96.9% and 83.7% respectively. This demonstrates that the proposed method achieves high detection accuracy and efficiency, making the model easy to deploy.

title: Low-Carbon and Secure Dispatch Strategy for Power Systems under Carbon Emission Constraints and Demand Response
authors: Guoliang Zhang, Siyu Chen, Dan Fang, Bingjie Wu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This paper proposes a low-carbon and secure dispatch strategy for power systems that integrates carbon emission constraints and demand response. First, a multi-objective optimization model incorporating carbon emission cost is formulated, combining generation economic cost and carbon emission cost into a single objective function. Power balance, unit operational limits, and security constraints are included to achieve low-carbon dispatch. Next, the supportive role of demand-side response (DSR) in low-carbon dispatch is investigated. Mathematical models for interruptible loads and transferable loads are developed, and real-time pricing and incentive mechanisms are introduced to guide users in adjusting electricity consumption. Then, high-accuracy forecasting methods (such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks) are employed to improve the prediction accuracy of renewable outputs (e.g. wind power) and loads. A hybrid intelligent optimization algorithm combining metaheuristic and exact methods is designed to enhance solving efficiency for large-scale dispatch problems. Meanwhile, robust optimization and N-1 contingency checks are applied to ensure the dispatch plan maintains system security. Finally, a case study on the IEEE 39-bus system compares economic costs, emissions, and performance under different dispatch scenarios. The results show that the proposed low-carbon dispatch strategy significantly reduces carbon emissions with only a slight increase in operating cost, achieving a clear trade-off between cost and emissions. The participation of demand response effectively reduces peak loads and fills valleys, improving renewable energy utilization and system flexibility. Incorporating intelligent methods such as reinforcement learning endows the dispatch strategy with self-learning and real-time decision-making capabilities, helping the grid maintain secure and stable operation under high renewable penetration and uncertainty. These findings provide an important reference for developing new low-carbon, secure, and efficient power system dispatch schemes.

title: Optimisation Method for Integrated Energy Stations Considering Supply Radius and Pipeline Network Transportation
authors: Zhaoxi Tian, Qifen Li, Liting Zhang, Fanyue Qian
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: To achieve the dual carbon goals, this paper proposes an optimised planning method for integrated energy stations that considers both supply radius and pipeline network transportation. Initial site selection and capacity determination are performed using k-means clustering. Subsequently, optimisation is conducted with economic efficiency as the primary objective, incorporating supply radius constraints and pipeline transportation costs. Validation using the Lingang area in Shanghai demonstrates that this approach reduces total investment costs by approximately 8.3% compared to traditional site selection methods, thereby effectively enhancing system economic viability.

title: PRvL: Quantifying the Capabilities and Risks of Large Language Models for PII Redaction
authors: Leon Garza, Anantaa Kotal, Aritran Piplai, Lavanya Elluri, Prajit Kumar Das, Aman Chadha
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Redacting Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from unstructured text is critical for ensuring data privacy in regulated domains. While earlier approaches have relied on rulebased systems and domain-specific Named Entity Recognition (NER) models, these methods fail to generalize across formats and contexts. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising alternative, yet the effect of architectural and training choices on redaction performance remains underexplored. LLMs have demonstrated strong performance in tasks that require contextual language understanding, including the redaction of PII in free-form text. Prior work suggests that with appropriate adaptation, LLMs can become effective contextual privacy learners. However, the consequences of architectural and training choices for PII Redaction remain underexplored. In this work, we present a comprehensive analysis of LLMs as privacy-preserving PII Redaction systems. We evaluate a range of Large Language Model (LLM) architectures and training strategies for their effectiveness in PII Redaction. Our analysis measures redaction performance, semantic preservation, and PII leakage, and compares these outcomes against latency and computational cost. The results provide practical guidance for configuring LLM-based redactors that are accurate, efficient, and privacy-aware. To support reproducibility and real-world deployment, we release PRvL, an open-source suite of finetuned models, and evaluation tools for general-purpose PII Redaction. PRvL is built entirely on open-source LLMs and supports multiple inference settings for flexibility and compliance. It is designed to be easily customized for different domains and fully operable within secure, self-managed environments. This enables data owners to perform redactions without relying on third-party services or exposing sensitive content beyond their own infrastructure.

title: AI-Enhanced Security: Mastering Multi-Cloud Posture Management
authors: R. G. Kumar, V. Sambasiva, Karri Harshini Rama Rajya Sai Lakshmi, P. Hariswar Reddy, R Hemanth, Ganga Balaji
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: The rapid adoption of multi-cloud environments has improved scalability and flexibility but introduced significant security complexity. Managing distributed workloads across heterogeneous cloud providers often results in inconsistent policies, hidden vulnerabilities, and delayed incident response. This paper proposes an AI-Enhanced MultiCloud Security Posture Management (MCSPM) framework to address these challenges. The approach integrates continuous risk assessment, machine learning–driven anomaly detection, and zero trust access control to proactively monitor and harden cloud infrastructures. By collecting telemetry from multiple platforms and applying predictive analytics, the system identifies misconfigurations, evaluates threat exposure, and automates remediation with minimal human intervention. A layered architecture combining cloud-level security intelligence, DevSecOps automation, and edge-aware analytics ensures scalability and faster response to dynamic attacks. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved detection accuracy, reduced false positives, and enhanced compliance alignment. This study highlights how AI-driven posture management can significantly strengthen cloud security resilience while supporting agile, large-scale enterprise operations.

title: A Non-Contact RFID Heart Rate Detection Method Based on Multi-Tag Adaptive Fusion
authors: Chao Xu, Wenyan Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Signal Processing, Computer Networks and Communications (SPCNC)
abstract: This paper proposes a non-contact heart rate monitoring system using multi-tag RFID fusion. The innovative twolayer architecture combines refined single-tag processing with intelligent multi-tag fusion. Through signal processing and algorithm integration at the single-tag level, and adaptive decisionmaking at the multi-tag level, the system achieves accurate heartbeat detection. Experimental results demonstrate excellent performance with 4.51 BPM MAE, 5.55 BPM RMSE, and 5.51% MAPE compared to ECG, effectively addressing RFID noise challenges for reliable non-contact health monitoring.

title: Statistical Eye-Diagram Estimation Method for PAM-4 Based High-Speed Channel Considering Crosstalk
authors: Yuchul Jung, Jonghyeon Lee, Jiwoon Moon, Siwook Park, Taei Kim, Youngwoo Kim
publish: 2025 IEEE Electrical Design of Advanced Packaging and Systems (EDAPS)
abstract: In this paper, we first propose a statistical eyediagram estimation method for pulse amplitude modulation4 (PAM-4) based high-speed channel considering crosstalk. To account for statistical crosstalk effects, both the three-level transition crosstalk and the single bit response (SBR) are considered as inputs when estimating each transition level. The statistical eye-diagram is derived through recursive convolution of the probability density functions of the SBR and crosstalk responses, which mutually affect each other. The proposed method provides both an eye-diagram including crosstalk effects and a bit-error rate (BER) analysis. The proposed method is validated by comparing the estimated PAM-4 statistical eye-diagrams with those obtained from a full transient simulation. Despite using orders of magnitude less data than the full transient simulation, the proposed method enables BER analysis at very low BER levels. Comparison of the eye-width and eye-height of the eyediagrams shows a good correlation between the simulated and the proposed method.

title: Computer Vision-Based Real-Time Clothing Size Prediction Using Pose Estimation and Distance Calibration
authors: Ntata Innocent Sethosa, Chunling Du, Pius Adewale Owolawi
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: The increasing demand for accurate clothing size prediction in online fashion retail has driven the development of intelligent virtual try-on systems. This study introduces a real-time, camera-based solution titled Computer Vision-Based Real-Time Clothing Size Prediction Using Pose Estimation and Distance Calibration. The system combines pose estimation, distance calibration, and machine learning to predict T -shirt sizes and visualize virtual garments. Using the DeepFashion2 dataset, geometric features such as shoulder width and garment height were extracted and used to train a Random Forest Classifier, which achieved 100 % accuracy on synthetically labeled test data. These labels were derived from image height due to the absence of standardized size annotations. The system supports gesture-based interaction for switching styles and overlays T-shirt images using alpha blending for realistic visualization. Comparative evaluation with SVM and MLP models confirmed the Random Forest model's superior performance. The system was tested on both iOS and Android devices, demonstrating strong cross-platform functionality. This work offers a scalable and user-friendly approach to reducing return rates and improving customer satisfaction in e-commerce, though further validation with real-world garment sizes is needed.

title: 2.9 A 0.24mJ/Frame Quadratic Interpolation 4DGS Processor with Recursive Computation Reuse and Tree-Based Parallel-Rendering
authors: Xiaolong Yang, Yang Wang, Wende Xu, Yubin Qin, Huanyu Wang, Ruiqi Guo, Zhiheng Yue, Jiangyuan Gu, Shaojun Wei, Yang Hu, Shouyi Yin
abstract: 4D Gaussian Splatting (4DGS) has widespread applications in fields such as VR, AR and industrial simulation. However, 4DGS suffers from significant memory requirements, redundant computations and low PE utilization. This paper introduces adaptive quadratic interpolation, recursive computation reuse and tree-based parallel rendering to tackle these challenges. The processor in the paper achieves a rendering energy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.24 ~\text{mJ} /$</tex> frame and improves energy efficiency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.11 \times$</tex> on the D-Nerf dataset.

title: Research on induction power supply experimental system for superconducting high speed maglev train
authors: Yu Sun, Qi Luo, Hao Jia, Zhen Li, Jie Chen
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Performance verification of auxiliary power supply for superconducting high-speed maglev trains is critical, yet full-scale high-speed testing poses significant challenges and high costs, hindering R&D. Addressing the auxiliary power requirements for electric maglev systems, this paper proposes an equivalent current injection scheme into the suspension coil. This approach eliminates the need for high-speed train movement, enabling precise simulation of power-generating magnetic fields at various speeds while stationary. An experimental induction power supply system has been designed to accommodate multiple operating conditions and deliver stable power output. The system comprises a front-end inverter, coils, and a rear-end rectifier. Based on the induction parameters of the suspension coils at different train speeds, matched currents are injected to reproduce the electromagnetic field, enabling the collector coils to generate equivalent induced voltages. Simulation and hardware-in-the-loop testing conducted for train operating speeds of 300~600 km/h demonstrate that the single-side bogie inductive power supply system stably outputs 600V DC voltage and 7200W active power. The power supply stability and output performance meet requirements, supporting subsequent optimization and real-vehicle application.

title: ICPE 2025 Index
authors: N/A

title: Runtime RTT Variability Predictors for Performance-Aware Scheduling in Edge Computing
authors: Panagiotis Giannakopoulos, Bart Van Knippenberg, Kishor Chandra Joshi, Nicola Calabretta, George Exarchakos
publish: 2025 8th Conference on Cloud and Internet of Things (CIoT)
abstract: Applications deployed in resource clusters often experience performance variability due to resource contention and system heterogeneity. This is particularly problematic for time-sensitive workloads, such as latency-critical IoT applications, which are often deployed in edge environments where co-located services compete for limited computational resources. Existing scheduling policies often overlook runtime performance fluctuations and server heterogeneity, which can result in unpredictable task execution times. To address this, we propose a methodology for predicting both the mean and standard deviation of application round-trip time (RTT) under dynamic co-location scenarios. These lightweight variability predictors are deployed alongside applications and use historical RTT data to model application performance. We evaluate our approach using a real-world electron microscopy workflow with diverse resource requirements. Our predictors achieve prediction accuracy up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4 \%}$</tex> while maintaining a low overhead under dynamic and heterogeneous conditions. Although the developed predictors are not yet integrated into the scheduler, simulation results demonstrate that they enhance application performance and reduce resource waste by enabling more informed allocation decisions.

title: Novel Classes of Dual-Band Filters with all Positively Coupled Resonators
authors: Wael M. Fathelbab
abstract: This work presents novel classes of dual-band filters comprising cascaded network configurations with all positively coupled resonators. The identification of these networks greatly simplifies the realization of the microwave filters and improves their reliability. The EM implementation of an X-Band dual-band waveguide filter is presented displaying close agreement with the circuit simulations.

title: A Digitally Reconfigurable Quasi-Yagi Antenna Enabled by Direct Spatial Antenna Modulation
authors: Shuping Li, Shubin Xie, Minning Zhu, Chung-Tse Michael Wu
abstract: This paper presents a quasi-Yagi antenna direct antenna modulation (DAM) for wideband digitally controlled phase modulation. A compact transistor-based switching network, driven by two DC bias voltages, enables dynamic feed direction control, achieving 180& far-field phase shifts over a wide frequency range. A 1D array of four DAM elements is fabricated and measured, showing strong agreement with simulations and effective beam steering across multiple digital states, within 1-GHz bandwidth covering the Wi-Fi 6E frequency range. The proposed quasi-Yagi DAM features low complexity and wideband digital reconfigurability, offering a promising solution for future 6G wireless communication and Internet-of-Things (IoT) applications.

title: CIoTSC 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)

title: Modeling and Improvement of Heat-Flow Paths Inside 3D-nand First-Level Packages With Internal Metal Structures
authors: Hobeom Han, Jung Su Yoon, Eun Pyo Hong, Suk-Kang Sung, Sang Won Yoon
abstract: Rising interface speeds and compact form factors have pushed <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nand</small> flash solid-state drives (SSDs) toward their thermal limits. This work establishes a package-accurate thermal framework that integrates experiments, finite-element method (FEM) simulations, and a compact network model to guide first-level package (FLP) design. A detailed 3-D model of a commercial <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nand</small> module was constructed and validated against decapsulated package measurements using an optical-fiber thermal sensor and infrared (IR) imaging, reproducing the measured saturation temperature near <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$80~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C. The calibrated model reveals two dominant heat-flow paths—upward through the epoxy molding compound (EMC) and downward through solder joints into the printed-circuit board (PCB)—with the EMC and PCB identified as primary resistance bottlenecks. To address these limits, four embedded-metal designs were proposed. Compared with the ~<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$80~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C baseline, bulk metal, exposed bulk metal, a thermal pad, and a bridged heat spreader reduced hotspot temperature to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$77.0~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$75.4~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$75.7~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$70.0~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, respectively, achieving up to 12.5% improvement. Resistance trends confirm that a thermal pad mainly enhances the topside path, whereas the heat spreader enables balanced upward and downward evacuation with the most uniform heat distribution.

title: Z-Shaped Slot-Excited Hybrid Polarization-Rotated Metasurface Antenna for A Wideband Quad Circularly-Polarized-Mode Radiation Using CMA
authors: Shuai Guo, Dongxu Chen, Wanchen Yang, Kuo-Sheng Chin, Qiubo Ye, Wenquan Che
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this work, a novel hybrid polarization-rotated metasurface (PRMS) structure is firstly proposed and excited by a Z-shaped circularly-polarized (CP) coupling slot to obtain a wideband quad CP-mode radiation. In detail, the hybrid PRMS structure comprising two types of corner-truncated unit cells, as a radiator, is employed to generate three distinct CP modes. Through rigorous optimization, the Z-shaped CP slot simultaneously excites these three CP modes while producing an extra fourth independent CP mode. By using characteristic mode analysis (CMA), these four CP modes can be combined for a wideband broadside radiation. Measured results demonstrate a large impedance bandwidth of 40.3% (6.07∼9.13 GHz) and a 3-dB axial ratio bandwidth of 41.1% (5.81∼8.83 GHz). Additionally, a peak gain of 9.52 dBic and a wide 2-dB gain variation bandwidth of 30.1% (6.21∼8.41 GHz) are achieved.

title: SSS-VIO: Strawberry Squid Asymmetrical Eyes Bio-Inspired Stereo Visual Inertial Odometry
authors: Yebei Wen, Hanyu Zhan, Jizhou Lai, Wei Fang, Shenghong Xie, Cheng Yuan
abstract: Visual-inertial odometry (VIO) serves as a dominant framework for real-time motion state estimation in micro aerial vehicles (MAVs). However, existing VIO techniques remain highly susceptible to high dynamic range (HDR) illumination conditions. Bio-inspired by the lateralized visual system of the Strawberry Squid, we propose a SSS-VIO technique that integrates a functionally asymmetric-eye design and perceptual attention mechanisms to overcome HDR-induced limitations. The proposed method is applicable both to the design of asymmetric stereo platforms and to cost-effective retrofitting of commercial stereo cameras. Experimental evaluations on an OAK-4P-New camera demonstrate a 40% increase in usable dynamic range with a single neutral density filter. Furthermore, SSS-VIO also outperforms multiple state-of-the-art VIO methods in MAV flight tests, reducing Absolute Trajectory Error by at least 46.1% and consistently improving Relative Pose Error across different trajectory segments. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first bio-inspired asymmetric-eye solution for enhancing the VIO dynamic range, which might unlock new potentials for MAV indoor-outdoor localization and applications.

title: Revealing Sleep Dynamics with PCT-CRV: A Novel Approach for Automatic Sleep Staging and Tracking Transitions using PSG Signals
authors: Tehreem Fatima Zaidi, Abhishek Dixit, Deepak Joshi, Shiv Dutt Joshi
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Polysomnography (PSG)-based accurate sleep staging is essential to monitor sleep quality and sleep-related disorders. Despite previous attempts for improving the performance of automatic sleep staging, there are certain limitations: 1) neglecting synchronization patterns in their time-frequency (TF) domain, 2) not utilizing both local and global features within sleep epochs, and 3) neglecting correlation patterns for tracking transitions between sleep stages. To address them, we propose a novel framework based on the polynomial chirplet transform-derived characteristic response vector (PCT-CRV) for the assessment of sleep stages. In this work, we perform the time-domain PCT (TPCT) and frequency-domain PCT (FPCT) to enhance the TF representation of nonstationary PSG signals. From these PCT representations, we construct correlation matrices across their frequency bins within short-time windows to obtain characteristic response vectors (CRVs), which are the sums of eigenvectors, weighted by their corresponding eigenvalues. Subsequently, a comprehensive set of local and global features is derived from PCT-CRVs, which is subjected to various machine learning-based classifiers. Our PCT-CRV excels on three datasets, surpassing existing methods, and outperforming wavelet-based and synchrosqueezed-based CRV methods. Furthermore, to track transitions of sleep stages, we form sub-band PCT-CRVs using eigenvectors with maximum information, depending upon the physics of our problem. We hypothesize that sleep stages are characterized by specific correlation profiles, within different frequency bins. Hence, sub-band PCT-CRVs corresponding to the dominant eigenvectors, would detect transition of sleep stages across all epochs. All these results highlight the efficacy of our method in tracking sleep stage transitions and improving their classification performance.

title: CineSteady: A Real-time Hybrid Video Stabilization and Distortion Correction Method_supp6-3669217.mp4
authors: Heng Sun
abstract: The rapid development of the consumer electronics industry has highlighted the significance of video stabilization technologies for devices like smartphones. Nevertheless, State-of-the-Art (SOTA) solutions face significant challenges in realworld deployment, primarily due to limitations in stability, image distortion control, black border elimination, and real-time processing capabilities. To address these challenges, we propose CineSteady, a novel and comprehensive video stabilization algorithm framework. First, by using gyroscope data and image sequences, CineSteady achieves high-order smoothing of video sequences under complex motion scenarios, delivering a top-tier stabilization experience for users. In addition, we introduce a forward-backward filtering algorithm that rigorously enforces boundary constraints. This approach guarantees black-border-free output within a fixed Field of View (FOV) while maximizing video stability. Moreover, to address practical deployment challenges on mobile devices, we present Optical Lens-based Rolling Shutter Correction (OLRC), a comprehensive camera and distortion correction model. By leveraging physical imaging models and 3D motion estimation, OLRC effectively eliminates the jello effect. Finally, the proposed algorithm demonstrates exceptional computational efficiency, achieving real-time performance on the Xiaomi 15 Ultra powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite platform, supporting 4K resolution at 60 frames per second. Evaluations in diverse real-world scenarios confirm that CineSteady surpasses SOTA video stabilization methods in user experience, demonstrating significant advantages of the proposed solution.

title: A Physically Unclonable Function With Less Than 4.16E–7 BER Featuring Secure In-Cell HCI Burn-In and nMOS-Dominant PUF Data Generation
authors: Kunyang Liu, Zihan Fu, Gen Li, Hongliang Pu, Zhibo Guan, Xingyu Wang, Shufan Xu, Kiichi Niitsu, Hirofumi Shinohara
abstract: Physically unclonable functions (PUFs) are a fundamental component of hardware security, enabling secure and low-cost on-chip secret key generation. However, achieving near-zero bit error rates (BERs) across wide supply voltage and temperature (VT) variations often requires access to secret PUF data during stabilization, which poses security risks. This work presents an eight-transistor (8T) SRAM-based PUF that employs a secure in-cell hot-carrier injection (HCI) burn-in technique to stabilize the PUF without requiring access to PUF data and without incurring redundancy, bitcell loss, or on-chip mask data storage. An nMOS-dominant PUF data generation technique is introduced to enhance HCI effectiveness and suppress long-term aging effects, achieving near-zero BER across VT corners even after long-term operation. Fabricated in a standard 130-nm CMOS process, the proposed design achieves a BER of less than 4.16E–7 across a temperature range from –20 °C to 120 °C and a supply voltage range of 1.17–1.43 V. Long-term reliability is validated through a 320-h accelerated aging test. In addition, the design attains a compact bitcell area of 434 F<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and low energy consumption of 8.52 fJ/bit.

title: CERVIA: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Explainable AI for Automated Cervical Cancer Classification from Pap Smear Images
authors: Nilasree Kannadoss, Kumar Rangasamy
abstract: Cervical cancer is a primary health concern worldwide. The World Health Organization (WHO) estimated that there were almost 350,000 deaths caused by the disease in 2020. Early detection by means of a Pap smear is vital. However, manual examination is prone to inter observer variability and is very time consuming. Deep learning techniques provide a potential solution. Hence, this research presents CERVIA, a hybrid deep learning network combining InceptionResNetV2 and MobileNetV3 architectures by feature fusion strategy to achieve higher discriminative power across NILM, LSIL, and HSIL categories. The problem of unbalanced classes is solved by Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks, so that the dataset becomes balanced for all the classes. Two explainable AI methods Local Interpretable Model agnostic Explanations (LIME) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) are used to give clear interpretability, with SHAP being more closely aligned with the diagnostic features. A clinical report generation component converts the predictions into the real world information. CERVIA has been able to get 97.1% accuracy, 96.7% precision, 97.1% recall, 96.9% F1 score, and 0.99 ROC AUC on the CRIC test set thus, performing far better than the most advanced methods. Independent validation with the Brown Multicellular ThinPrep Database was also able to confirm the resulting high accuracy of 96.8%, hence, generalization is very robust. The amalgamated framework sets up a comprehensive solution that a medical professional can employ in various clinics and hospitals, and other healthcare settings.

title: Robust HFI-Based Sensorless Control of SMPMSMs in the Presence of Large Position Estimation Errors
authors: Akshay Vijayrao Deshmukh, Feyzullah Ertuk, Kudra H. Baruti, Sritam Jena, Emre Efe, Yanming Luo, Bilal Akin
abstract: Due to the inherently low saliency of SMPMSMs, achieving stable high-load operation with HFI-based sensorless control at startup or low speeds is challenging. Moreover, even small cross-saturation inductances can cause significant errors in the estimated rotor angle. This paper proposes a physics-informed strategy that adjusts the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$d$</tex-math></inline-formula>-axis reference current to ensure a minimum saliency while running FOC with the estimated angle, without explicit angle compensation. Positive <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I_{d}$</tex-math></inline-formula> injection intentionally saturates the motor, reducing inductances and creating sufficient saliency for stable HFI operation. The method defines the minimum <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I_{d}$</tex-math></inline-formula> for maximum torque operation, ensures accurate torque tracking across the full torque range, and provides an initial position detection (IPD) scheme that eliminates the need for a polarity check. Resonant controllers are incorporated to mitigate inverter nonlinearities and spatial harmonic effects, reducing speed and angle ripple. Experiments on a 400 W SMPMSM demonstrate reliable low-speed operation, startup under load, smooth transitions to a B-emf observer, and robust performance under varying torque conditions, validating the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed approach, which is also mathematically formulated and rigorously analyzed.

title: Improving Electronic PCB Quality Inspection through Multi-Dimensional Pixel Attention and Small-Object-Aware Learning
authors: Zhe Wang, Shuang Kang
abstract: Ensuring the reliability of electronic products critically depends on accurate and efficient printed circuit board (PCB) defect inspection. However, the detection of small-scale defects remains challenging due to their limited pixel footprint, subtle visual characteristics, and the presence of complex circuit backgrounds. To address these limitations, this paper proposes MLA-YOLO, a small-object-aware PCB defect detection framework enhanced by a Multi-Dimensional Pixel Enhanced (MDPE) attention mechanism and a lightweight dynamic upsampling strategy. The MDPE module integrates pixel-level and channel-level attention to strengthen fine-grained feature representation, while the Litesample upsampler improves spatial detail recovery with minimal computational overhead. In addition, an improved regression loss, ARSIOU, is introduced to enhance localization accuracy for small and irregular defects. Extensive experiments on a high-resolution PCB dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves competitive detection performance and superior results among lightweight detectors, offering higher precision in identifying subtle defect types while maintaining efficient inference. These results highlight the potential of the proposed framework for deployment in practical electronic manufacturing inspection systems.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning based Service Caching and Computation Offloading Algorithm for Multi-UAV-Enabled MEC Systems
authors: Luyinru Yang, Jun Zheng, Yuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper investigates the joint service caching and computation offloading optimization problem in a multi-UAV-enabled mobile edge computing (MEC) system. The problem is formulated as a non-linear integer programming problem with an objective to minimize the average service delay of all computing tasks generated by ground users in the system. To solve the formulated problem, the original problem is first decomposed into two coupled subproblems, i.e., service caching and computation offloading, and a two-layer learning framework is then introduced to solve the subproblems. The learning framework consists of an upper layer and a lower layer, which are used for service caching policy learning and computation offloading policy learning, respectively. Based on the learning framework, a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based joint optimization algorithm, which consists of a lower-layer algorithm and an upper-layer algorithm, is further proposed to perform service caching and computation offloading. The lower-layer algorithm uses a graph neural network (GNN) and an attention mechanism to extract relevance information on the states of ground users in the system and uses a deep Q-network (DQN) to make UAV assignment decisions based on the extracted information. The upper-layer algorithm uses an actor-critic structure to make optimal service caching decisions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can significantly reduce the average service delay of all computing tasks in the system compared with benchmark algorithms.

title: Accepted with Minor Revisions: Value of AI-Assisted Scientific Writing
authors: Sanchaita Hazra, Doeun Lee, Bodhisattwa Prasad Majumder, Sachin Kumar
publish: Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Intelligent User Interfaces
abstract: None

title: Semantic See-through Goggles: Wearing Linguistic Virtual Reality in (Artificial) Intelligence
authors: Goki Muramoto, Yuri Yasui, Hirosuke Asahi, Masaharu Hirose, Masahiko Inami
publish: Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Intelligent User Interfaces
abstract: None

title: EFRC is Adapted to Access-rate Imbalance: A Class of Efficient and Fair Replacement Algorithms for Cache Sharing
authors: Liang Ai, Pengfei Chen, Guangba Yu, Yuhui Deng, Xiao Qin
abstract: None

title: Research on Provincial Multi-time Scale New Energy Storage Planning Considering Power Supply Guarantee and New Energy Consumption
authors: Zhen Pan, Shizhong Zhang, Lijuan Huang, Hui Qin, Yining Miao, Zhen Wu
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: New energy storage is a key technology for the construction of a new power system with an increasing proportion of new energy. The reasonable planning of new energy storage at the provincial level can maximize the diverse roles of new energy storage. This paper proposes a new energy storage planning method for provincial regions. Firstly, a new energy storage scale calculation model considering new energy consumption and power supply security is constructed based on power production simulation, and the reasonable scale of new energy storage is obtained through the fixed-step method. Further, a new energy storage regional layout index system is constructed based on new energy consumption and power supply security, and a regional layout method is proposed to achieve the reasonable layout of new energy storage. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through a case study of a certain province.

title: Research on 3D Modeling and Interactive Visualization of Industrial Pipeline Defects Based on Open3D
authors: Huan Shu, Zeqin Liu, Weixin Gao
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: To address the limitation of 2D charts in visually presenting the spatial distribution of defects during industrial pipeline safety assessment, this paper proposes a weld defect 3D visualization system based on a single X-ray projection. Physically grounded in the Beer-Lambert law, the system first retrieves defect depth by contrasting gray values, then converts the scattered defect point cloud into a smooth triangular mesh via Delaunay-Taubin surface reconstruction, and finally realizes real-time, interactive visualization of the "pipe-weld-defect" ensemble on an ordinary PC with the lightweight Open3D library. The solution offers a lightweight and extensible 3D visualization tool for rapid in-service pipeline safety evaluation.

title: Grid-Computer Symbiosis: Towards the Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Danielle McGuire
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Current cybersecurity models for the electric power grid rely upon certain assumptions about its functionality which have held true for over 50 years of IT/OT convergence. This paper argues that emerging grid technologies (such as automated demand response, advanced metering infrastructure, distributed energy resources, battery energy storage systems, electric vehicle supply equipment, Vehicle2Grid, and cloud-first SCADA) undermine these assumptions and so necessitate a new model for electric power cybersecurity. This model must describe a ‘network composed of heterogeneous subnetworks which is managed via asynchronous control protocols’, a definition which is shown to map well to the Industrial Internet of Things as described by NIST 800-82. Existing recommendations for ICS/OT/SCADA cybersecurity are mapped to this definition. Interdependencies between various technological systems and layers are also explored, underscoring the complexities of the emerging top-level system.

title: Uncertainty Quantification for Deep Learning-Based Medical Imaging Classification Model Evaluation and Individualized Risk Estimation
authors: Jiren Li, Dooman Arefan, Shandong Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have been applied in numerous medical image analysis tasks, yet the lack of reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) limits safe clinical deployment. In high-stakes medical decision making, model overconfidence can lead to severe consequences, underscoring the need for methods that evaluate and interpret model behaviors. In this work, we propose two methods that extend the prediction uncertainty to characterize deep learning model behaviors and assess samplelevel prediction risk (i.e., error rate). Specifically, one method is for model-level evaluation that compares UQ calibration across medical imaging classification models using the Risk-Coverage Curve, and another method is for risk estimation at the sample level through translating raw uncertainty scores into context interpretable prediction error probabilities for individual samples. The two methods can utilize either the softmax probability from a standard convolutional neural network or the variance-based uncertainty outputs from a newly customized heteroscedastic binary cross-entropy (HBCE) model. We performed experiments on a breast cancer imaging dataset for a binary classification model for computer-aided diagnosis. Results show the feasibility and promising effects of our methods in evaluating the overall calibration of UQ and individualized risk. The HBCE-generated uncertainty provides superior efficacy compared to the standard softmax probability in our dataset and classification task. These results highlight the potentials of the proposed methods as a practical tool for clinical utilitysupporting 1) systematic model evaluation for diagnostic model selection and benchmarking under varying clinical settings and 2) individualized uncertainty interpretation to assist model users (e.g., radiologists) for personalized decision making when working with models. This work contributes new methods and tools that can enhance human-AI collaboration for medical imaging tasks.

title: MAVUL: Multi-Agent Vulnerability Detection via Contextual Reasoning and Interactive Refinement
authors: Youpeng Li, Kartik Joshi, Xinda Wang, Eric Wong
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: The widespread adoption of open-source software (OSS) necessitates the mitigation of vulnerability risks. Most vulnerability detection (VD) methods are limited by inadequate contextual understanding, restrictive single-round interactions, and coarse-grained evaluations, resulting in undesired model performance and biased evaluation results. To address these challenges, we propose MAVUL, a novel multi-agent VD system that integrates contextual reasoning and interactive refinement. Specifically, a vulnerability analyst agent is designed to flexibly leverage tool-using capabilities and contextual reasoning to achieve cross-procedural code understanding and effectively mine vulnerability patterns. Through iterative feedback and refined decision-making within cross-role agent interactions, the system achieves reliable reasoning and vulnerability prediction. Furthermore, MAVUL introduces multi-dimensional ground truth information for fine-grained evaluation, thereby enhancing evaluation accuracy and reliability. Extensive experiments conducted on a pairwise vulnerability dataset demonstrate MAVUL's superior performance. Our findings indicate that MAVUL significantly outperforms existing multi-agent systems with over 62% higher pairwise accuracy and single-agent systems with over 600% higher average performance. The system's effectiveness is markedly improved with increased communication rounds between the vulnerability analyst agent and the security architect agent, underscoring the importance of contextual reasoning in tracing vulnerability flows and the crucial feedback role. Additionally, the integrated evaluation agent serves as a critical, unbiased judge, ensuring a more accurate and reliable estimation of the system's real-world applicability by preventing misleading binary comparisons.

title: A 90.7%-Efficiency Hybrid Optogenetic Stimulation System with Sub-Threshold Electrical Stimuli Achieving 70.6% Optical Energy Saving
authors: Joonghoon Kang, Hyeonhee Roh, Kyeongho Eom, Hyun-Su Lee, Hojae Chon, Maesoon Im, Hyung-Min Lee
abstract: This work presents a hybrid optogenetic stimulation system with sub-threshold electrical stimuli that lower the optical threshold and reduce energy consumption. It achieves 90.75% LED driving efficiency through efficient capacitor charge-discharge operation and generates decaying-exponential irradiance to enhance neural responses. Ex vivo experiments in mice validate equivalent neural activation with a 70.6% optical energy saving compared to conventional optogenetics.

title:
A 1.2GHz 12.77GB/s/mm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    3D Two-DRAM-One-Logic Process-Near-Memory Chip for Edge LLM Applications
authors: Yue Cao, Jinghao Jiang, Haijun Jiang, Qian Zhang, Xuanzhi Liu, Jinhui Cheng, Zhongze Han, Xiping Jiang, Fengguo Zuo, Song Wang, Fujun Bai, Yixin Guo, Chunmeng Dou, Jianguo Yang, Hangbing Lv, Qi Liu, Ming Liu
abstract: A high-bandwidth-density <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(12.77 \text{GB} / \mathrm{s} / \text{mm}^{2})$</tex> high-memory-density <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.4 \text{Mb} / \text{mm}^{2})$</tex> low-energy-consumption <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.67 \text{pJ} / \mathrm{b})$</tex> 3D PNM design that operates at 1.2 GHz is presented. The design adopts a two-DRAM-one-logic architecture that enables near-DRAM computing through a high-density 3D integration path, reducing memory-access latency by up to 93% and GEMM execution time by up to 98%, demonstrating strong potential for edge-LLM workloads.

title: Edge AI Integration for Adaptive Control in Embedded Robotics Platforms
authors: Sudharsan Vaidhun Bhaskar, Lakshmi Sree Lakshmanan
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Robot-assisted surgery (RAS) has transformed joint and bone surgery. A major challenge in RAS is the segmentation of images to aid the surgery process, which requires huge computing and real-time analysis. The traditional applications of cloud systems might not be viable when it comes to long operations because of their increased latency and security concerns, as well as the need to have a good internet connection. Edge Artificial Intelligence (Edge AI) is a game-changing concept of robotics. Edge AI is especially suitable in situations when high processing and power consumption cannot be considered a feasible option, and low-latency response time is a must. This paper investigates edge AI image segmentation in RAS in orthopedic surgery. This paper examines how Edge AI enables the reduced reliance on external systems and minimization of the segmentation to the surgical location, and is reasonably accurate in real-time segmentation. This paper will discuss three deep learning (DL) technologies, namely, Transformer, DeepLabVe, and U-Net, that fulfill the edge design based on approaches such as quantization, trimming, and data distillation. Operative images would be best analyzed with a real-time analysis workflow that is founded on edge AI systems, including the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier and the Google Coral TPU. The findings of the study show that real-time predictions with Edge AI segmentation have a latency within 100 milliseconds and are highly accurate. The research issues explored include the generality of the systems used in working with different anatomies of patients, limitations on the hardware used, and legal adherence. Therefore, by enabling the fast, precise, and regional analysis of the image, Edge AI would enhance the quality of RAS in orthopedics. This would enhance patient outcomes and the accuracy of operations.

title: Towards Sustainable Energy: Improved Solar Forecasting with Deep Learning Models
authors: Devikarthika P, S. Dheenathayalan
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Accurate solar power forecasting is crucial for energy management optimization and grid stability. The Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) deep learning models are employed to predict solar power generation using historical data. An 80/20 train-test split is employed to evaluate forecasting performance. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) are employed as key performance metrics for comparison. The LSTM model effectively detects sequential patterns, and the TFT model uses attention mechanisms to enhance interpretability and feature selection. The results demonstrate the predictive ability of the two models, making them valuable contributions to understanding their potential for solar power forecasting and enhancing deep learning techniques in renewable energy.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Transformer-Based and Baseline NER Models for Kenyan Financial Text
authors: Chandaka Giri Babu, Priyanka Soni, Ramesh Chundi, Senthil S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Named Entity Recognition (NER) plays a critical role in extracting relevant information from larger texts. Their role in financial text processing allows information extraction. from customer transactions, banking statements and regulatory communications.The Kenyan financial domain presents unique challenges due to mixed-language usage, informal token patterns and scarcity of annotated corpora. This study conducts a comparative evaluation of baseline and transformer based NER models on a newly prepared Kenyan financial text dataset. The evaluated models include a rule based SpaCy NER baseline, BERT, DistilBERT, FinBERT (domain adapted), AfriBERTa (African language adapted) and XLM RoBERTa (multilingual). Each model was fine tuned on identical training and validation splits and assessed using Precision, Recall, F1 Score and token level Accuracy. Experimental results indicate that SpaCy performs poorly (F1 = 0.2674), indicating limited suitability in this domain. BERT and DistilBERT have a moderate level of performance (F1 = 0.79) while FinBERT and AfriBERTa indicate an enhanced domain adaptation (F1 = 0.82). The highest accuracy is achieved by XLM RoBERTa (F1=0.8480, Accuracy=0.9774) Benefit of multilingual contextual representations for Kenyan English and code-switch text. These findings show that is accomplished by using language model that is multilingual and regionally sensitive is greatly improves financial NER accuracy. The research culminates in recommendations for extending the types of entities.

title: Remote Sensing Image Object Detection Method Based on Weighted-IoU
authors: Zhongyu Li, Xiaoping Jing, Yimin Shen, Rui Sun, Di Zhou, Haifeng Zheng, Ximin Tang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Due to the dense concentration of small targets and complex backgrounds in remote sensing images, the intersectionunion ratio (IoU) between the predicted and ground truth bounding boxes generated by target detection models is low, resulting in significant regression errors and impacting the performance of the target detection model. This paper proposes Weight-IoU based on the Wise-IoU concept and integrates it into the YOLO11 model. Using exponentially complex sample weights to amplify the gradients of low-IoU samples and employing centerdistance normalization to constrain the offsets of the predicted bounding boxes improve the detection model’s performance. Ablation experiments based on VisDrone and comparative experiments with state-of-the-art target detection algorithms effectively demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Furthermore, detection results on remote sensing images of random disasters also illustrate the method’s superior performance.

title: A Hybrid ML–DL Framework for Automated DDoS Detection in IoT Networks Using the CICIoT2023 Dataset
authors: Ishrath Sulthana, Aadhitya S.V, Sandhya Chengaiyan, A.K.Krithika, Ekshita Venkatesh, K.P.Jhayasuriya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Modern IoT devices are expanding digital interconnectivity at a very fast rate and, at the same time, intensifying the risks of possible device malfunctions and DDoS attacks. Classical intrusion detection systems (IDS), dependent on static rules and old datasets, are incapable of keeping up with the changing traffic behavior of modern IoT networks. This paper proposes an innovative Hybrid Machine Learning – Deep Learning (ML-DL) model which autonomously detects IoT anomalies by combining the interpretability of ML algorithms and the representational strength of DL architectures. The proposed framework combines Random Forest (RF) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) models with a two-stage fusion process involving a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) on the CICIoT2023 dataset, collected through traffic network data from 105 IoT devices and 33 categories of diverse attacks. Evaluation of the model included accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-Score and was marked as a through-and-through rigorous experiment. The hybrid model achieved an accuracy of 99.1%, with precision and F1-Score also valued at 0.99, thus outperforming standalone ML and DL models. The hybridization, then, does enhance detection reliability and generalization while being computationally inexpensive for IoT edge deployment. Thus, this work lays down a practically implementable and scalable basis for real-time automation of malfunctioning and intrusion detection in the IoT space.

title: Armature Reaction Compensation for the High Speed High Frequency Electric Drive System Testing
authors: Ronghui Zhou, Bin Lu
abstract: The paper introduces a low cost back to back testing bench setup process for validation of high speed high frequency permanent magnet synchronous machine and drive systems, e.g. with speed above 30000rpm, targeting for electric aviation integrated starter - generator drive system and electric vehicle traction drive system applications. Based on the armature reaction theory and equivalent impedance circuit analysis, the value of the capacitive and resistive loads (connected in the load machine side (generator mode)) is optimized and selected. The capacitive load can compensate the air gap flux weakening effect caused by the inductive characteristics of the load machine, thus enable the drive machine (motor mode) works in full power at high speed working condition. With proposed testing system, a 36600rpm/65kW high speed high frequency electric drive system is validated successfully and achieves reliable operation at full speed full load working conditions. The testing methodology introduced in the paper is straightforward and the setup is with low cost, can be applied to various high speed high frequency electric drive system testing.

title: Detection Performance Based on Levitated Optomechanical E-Field Sensing under High-Intensity Electric Fields
authors: Dun Qian, Yifan Wang, Mingquan Zeng, Wenbo Li, Yuehao Wang, Yiren Sun
abstract: Based on the unique theoretical advantages of levitated optomechanics, including non-contact measurement, intrinsic insulation, and a wide dynamic range, this study provides a promising pathway for developing next-generation strong electric field sensing technology. However, experimental validation of its performance in practical high-field environments had been lacking. Here, we demonstrate for the first time the stable operation of a levitated optomechanical system under strong electric fields and systematically evaluate its low-frequency measurement performance. Experimental results show that the system achieves a conservative signal-to-noise ratio of 20.26 dB at 40 kV/m and maintains excellent linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.99) across a measurement range of up to 160 kV/m under standard atmospheric conditions. The effective measurement range demonstrated in this work represents approximately an order-of-magnitude improvement over previous reports, confirming the simultaneous stability and wide-range detection capability of levitated optomechanics in high-field environments. These findings not only address a critical research gap in strong-field sensing but also establish a solid experimental foundation for translating this technology from laboratory research to practical engineering applications.

title: A Method for Assessing the State of Ejector Pins in Press-Pack IGBT Devices Based on Current Distribution Inversion
authors: Xin Chen, Ran Yao, Hui Li, Peng Yang, Xinrui Chen, Wei Lai
abstract: Press-pack IGBT devices are core components of flexible HVDC transmission equipment. The equilibrium of internal multi-chip parallel stress distribution directly determines the operational reliability of the equipment. However, under high voltage and high current conditions, differences in gate ejector pin damage lead to non-uniform dynamic current distribution in parallel branches, and then inducing local overload and thermal stress concentration, which accelerates device damage and failure. Accurately perceiving the internal current distribution and subunit load equilibrium, and accurately assessing the damage state of the gate ejector pins, are crucial for improving the operational reliability of press-pack IGBT devices. This chapter proposes a method for assessing the state of gate ejector pins in press-pack IGBT devices based on current distribution inversion. Firstly, an electromagnetic coupling simulation model of the press-pack IGBT module is established to analyze the dynamic correlation characteristics between current distribution and spatial magnetic field during the transient switching process. Secondly, the multi-head self-attention mechanism of the Transformer neural network is utilized to deeply mine the spatio-temporal correlation features between the external magnetic field distribution of the device and the internal current state, constructing an end-to-end mapping model from magnetic flux density observation data to chip current distribution. Finally, based on observation points of the external spatial magnetic field of the device, the measurable external magnetic flux density is used as input to the Transformer neural network algorithm to invert and reconstruct the transient current waveform of each sub-unit, quantitatively characterize the dynamic imbalance during the switching process, and evaluate the damage state of the gate ejector pins of each IGBT sub-unit based on current distribution characteristics. Simulation and validation results show that the proposed method can effectively invert the internal current distribution and accurately assess the damage state of the gate ejector pins.

title: Architectural Approaches to Fault-Tolerant Distributed Quantum Computing and Their Entanglement Overheads
authors: Nitish Kumar Chandra, Eneet Kaur, Kaushik P. Seshadreesan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Fault tolerant quantum computation over distributed quantum computing (DQC) platforms requires careful evaluation of resource requirements and noise thresholds. As quantum hardware advances toward modular and networked architectures, various fault tolerant DQC schemes have been proposed, which can be broadly categorized into three architectural types. Type 1 architectures consist of small quantum nodes connected via Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ) states, enabling nonlocal stabilizer measurements. Type 2 architectures distribute a large error correcting code block across multiple modules, with most stabilizer measurements remaining local, except for a small subset at patch boundaries that are performed using nonlocal CNOT gates. Type 3 architectures assign code blocks to distinct modules and can perform fault tolerant operations such as transversal gates, lattice surgery, and teleportation to implement logical operations between code blocks. Using the planar surface code and toric code as representative examples, we analyze how the resource requirements, particularly the number of Bell pairs and the average number of generation attempts, scale with increasing code distance across different architectural designs. This analysis provides valuable insights for identifying architectures well suited to fault tolerant distributed quantum computation under near term hardware and resource constraints.

title: Case Study on Temperature Rise and Ampacity Evaluation of Complex Duck-Buried Cable Groups in Urban Power Grids
authors: Yingying Zhao, Wenrong Si, Chenzhao Fu, Yongchun Liang, Wei Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: With the rapid development of urban power grids, the high-density underground laying of cable groups within ducts has become a common method for power transmission in large cities like Shanghai. The ampacity of existing cables is often conservatively limited, leading to insufficient utilization of assets. Assessing cable ampacity accurately requires precise calculation of temperature rise, which for closely packed cable groups is complex due to mutual heating and electromagnetic coupling effects. Traditional methods based on IEC standards or numerical simulations are either too generalized for specific layouts or too computationally intensive for real-time engineering applications. This paper presents a case study applying a fast calculation method for transient temperature rise in duct-buried cable groups, based on self-heating and mutual-heating thermal circuit models, to an actual cable route in Shanghai. The method enables dynamic ampacity assessment for multi-circuit, complex cable groups, the verification through model calculation results shows that: under transient conditions, specifically with step loads and periodic loads, the errors are found to be 2 K and 3 K, respectively. This performance meets the demands of engineering applications.

title: Non-Contact Diagnosis of Local Electric Field in Distribution Lines Using Optical Sensing
authors: Bingchang Zhuang, Ye Tian, Xinhui Kang, Taiyu Gu
abstract: To meet the technical demands of condition monitoring and fault localization in distribution networks, and address the limitations of existing non-contact fault diagnosis methods, this study proposes a novel non-contact diagnostic technique based on optical sensing to detect local electric fields in distribution lines. The research focuses on developing non-contact electric field sensing methods with high sensitivity and fast response, as well as integrating a complete sensing system. The core objective is to realize accurate measurement of electric field signals and their dynamic variations in distribution lines. The sensitivity of the integrated electro-optic sensor reaches 0.58 mV/(kV/m), and the output signal of the sensor maintains a good linear relationship with the applied external electric field, with a linear fitting goodness (R-square value) of 0.9999. To verify the technical feasibility of the proposed technique, a simulated partial discharge test system for overhead distribution lines was built, and performance evaluation was conducted on the integrated electro-optic crystal sensor. The results show that discharge defects can be effectively identified by measuring the electric field signals of overhead distribution lines. Furthermore, by analyzing the characteristic parameters of electric field waveforms, it is expected that further differentiation of discharge types and estimation of partial discharge intensity can be realized This study provides a new technical approach for the accurate diagnosis and condition assessment of discharge defects in distribution networks.

title: Thermal Stress Numerical Investigation of CSP Steam Generator Based on Multi-physics Coupling
authors: Yaoxin Wang, Lijun Jiao, Xianghui Song, Zhenjun She
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: This study presents a numerical investigation focusing on thermo-mechanical behavior in solar thermal power plant steam generators. A multi-physics coupled model integrating computational fluid dynamics and finite element analysis was developed to analyze stress distribution under various operating conditions. The research examines the effects of inlet molten salt temperature (550-600°C), shell-side molten salt velocity (0.8-2.5 m/s), and inlet water/steam temperature (240-320°C) on equivalent stress distribution along heat exchange tubes. Numerical simulations reveal stress variations of 70 MPa, 15 MPa, and 9 MPa for molten salt temperature, velocity, and water/steam temperature variations, respectively. Sensitivity analysis indicates that molten salt temperature demonstrates dominant influence with a coefficient of 1.52, followed by water/steam temperature (0.39) and flow velocity (0.26). Stress concentration predominantly occurs at the fourth and fifth baffle support locations along the tube length. Optimal operational parameters are identified as 565°C molten salt temperature, 2.2 m/s flow velocity, and 320°C water/steam temperature. The findings provide valuable references for design optimization of steam generators in solar power systems.

title: Risk Analysis and Mitigation Strategies for High-Frequency Oscillations in Multi-Infeed HVDC Receiving-End Systems
authors: Xiao Zhou, Zhenyu Du, Yuexi Yang, Yanying Sun, Xue Jiang, Hongbo Hao
abstract: The dominant cause of high-frequency oscillations in multi-infeed HVDC receiving-end systems is control loop delay, while the impact of parameter mismatches among converter stations is less studied. To address this, an impedance model of the MMC and its control circuit, explicitly considering interaction delays, is developed using the multi-harmonic linearization method. Oscillations are then analyzed under varying power outputs and control delays. To mitigate them, a passive damping strategy based on a double-tuned filter is proposed, which enhances positive damping in the high-frequency range and is broadly applicable to diverse interaction scenarios in multi-infeed HVDC systems.

title: PQC-LEO: An Evaluation Framework for Post-Quantum Cryptographic Algorithms
authors: Callum Turino, William J. Buchanan, Owen Lo, Christoph Thümmler
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems, and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: Advances in quantum computing threaten digital communication security by undermining the foundations of current public-key cryptography through Shor's quantum algorithm. This has driven the development of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), a new set of algorithms resistant to quantum attacks. While NIST has standardised several PQC schemes, challenges remain in their adoption. This paper introduces the PQC-LEO framework, a benchmarking suite designed to automate the evaluation of PQC computational and networking performance across x86 and ARM architectures. A proof-of-concept evaluation was conducted to demonstrate the framework's capabilities and highlight its application in supporting ongoing research on the adoption of PQC algorithms. The results show that there is a greater performance reduction in implementing PQC methods with higher security on ARM architectures than on the x86 architecture.

title: Asymmetric IR Tracking with Cross-Scale Attention Interaction
authors: Zheng Yuan, Jun Ma, Yueteng Feng
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Intelligent Control, Measurement and Signal Processing (ICMSP)
abstract: The near infrared (NIR) target is significant in the recognition of UAV, whereas it is difficult to track small infrared targets under complex background. Due to the weak thermal feature, small size and noise like similar background, it is difficult to estimate state reliably. To this end, we proposed a novel lightweight dual-stream network for infrared target tracking. After the decoupling and feature matching, the proposed method can effectively represent and accurately localize small targets. A feature enrichment and multi-scale fusion mechanism is also proposed to enhance robustness, whereas structural optimization and parameter reduction guarantee a lightweight model. The experimental results on several infrared datasets show that our method has high tracking accuracy and real-time performance, which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Microwave Methods Applied to Acoustic Time Domain Reflectometry
authors: Charles M. Jackson
abstract: There is interest in developing acoustic S-parameter test sets based on microwave approaches. Common microwave transmission line filter designs can be applied to design filters, and these circuits provide known standards that can be used to evaluate the accuracy of a S-parameter test set. Additive manufacturing enables low cost fabrication of the filters. This paper will present a description of an acoustic time domain reflectometer and use acoustic filters to evaluate the performance. A new topology for a quarter wave stub bandpass filter, that can be implemented for acoustic transmission lines, but would be complicated for the microwave case, will be presented. The new acoustic TDR test uses signal averaging, automation, multiple pulse widths, and apodization to improve the results over an earlier test set. Measurements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert S_{21}\vert$</tex> based on a method demonstrated for a microwave equipment will be presented.

title: Coordinated Source–Grid–Load–Storage Optimization for Photovoltaic Power Accommodation in New-Type Power Systems
authors: Ning Wang, Xue Wang, Wei Li
publish: 2025 6th International Symposium on New Energy and Electrical Technology (ISNEET)
abstract: Under the dual-carbon targets and the rapid construction of new-type power systems, large-scale photovoltaic (PV) integration has led to pronounced output volatility and severe curtailment in low-load periods, which significantly restricts further improvement of renewable accommodation capability. Focusing on PV accommodation in new-type power systems, this paper develops a coordinated source–grid–load–storage optimization framework that explicitly models PV output uncertainty, flexible demand response, and energy storage regulation. First, mathematical models are established for PV output and net load to capture the impact of high PV penetration on conventional generation scheduling. Then, an integrated model is constructed for energy storage, demand response, and system power balance, and curtailment and PV utilization indicators are defined to quantify accommodation performance. System flexibility margins and PV hosting capacity indices are further introduced to evaluate the ability of the grid to withstand short-term fluctuations. Finally, a multi-objective optimization model is formulated that jointly minimizes operating cost, increases PV utilization, and maintains sufficient flexibility margins, thereby deriving coordinated scheduling strategies for conventional units, energy storage, and responsive loads. The proposed framework provides a systematic modeling and decision-making approach for enhancing PV accommodation in new-type power systems.

title: GD-MVSNet: Multi-View Stereo with Global Grouped Coordinate Attention and Dual Feature Fusion
authors: Duokui Wu, Bin Zheng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
abstract: Learning-based multi-view stereo (MVS) methods have significantly advanced 3D reconstruction. However, existing approaches still offer considerable potential for enhancing reconstruction quality, particularly in texture-deficient regions. To address these challenges, we propose a novel MVS framework, termed GD-MVSNet. Specifically, we design a Global Grouped Coordinate Attention (GGCA) module, integrating it into multistage feature extractors. GGCA is proposed to capture long-range dependencies and fine-grained local features across different spatial scales by employing channel grouping and dual-dimension global pooling, thereby improving the model’s understanding of complex scenes. Additionally, we design a Dual Feature Fusion Module (DFFM) that adaptively guides semantic information from coarse stages into finer stages, achieving an organic fusion of cross-scale features. Extensive experiments on the DTU dataset demonstrate that our method achieves excellent performance relative to state-of-the-art MVS approaches.

title: A 16-Channel High-Rate Readout ASIC for CdZnTe Detector and SPECT with Preventing Signal Accumulation Technique
authors: U-Fat Chio, Jiulin Jiang, Dingdong Zhang, Miao Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: A 16-channel high-rate front-end readout application specific integrated circuit (ASIC) has been designed for CdZnTe (CZT) detectors and single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) with $0.35 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ CMOS technology. Each channel includes a charge-sensitive amplifier (CSA), a leakage current compensation (LCC) circuit, a shaper circuit, a baseline holding circuit (BLH), a peak detection holding (PDH) circuit and a time discriminator (DIS). At high event rates, a compact Preventing Signal Accumulation (PSA) logic is proposed to solve the signal accumulation at the output when the interval between events is very short. In addition, detection accuracy is affected by charge sensitive amplifier (CSA) saturation, shaper baseline shift. A novel leakage current compensation circuit and a shaper with baseline holding circuit are proposed, and thus, at an event rate of 500 kHz, the CSA does not saturate and the baseline shift of shaper less than 1 mV. Postsimulation results show that the PSA logic can effectively avoid signal accumulation. The input energy range is $29 \sim 430 \mathrm{keV} (1 \sim 15 \mathrm{fC})$, the peak time is 150 ns, the equivalent noise charge (ENC) is $87.6 \mathrm{e}^{-}+4.5 \mathrm{e}^{-} / \mathrm{pF}$, the power consumption of each channel is less than 1.8 mW, and the nonlinearity is better than 1%.

title: Enhanced Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm Using Adaptive Fitness Function and Penalty
authors: Shanmugaraj D, S. Lakshmi Priya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Multi-objective optimization (MOO) plays a critical role in real-world decision-making systems where multiple conflicting objectives such as maximizing classification accuracy, minimizing validation loss, and preserving model generalization must be optimized simultaneously. Traditional MOO techniques, including NSGA-III, rely on static fitness functions and fixed penalties, which limit adaptability and often result in premature convergence and reduced solution diversity. This paper proposes an enhanced NSGA-III framework that integrates dynamic fitness evaluation and adaptive penalty weighting guided by population evolution and dataset sensitivity. The proposed method is evaluated on 11 real-world medical datasets, demonstrating improved convergence, diversity, and robustness compared to conventional NSGA-III and baseline models.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Segmentation of Fetal Heart Standard Views for Chd Screening Backbone Ablation, Clinical Validation and Robustness Analyses
authors: Jaishree S. R, Raunesh Kumar C, Sharmila D, Madhumithaa S. R
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Congenital heart defects (CHDs) are among the most common congenital malformations and remain a leading cause of neonatal mortality. Early prenatal identification via fetal echocardiography improves outcomes, yet manual interpretation of standard views is operator-dependent and vulnerable to variability. This work presents a deep learning-based computeraided diagnosis (CAD) framework using a Mask R-CNN instancesegmentation network to delineate cardiac structures in the four key fetal echocardiographic planes: four-chamber (4CH), left ventricular outflow tract (LVOT), right ventricular outflow tract (RVOT), and three-vessel trachea (3VT). A clinically verified dataset of 1,149 images acquired at 18-24 weeks' gestation was annotated by fetal-medicine specialists. We conduct backbone ablations (ResNet50, ResNet101, MobileNetV1) and compare against U-Net, DeepLabV3+, Swin Transformer, and ConvNeXt under uniform protocols. With ResNet50, the framework achieves mean Intersection-over-Union (IoU) 79.97%, Dice 89.70%, and mean average precision (mAP) 96.59%. Receiver-operating characteristic (ROC) analysis, bootstrap significance testing, and blinded clinical review confirm robustness and diagnostic reliability. The proposed approach offers a reproducible, clinically aligned benchmark to support CHD screening in prenatal ultrasound.

title: Active-RC Integrators with Negative-R Assistant Circuitry for Continuous-Time Delta-Sigma ADCs
authors: Meiqin Xiong, Han Shi, U-Fat Chio, Ting Liu, Yuxin Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: Current loss is an inevitable phenomenon in the active-RC integrator of continuous-time delta-sigma analog to digital converters (ADCs). The introduction of negative-R can provide current compensation for the integrator and reduce its noise. Therefore, an analysis was conducted on the negative-R characteristics of the traditional cross-coupled pair (XCP) and the impedance characteristics of the traditional diode-loaded pair, and specific improvement methods were proposed for the design and implementation of negative-R circuits. A small-signal negative- R is formed by paralleling the cross-coupled positive feedback of metal-oxide-semiconductor (MOS) transistors with diode-connected MOS transistors.
