By setting the width-to-length ratio of MOS transistors, the transconductance of the diodes is adjusted, which further modifies the resistance value of the negative-R. If the transconductance is too small, leading to excessively high impedance, the resistance value will be affected by the channel-length modulation effect of MOS transistors, and the output impedance of the entire structure will be the small-signal resistance of the MOS transistors. This structure has a wide output impedance range, making it suitable for constructing large-value negative-R. It can be applied to the integrator of continuous-time delta-sigma ADCs to achieve noise reduction. Simulation results show that this negative-R structure can reduce noise in active resistor-capacitor (RC) integrators.

title: Intent-to-AVM: MCP-Orchestrated AVM Terraform Stacks for Regulated Azure Environments
authors: Sudhakavya Bodapati Venkata
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Data Science (ICCIDS)
abstract: Azure Verified Modules (AVM) and Terraform are widely used to improve structure and repeatability in regulated Azure environments. In practice, however, teams still rely on specialists to translate informal requests, such as multi-tier environments with private endpoints, centralized Key Vault, and diagnostics, into concrete AVM-based Terraform stacks and Terraform Cloud workspace layouts. This manual translation does not scale and often introduces subtle inconsistencies in networking, observability, and key management, areas where regulators expect uniform behavior and traceable change history. This paper presents Intent-to-AVM, a framework that uses a model context protocol (MCP) orchestrator to map structured environment intent to curated AVM modules and Terraform stacks. The framework defines an intent model capturing tiers, connectivity, key management, and diagnostic routing, and coordinates plan-only runs in Terraform Cloud while keeping apply explicitly human-gated. The approach reduces repetitive engineering effort and improves configuration consistency for private-endpoint-centric workloads.

title: Multi-Horizon Demand Forecasting Using Hierarchical Attention Mechanism with Learned Temporal Embeddings
authors: Lei Lei, Cai Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: Accurate demand forecasting is critical for supply chain management and enterprise competitiveness, yet traditional methods struggle with complex temporal patterns, promotional effects, and demand volatility. This paper proposes a Transformer-based intelligent supply chain management system for multi-horizon demand prediction and inventory optimization. The architecture employs learned positional embeddings capturing domain-specific periodicities, hierarchical prediction heads for different forecasting time scales, and quantile regression for probabilistic forecasting with uncertainty quantification. Comprehensive experiments on the M5 Forecasting benchmark dataset demonstrate superior performance with mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.24 and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 8.2%, representing 22.5% and 24.1% improvements over vanilla Transformer baselines, and 54 % improvement over traditional ARIMA methods. Comparative analysis against six baseline methods including Random Forest, LSTM, GRU, and TCN validates the effectiveness of attention mechanisms for capturing long-range dependencies in retail demand data. Business impact assessment confirms 15-25% inventory reduction while maintaining 95%+ service levels, directly enhancing enterprise competitiveness through improved cash flow and customer satisfaction. The system enables risk-aware decision-making through prediction intervals, supporting robust inventory optimization under demand uncertainty.

title: Smart Attendance Tracking System with Cloud Integration
authors: Naveena N, Dharshini K, Bharathi M, Dharshana V, Shamuga Priya M, Rajalakshmi K
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: This project proposes a secure, cloud-based smart attendance system that integrates multiple layers of verification[15] to ensure accuracy, authenticity, and tamper-proof logging. The system begins with a secure web or mobile portal for user login, protecting against unauthorized access. To verify physical presence, real-time GPS location is captured and matched with predefined classroom or block coordinates. Further validation is achieved by checking the Wi-Fi SSID and frequency band to ensure users are connected only to authorized networks, preventing misuse of mobile hotspots. Device authenticity is enforced through MAC address filtering, allowing only registered devices to participate in attendance marking. To enhance reliability, the system incorporates real-time selfie verification for stronger identity validation. All data are stored securely on cloud platforms such as Firebase or AWS, enabling real-time synchronization and seamless access for administrators. This multi-factor verification framework minimizes fraud, strengthens accountability, and ensures transparent attendance management. By combining locationbased services, device-level authentication, and cloud integration, the system provides a robust, scalable, and efficient alternative to traditional attendance methods. It is designed to improve security, reduce manual errors, and support modern institutional needs for reliable attendance tracking. [1]–[6]

title: Brain Lesion Detection using a Robust Variational Autoencoder and Transfer Learning
authors: V. Karthik, R Aswitha, G Harinisree, K Harshitha
publish: 2025 IEEE First International Conference on Innovations in Engineering and Next-Generation Technologies for Sustainability (ICINVENTS)
abstract: Precision of brain lesions on MRI scans is important in the process of determining and treating neurological disarranges. Traditional manual translation procedures are often moderate, subjective, and prone to errors, and the need to have reliable mechanized arrangements shows. This ponder suggests a new deep learning framework that entails aligning a Robust Variational Autoencoder (RVAE) and Exchange Learning in order to initiate steps toward the injury location precision and generalization. The RVAE element gathers small, noise-resistant forms of MRI images by reproducing solid constructions of the brain and pinpointing anomalies as possible damages. Its probabilistic encoding enables the operation of compelling taking care of of poor quality and artifact-contaminated filters whilst maintaining sound flexibility across multiple datasets. In order to motivate upgrade execution, the fine-tuning of pre-trained convolutional neural systems which include ResNet and VGG is performed through exchange learning where skillful high light drawing is possible even when limited clarified data is accessible. Exploratory tests depict common accuracy, influenceability, and particularity in contrast to habitual CNN strategies. In addition to this, the system provides interpretability with recreation blunder maps and certainty scores, which ensures clinical unwavering quality. The proposed framework is versatile and meets the requirements of real-time transmission, providing an energetic and versatile framework in mechanized brain injury localization in an environment of transposed therapeutic imaging scenarios.

title: Accelerating the Snooping & Snooping Response Process of Cache-Coherent Network-on-Chip with Multicast Adaptive Routing
authors: Dongwei Hu, Linan Wang, Xiaobo Jiang, Gengting Liu, Xiaohui Ba
publish: 2025 International Conference on Frontiers Technology in Circuits and Systems (FTCS)
abstract: In Cache-Coherent Network-on-Chip (NoC) of Many-core CPU, Snooping and snooping response Process (SNP Process) incurs long latency. To address this, two techniques: Multicast Routing and Adaptive Routing, are proposed in this paper. According to the requirements of these two techniques, the NoC packet formats for Snooping Request Channel (SNP REQ Ch) and Snooping Response Channel (SNP RESP Ch) are proposed, and furthermore, the NoC Routers of SNP REQ Ch and SNP RESP Ch are VLSI implemented. The implementation results show that the Routers for both SNP REQ Ch and SNP RESP Ch are of $85940.3 \mu m^{2}$ or $103518.5 \mu m^{2}$, while an 8 by 8 network occupies $5.57 m \mathrm{~m}^{2}$, which is feasible for large-scale chips. Simulations are employed to compare the latencies of 4 configurations: unicast determined routing, unicast adaptive routing, multicast determined routing, and multicast adaptive routing. Simulation results show that the latency of SNP Process with multicast adaptive routing could be cut by 45% for a single snooping request comparing to that with unicast determined routing, resulting in a much shorter latency than DDR/HBM access, and by 73% for 32 consecutive snooping requests with outstanding technique employed at the Point of Coherency (PoC), which validate the effectiveness of the techniques we proposed.

title: 20.10 A 214-to-242GHz Miniaturized Co-Packaged PA-Antenna Array with 29dBm Lens-Less Eirp in a 0.13μm SiGe Process
authors: Qianqi Meng, Zhihua Wang, Peigen Zhou, Dawei Tang, Siyuan Tang, Junyue Xiao, Rui Zhou, Jinben Li, Zhe Chen, Jixin Chen, Hao Gao, Wei Hong
abstract: This work presents a miniaturized, high-power, and wideband THz PA-antenna array, realizing 29 dBm lens-less peak EIRP from 214 to 242 GHz. To improve the BW and P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sat</inf> of the PA, a folded 10th-order power-splitting/combining and staggered-matching method is proposed, achieving 17.4 dB peak gain and 16.5 dBm peak P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sat</inf> from 160 to 248 GHz. To address the efficiency and BW limitation of the AoC, a co-packaged ULP slot-patch antenna is integrated, extending the radiation efficiency to 66.7 % and BW to 36 GHz.

title: A 144mW 161Mpixels/s Tensor Display Processor for 3D Virtual Reality
authors: Ching-Yen Lee, Yueh-Feng Tsai, Ying-Sheng Lin, Chia-Hsiang Yang
abstract: This work presents a 3D display processor that converts the focal stack to tensor display. Algorithm-architecture co-optimization is applied to reduce the computational complexity and hardware complexity. It achieves the maximum throughput of 161 Mpixels/s (equivalent to 87 fps at HD resolution) at a clock frequency of 200 MHz, with normalized energy consumption of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9 \text{nJ} /$</tex> pixel, and a PSNR of 23.7 to 24.8 dB,

title: 1.2 Quantum Computing – Toward Large-Scale Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing
authors: Heike Riel
abstract: Quantum computing is advancing rapidly, offering the potential to transform computational paradigms and tackle problems that are intractable for classical systems. Realizing practical quantum systems demands the development of an entirely new computing stack—from qubits and quantum processors to components, wiring and control electronics, transpilers, error handling, software, algorithms, and the compute architecture to integrate quantum and classical resources at scale. This plenary will provide an overview of the current state of quantum computing and outline the milestones on the path toward building a large-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer by 2029. The significant improvements in hardware, software, and system integration, pushing the performance of quantum computing to reach quantum utility and advance toward quantum advantage, will be presented. Breakthroughs in error correction and a modular approach indicated in the IBM Quantum Roadmap outline a clear path to quantum systems using 200 logical qubits and 100 million quantum operations by 2029 and 2,000 logical qubits a few years later. Furthermore, advances in quantum algorithms, combined with the integration of quantum systems and high-performance computing (HPC), will unlock powerful synergies—accelerating the timeline for applications previously considered to be far in the future.

title: 15.9 A 48Gb/s 24Gb GDDR7 DRAM for Mid-Range Inference AI with Symmetric 2CH-Mode Operation, Clock-Path Optimization, and RAS Features
authors: Hyunsu Park, Sungkwon Lee, Kyunghoon Kim, Jinyoup Cha, Hyeongjun Ko, Jaehyeok Yang, Seongjin Kim, Youngtaek Kim, Minsoo Park, Gangsik Lee, Keonho Lee, Sanghoon Lee, Jiseok Lee, Gyunam Jeon, Sera Jeong, Yongsuk Joo, Jaehoon Cha, Seonwoo Hwang, Seulgi Kim, Eunji Song, Junghwan Ji, Byungjun Kang, Boram Kim, Sangyeon Byeon, Hankyu Chi, Hyungsoo Kim, Jonghwan Kim
abstract: This paper introduces a GDDR7 DRAM for mid-range inference AI. A center GIO architecture is used to remove the speed penalty of the 2CH mode, which doubles memory density and reduces the clock path. This work supports ECC, CRC, and DPP to meet the RAS features required in server systems. Additionally, energy efficiency is enhanced for on-device AI applications through a low- V_DDQ mode. For nominal conditions, with a 1.2 V supply voltage, a 48 Gb/s data transmission speed is achieved.

title: Spatially-Aware Gaussian Mixture Models for Structured Segmentation in Multispectral Satellite Imagery
authors: Nhat-Trinh Le, Nhat Tan Thai, Cao Vu Bui
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) are widely used in unsupervised learning for their ability to model complex, multimodal data distributions. However, conventional GMMs ignore spatial dependencies, making them poorly suited for satellite image segmentation where spatial continuity and contextual coherence are essential. In this paper, we propose a novel Spatially-Aware Gaussian Mixture Model (SA-GMM) that integrates spatial regularization directly into the Expectation-Maximization (EM) framework. By encouraging neighboring pixels to share similar cluster assignments through edge-aware spatial priors, the proposed method achieves smoother and more accurate segmentations while preserving important land cover boundaries.We evaluate SA-GMM on multispectral satellite imagery from Sentinel-2 (EuroSAT) and Landsat 8, comparing it against standard GMM and K-Means baselines. Experimental results show that SA-GMM improves Calinski-Harabasz score by 2.5× (794.4 vs. 316.0) over classical GMM and increases edge preservation accuracy by 18.3%, confirming the model’s effectiveness in capturing spatial structure. These findings highlight the benefit of incorporating spatial context into probabilistic clustering and establish SA-GMM as a robust tool for unsupervised land cover mapping in remote sensing.

title: Study on Resilience Assessment of Urban Rail Transit Networks for Operational Safety
authors: Shuangen Gong, Ge Li, Hongbing Xiao, Yu Wang, Peiyuan Guo, Man Bao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: With the accelerating pace of urbanization, urban rail transit systems are playing an increasingly vital role for alleviating traffic congestion, and their operational reliability and safety have been paid significant attention. This study addresses the risk of cascading failures faced by urban rail transit (URT) networks under natural disasters and unexpected events. A resilience assessment model— URT-CML—is proposed, based on the Coupled Map Lattice (CML) theory. This model innovatively integrates station heterogeneity, dynamic load redistribution mechanisms, and multi-modal redundant connectivity to construct a dynamic propagation model of cascading failures in URT networks. Monte Carlo simulations are employed to model failure pathways under various attack strategies, and system resilience is evaluated from the perspectives of robustness and service coverage. Using the Beijing metro network as a case study, results demonstrate that the URT-CML model improves the prediction accuracy of failure propagation speed by 22.7% compared to conventional models, offering new theoretical foundations and decision-making support for resilience-oriented design and emergency management in urban rail transit systems.

title: How do Top Managers in Small-and-Medium-Sized Enterprises Form Opportunity Beliefs for Digital Transformation Strategy? An Attention-based View_supp1-3669411.docx
authors: Chenguang Hu
abstract: The engineering management (EM) literature has widely accepted that digital technologies provide new opportunities for small-and-medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)' growth. Identifying and acting upon opportunities require top managers (TMs) to allocate and sustain their attention toward monitoring and interpreting signals from the external environment. However, present studies largely view TMs as invisible or perfectly rational, thus lacking sufficient investigation of their attention-based practices in SMEs' digital transformation (DT). Drawing on the attention-based view (ABV), we examine how TMs in SMEs utilize attention engagement efforts to form opportunity beliefs for DT strategies. Specifically, we adopt a case study within a typical SME in China, which has transformed from a traditional manufacturer to a digital platform enterprise over the period of 2009–2025. The analyses are based on the fundamental logic of “noticed environmental changes―attention engagement efforts―opportunity belief” in ABV. With rigorous case study tactics for validity, reliability, and generalizability, we develop a framework with different aggregates of TMs' attention engagement efforts in forming four opportunity beliefs successively for DT strategies (i.e., market development, market penetration, product development, and platform development). Overall, by investigating the role of TMs' attention engagement efforts in DT, we contribute to the micro foundation of DT within the EM literature in the context of SMEs.

title: A High-Fill-Factor Piezoelectric MEMS Mirror Based on Bow-Shaped Unimorph AlN Actuators
authors: Hao Chen, Xinyu Ding, Wenlong Jiao, Qiangqiang Liu, Ke Cao, Xiaodan Mao, Senlin Jiang, Yue Tang, Tao Wu, Xiaoyi Wang, Huikai Xie
abstract: Piezoelectric MEMS micromirrors for light modulation and optical beam steering have been drawing extensive attention recently, but their applications are still limited due to low fill factor and low resonant frequency, especially when the well-established piezoelectric material–AlN is employed. This study proposes a bow-shaped unimorph piezoelectric actuator that can generate multidegree-of-freedom motion with high fill factor. Particularly, a tip-tilt-piston (TTP) piezoelectric micromirror made of such actuators is designed, fabricated, and characterized. The fill factor is as large as 73% with a mirror plate size of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.0\times 1.0$</tex-math> </inline-formula> mm. The measured resonant frequencies for the piston, tip, and tilt modes are 0.890, 1.747, and 1.781 kHz, respectively. The measured maximum piston stroke and tip or tilt angle reach <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.3~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m and 14.4 mrad with high linearity under 24 V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">pp</sub>. This bow-shaped actuator design provides a new direction for enhancing piezoelectric actuation efficiency and capability.

title: Ultra-Wideband MUTC Photodiode Source for Waveguide-Based THz Device Testing
authors: Shuya Iwamatsu, Himanshu Gohil, Marcel Grzeslo, Hui Wang, Nick Brewster, Mark Merritt, José Luis Fernandez-Estevez, Jessica Strah, Tom Neerfeld, Jonas Tebart, Sumer Makhlouf, Daniel Headland, Muhsin Ali, Guillermo Carpintero, Peter G. Huggard, Andreas Stöhr
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: The development of terahertz (THz) technology exploiting untapped bandwidth is essential for sensing and communications in 6G and beyond. Instrumentation systems for evaluating the performances of THz devices are of increasing interest. Currently, THz device testing with electronics-based vector network analyzers (VNAs) requires a series of frequency extender pairs to cover all individual bands in the THz range. This increases system complexity and cost. Here, we report on an ultra-wideband (UWB) photonic THz source, which will enable simpler and cost-effective THz device testing. The source employs a modified uni-traveling-carrier photodiode (MUTC-PD) operating from 100 GHz to 600 GHz. The InP based MUTC-PD is hybrid-integrated with a UWB high resistivity Si waveguide interface to efficiently couple the optically generated THz waves to the device under test. By utilizing a UWB THz power detector, a waveguide-based scalar transmission measurement system is demonstrated, a crucial step towards a future waveguide-based photonic VNA.

title: RT-SAM: Visual-Prompt Fusion and Uncertainty Enhancement for Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma Radiotherapy Target Delineation
authors: Hee Guan Khor, Xin Yang, Yihua Sun, Sijuan Huang, Yingni Wang, Jie Wang, Shaobin Wang, Lu Bai, Longfei Ma, Hongen Liao
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Precise delineation of the clinical target volume (CTV) and nodal CTV (CTV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{{\mathit{nd}}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) is crucial for effective radiotherapy planning in nasopharyngeal carcinoma (NPC). Manual contouring is labor-intensive and subject to substantial inter-observer variability, particularly in regions with complex anatomy and indistinct boundaries. This study presents RT-SAM, a novel framework that adapts the Medical Segment Anything Model 2 (MedSAM-2) for automated CTV (i.e., primary CTV and CTV<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{nd}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) contouring in NPC computed tomography (CT) images. The framework synergistically integrates a generalist foundation model (MedSAM-2) with a domain-specific specialist network (2D U-Net) through three principal contributions: (1) automated generation of multi-modal prompts—comprising mask, bounding box, and point representations—derived from specialist network predictions to guide the generalist model; (2) a Visual-Prompt Fusion Attention (ViPFA) mechanism that optimizes feature-prompt interactions through bidirectional cross-modal attention; and (3) an Uncertainty-Enhanced Prediction Adjustment (UEPA) mechanism that enhances model robustness via confidence-based refinement and selective domain adaptation. Comprehensive evaluation on a multi-center cohort of 256 clinical NPC cases from Sun Yat-sen University Cancer Center and 212 public NPC cases from the SegRap2025 lymph node CTV dataset using 5- fold cross-validation demonstrates that RT-SAM achieves a mean DICE coefficient of 0.796 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.033 (mean <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> standard deviation), significantly outperforming current state-of-the-art methods. Clinical validation by eight radiation oncologists demonstrates that RT-SAM contours are clinically indistinguishable from expert delineations in blinded Turing assessments, achieve superior quality ratings in 75% of comparisons with mean scores of 2.73 for RT-SAM versus 2.66 for manual expert contours, and attain clinically acceptable ratings in over 97% of cases. These results demonstrate that RT-SAM is a clinically feasible solution for automated CTV contouring, with strong potential to standardize treatment planning and mitigate inter-observer variability in NPC radiotherapy.

title: Graph-Embedded Deep Generative Clustering for Single-Cell Multi-Omics Data Integration_supp1-3667525.pdf
authors: Cheng Liang
abstract: The advancement of sequencing technologies has generated an unprecedented volume of single-cell multi-omics data, providing new opportunities for biological discovery and medical research. However, due to the high heterogeneity across different omics types, effective integration of single-cell multi-omics data remains a critical challenge. Existing methods generally ignore the graph structure information among cells or resort to additional knowledge to construct the cell graphs, leading to suboptimal performance and potentially limited practical utility. In this study, we propose a novel Graph-embedded Deep Generative Clustering model (GeDGC) for single-cell multi-omics data integration. Specifically, GeDGC simultaneously learns the shared latent representations and cluster factors across multiple omics by leveraging Gaussian mixture models. Moreover, we impose the graph embedding constraint on both the latent representations and the cluster assignments to ensure the preservation of intrinsic local data structure among cells. As a result, our model captures complex correlations across omics and obtains informative shared latent embeddings for downstream tasks. Extensive experimental results with seventeen competing methods on ten datasets confirm the superiority of GeDGC in single-cell multi-omics data integration.

title: Context2Context: A Zero-Shot SAR Image Speckle Filter
authors: Lin-Yu Dai, Si-Wei Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Advanced speckle filters can well preserve textural features and fine details in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images, facilitating subsequent interpretations and applications. In recent years, a multitude of deep learning-based despeckling methods have been developed. However, most of them belong to supervised learning fashion trained on simulated datasets which may affected by the domain gap between simulated and real measured SAR data. Meanwhile, two hinds of self-supervised learning methods have been reported. The first hind mainly requires multiple SAR images of the same region for training, while these images are costly or not always available. The second hind mainly constructs lower resolution image pairs for training with sublook decomposition, which may degrade final speckle reduction performance. In order to fulfill aforementioned gaps laying in self-supervised learning methods, a zero-shot SAR image speckle filter is proposed. Its innovation contributions are three-fold. Firstly, the theoretical principle that model training with speckle-speckle image pairs is equivalent to speckle-clean image pairs is further manifested. Secondly, a similar pixel bank scheme is established which can collect sufficient similar pixels and construct plenty of independent training samples based on contextual information without necessity of multiple observation images and image resolution degradation. Finally, a zero-shot speckle filter framework is developed and well trained. Extensive experiments are performed using various SAR datasets in terms of diverse frequency bands and spatial resolutions from Radarsat-2, MiniSAR, and ALOS-2. Comparisons with state-of-the-art methods demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method. Experimental results show that the proposed Context2Context speckle filter can effectively smooth homogeneous regions while preserving target characteristics from both visual quantitative examinations effects.

title: A UAV-Aided Digital Twin Framework for IoT Networks with High Accuracy and Synchronization
authors: Ghofran Khalaf, May Itani, Sanaa Sharafeddine
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Digital Twin (DT) technology has emerged as a promising link between the physical and virtual worlds, enabling simulation, prediction, and real-time performance optimization in different domains. In this work we develop a high-fidelity digital twin framework, focusing on synchronization and accuracy between physical and digital systems to enhance data-driven decision making. To achieve this, we deploy several stationary UAVs in optimized locations to collect data from IoT devices, which were used to monitor multiple physical entities and perform computations to evaluate their status. We formulate a mixed-integer non-convex program to maximize the total amount of data collected from all IoT devices while ensuring a constrained age of digital twin threshold and solve it using successive convex approximation (SCA). To cope with realistic scenarios involving unpredictable environments and large network sizes, we model our problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), and propose a deep reinforcement learning-based approach using a Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) to optimize the unmanned aerial vehicle positions and the sum rate. Finally, we present different simulation results of the SCA and TD3 based solutions together with two baseline approaches and evaluated the sum rate in terms of IoT device count, AoDT threshold, task arrival rate and UAVs’ computational capacity. In all simulation results, the proposed TD3-based approach consistently proved to be superior as compared to the baseline solutions.

title: Highly Stable Microwave Transmission Over Optical Fiber With Accurately Characterized Absolute Delay
authors: Jinping Xiao, Wei Wei, Jinchuan Yao, Weilin Xie, Yi Dong
abstract: In distributed interferometric antenna arrays, accurate measurement and long-term stabilization of transmission delay are essential for coherent combining and accurate target localization. This paper presents a fiber-based delay measurement and stabilization system. The employed dual-tone probe enables accurate measurement of delay variations and the absolute delay simultaneously, while a phase-locked loop provides long-term delay stabilization. Experimental results demonstrate a delay measurement accuracy better than 20 fs and a stabilized delay fluctuation of 15.7 fs (standard deviation) over a 45 km fiber link for 3600 s. The proposed system realizes highly stable microwave transmission with accurately measured absolute delay, making it well-suited for coherent combining and localization in distributed antenna arrays.

title: Designing AI Systems with Value Alignment Mechanisms
authors: Abhignan Srivatsava Sribhashyam, Santosh Gore, Ashish Sharma, Babasaheb Jadhav, Suriya M, Gotte Ranjith Kumar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Network Systems (CINS)
abstract: The Rapid Development. In AI, there has been an increasing need for AI systems to be closer to human values and ethical concerns. Conventional models mostly prioritize performance over ethical issues that can cause potential misalignments. This paper overviews AI system design using inductive value alignment based on the comparison of five frontline methods: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), Iterative Human Feedback Refinement, Hybrid Symbolic Reasoning and Learning, Value Uncertainty Modeling, and Ethical Constraint Enforcement. Each approach was evaluated in terms of its impact on alignment accuracy, ethical adherence, interpretability, and robustness. The results verify that Iterative Human Feedback Refinement was best in alignment accuracy at 85.7%, Ethical Constraint Enforcement performed best on ethical compliance at 91.4%, Hybrid Symbolic Reasoning performed best in interpretability, and Value Uncertainty Modeling had best robustness with 85.3%. Such results highlight the fact that it is not feasible to employ a single technique alone, but rather a combination of multiple techniques produces AI systems that are more safely aligned, morally sound, and appropriate for safe deployment in the real world.

title: Is It Really You? Exploring Biometric Verification Scenarios in Photorealistic Talking-Head Avatar Videos
authors: Laura Pedrouzo-Rodriguez, Pedro Delgado-DeRobles, Luis F. Gomez, Ruben Tolosana, Ruben Vera-Rodriguez, Aythami Morales, Julian Fierrez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Photorealistic talking-head avatars are becoming increasingly common in virtual meetings, gaming, and social platforms. These avatars allow for more immersive communication, but they also introduce serious security risks. One emerging threat is impersonation: an attacker can steal a user’s avatar, preserving his appearance and voice,making it nearly impossible to detect its fraudulent usage by sight or sound alone. In this paper, we explore the challenge of biometric verification in such avatar-mediated scenarios. Our main question is whether an individual’s facial motion patterns can serve as reliable behavioral biometrics to verify their identity when the avatar’s visual appearance is a facsimile of its owner. To answer this question, we introduce a new dataset of realistic avatar videos created using a stateof-the-art one-shot avatar generation model, GAGAvatar, with genuine and impostor avatar videos. We also propose a lightweight, explainable spatio-temporal Graph Convolutional Network architecture with temporal attention pooling, that uses only facial landmarks to model dynamic facial gestures. Experimental results demonstrate that facial motion cues enable meaningful identity verification with AUC values approaching 80%. The proposed benchmark and biometric system are available<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> for the research community in order to bring attention to the urgent need for more advanced behavioral biometric defenses in avatar-based communication systems.

title: 14.2 An $8\lambda\times 38\text{Gb}/\mathrm{s}/\lambda$ 106fJ/b Optical WDM Transmitter in 45nm CMOS SOI
authors: Amirreza Shoobi, Kaisarbek Omirzakhov, Zhehao Yu, Ali Pirmoradi, Firooz Aflatouni
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-energy-efficient MRM based 8-channel optical WDM NRZ transmitter, employing a scalable driver topology combined with monolithic electronicphotonic integration, which minimizes packaging parasitics and enables a compact design within a footprint of 0.018 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> per-channel. The chip incorporates an autonomous concurrent wavelength-locking mechanism with near-zero static power consumption, achieving an aggregate data rate of 304 Gb/s at 106 fJ/b energy efficiency.

title: FakeIDet: Exploring Patches for Privacy-Preserving Fake ID Detection
authors: Javier Muñoz-Haro, Ruben Tolosana, Ruben Vera-Rodriguez, Aythami Morales, Julian Fierrez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Verifying the authenticity of identity documents (IDs) has become a critical challenge for real-life applications such as digital banking, crypto-exchanges, renting, etc. This study focuses on the topic of fake ID detection, covering several limitations in the field. In particular, there are no publicly available data from real IDs for proper research in this area, and most published studies rely on proprietary internal databases that are not available for privacy reasons. In order to advance this critical challenge of real data scarcity that makes it so difficult to advance the technology of machine learning-based fake ID detection, we introduce a new patch-based methodology that trades off privacy and performance, and propose a novel patch-wise approach for privacy-aware fake ID detection: FakeIDet. In our experiments, we explore: i) two levels of anonymization for an ID (i.e., fully- and pseudo-anonymized), and ii) different patch size configurations, varying the amount of sensitive data visible in the patch image. State-of-the-art methods, such as vision transformers and foundation models, are considered as backbones. Our results show that, on an unseen database (DLC-2021), our proposal for fake ID detection achieves 13.91% and 0% EERs at the patch and the whole ID level, showing a good generalization to other databases. In addition to the path-based methodology introduced and the new FakeIDet method based on it, another key contribution of our article is the release of the first publicly available database that contains 48,400 patches from real and fake IDs, called FakeIDet-db, together with the experimental framework<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: Performance Limits of Monofacial Current-Matched Tandem Solar Cell Modules
authors: Anupam Yedida, Revathy Padmanabhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Emerging Electronics (ICEE)
abstract: Current-matched (CM) tandems are widely used, owing to their relative ease of fabrication. Although experimentally realized, an analytical framework based on the principle of detailed balance for N-layer CM tandems with area-decoupled subcells has not been reported. In this work, we present an analytical framework to determine the performance limits of N-layer CM tandems with and without area-decoupled subcells, incorporating radiative coupling between layers. The framework provides expressions for the optimal number of subcells in each layer and evaluates efficiency limits based on the principle of detailed balance. In addition, we investigated the impact of variations in bandgaps, which is strongly influenced by material and processing variations, on efficiency. This work provides design guidelines for the optimal number of subcells in CM tandems and can be extended to voltage-matched and bifacial tandems.

title: CHAMP: A Configurable, Hot-Swappable Edge Architecture for Adaptive Biometric Tasks
authors: Joel Brogan, Matthew Yohe, David Cornett
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: What if you could piece together your own custom biometrics and AI analysis system, a bit like LEGO™ blocks? We aim to bring that technology to field operators in the field who require flexible, high-performance edge AI system that can be adapted on a moment’s notice. This paper introduces CHAMP (Configurable Hot-swappable Architecture for Machine Perception), a modular edge computing platform that allows operators to dynamically swap in specialized AI "capability cartridges" for tasks like face recognition, object tracking, and document analysis. CHAMP leverages low-power FPGA-based accelerators on a high-throughput bus, orchestrated by a custom operating system (VDiSK) to enable plug-and-play AI pipelines and cryptographically secured biometric datasets. In this paper we describe the CHAMP design, including its modular scaling with multiple accelerators and the VDiSK operating system for runtime reconfiguration, along with its cryptographic capabilities to keep data stored on modules safe and private. Experiments demonstrate near-linear throughput scaling from 1 to 5 neural compute accelerators, highlighting both the performance gains and saturation limits of the USB3-based bus. Finally, we discuss applications of CHAMP in field biometrics, surveillance, and disaster response, and outline future improvements in bus protocols, cartridge capabilities, and system software.

title: Privacy-enhancing Sclera Segmentation Benchmarking Competition: SSBC 2025
authors: M. Vitek, D. Tomašević, A. Das, S. Nathan, G. Özbulak, G. A. T. Özbulak, J. P. Calbimonte, A. Anjos, H. H. Bhatt, D. D. Premani, J. Chaudhari, C. Wang, J. Jiang, C. Zhang, Q. Zhang, I. I. Ganapathi, S. S. Ali, D. Velayudan, M. Assefa, N. Werghi, Z. A. Daniels, L. John, R. Vyas, J. N. Khiarak, T. A. Saeed, M. Nasehi, A. Kianfar, M. P. Panahi, G. Sharma, P. R. Panth, R. Ramachandra, A. Nigam, U. Pal, P. Peer, V. Štruc
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: This paper presents a summary of the 2025 Sclera Segmentation Benchmarking Competition (SSBC), which focused on the development of privacy-preserving sclera-segmentation models trained using synthetically generated ocular images. The goal of the competition was to evaluate how well models trained on synthetic data perform in comparison to those trained on real-world datasets. The competition featured two tracks: (i) one relying solely on synthetic data for model development, and (ii) one combining/mixing synthetic with (a limited amount of) real-world data. A total of nine research groups submitted diverse segmentation models, employing a variety of architectural designs, including transformer-based solutions, lightweight models, and segmentation networks guided by generative frameworks. Experiments were conducted across three evaluation datasets containing both synthetic and real-world images, collected under diverse conditions. Results show that models trained entirely on synthetic data can achieve competitive performance, particularly when dedicated training strategies are employed, as evidenced by the top performing models that achieved F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> scores of over 0.8 in the synthetic data track. Moreover, performance gains in the mixed track were often driven more by methodological choices rather than by the inclusion of real data, highlighting the promise of synthetic data for privacy-aware biometric development. The code and data for the competition is available at: https://github.com/dariant/SSBC_2025.

title: Secure Cloud Migration of SAP Workloads with Cybersecurity-Centric Architectures for Enterprise Continuity
authors: Ram Reddy Jonnalagadda, Tarun Pashya, Ramesh Julakanti, Sumanth Bontha, Kiran Kumar Reddy, Tejas Vepala
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Cloud migration is a relevant process that entails the movement of programs, services, and data in the physical servers to the open and secure or hybrid clouds. Moving SAP workloads to the cloud includes challenges such as data confidentiality, safety, system interruptions, and operational administration. Overseeing the migration process reduces the amount of disruption caused to operations and ensures the most benefits of cloud innovations are leveraged, including greater flexibility, real-time analysis, and cost control. The traditional migration procedures are tedious to prepare and make decisions guided by rules, which are tedious, prone to errors, and not efficient. Machine learning (ML) has transformed the cloud migration process through the ability to predict analytics, intelligent categorization of workloads, automated routing of resources, resource cost reduction, and detection of anomalies. This research will attempt to explore ML algorithms in secure cloud migration of SAP workloads at firms. The data used in the study is unprocessed, which is preprocessed and then normalized. The classification system was also tested on a different database to predict system reliability after it was trained through a range of ML techniques. The classification system that made use of ML algorithms correctly forecasted the reliability of the system with a TPR of greater than 96. In a number of cases, ML-based SAP workload cloud migration led to a cost reduction of 20-30 percent on cloud costs and offered enterprises significant economic benefits. The proposed framework is a form of an Enhanced Smart Management framework, and it will contribute to the Cyber-Physical Frameworks of secure cloud migration of SAP workloads in companies.

title: Enhancing Privacy in Face Recognition With Dual-Path Feature Compression and Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Wencheng Yang, Song Wang, Di Wu, Zhaohui Tang, Xu Yang, Hui Cui, Michael N. Johnstone, Yan Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Face recognition offers seamless human-machine interaction and efficiency. However, its widespread adoption has heightened security and privacy concerns due to the risks associated with compromised biometric data, such as spoofing and unauthorized tracking. To mitigate these concerns, this paper introduces a novel privacy-preserving face recognition framework that integrates an enhanced dual-path feature compression approach with homomorphic encryption (HE) for secure and efficient authentication. We leverage the robust deep neural network model FaceNet to extract discriminative 512-dimensional feature vectors and propose two significantly improved complementary feature compression methods tailored specifically for encrypted biometric systems: (1) Partitioned Principal Component Analysis (P-PCA), which employs a novel segment-wise PCA transformation, preserving localized discriminative information and supporting revocable biometric templates; and (2) Segment-wise Locality-Sensitive Hashing (S-LSH), introducing segment-specific hashing optimized for efficient binary representation and privacy-preserving encrypted-domain computations. Both compressed real-valued and binary features are securely encrypted using HE, enabling direct encrypted-domain similarity computations without exposing sensitive biometric data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves competitive authentication performance while maintaining computational efficiency and practical feasibility.

title: Development of Compensation Based 4:2 Compressor-Based Ultra-Low Power Approximate Multipliers
authors: Sakthimohan M, Hansa Hansa
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: For applications including image processing, digital signal processing, neural networks, as well as IoT devices that are both energy-constrained and error-tolerant, the UltraLow Power Approximate Multiplier (ULPAM) presents a novel architecture for multiplication operations. Although traditional precise multipliers are accurate, they are not well-suited for battery-powered as well as edge computing contexts because of the large amounts of power and space used by their reduction trees, which are caused by intricate carry propagation. Random or approximation computation methods, on the other hand, may reduce power consumption, but they often sacrifice precision or experience higher delay as a result. Presented here is a Verilog implementation of a hybrid ULPAM architecture with two versions: one baseline (approximate_multiplier.v) that uses compensation-based 4:2 compressors using error compensation components (ECM) to strike a balance between accuracy and efficiency, and the other enhanced (hybrid_approx_mul.v) that incorporates a carry-lookahead adder (CLA) to boost speed while preserving ultra-low power characteristics. To achieve substantial reductions in switching activity and dynamic power, the design makes use of Unbiased Compressor Approximate Circuits (UCAC) variants for aggressive approximation in low-erbit rows, with ECM compensating to feed corrective carries to exact 4:2 compressors in higher bits. Validated on common FPGA platforms, the ULPAM is able to decrease the powerdelay product (PDP) through up to 40% and the area-delay product (ADP) by 25% when compared to traditional binary multipliers. This is achieved through optimising the partial product reduction tree using reordered inputs with OR-based error compensation. The functionality is confirmed across many test cases, such as 0×0, 1×1, 3×7, and 90×13, by means of comprehensive test benches (tb_multiplier.v as well as tb_hybrid_mul.v), which achieve mean error distances around 5% for the majority of inputs. The ULPAM is an adaptable framework for ultra-low power approximate computing, thanks to its modular architecture that allows for scalability to larger bit-widths and easy integration through AI accelerators or embedded systems. It also has the potential to be extended to fully stochastic bitstreams or fault-tolerant designs, which would be useful for critical uses in automotive or biomedical fields.

title: LivDet2025: Toward Robust and Generalizable Fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection
authors: Giulia Orrù, Marco Micheletto, Roberto Casula, Simone Zedda, Daniele Fenu, Lambert Igene, Jannis Priesnitz, Christoph Busch, Christian Rathgeb, Stephanie C. Schuckers, Gian Luca Marcialis
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: The Fingerprint Liveness Detection Competition (LivDet) is a recurring benchmark series that evaluates the effectiveness of software-based Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) algorithms in fingerprint recognition. LivDet2025 presents three challenges: (1) "Liveness Detection in Action", requiring the integration of PAD with user-specific recognition; (2) "Fingerprint Representation", evaluating the compactness and discriminability of feature vectors; and (3) "Adversarial Robustness", assessing the resilience of PADs to adversarially-crafted presentation attack instruments. This edition marks a significant milestone with the inclusion of contactless fingerprint data, promoting interoperability and robustness across acquisition technologies. Furthermore, no training data was provided; participants must select and declare external datasets for model development. The competition was open to academic and industrial research groups, with all submitted algorithms evaluated on common datasets and under standardized protocols. LivDet2025 aims to provide a comprehensive assessment of PAD performance under realistic, multi-sensor, and multi-attack scenarios. Results reveal important trade-offs between PAD accuracy, usability, and computational efficiency. For instance, some systems achieved high presentation attack rejection at the cost of extremely high false rejection rates, while others optimised speed and generalizability but exhibited limited attack resilience.

title: 3.1 A Multimodal Biosensing System-on-Chip with Integrated Wireless Transceiver and Power Management for Stress Monitoring
authors: Min-Hua Chang, Lin Chou, Yen-Jen Liu, Chen-Hsing Hsu, Ying-Yu Yang, Bo-Cheng Juan, Guan-Yu Lin, Yi-Jie Lin, Yao-Wei Huang, Nitish Kumar, Shu-Ping Lin, Yu-Te Liao
abstract: A highly integrated SoC incorporates ECG, PPG, GSR, and ECH biosignal acquisition interface circuits with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$920 \text{MHz}, 2 ~\text{kb} / \mathrm{s}, 1.1 \mu ~\mathrm{W} \text{WuRx}$</tex>, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4 \text{GHz}, 1 \text{Mb} / \mathrm{s} \text{Tx}$</tex>, and a PMU with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<5 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> recovery time under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$600 \mu ~\mathrm{A}$</tex>-to-50 mA load steps in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> CMOS. A shared ECG/ECH AFE reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.32 \mu ~\mathrm{V}_{\text {rms }}$</tex> IRN and 130dB CMRR. The PPG circuits attain a 113.9dB DR using a precharge scheme, while the GSR AFE achieves a 99dB DR with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1 \text{pA}_{\text{rms}}$</tex> IRN. The biosensing interface consumes only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$125.8 \mu ~\mathrm{W}$</tex>.

title: A 40.5-to-58.5GHz 36%-Fractional-Chirp-Bandwidth 18GHz-Absolute-Chirp-Bandwidth 2.2GHz/μs-Chirp-Rate 0.02%-Chirp-Error Post-Mixing Bandwidth-Extending Sawtooth-FMCW Frequency Synthesizer Employing a Chirp-Tracking ILFT and a Fractional-Bandwidth Doubler
authors: Yi Liu, Zixi Jing, Wen Yang, Hanlin Yang, Bodong Zhang, Howard Cam Luong
abstract: An 18GHz-bandwidth (BW) sawtooth-FMCW synthesizer is presented. First, a chirp-tracking ILFT with feed-forward and frequency-tracking loop ensures robust and continuous tracking of fast chirps. Second, a fractional-BW (FBW) doubler enables sawtooth generation and extends both the BW and FBW while halving the chirp error. The synthesizer measures a 36% FBW sawtooth FMCW chirp from 40.5 to 58.5 GHz with a 0.02% chirp error at a chirp rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.2 \text{GHz} / \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex> without DCO-nonlinearity digital pre-distortion.

title: Distributed Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Algorithm Based on Multi-Head Attention Transformer Models
authors: YiWen Huo, JiaQing Ma, Feng Yang, Fuhong Min, Fei Xie, Jiaming Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: With the rapid development of renewable energy, distributed photovoltaic (PV) systems have been widely applied worldwide. In particular, in China, the growth of installed distributed PV capacity has been driven by favorable policies and technological advancements. However, the power output of distributed PV systems is highly volatile and significantly influenced by weather conditions, especially under non-clear-sky conditions, where forecasting accuracy faces significant challenges. To improve the accuracy of day-ahead power forecasting for distributed PV at the substation level, this paper proposes a DA-Transformer model that integrates a dual-attention mechanism. The model effectively learns long-term temporal dependencies of PV sequences using multi-head attention and introduces channel convolutional attention to weight meteorological features, modeling complex correlations between multiple features. Using two typical PV scenarios as datasets, the input consists of NWP features for the forecast day and the power data from the previous day, while the output is the target day’s PV power. Experimental results show that, under a unified dimension (32) and evaluation with five error metrics (E1– E5), the DA-Transformer model outperforms the baseline CNN model under four weather conditions (clear, cloudy, overcast, and rainy), with forecast errors reduced by 20.08%, 33.00%, 27.10%, and 27.57%, respectively. The results demonstrate that the dual-attention mechanism, combining temporal dependencies and multi-feature weighting, significantly improves the accuracy of PV power forecasting, particularly under complex weather conditions, making it crucial for the effective utilization of distributed PV and the secure operation of the power grid.

title: A Miniaturized Flexible Fractal Rectenna for Wireless Communication and Power Transfer
authors: Zhongyi Wang, Jingze Zong, Qiang Hua, Shuangyao Huang, Qin Qian, Tingting Du, Wenzhang Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer, Internet of Things and Smart City (CIoTSC)
abstract: This paper proposes a miniaturized fractal rectenna to address the demands of integration and small size in wireless communication and wireless power transfer (WPT) system. The miniaturized fractal antenna adopts a three-layer symmetric stacked structure, all fabricated by felt material that provide mechanical stability and electromagnetic isolation. The radiation patch layer uses a square patch with slots etched at the centers of its four sides to optimize miniaturization and radiation performance; the transmission line layer employs two vertically placed parallel lines to enhance electromagnetic coupling attenuation and reduce port crosstalk; the ground layer uses a PEC plate matching the substrate size to ensure good grounding and electromagnetic shielding. For the WPT system, a rectifier is integrated to convert the AC power received by the antenna into usable DC power. In terms of performance, the antenna with two ports both resonates at 2.45 GHz, with a high-gain of 3.8 dBi and 3.8 dBi; the integrated WPT system effectively achieves power reception and conversion via the rectenna, enabling 65% conversion efficiency. This miniaturized fractal rectenna can potentially provide structural design references and performance data support in wireless communication fields (e.g., small-scale IoT devices, portable communication terminals) and low-power SWIPT scenarios (e.g., wirelessly powered sensors, wearable electronic devices).

title: 24.6 A Touch-Embedded OLED Display-Driver IC with Display Noise Referencing and Display Coupling Noise Prediction Based on Dedicated Neural Networks for Mobile Applications with CoE Display
authors: Yun-Rae Jo, Sewhan Na, Sanho Byun, Junchul Park, Gyeongmin Ha, Jiheon Ok, Tae-Gyun Song, Woo-Nyoung Lee, Jihyun Lee, Hyun-Wook Lim, Siwoo Kim, Jae-Youl Lee
abstract: This paper proposes a TDDI, integrating display driver and touch controller to overcome the interference issue between touch and display electrodes. In addition, an analog front-end (AFE) circuit is introduced to suppress coupling noise from display, and a dedicated neural network engine is used to improve interference based on displayed image and display driving condition. The TDDI can achieve SNRs of 43.4 and 41.8 dB with white and 1 H zebra images, respectively.

title: Mapping POPIA Research in South Africa: A Bibliometric Analysis of Data Protection Scholarship
authors: Phumezo Ntlatywa, Darelle Van Greunen
publish: 2025 5th International Multidisciplinary Information Technology and Engineering Conference (IMITEC)
abstract: The Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA), enacted in 2013 and fully enforced from 2021, represents South Africa's most comprehensive data protection legislation, aligning the country with global standards of information privacy and security. Over the past decade, POPIA has generated growing academic interest, yet the scope, focus, and trajectory of this research have remained underexplored. This paper presents the first bibliometric analysis of published scholarship on POPIA in South Africa between 2013 and 2025, based on over 1,200 records retrieved from Scopus, Web of Science, IEEE Xplore, and Sabinet. Using bibliometric tools, the study maps publication outputs, collaboration networks, and thematic clusters. The analysis shows a steady rise in publications from 2013, with a marked surge after the Act's full enforcement in 2021. Research has been dominated by legal and technical perspectives; privacy, data protection, security, and compliance frameworks while social, ethical, and organizational dimensions remain less developed. Keywords such as POPIA, privacy, data protection, and personal information have grown in prominence over time, reflecting a shift from legislative interpretation toward applied studies on compliance and ethical handling of data. However, notable gaps persist: limited attention to community-based organizations, civil society, and small enterprises; limited research outside major urban centers; and minimal focus on vulnerable groups or citizen awareness programs. By charting the growth, focus, and gaps of POPIA-related scholarship, this study contributes a comprehensive evidence base to guide future research and inform policymakers, practitioners, and academics seeking to foster inclusive and effective data protection practices in South Africa.

title: AECTSD 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)

title: 20.4 An Ultra-Compact, Inherently Resilient FR3 Source-Follower Power Amplifier with 4:1 VSWR Resilience and RIMD Immunity for Large-Scale SATCOM and 6G Phased Arrays
authors: Mohamed Eleraky, Jianping Zeng, Hua Wang
abstract: This paper presents a source-follower power amplifier (PA) designed to combat high VSWR and reverse intermodulation distortion (RIMD) in phased arrays. Its inherently low output impedance enables conjugate matching and robust resilience to load variations, mitigating degradation from antenna mismatches up to 4:1 VSWR. The design maintains strong largesignal performance without compromise, proving its suitability for compact, calibration-free integration in large-scale SATCOM and 6G systems.

title: A Study on the Ethics of using Artificial Intelligence in Mental Health Treatment and its Legality
authors: Petikam Sailaja, Hakalyah Palaparthy, B Rajeswari, Peketi Kasi Visweswara Sita Rama Rao, D. Prema, Ajay Sundar. M
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: The development of artificial intelligence (AI) has transformed mental health care through offering new and feasible solutions to old assumptions. The moral concerns related to the use of AI in the mental health, however, could not be overlooked. A thorough grasp of how AI can be used throughout the patient journey is essential to advancing AI technology in the realm of mental health and overcoming its present restrictions. To reduce it to three columns, or one dataset, five Facebook datasets were gathered from Kaggle. The preprocessing procedure enhances the dataset's quality by using user tweets. Four datasets about depression were taken from the Kaggle website. After the preprocessing is finished, we will refine four pre-trained BERT models using the Hugging Face package. We will be able to create a predictive model for identifying depression with this method. The effectiveness of our refined BERT models for depression identification was assessed using a number of metrics. Our healthcare system could be greatly enhanced by AI, but we can only realise this potential if we begin addressing the moral and legal issues that currently confront us.

title: An Empirical Study on Transfer Learning Models for Precision Leaf Disease Prediction
authors: Sarojini Balakrishnan
publish: 2025 First International Conference of Advances in Engineering and Computing Technologies for Sustainable Development (AECTSD)
abstract: Agriculture is vital in ensuring global food security and economic stability. Farmers face significant challenges in detecting diseases that affect plant leaves. Early detection of the disease is crucial, as undiagnosed diseases can significantly reduce crop yield. Manual detection of leaf diseases in large-scale agricultural operations is time-consuming and prone to error. This research leverages pre-trained models for leaf disease detection, thereby addressing challenges related to the limited availability of annotated datasets and the varying leaf conditions in agricultural settings. The pre-trained models ResNet, Mobilenet, and Googlenet are used to predict plant leaf disease detection. The models are retrained and fine-tuned on the target dataset, the new plant leaf disease dataset. The classification metrics - accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score are used to evaluate each model. The results demonstrate that ResNet-101 outperforms both GoogleNet and MobileNet across all evaluation metrics, offering a promising solution for the efficient, automated identification of plant leaf diseases.

title: A Fully Integrated Piezoelectric Energy-Harvesting Interface with Single-Stage Bias-Flip and MPPT Achieving $\mathbf{5. 6 3} \boldsymbol{\times}$ Maximum Output Power Improving Rate
authors: Xiaonan Wu, Jinyi Yuan, Minghao Shang, Baochuang Wang, Sijun Du, Lin Cheng
abstract: This paper presents a fully integrated PEH interface that enhances output power while minimizing on-chip capacitor area. The proposed design employs a SC structure that reuses both the piezoelectric capacitor and the rectifier capacitor as flying capacitors. Meanwhile, on-chip capacitors serve as DC capacitors, thereby eliminating parasitic-capacitance losses. As a result, the interface achieves a maximum output power improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.63 \times$</tex> and a normalized on-chip capacitor area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.09 \text{mm}^{2} / \text{nF}$</tex>.

title: Spatial Continuity-Aware OCT Fingerprint Reconstruction Using Iterative Feature Enhancement
authors: Yilong Zhang, Xuanbing Chen, Shengming Zhu, Haohao Sun, Haixia Wang, Jian Liu, Yuanjie Dang, Ronghua Liang, Peng Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: Optical coherence tomography (OCT) is a non-invasive imaging technique capable of acquiring depth information up to 1-3mm beneath the skin surface, including the stratum corneum and viable epidermis regions. This technique allows for the reconstruction of internal and external fingerprint images from grayscale data. However, existing fingerprint extraction methods heavily rely on contour features and current 2D approaches overlook the spatial continuity of biometric features in OCT images. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel iterative algorithm for internal and external fingerprint extraction from OCT images. This algorithm incorporates the spatial continuity of OCT slice images and an iterative feature enhancement module during the prediction phase to improve segmentation continuity. Additionally, a soft label technique is employed to reduce contour dependence and mitigate interference from noise and anomaly interference. Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate significant improvements in segmentation accuracy with higher fingerprint quality, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Iris Liveness Detection Competition (LivDet-Iris) – The 2025 Edition
authors: Mahsa Mitcheff, Afzal Hossain, Samuel Webster, Siamul Khan, Katarzyna Roszczewska, Juan E. Tapia, Fabian Stockhardt, Lázaro J. González-Soler, Ji-Young Lim, Mirko Pollok, Felix Kreuzer, Caiyong Wang, Lin Li, Fukang Guo, Jiayin Gu, Debasmita Pal, Parisa Farmanifard, Renu Sharma, Arun Ross, Geetanjali Sharma, Shubham Ashwani, Aditya Nigam, Raghavendra Ramachandra, Lambert Igene, Jesse Dykes, Ada Sawilska, Aleksandra Dzieniszewska, Jakub Januszkiewicz, Ewelina Bartuzi-Trokielewicz, Alicja Martinek, Mateusz Trokielewicz, Adrian Kordas, Kevin Bowyer, Stephanie Schuckers, Adam Czajka
publish: 2025 IEEE International Joint Conference on Biometrics (IJCB)
abstract: LivDet-Iris 2025 is the sixth edition of the iris liveness detection competition. Held every two to three years, the competition aims to foster the development of robust algorithms capable of detecting a wide range of physically-and digitally-presented attacks in iris biometrics. The 2025 edition obtained the largest number of submissions in the history of the competition: ten algorithms from five institutions, and one commercial iris recognition system. LivDet-Iris 2025 also introduced new tasks compared to previous editions: (Task 1) a benchmark offered by an industry partner, (Task 2) morphed iris images, in which two different-identity samples were blended into one image, and (Task 3) evaluation of presentation attack detection robustness against advanced manufacturing techniques for textured contact lenses. This edition, for the first time in the series, offers a systematic testing of a commercial iris recognition system (software and hardware) using physical artifacts presented to the sensor. Dermalog-Iris team submitted algorithms that won all tasks, achieving the area under the ROC curve of 90.57%, 68.23% and 99.99% in tasks 1, 2, and 3, respectively. Additionally, we include results for baseline algorithms, based on modern deep convolutional neural networks and trained with all available public datasets of iris images representing bona fide samples and anomalies (physical attacks, eye diseases, post-mortem cases, and synthetically-generated iris images). Test samples created for tasks 2 and 3, and baseline models are made available to offer the state-of-the-art benchmark for iris liveness detection.

title: A 1ppm/°C and ±0.066% 3σ Accuracy Bandgap Reference with Temperature-Adaptive PTAT Scaling
authors: Jiaqing Rui, Liangjian Lyu, Yuting Wan, Kexin Shan, Wenxian Gu, C.-J. Richard Shi, Xing Wu
abstract: This paper presents a process-independent, curvature-compensated bandgap reference. By introducing a temperature-adaptive duty-cycled resistor to scale the PTAT voltage, the design effectively compensates for CTAT nonlinearity without introducing offset errors. Fabricated in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> CMOS, the prototype chip occupies <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.068 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> and consumes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex>. After two-point trimming, it achieves an average temperature coefficient of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \text{ppm} /{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and a 3 o output accuracy within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.066 {\%}$</tex>.

title: Body-Interfaced Biosensors
authors: Wei Gao
abstract: The rise of personalized medicine is transforming healthcare through predictive and tailored strategies. I will present our progress on wearable, implantable, and ingestible biosensors for real-time molecular analysis across diverse body fluids. These scalable, nanomaterialbased systems enable continuous monitoring of metabolites, hormones, and drugs, with applications in stress, nutrition, chronic disease, and personalized therapy.

title: 10.2 A Dynamic Performance Augmentation in a 3nm-Plus Mobile CPU
authors: Chien-Yu Lu, Bo-Jr Huang, Sung S.-Y. Hsueh, Trong-Hieu Tran, Eric Jia-Wei Fang, Chao-Yang Yeh, Quan Sun, Tao Chen, Hsinchen Chen, Huaichung Chang, C.-J. Tsai, Yi-Chang Zhuang, Barry Chen, Ericbill Wang, Hugh Mair, Shih-Arn Hwang
abstract: This work presents dynamic mobile-performance augmentation (DMPA), featuring boosting-duty control (BDC) and an adaptive thermal cooler (ATC) to augment CPU maximum performance. The BDC uses an aging sensor (A-sensor) to leverage an aging budget for performance boosting. For thermal, the ATC integrates temperature sensors (T-sensors) across the CPU and works with multiple sensors and a throttler to optimize clock speed effectively cooling hotspots. The DMPA offers a score of 3917 in the GeekBenchv6 single-core benchmark on a flagship smartphone, consuming 0.078% or less total power, while occupying only 0.0122% of the CPU area.
